title: Stability of low voltage hygroscopic insulator P3HT transistors
abstract: Hygroscopic insulator field effect transistors require moisture for low voltage operation. However, long term exposure to moisture degrades transistor performance.

title: Non-halogen solvent processed all-polymer organic solar cells with improved vertical phase morphology
abstract: Layer-by-layer PSC based on a PM1/PYF-T- o system was achieved for the first time using a non-halogenated solvent, and the vertical phase morphology was further improved by adding PBDB-TF into the donor layer.

title: Novel plasmonic–photonic Janus films with aluminum nanoisland-coated colloidal arrays for versatile applications
abstract: Novel plasmonic–photonic Janus films are developed by introducing aluminum nanoislands on the colloidal photonic crystal (CPC) arrays, which can be used as advanced rewritable papers and express waybills.

title: Electric control of Chern number in valley-polarized quantum anomalous Hall insulators
abstract: The Chern number of VP-QAH insulators can be effectively modulated from C = −1 to C = −3 by applying out-of-plane electric fields.

title: Tuning thermomechanical properties of hydrogen-bonded materials by using a mixed cocrystal approach
abstract: The thermal and thermomechanical behavior of multi-component organic solids is tuned at the molecular level via preparation of mixed cocrystals.

title: Excellent energy storage performance in cross-linked polyimide dielectrics with positively charged cross-linking points
abstract: The introduction of cross-linking structures into PI inhibits carrier transport, which improves the breakdown strength of PI, reduces the dielectric loss, and ultimately improves the energy storage performance of PI at high temperatures.

title: Rapid stress release through thiol–thioester exchange in phenylic crosslinked polymers
abstract: This study presents a method to mitigate distortion of crosslinked films through photochemical crosslinking and catalytic thiol–thioester exchange. Bond rearrangement is catalysed by base liberated on photolysis of a photobase.

title: The effect of machining-generated residual stress on the properties of single crystal piezoelectric layers in high-frequency ultrasonic transducers
abstract: Machining-induced residual stress degraded ultrathin (∼100 μm) PIN-PMN-PT SC sheets performance. High-temperature annealing post-thinning effectively eliminates stress, mitigating adverse effects.

title: Rational molecular doping to enhance interfacial carrier dynamics for reliable hole transport layer-free perovskite solar cells
abstract: The electron affinity of TCNQ and F4TCNQ is pivotal in enhancing the interfacial charge collection and optimizing the energy-level alignment of hole transport layer (HTL)-free inverted perovskite solar cells.

title: High-efficiency blade-coated organic solar cells enabled via an in situ nitrogen-blowing and heating strategy
abstract: A nitrogen-blowing and heating blade-coating is developed to fabricate high-performance large-area organic solar cells (OSCs), with significant potential in cost-effective industrial-scale production.

title: Wireless, flexible, and disposable sensing devices enabling real-time long-term patient medical care for pressure injury prevention
abstract: Compressible conducting networks inside the porous paper matrix built by multilayered gradient distribution of CNT–PEDOT composites realize a wireless, flexible, and disposable piezoresistive pressure sensor promising for pressure injury prevention.

title: Photochromic and photoluminescence modulation properties of Zr 6 Nb 2 O 17 ceramics doped with a variety of rare earth ions
abstract: By controlling the excitation energy of luminescent centers, we regulated the photochromic contrast and photoluminescence modulation ratio of the materials.

title: Tunable photoluminescence, visible-light excitation, and high quantum yield in scalable copper cluster halide emitters for optoelectronics
abstract: A bromide–iodide alloyed copper cluster phosphor, TPA 2 [Cu 4 Br 2 I 4 ], exhibits visible-light excitation, near-unity quantum yield (∼95%), and dual emission from cis / trans isomers, demonstrating excellent processability and scalability.

title: Constructing bimetallic conjugated coordination polymers as efficient electrocatalysts for water splitting
abstract: This study constructed bimetallic Co x Ni 1− x –DHBQs with dual active sites and examined their potential as both OER and HER electrocatalysts. The optimized Co 1/3 Ni 2/3 –DHBQ demonstrated outstanding activities surpassing many reported materials.

title: Fabrication of lightweight multifunctional microwave-absorbing composites with load-bearing and infrared stealth capabilities using a modified 3D integrated hollow E-glass fiber
abstract: A lightweight multifunctional microwave-absorbing composite with load-bearing and infrared stealth capabilities. The unique structural design and material composition lead to an effective absorption bandwidth of 11.65 GHz.

title: Ferroelectricity in tetragonal polycrystalline ceramics: matrix transformation of electric dipoles
abstract: The constitutive relationships between electric dipole at unit cell scale and macroscopic ferroelectricity are revealed by the proposed matrix transformation model, where the theoretical S – E and P – E loops are coincident with the experimental results.

title: A highly stretchable thermoelectric generator developed from polyaniline-based nanocomposites for body heat harvesting
abstract: A highly stretchable thermoelectric generator developed from polymer-based nanocomposites shows promising functionality as a body heat harvester to improve wearable electronics.

title: Transforming Mo 0.5 W 0.5 O 3 to MoS 2 : leveraging selective sulfurization for enhanced electrocatalysis
abstract: A selective sulfurization strategy for the development of 2D layered materials and heterostructures is presented.

title: Correction: Space charge-induced electrofluorochromic behavior for C12-BTBT-based thin-film devices
abstract: Correction for ‘Space charge-induced electrofluorochromic behavior for C12-BTBT-based thin-film devices’ by Yuanwei Zhu et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/d5tc00564g.

title: Tailored ligand design enabling comprehensive passivation of perovskite nanocrystals for light-emitting diodes
abstract: Rational ligand design enabled comprehensive passivation of perovskite nanocrystals, boosting LED performance, and underscoring its transformative potential.

title: Enhancing the electronic-coupling and conductivity of a monolayer film of ferrocenyl molecules by Pd and Cu doping
abstract: Improving the conductivity and modulating the electronic structure of molecular films holds significance in using them in thin film-based electronic applications. We have improved the conductivity of films of ferrocenyl molecules with Pd/Cu doping.

title: Thermally driven morphology and lattice transformation of NiCo alloy for accelerating electrocatalytic water splitting
abstract: Temperature promoted water splitting involves HER and OER. In the HER process, the dendritic NiCo alloy catalyst changed into nanosheets with nanowires. In the OER process, the dendritic catalyst changed into nanosheets and nanoparticles.

title: Femtosecond laser direct writing of CsPb(I/Br) 3 quantum dot films for displays and holography
abstract: To expand the application of perovskite quantum dots (QDs) in next-generation displays and holography, it is quite significant to develop an efficient and advanced QD patterning technology.

title: Homotypic heterojunction of metal oxygen/sulfide as an electron transport layer for high-performance perovskite solar cells
abstract: SnS 2 nanosheets are introduced into a SnO 2 film to prepare a SnS 2 –SnO 2 homotypic heterojunction (HHJ) as an efficient ETL, which modulate the electron transport and energy level arrangement and thus improve the device performance.

title: Carmine–PVA composites as optical limiters: effect of carmine concentration and the role of laser intensity in optical limiting
abstract: Carmine–PVA composite exhibiting reverse saturable absorption when placed in a Z-scan setup.

title: 3D-printed organic electrochemical transistors on microfluidic paper for multianalyte point-of-care testing
abstract: This study represents the first successful integration of three-dimensional microfluidic paper-based devices with fully printed OECTs, resulting in the development of a low-cost, flexible, and user-friendly multi-analyte biosensing platform.

title: Enhancing photocatalytic hydrogen evolution of metal sulfides via a Co-CdS/Ni 5 P 4 ohmic junction
abstract: This study constructs an ohmic junction between Co-doped CdS and Ni 5 P 4 nanoflowers to boost photocatalytic hydrogen evolution. The junction facilitates electron migration and offers abundant active sites, enabling a sustainable hydrogen production.

title: Electric field-induced intramolecular charge transfer of two-dimensional covalent organic frameworks with D–A systems for organic memristors
abstract: We obtained better stability (550 cycles) using COFs that are rapidly synthesized at room temperature and pressure. Furthermore, we experimentally confirmed the resistance mechanism as electric field-induced intramolecular charge transfer.

title: Multimode luminescence and highly anti-thermal quenching in Sb 3+ /Yb 3+ /Pr 3+ Co-doped Cs 2 NaYCl 6 double perovskites
abstract: The down-shifting emission and the up-conversion emission in Sb 3+ /Yb 3+ /Pr 3+ co-doped Cs 2 NaYCl 6 double perovskites show excellent multimode luminescence and anti-thermal quenching behavior with increasing temperature.

title: Advancing thermoplasmonic sensing: gold nanobipyramids for enhanced light-to-heat conversion
abstract: The in-depth evaluation of the thermoplasmonic performances of gold nanobipyramids in colloidal solution and immobilized on a filter paper substrate revealed their ability for efficient thermoplasmonic detection of simple and complex molecules.

title: Ferroelectric to relaxor crossover in Zr-doped BaTiO 3 -based ceramics and its consequences for the electrocaloric effect
abstract: Zr-doping at the B-site of 0.7BaTiO 3 -0.3Na 0.5 Bi 0.5 TiO 3 solid solution causes a crossover from normal ferroelectric to relaxor ferroelectric behaviour and increases the T span of the electrocaloric effect.

title: Room temperature multiferroicity and magnetoelectric coupling in Ca/Mn-modified BaTiO 3
abstract: Materials with magnetoelectric coupling (MEC) between ferroic orders at room temperature are an emerging field in modern technology and physics.

title: A high-performance infrared photodetector based on GeTe films and In/Ag bimetallic electrodes
abstract: Photodetectors utilizing the annealed crystalline GeTe films were developed. This research demonstrates the feasibility of employing GeTe films for infrared detection.

title: Multifunctional Bi 2 TeO 5 :Er 3+ with dual-stimuli photochromism and switchable upconversion for optical security
abstract: Er 3+ -doped Bi 2 TeO 5 ceramics combine reversible dual-stimuli photochromism with switchable upconversion luminescence, offering multifunctional applications in optical security, anti-counterfeiting, and latent fingerprint detection.

title: A first-principles study of the structural, electronic, and optical properties of NaF and CsPbX 3 heterojunctions under strain
abstract: Through first-principles calculations, a NaF/CsPbX 3 heterojunction is constructed, improving its stability. The effect of strain on the effective mass and absorption coefficient is studied, enhancing stability and optical absorption.

title: Evolution of intrinsic defect density in UV laser conditioning at the KDP crystal stress affected zone and its role in improving the laser induced damage threshold
abstract: Density evolution of intrinsic defects during UV laser conditioning is obtained via PL tests and verified by TCSPC. The mechanism of laser induced damage threshold increase by UV laser conditioning is revealed via first-principles calculations.

title: An ambient air sinterable Cu@Ag nanoparticle ink with exceptional oxidation resistance and stability for EMI shielding and printed RFID applications
abstract: Oxidation-resistant Cu@AgNPs were synthesized via galvanic replacement, and an ambient air sinterable ink was formulated for screen-printing. The resulting ink enabled highly conductive traces suitable for EMI shielding and RFID tag applications.

title: Recent progress in spiro-type hole transport materials for efficient and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: This review summarized the efficient spiro-type HTMs reported in the last 8 years, discussed their structure–property relationship and photovoltaic performance, and proposed the general principles for designing HTMs.

title: The experimental phase equilibria studies of the Ni–Sn–S system
abstract: It exhibits a miscibility gap, enabling the simultaneous recycling of Ni- and Sn-containing liquids in industrial pyrometallurgical reactors. Formation of two matte phases (Ni- and Sn-rich) allows separate refinement of each metal.

title: Acidochromic pyrimidine-based dyes for highly efficient dye-sensitized solar cells
abstract: A charge separation acidochromic dye, MOTS2P-Pyc, with an acidochromic core, is designed as a sensitizer for DSCs. Upon acid treatment, the spectral response extends into the infrared by 100 nm, and J SC increases from 10 to 18 mA cm −2 .

title: Highly directional and tunable mid-infrared transmission induced by resonant optical tunneling with VO 2
abstract: Schematic and performance of the proposed switchable band-pass filter, which comprises dual identical dielectric thin film stacks coupled to KRS-5 prisms and separated by a VO 2 thin film.

title: Long-lasting time-dependent tunable afterglow in Zn 2 SiO 4 :Mn 2+ glass ceramics for dynamic anti-counterfeiting
abstract: Phase-change-regulated dynamic time-dependent tunable persistent luminescence in α/β-Zn 2 SiO 4 :Mn 2+ embedded glass ceramics.

title: Intense and repeatable orange mechanoluminescence of Mn 2+ activated CaGa 4 O 7 for visualized mechanics sensing
abstract: This work reports the mechanoluminescence of CaGa 4 O 7 :Mn 2+ in terms of relatively long wavelength, high brightness, repeatability, and mechanical responsiveness, which can penetrate biological tissue for visualized biomechanics sensing.

title: DMF-mediated polycrystalline phase transition of Cd-based perovskites: dual-emission tuning for temperature sensing and single-component white LEDs
abstract: By systematically varying the DMF/HCl solvent ratio (0, 1, and 1.5), high-purity CsCdCl 3 , Cs 3 Cd 2 Cl 7 , and Cs 2 CdCl 4 phases were successfully obtained via DMF solvent modulation.

title: Optical nonlinearity of Janus material TaSSe and its applications in fiber lasers
abstract: TaSSe is a novel nanomaterial that, due to its excellent physical and photonic properties, finds extensive applications in areas such as metal-reinforced composite materials, microelectronics, and waveguide lasers.

title: A novel AlF 3 :Cr 3+ NIR phosphor with high thermal quenching stability for advanced pc-LEDs
abstract: Near-infrared phosphor-converted light-emitting diodes (pc-LEDs), as the core light source for smart broadband near-infrared spectroscopy, face critical challenges in balancing luminous efficiency and thermal stability.

title: Alloying strategy enables record-low melting temperature in 2D metal halide perovskites
abstract: This study demonstrates the applicability of the alloying strategy to reduce melting temperature ( T m ) and opens the way for advancing melt-processing techniques and the applications of two-dimensional metal halide perovskites (2D MHPs).

title: Waste-heat harvesting using a thermoelectric generator coupled with a hygroscopic hydrogel for use in the energy industry
abstract: An integrated thermal management system containing a TEG and a hygroscopic hydrogel was proposed in this work. The TEG can capture waste heat and convert it into electricity with the assistance of hygroscopic hydrogel.

title: Side-chain engineering of two-dimensional polymer thin films for high-performance organic non-volatile memories
abstract: The impact of side chains on the memory performance of charge-trapping layers in 2D polymer thin films with identical backbones but differing side chains was investigated.

title: A significant self-heating effect in high-power high-speed organic field-effect transistor arrays
abstract: The significant self-heating of high-power high-speed organic field-effect transistors was investigated, and an effective thermal management method was proposed.

title: Dual ligand modification enabling bright and stable CsPbBr 2.8 Cl 0.2 nanoplatelets in polar solvents
abstract: The nanoplatelets in polar solvents are created with the assistance of HCl acid and O -diphenylphosphinylhydroxylamine. And they can remain optical property after 15 days and 80% of emission intensity after 1 hour of UV irradiation.

title: Hybrid piezoelectricity enabled flexible AZO/SiC thin films sensor with a high output voltage and broad temperature range
abstract: A high-temperature resistant flexible AZO/SiC pressure sensor is proposed. The sensor presenting high sensitivity, fast response and high reliability, the incorporation of SiC enhances charge transfer capability and conductivity of AZO.

title: Brominated trityl radical nanoparticles: metal-free ratiometric nanothermometer with near-infrared excimeric emission in water
abstract: Metal-free Br-ONPs doped with brominated trityl radicals exhibit dual NIR-emission and excimer formation, enabling water-dispersible ratiometric nanothermometry for advanced bioimaging.

title: Novel PDI-C 8 /paraffin fluorescent materials with phase-change modulated luminescence for enhanced anti-counterfeiting security feature
abstract: PDI-C 8 /paraffin fluorescent materials for anti-counterfeiting identification are reported. The anti-counterfeiting identification feature is influenced by aggregation-induced emission modulated through temperature and concentration regulation.

title: Largely enhanced bulk photovoltaic effects in a two-dimensional MoSi 2 N 4 monolayer photodetector by vacancy-doping and bending-increased device asymmetry
abstract: Two-dimensional MoSi 2 N 4 monolayer semiconductors have garnered significant research attention for ultraviolet (UV) photodetection due to their outstanding performance, including ultrafast response, high responsivity, and low dark current.

title: Er 3+ -sensitized upconversion emission of Tm 3+ via Yb 3+ -mediated energy migration for nanothermometry
abstract: A ratiometric luminescent nanothermometer with high sensitivity is developed with Er 3+ -sensitized upconversion emission of Tm 3+ via Yb 3+ -mediated energy migration.

title: Phonon bottleneck limits vortex-induced quasiparticle relaxation in epitaxial NbN thin films
abstract: Vortex instabilities in epitaxial NbN films reveal quasiparticle recombination times of 10–80 ps. A universal phonon bottleneck emerges as the intrinsic limit for ultrafast superconducting devices.

title: Stable partial dislocation complexes in GaN investigated by molecular dynamics and first-principles simulations
abstract: Formation of stable partial dislocation complexes in GaN is shown via molecular dynamics simulations. These complexes introduce energy levels in the band gap and may be considered for modifying the charge carrier lifetime for THz device applications.

title: Improving voltage and quantum efficiency in blade-coated ITO-free organic solar cells processed with a non-halogenated solvent
abstract: ITO-free OSCs (PM6:Y6) blade-coated with o -xylene show enhanced donor–acceptor separation and trap density, leading to limited V OC . Acceptor side chain modification enhances the morphology and reduces traps, boosting photovoltaic performance.

title: Prolonging the lifetime of quasi-2D perovskite blue LEDs via DMAcPA doping for defect passivation
abstract: Incorporation of DMAcPA into quasi-2D perovskite films enables significant trap passivation, enhanced crystallinity, and extended operational lifetimes in efficient deep-blue and sky-blue PeLEDs.

title: Centimeter-level double perovskite single crystals with strong interlaminar hydrogen bonds for high-performance X-ray detection
abstract: Strong interlaminar O–H⋯O hydrogen bonds are created to eliminate the van der Waals gap in 2D double perovskites, which facilitates the growth of large crystals toward high performance X-ray detection.

title: Computational design of GUA 0.5 X 0.5 SrI 3 (X = Cs, K) perovskites: exploring the potential for optoelectronic applications
abstract: The structural, electronic, and optical properties of GUA 0.5 X 0.5 SrI 3 (X = Cs, K) halide perovskite materials were theoretically analyzed using the CASTEP software package, which employs density functional theory (DFT).

title: Active optical modulation in hybrid transparent-conductive oxide/electro-optic multilayers
abstract: Low-voltage-enabled modulation of the optical response at room temperature in the IR spectral range has been detected by means of in operando spectroscopic ellipsometry in a Al-doped ZnO/BaTiO 3 bilayer deposited on Nb-doped SrTiO 3 substrate.

title: The physics and promise of phase change memory in non-von Neumann computing and machine learning
abstract: As artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) push the limits of conventional computing architectures, the need for high-speed, energy-efficient, and scalable memory technologies has become paramount.

title: Improved neuromorphic functionality in organic electrochemical transistors using crosslinked-polyvinyl alcohol for fast ion transport and its application to Pavlovian transistors
abstract: Cross-linked polyvinyl alcohol (CX-PVA) enhances ion transport in OECTs, boosting transconductance, switching speed, and synaptic plasticity, thereby advancing neuromorphic computing and bioelectronic applications.

title: Allyl-terminated small molecule donors effectively regulate miscibility to improve the performance of organic solar cells
abstract: Optimization of small molecule donor morphology in OSCs: allyl group-driven enhancements in H35 for superior PCEs of 15.21% (ASM) and 10.78% (SMD:PA) via improved miscibility, domain structure, and charge transfer.

title: Superior lithium storage performance of an Fe 3 O 4 anode encapsulated by dual-layered interwoven carbon nanostructures using a facile one-step pyrolysis approach
abstract: Magnetite (Fe 3 O 4 ) has garnered significant attention as a promising anode material for next-generation lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) due to its cost-effectiveness, high theoretical capacity, and environmental sustainability.

title: Introduction of sidewall CN: a high-temperature-resistant COF for lithium-ion storage
abstract: COF-HHTP-CN was synthesized with CN modified on the sidewall, which displayed superior structure stability during pyrolysis, generating an advanced LIB anode.

title: Synthesis of layered double hydroxide derivative-decorated nitrogen-doped graphene composite aerogels with a unique hierarchical porous network structure for microwave absorption
abstract: Hierarchical porous NRGO/NiO/Co 3 O 4 microwave absorbing materials can reach a reflection loss of −59.77 dB and an effective absorption bandwidth of 6.8 GHz at a low filling ratio of only 3 wt%.

title: Interface exciplex formation in TADF organic light-emitting transistors
abstract: Field-effect driven formation of both bulk and interface excitons in thermally-activated delayed fluorescence-based organic light emitting transistors (TADF-OLETs) as a tool to tune the wavelength of generated light.

title: Differentiation between bulk and interfacial properties: analysis of time-dependent carrier injection in perovskite solar cells
abstract: Time-dependent carrier injection characters in perovskite solar cells are investigated by excitation power dependence on photoluminescence lifetime measurements to differentiate bulk and heterointerface properties in PSCs.

title: Optical and optoelectronic properties of 2D, quasi-2D and 3D metal halide perovskites
abstract: Metal halide perovskites have emerged as highly promising semiconductor materials owing to their versatility across a broad spectrum of optoelectronic applications. Through dimensional engineering one can tune their properties to facilitate their use in specific device applications.

title: Ferroelectrically gated sub-6 nm monolayer MoS 2 transistors for high-performance and low-power applications
abstract: A schematic device model and the corresponding transfer characteristics with upward ( P ↑ ) and downward ( P ↓ ) polarizations of BiAlO 3 at V b = 0.64 V are presented.

title: Oxygen incorporation in YBa 2 Cu 3 O 7− δ thin films: surface activation and degradation
abstract: We studied oxygen kinetics in YBa 2 Cu 3 O 7− δ thin films using electrical probes and found strong degradation of the surface kinetics, which can be bypassed via the catalytic activity of silver and allows for improved oxygenation treatments.

title: Anion ratio-directed design of CaB 4 O 5 F 4 and CaB 6 O 8 F 4 : [BO 3 ]/[BO 2 F 2 ] hybridization tailoring deep-ultraviolet nonlinear optical performance
abstract: This article predicts two new types of DUV NLO fluoroborates: CaB 4 O 5 F 4 ([BO 3 ]/[BO 2 F 2 ] = 1) and CaB 6 O 8 F 4 (ratio = 2).

title: Negative differential resistance and transport regularity in aromatic cyclo[ n ]carbon-based ( n = 4 k + 2) molecular devices
abstract: Large-diameter cyclo[ n ]carbon-based devices exhibit the negative differential resistance (NDR) effect. The magnitude, position, and number of NDR peaks are remarkably affected by the number of carbon atoms and the type of electrode.

title: Ag 2 S thin films with optimized thickness fabricated by thermal sulfurization for near-infrared photodetectors
abstract: Ag 2 S photodetector demonstrates a detection range from 405 to 980 nm, achieving ultrafast response times (42.9/32.6 μs) and a detectivity of 2.39 × 10 12 jones at 638 nm. DLTS reveals that silver and sulfur vacancies influence the carrier dynamics.

title: Structural and thermal photobehaviour characterizations of (DPA) 2 MnBr 4 : an environmentally friendly organic–inorganic hybrid metal halide perovskite with reversible emission colour switching for anti-counterfeiting and solvent vapour photosensing
abstract: A novel Mn 2+ -based perovskite was synthesized and characterized. The results provide direct evidence regarding its phase transition, showing dual emission, which can be used in encryption. Green emission has been exploited for down-converter LEDs.

title: Optical frequency-comb generation in phase-locked random lasers using MAPbI 3 nanocrystals
abstract: Optical frequency-comb generation based on cascaded four-wave mixing, along with the phase-locked random lasing (RL), was achieved in a microdisk consisting of MAPbI 3 nanocrystalline particles randomly distributed in shape and size.

title: Anti-fatigue, self-bonding, adhesive gels for easy-to-prepare 3D stacking flexible electronics
abstract: A polymer gel with tissue-compatible modulus, excellent fatigue resistance, minimal strain hysteresis, robust self-bonding and skin adhesion, and superior insulation, which can be used for developing compact, high-performance flexible electronics.

title: Activated circularly polarized luminescence in a chiral supramolecular template triggered by electrostatic and arene–perfluoroarene interactions
abstract: D/L-cC3-Py1-OFN displayed pronounced CPL signals using a pre-established supramolecular template through electrostatic and AP interactions, providing a simple and facile approach to the generation of CPL activity.

title: Revealing key surface contaminants via stack-flipping strategy: investigating rinsing protocols for clean graphene transfer and enhanced electrical performance
abstract: For PMMA-assisted wet transfer of CVD-grown graphene, the surface cleanliness can be effectively assessed by examining key carbon-based contaminants on the flipped PMMA/graphene surface following treatment with different rinsing solutions.

title: Impacts of linking units of porphyrin dimer donors on the performance of organic solar cells
abstract: In this study, we synthesized three dimer donors utilizing different linking units and discovered that these linking units significantly impact their photovoltaic performance.

title: Performance improvement of resistive switching memory achieved by reducing the size of MoS 2 embedded in poly(vinyl alcohol) films
abstract: By ultrasonic-assisted liquid-phase exfoliation, MoS 2 nanosheets were reduced in size to quantum dots to obtain MoS 2 QDs. And they were embedded in PVA to construct RRAM devices to explore the effect of MoS 2 size reduction on RS performance.

title: Tetrabromobenzene-based molecular alloys – a tool for tailoring the temperature of the thermosalient phase transition
abstract: The temperature of the thermosalient phase transition can be tuned within a range of 15 °C by varying the TBB content by 8% in the molecular TBB–TCB alloys.

title: Defect passivation of SnO 2 doped with 2-FN for high-performance perovskite detectors
abstract: Tin dioxide (SnO 2 ) is a widely used electron transport layer (ETL) in perovskite detectors, and optimizing the ETL is a common strategy to enhance detector performance.

title: High-density, ultraflexible organic electrochemical transistor array for brain activity mapping
abstract: Scalable high-density OECT arrays are designed to precisely map and record electrocorticogram signals, facilitating high-resolution brain activity monitoring.

title: Photo-driven all-2D van der Waals metal–semiconductor field-effect transistors for high-performance photodetection
abstract: A photo-driven all-2D vdWs MESFET utilizing NbSe 2 /MoS 2 heterostructures achieves concurrent optimization by decoupling gain and carrier lifetime, delivering high responsivity (9.56 A W −1 ), fast response (206 μs), and ultralow dark current (2.9 pA).

title: Multifunctional organic artificial optoelectronic synapses for neuromorphic computing and a weak-light-sensitive visual system
abstract: The artificial optoelectronic synaptic device based on the PBDB-T-2F:BTP-eC9 heterojunction demonstrates multifunctional characteristics through synergistic photonic-electrical co-modulation.

title: Role of Br–Cl distribution uniformity on the spectral stability of blue emitting mixed-halide perovskites
abstract: Our findings show that the spectral stability of the quasi-2D perovskite system is mainly affected by the uniformity of the Br–Cl distribution, rather than defects.

title: Correction: Binder polymer influence on the electrical and UV response of organic field-effect transistors
abstract: Correction for ‘Binder polymer influence on the electrical and UV response of organic field-effect transistors’ by Jinghai Li et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2023, 11 , 8178–8185, https://doi.org/10.1039/D2TC05066H.

title: Highly sensitive ratiometric luminescence manometers based on the multisite emission of Cr 3+
abstract: In this work it has been shown how materials with multisite Cr 3+ ion emission can be used for remote pressure sensing.

title: Efficient Sr 2 Ta 2 O 7 :Bi 3+ ,Ln 3+ (Ln 3+ = Eu 3+ ,Sm 3+ ) phosphors for optical thermometry and anti-counterfeiting
abstract: A series of novel optical thermometers with an optimal relative sensitivity of 2.98% K −1 were designed based on the luminescence of the Bi 3+ and Ln 3+ ions with different temperature sensitivities.

title: Ferroelectric polarization in 2D halide hybrid perovskites: influence on bulk crystals, thin films, and applications
abstract: Halide hybrid perovskite ferroelectrics can exist in bulk (single- or polycrystalline) and thin-film forms, exhibiting multifunctional properties suitable for applications in energy harvesting, sensing, and memory devices.

title: Enhancing molecular aggregation and decreasing the optical gap by a dual-additive to reduce the energy loss of all-polymer organic solar cells
abstract: 1. The introduction of dual-additive extend the aggregation time of PYIT; 2. A better vertical phase separation structure has been formed; 3. E loss : 0.500–0.476 eV; PCE: 14.58–16.67%.

title: Hot carrier cooling and direct observation of electron–phonon coupling in two-dimensional butylammonium lead iodide perovskites
abstract: Two-dimensional butylammonium lead halide perovskite thin films were photoexcited using 390 nm pulses. The instantaneously-generated hot charge carriers relax to the band edge with a time constant of ∼0.4 ps by emitting low-frequency optical phonon.

title: Magnetic iongel/carbon nanotube composites for ultra-broadband electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: The C 12 IMSO 3 [FeCl 4 ]/CNT composite attenuates electromagnetic waves through multiple dielectric and magnetic loss mechanisms, achieving an effective absorption bandwidth (EAB) of 7.44 GHz.

title: Construction of an MoSe 2 /MoS 2 nanosheet heterostructure for enhanced sodium storage performance
abstract: An MoSe 2 /MoS 2 heterostructure composed of ultrasmall MoSe 2 nanosheets embedded in MoS 2 nanosheets was designed. Benefitting from the rich well-matched heterointerfaces between MoS 2 and MoSe 2 , it showed an excellent sodium storage performance.

title: Hierarchically structured conducting polymer hydrogels with enhanced stretchability and conductivity via freeze-casting and salting-out with oxidant salts
abstract: A stretchable electrically conductive hydrogel was developed by combining the use of a unique salt with both oxidizing and salting-out abilities and directional freezing to polymerize a conducting polymer into a hierarchically structured hydrogel.

title: Optimization of sensing sensitivity and coherence properties of spin defects in hexagonal boron nitride
abstract: The experimental results provide a framework for optimizing the spin properties of the V B − ensemble in hBN as layered quantum sensors and offer insights into the mechanisms that limit the coherent properties.

title: Phonon softening enhanced superconductivity in the YTiSi electride under pressure
abstract: YTiSi possesses localized interstitial electrons and can be regarded as a typical zero-dimensional electride. Its electron–phonon coupling and superconductivity can be enhanced by pressure-induced phonon softening.

title: Ultra-high energy density in all-organic copolymeric blends by grain refinement
abstract: Grain refinement and phase transition engineering in all-organic P(VDF-HFP)/P(VDF-CTFE) blends lead to ultra-high energy density and enhanced breakdown strength for advanced dielectric capacitors.

title: Light-assisted Schottky barrier enhancement for selective detection of l -cysteine using Ni/Cu 2 O/Ag nanocubes
abstract: The interfacial energy barriers at Cu 2 O/Ag drive the electrochemical response to l -cysteine, while illumination further enhances the tunability of the energy barrier.

title: Neuroevolution potential-driven accurate and efficient discovery of mechanical behavior and nanocluster dynamics in TiAl alloys
abstract: High-accuracy simulation of TiAl alloys achieved with a neuroevolution potential trained on diverse structures.

title: Lower temperature and solvent dual response induced reversible information encryption and decryption hydrogels with host–guest recognition regulation
abstract: Ad groups are introduced into Ad-PNIPAm as hydrophobic guest molecules by free radical polymerization, realizing the temperature response, solvent response and host–guest recognition of the Ad-PNIPAm gel, which is suitable for information processing.

title: Synergy of sp 2 -hybridized carbon doping and photogenerated surface oxygen vacancies for the enhanced photocatalytic performance of BiOCl and solvent effects
abstract: Here, predominantly sp 2 hybridized carbon-doped BiOCl was constructed. sp 2 carbon doping enhances light utilization, offers new adsorption sites and accelerates hole transport, showing a synergistic effect with photogenerated oxygen defects.

title: High corrosion resistance and electromagnetic shielding properties of Ni–P@C f paper by a chemical plating process
abstract: Pd-free electroless Ni–P coating on carbon fiber paper achieves 89.8 dB EMI shielding (X-band) and 455 S cm −1 conductivity at 0.2 mm thickness, with 73.5 dB retention after saltwater corrosion, enabling lightweight marine electronic shielding.

title: Regulating Förster resonance energy transfer and cascade energy offset achieves 19.6% efficiency in ternary organic solar cells
abstract: The energy transfer between IDIC and Y6 improves the photoluminescence quenching efficiency and promotes effective charge transport/extraction within the ternary blend, resulting in a PCE of 19.67% for the ternary organic solar cell.

title: High quality white light emission from Sb 3+ , Tb 3+ and Mn 2+ co-doped Cs 2 NaScCl 6 double perovskites
abstract: The high quality white light was obtained by co-doping Sb 3+ , Tb 3+ , Mn 2+ in Cs 2 NaScCl 6 double perovskite.

title: Hydrogen bonding-driven structural modulation in narrowband green-emitting Mn 2+ hybrids for enhanced X-ray imaging performance
abstract: Schematic illustrations show the crystal structures and H-bonding of [MePi]MnBr 4 and [EtPi]MnBr 4 , highlighting their differences. The [EtPi]MnBr 4 -based film, its improved properties, and X-ray imaging application are also presented.

title: An ultra-low dark current, high-performance photodetector based on CVD-grown Bi 2 TeO 5
abstract: This study successfully prepared low-dimensional Bi 2 TeO 5 nanosheets via CVD and developed a high-performance photodetector based on this material, indicating that Bi 2 TeO 5 nanosheets have significant application potential in the field of photodetection.

title: First-principles study of metal and ligand substitution effects on EUV absorption and electron energy loss
abstract: TDDFT calculations reveal that iodine substitution in metal–organic photoresists enhances EUV absorption and reduces the inelastic mean free path of low-energy electrons, and significance of electronic structure effects is demonstrated.

title: Unveiling dual-pathway H 2 O 2 activation in a MgFe 2 O 4 /CNT composite for enhanced electrocatalytic degradation of phenol
abstract: MgFe 2 O 4 /CNT electrocatalyst degrades 99% phenol in 30 minutes by enhancing electron transfer, defect sites, and active oxygen, boosting H 2 O 2 generation and its conversion to ˙OH via homogeneous/heterogeneous activation.

title: Crosslinking site sharing-driven interface engineering to enhance adhesion between PDMS substrates and Ag–PDMS conductors
abstract: Adhesion at the polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS)–conductor interface was improved via crosslinking site sharing, and polymer chain tuning of the PDMS substrate revealed key factors for interfacial crosslinking and favorable adhesion conditions.

title: Zinc oxide-decorated MIL-53(Al)-derived porous carbon for supercapacitor devices
abstract: MIL-53(Al)-derived carbon decorated with ZnO was used for supercapacitors. The channel-like morphology, bimodal porosity, and defect-rich C structure lead to energy and power densities up to 30.5 W h kg −1 and 388 kW kg −1 , and coulombic efficiencies >95%.

title: Molecular spectroscopies with semiconductor metasurfaces: towards dual optical/chemical SERS
abstract: Semiconductor metasurfaces enable dual SERS enhancement by combining strong optical resonances with charge-transfer interactions, offering a powerful platform for highly sensitive molecular detection.

title: A p-type doping strategy for a hole-transport polymer in blue perovskite light-emitting diodes
abstract: The doping of F 4 TCNQ in the TFB hole transport layer significantly enhances the hole mobility and optimizes the HOMO energy level alignment for blue PeLEDs.

title: Machine learning guided design of Ce-rich rare earth permanent magnets with outstanding comprehensive magnetic properties
abstract: This work pioneers the integration of single- and multi-objective machine learning using Pareto frontier analysis for sustainable permanent magnet design.

title: Chemical conversion of recovered carbon black (rCB) from end-of-life tires (ELTs) pyrolysis to reduced graphene oxide (rGO): from waste to advanced materials
abstract: Diagrammatic representation of the chemical transformation of recovered carbon black (rCB) obtained from the pyrolysis of end-of-life tires (ELTs) into reduced graphene oxide (rGO).

title: Experimental and theoretical insights into light confinement within 2D waveguides of alkylphenyl benzothiadiazole crystals
abstract: In this work, an experimental and theoretical investigation was carried out to explore the key factors influencing light transmission mechanisms in organic crystalline materials.

title: Broadband and warm white emission in Cs 2 In 1− x Cl 5 ·H 2 O: x Ag + phosphors enabled by H 3 PO 2 -mediated stabilization
abstract: This study presents Cs 2 InCl 5 ·H 2 O, a material that achieves broadband white-light emission through the doping of Ag + and the strategic incorporation of H 3 PO 2 .

title: Temperature-responsive hydrogels with adaptive coloration and superior mechanical performance
abstract: The AP-β-Py hydrogel offers temperature-responsive color change, >1000% stretchability, strong adhesion, puncture resistance, and self-recovery, making it a robust, multifunctional material for diverse advanced applications.

title: Iodide substituted halide-rich lithium argyrodite solid electrolytes with improved performance for all solid-state batteries
abstract: Cl–I mixed halide argyrodites studied via DFT and AIMD simulations, demonstrate enhanced stability and comparable ionic conductivities to Cl–Br argyrodites, with Li 5.5 PS 4.5 Cl 0.75 I 0.75 exhibiting a high conductivity of 23.5 mS cm −1 at 300 K.

title: Efficiency optimization of BaY 2 Al 2− y Sc y Ga 2 SiO 12 : x Cr 3+ garnet phosphors with sustained anti-thermal quenching behavior
abstract: Systematic substitution of Al 3+ with Sc 3+ modulates the crystal field environment around Cr 3+ , leading to enhanced luminescence efficiency while preserving anti-thermal quenching behavior at elevated temperatures.

title: Room temperature ferromagnetic ordering from bound magnetic polarons in rare-earth-doped ultrathin MoS 2 nanosheets
abstract: Rare-earth (Tb 3+ , Re 3+ and Eu 3+ )-doped MoS 2 nanosheets were synthesized and assessed as dilute magnetic semiconductors. Their room temperature ferromagnetic properties were ascribed to bound magnetic polarons with a T C of >300 K.

title: Spin dynamics of tri-addition endohedral nitrogen fullerene with transversal chemical functionalization
abstract: The trans -3 tris-addition endohedral nitrogen fullerene derivatives with transversal stretching, microsecond-scale quantum coherence time, and addressable energy levels were synthesized, offering a scalable approach for future 2D spin assemblies.

title: Unveiling the roles of aromaticity in the optoelectronic and charge-transport properties of dehydrobenzo[ n ]annulenes
abstract: Systematic investigation on the ring-size-dependent optoelectronic and electric properties of hexa- n -dodecyloxy-substituted [ n ]DBA derivatives reveals enhanced hole mobility in antiaromatic systems.

title: Heterojunction floating-gate phototransistors for ultraweak short-wavelength infrared photodetection
abstract: Short-wavelength infrared (SWIR) photodetectors with high sensitivity are indispensable for detecting subtle object variations under low-light conditions, a capability that supports advancements in artificial intelligence.

title: Damage mitigation as a strategy to achieve high ferroelectricity and reliability in hafnia for random-access-memory
abstract: This study presents a low-damage metallization process for ultra-thin hafnia-based ferroelectric films, achieving high polarization, low leakage currents, and reduced wake-up effect, paving the way for scalable and reliable FeRAM applications.

title: Synergistic effects of La and Mn vacancies on the structural and magnetoelectric transport properties of LaMn 1+ x O 3+ δ manganites
abstract: With increasing x , the concentration of La vacancies increases, while that of Mn vacancies decreases. This study demonstrates that Mn vacancies play a dominant role in determining the magnetoelectric transport properties of LaMn 1+ x O 3+ δ ceramics.

title: Unraveling the chemistry of PVP in engineering CdS nanoflowers for sunlight-driven photocatalysis
abstract: The present work focuses on the chemistry of PVP in tailoring the morphology and optoelectronic properties of CdS nanoflowers along with their mechanism for sunlight-driven photocatalysis with improved stability and recyclability.

title: Janus MoSeS nanoscrolls for ultrasensitive detection of surface-enhanced Raman scattering
abstract: The intercalated-Janus MoSeS nanoscroll, as a metal nanoparticle-free SERS substrate, exhibits ultrasensitive detection of probe molecules.

title: Ruthenium-based medium-entropy alloy oxide for hydrogen evolution linked to biomass sucrose upcycling
abstract: Wasted biomass upcycling is an environmentally friendly approach that contributes to protecting the Earth.

title: Stretchable light-emitting electrochemical cells fabricated by spray-coating
abstract: A stretchable light-emitting electrochemical cell is designed and fabricated by ambient-air spray-coating, which delivers uniform light emission during 30% elongation.

title: HCOO − -induced defect passivation of Cs 3 Cu 2 I 5 for enhanced blue emission and application in white light emission
abstract: Excess HCOO − passivation can suppresses iodine-vacancy defects in Cs 3 Cu 2 I 5 via carboxyl ligand coordination, boosting PLQY from 30.2% to 87.5%. A resulting WLED achieves CIE coordinates of (0.32,0.33) and a CRI of 83.7.

title: Detection of traps in thin-film transistors using evolutionary algorithms
abstract: We present a novel approach to detect traps in thin-film transistors (TFTs) by analyzing their current-related characteristics.

title: Ultra-high nonlinear absorption coefficients based on multiphoton-excited self-trapped excitons in perovskite-inspired copper halides
abstract: Ultra-high nonlinear absorption properties were demonstrated in CsCu 2 I 3 and Cs 3 Cu 2 I 5 single crystals due to a three-photon absorption (3PA) while a two-photon absorption (2PA) for the CsCu 2 I 3 and Cs 3 Cu 2 I 5 thin films.

title: Wireless technologies in stretchable bioelectronics
abstract: This review highlights recent advances in wireless technologies for stretchable bioelectronics, including wireless power transfer, wireless data communication, material selection, integration approaches, and applications in bioelectronic devices.

title: Polarity from the bottom up: a computational framework for predicting spontaneous polar order
abstract: The spontaneous long-range mutual orientation of their electric dipole moments that gives rise to polar liquid crystals is shown to result from subtle intermolecular interactions.

title: Highly efficient, ultra-stable multi-interlayer luminescent solar concentrators based on green and red-emitting perovskite nanocrystal composites
abstract: The green-emitting Zn 2+ -doped Zn-CsPbBr 3 @SiO 2 and red-emitting Zn-CsPbBrI 2 @SiO 2 composites (denoted as ZB and ZBI) are synthesized for the development of highly efficient and ultra-stable luminescent solar concentrators (LSCs).

title: Phosphor-converted warm white laser diodes with high saturation threshold through a PiGF-dual sapphire converter
abstract: Phosphor-converted warm white LDs with phosphor-in-glass film-dual sapphire (PiGF-D) converter were proposed. The Y/R-PiGF-D based white LD achieves a warm white light with a CRI of 87.5 and a high LF of 2010 lm@31.7 W mm −2 .

title: Boosting the light-driven pyroelectric response of poly(vinylidene difluoride) by constructing Mn-doped BZT-BCT/PVDF composites
abstract: PVDF films exhibit excellent photo-pyroelectric response under visible light upon adding light-active ferroelectric BZTM-BCT particles, caused by simultaneously improved photothermal conversion and pyroelectric coefficient.

title: Recent development of magneto-optical thin films and integrated photonic devices
abstract: This review summarizes recent advances in magneto-optical (MO) thin films, their integration into nonreciprocal photonic devices, and discusses key challenges and future prospects.

title: Enhanced photothermal catalytic CO 2 reduction by CeO 2 -based multicomponent catalysts
abstract: Based on the understanding of photothermal catalysis, this review summarizes the recent progress of CeO 2 -based multicomponent catalysts for photothermal catalytic CO 2 reduction.

title: Solid additive-enhanced performance in near-infrared organic photodetectors for broadband–narrowband dual-mode detection
abstract: Schematic and performance of a semi-transparent organic photodetector integrated with a perovskite optical filter for high-speed, narrowband NIR detection at 830 nm.

title: Self-healing, adhesive liquid metal hydrogels based on PNIPAM microgels for high-performance temperature and strain sensors
abstract: Self-healing, adhesive liquid metal hydrogels based on PNIPAM microgels for high-performance temperature and strain sensors.

title: Defect formation in CsSnI 3 from density functional theory and machine learning
abstract: This study used density functional theory calculations to identify dopants that suppress the p-type self-doping of CsSnI 3 . Machine learning algorithms are used to predict the defect formation energetics from elemental features of the dopants.

title: Impact of thermally activated ionic dynamics on the trap-mediated current–voltage characteristics of a mixed-halide hybrid perovskite
abstract: Thermally activated ionic dynamics in the mixed-halide organic–inorganic perovskite FAPbBr 2 I is investigated using temperature-dependent dielectric spectroscopy and space charge limited current measurements.

title: Polymer-engineered perovskite solar cells: synergistic strategies for multiscale stability and high-efficiency photovoltaics
abstract: This review highlights the multifaceted roles of polymers in perovskite solar cells, with future efforts focused on multifunctional designs and scalable processing for practical applications.

title: Revealing energy transfer mechanisms and accelerating intelligent detection: Cr 3+ and Ni 2+ co-doped Lu 2 CaMg 2 Si 3 O 12 phosphors for NIR applications
abstract: Cr 3+ enhances Lu 2 CaMg 2 Si 3 O 12 :Ni 2+ for better NIR-II emission, efficiency, and food detection via a convolutional neural network model.

title: Circularly polarized electroluminescence from light-emitting diodes: mechanisms, materials, and applications
abstract: Circularly polarized luminescence (CPL) has gained significant interest in applications ranging from quantum computing and optical communications to data encryption and bioimaging.

title: Interfacial electronic structure modulation by facet orientation and sulfur vacancies in CdS/MoS 2 heterojunctions
abstract: Select sulfur vacancy configurations in CdS/MoS 2 heterojunctions introduce midgap states which enhance the HER, while CdS facet termination controls interfacial charge transfer and affects overall band alignment.

title: In situ construction of a multi-hierarchical CdS-DETA/In(OH) 3 /Ag 2 S dual-S-scheme heterojunction for accurate depolymerization of the β-O-4 bond in lignin compounds
abstract: Nowadays, photocatalysis has emerged as a highly promising approach for the selective depolymerization of lignin under mild conditions due to its ability to generate reactive species with high redox capabilities in a single step.

title: A mechanically strong, ultra-tough and room-temperature self-healing ionic conductive elastomer based on octopus-like physical cross-linker tannic acid and a robust polyurethane substrate
abstract: A new approach for preparing ionic conductive elastomers with excellent overall properties based on a robust substrate and a high-efficiency physical crosslinker is proposed.

title: Breathable, recyclable, and solvent-free conductive films: versatile processing for flexible biointerfaces
abstract: We present a novel solvent-free ionic conductive (SIB) film by polyethylene oxide and phytic acid, which synergistically integrates air permeability, self-adhesiveness, and superior conductivity into one system.

title: Performance analysis of photo-liquefiable azobenzene derivatives for improving the responsive ability of their functional devices
abstract: Correlation and consistency between microcosmic photochemical reactions, macroscopic phase transitions, and functional devices in photo-liquefiable azobenzene derivatives.

title: A transparent ionogel with mechanical robustness enabled by synergistic noncovalent interactions
abstract: A transparent ionogel with mechanical robustness enabled by synergistic noncovalent interactions was successfully prepared by “one-step” photopolymerization.

title: Correction: Antiferromagnetic semiconductor La 0.6 Ba 0.15 Ca 0.15 Ag 0.1 MnO 3 perovskite with strong magnetocaloric and giant magnetoresistance effects
abstract: Correction for ‘Antiferromagnetic semiconductor La 0.6 Ba 0.15 Ca 0.15 Ag 0.1 MnO 3 perovskite with strong magnetocaloric and giant magnetoresistance effects’ by Ala Eddin Mabrouki et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TC03042K.

title: Multifunctional high-mobility polymer semiconductors: design, synthesis and applications
abstract: This review provides an overview of the developments in high-mobility polymers, integrating the core themes of molecular design, synthetic methods, and functional integration.

title: Halogen-bonded ionic liquid crystals: supramolecular organization and ionic transport
abstract: We present a new family of halogen-bonded pyridinium salts where both ionic partners participate in halogen bonding, leading to self-assembled smectic phases with nanosegregated ionic channels and high ionic conductivity.

title: Unraveling the interlayer coupling effect on layer-dependent electronic and optoelectronic properties in two-dimensional semiconductors
abstract: The large difference in the layer-dependent electronic and optoelectronic properties in two-dimensional (2D) semiconductors such as PtSe 2 and HfSe 2 originates from a competition between the out-of-plane and in-plane orbital interactions.

title: Self-assembly and ambipolar charge transport in columnar phases of polynuclear gold isocyano–triphenylene complexes
abstract: An uncommon approach to the synthesis of ambipolar semiconductors based on di- and tri-nuclear gold isocyano–triphenylene complexes of the formula [(AuX) n (CN–C 6 H 4 –O–(CH 2 ) 6 ) n –TriPh] ( n = 2, 3; X = Cl, CC–Ph) is described.

title: Blue electroluminescence and hyperluminescence facilitated by Ir( iii ) phosphors with 5-cyano-imidazo[4,5- b ]pyridin-2-ylidene cyclometalates
abstract: Two blue-emitting Ir( iii ) carbene complexes were designed and utilized to fabricate OLED devices. The f-ct3b-based PhOLED exhibited superior performance, while the hyper-OLED achieved a max. EQE of 29.0% with narrowband blue emission.

title: Enhancement of the thermoelectric performance of SnTe via Mn solubility control
abstract: Thermoelectric materials have drawn attention due to their capability of directly converting heat and electricity, which helps utilize waste heat and provides an alternative energy source.

title: Ca 2 YScAl 2 Si 2 O 12 :Cr 3+ phosphor-in-glass film for laser-driven high-power near-infrared lighting
abstract: A new Ca 2 YScAl 2 Si 2 O 12 :Cr 3+ PiGF was synthesized using rapid infrared firing, highlighting luminous output of 2626 mW under blue laser excitation, for near-infrared lighting applications in nondestructive detection, night vision and bioimaging.

title: Low bandgap conjugated polymers based on thiadiazoloquinoxaline for high performance shortwave infrared photodetection
abstract: The photodiode device based on P5 exhibited the highest specific detectivity of 2.0 × 10 10 Jones at 1200 nm under −1 V bias, making it a promising material for applications including bioimaging, environmental sensing, and optical communications.

title: Edge-directed rapid chiral assembly of gold nanorods by 2D hexagonal nanosheets of helical poly(phenylacetylene)s and the synergistic communication of circularly polarized luminescence
abstract: A rapid 2D co-assembly creates chiral hybrid materials by templating gold nanorods (GNRs) with poly(phenylacetylene) (PPA) nanosheets. GNRs quickly form precise chiral arrangements with strong plasmonic dichroism and PPAs possess enhanced CPL.

title: Thermally removable sidechain stabilized benzodipyrrole as an electron-rich building block used in donor–acceptor conjugated polymers
abstract: Pei et al. synthesized BDPBoc, which can be thermally deprotected to yield BDP fragment with record-high electron-donating ability in conjugated polymers. It induces a strong donor–acceptor effect, resulting in optimal charge transport performances.

title: A waveguide-integrated SnSe photodetector on a lithium niobate thin film with high responsivity, fast response, and self-powered capability
abstract: This study demonstrates a high-performance SnSe waveguide-integrated photodetector on LNOI. The device features an extended light-matter interaction distance, significantly enhancing the performance of the SnSe photodetector at near-infrared band.

title: Non-spherical pore scattering-enhanced YAG:Ce 3+ , Cr 3+ ceramics: efficient light converters for high-brightness laser illumination
abstract: The purpose of this investigation is to address the issues of insufficient thermal stability and spectral narrowing of conventional phosphor materials in high-power laser illumination.

title: In situ visual monitoring strategies for wound healing based on hydrogel materials
abstract: Monitoring wound biomarkers is critical for chronic wound management. This review highlights the advanced hydrogel dressings for in situ visual monitoring of a series of key wound biomarkers like pH, glucose, ROS and bacterial products.

title: A solvent crafted alternate [Zn 3 /Zn 4 ] cluster driven MOF and its analogous MOG: structural elucidation integrated with advanced bilirubin detection and encryption strategies
abstract: A single metal-ligand system yields both a crystalline MOF and a soft gel as analogous architectures. Their luminescent features enable advanced sensing and anticounterfeiting applications.

title: Selective phase growth of van der Waals high- κ Sb 2 O 3 films
abstract: Oxygen-assisted PLD enables phase-selective growth of wafer-scale α-/β-Sb 2 O 3 films with high dielectric constants (>100). Pure α-Sb 2 O 3 shows excellent dielectric properties, and its relaxation mechanisms are elucidated.

title: Sr/Ho-codoped (K 0.5 Na 0.5 )NbO 3 photochromic-transparent ceramics in non-destructive optical data storage and multi-modal anti-counterfeiting
abstract: Sr/Ho-codoped (K 0.5 Na 0.5 )NbO 3 ceramics exhibit high optical transparency, remarkable photochromic contrast and outstanding cycling stability, which can be applied in high-security anti-counterfeiting labels and rewritable information storage.

title: Natural waxes from plant and animal origin as dielectrics for low-voltage organic field effect transistors
abstract: Six naturally occurring waxes of plant and animal origin are demonstrated to be excellent dielectric materials for organic field effect transistors operating at low voltage in the frame of sustainable electronics development.

title: Enhanced intrinsic thermal conductivity of liquid crystalline polyester through monomer structure optimization in main chains
abstract: In order to investigate the influence of intermolecular interactions on polymer crystallization, a series of liquid crystal polyesters with high thermal conductivity (B-LCPs) were synthesized.

title: Surface micro-etching of Bi 2 Te 3 thermoelectric legs enables a high-efficiency power generator
abstract: A micro-etching method is used to enhance tensile strength and reduce the contact resistance between Bi 2 Te 3 -based thermoelectric leg and copper electrode, leading to effective and reliable output performance in thermoelectric power generators.

title: Electrochemical synthesis of nitrogen-doped carbon dot inks and evaluation of their use for anticounterfeiting applications
abstract: This work presents a novel in depth analysis of electrochemically produced nitrogen-doped carbon dots and shows a route for the fast and low-cost production of fluorescent markers for applications in information security and flexible optics.

title: Single crystals of organometallic manganese halides as sustainable high-luminescence materials for X-ray scintillation
abstract: Manganese-based zero-dimensional organometallic halide crystals were synthesized using a solvent evaporation method, exhibiting high PLQY, stability, and exceptional X-ray scintillation properties with a flexible, ecofriendly scintillator screen.

title: Enhanced energy storage performances under low electric fields in (1 − x ) Na 0.5 Bi 0.5 TiO 3 – x Sr 0.7 Bi 0.2 (Al 0.5 Ta 0.5 )O 3 relaxor ferroelectric ceramics: impact of Sr 0.7 Bi 0.2 (Al 0.5 Ta 0.5 )O 3 on structure and electrical properties
abstract: The introduction of Sr 0.7 Bi 0.2 (Al 0.5 Ta 0.5 )O 3 into Na 0.5 Bi 0.5 TiO 3 induces the coexistence of R 3 c and P 4 bm phases and disrupts ferroelectric domains, thereby optimizing the P – E loops with relaxor behavior and enhancing the energy storage performances.

title: Synaptic plasticity and handwritten digit recognition of a memristor based on a high-stability lead-free Cs 3 Bi 2 Br 9 perovskite thin film
abstract: Based on the constructed W/Cs 3 Bi 2 Br 9 /ITO memristor, the synaptic plasticity aspects, such as STP and LTP, are successfully simulated, making it suitable for artificial neural systems.

title: Construction of oxygen-rich vacancy Bi 3 O 4 Br:Yb 3+ ,Er 3+ nanosheets for enhanced photoreversible color switching and upconversion luminescence
abstract: Bi 3 O 4 Br:Yb 3+ ,Er 3+ (BYE-OV) nanosheets generate more oxygen vacancies under UV irradiation, causing the color to change from light yellow to brown-black. At the same time, the generated intermediate energy levels enhance upconversion luminescence.

title: Synthesis of coal pitch-derived carbon dots with yellow emission and improved photoluminescence quantum yield through pre-surface engineering and nitrogen doping
abstract: A strategy to boost the emission wavelength redshift and improve the photoluminescence quantum yield (PLQY) of coal pitch-derived CDs is proposed. The obtained CDs emit yellow light and exhibit a PLQY of 34%, showing potential for LED applications.

title: Assessment of rare earth ion-doped near-infrared glass scintillators
abstract: The effects of the glass matrix, luminescent centers, and doping concentrations on the optical and scintillation properties of NIR-II scintillating glasses were investigated.

title: Birefringence inversion in liquid crystalline poly(substituted methylene)s bearing side-on mesogens
abstract: PMs comprising a negative Δ n backbone and positive Δ n side-on mesogens form two types of LCs with opposite Δ n sign.

title: Flower-shaped NiMnO 3 @NiCo 2 S 4 heterojunction nanosheets for a high-performance asymmetric supercapacitor
abstract: NiMnO 3 @NiCo 2 S 4 is a high-performance supercapacitor material with excellent capacitance, cycling stability, and energy density.

title: Enhancing triboelectric nanogenerator performance using chitosan-modified multiwalled carbon nanotubes
abstract: The structure and fabrication process of the CS–S@CNTs/PDMS-based TENG device are presented.

title: Facile synthesis of silicon quantum dots with photoluminescence in the near-ultraviolet to violet region via wet oxidation
abstract: We report a facile wet oxidation treatment to obtain SiQDs with PL emission in the NUVV region while elucidating their emission mechanism.

title: Cr 3+ –Cr 3+ pairing and multi-site engineering in Sr 2 ZnAl 22 O 36 phosphors for broadband near-infrared emissions
abstract: Broadband near-infrared emission and strong thermal stability are achieved in novel Sr 2 ZnAl 22 O 36 :Cr 3+ phosphors through site-engineered Cr 3+ incorporation, and Cr 3+ –Cr 3+ pairing supporting night vision.

title: Bioinspired multisensory fusion using a MoTe 2 optoelectronic memristor with oxygen plasma treatment for in-sensor reservoir computing
abstract: Biological multimodal perception systems play a pivotal role in environmental interactions through the sophisticated integration of multisensory information.

title: Atomic-scale B-site deficiency stabilizes enhanced thermoelectric properties of calcium manganese oxides
abstract: A strong vacancy compensation mechanism was developed. The twin-boundary shear and several lattice defects were nucleated. A low κ lat value of 1.14 W m −1 K −1 was observed at 1073 K.

title: Broadband photoluminescence toward the NIR II region and stable green ceramic pigments based on a novel NaBaScSi 2 O 7 : x Cr silicate phosphor
abstract: Cr 3+ shows broadband near infrared luminescence towards the NIR II region in NaBaScSi 2 O 7 due to the strong charge polarisation effect, and the proposed materials show promising applications in NIR pc-LEDs and green ceramic pigments.

title: Enhanced thermoelectric performance and reversed anisotropy in the Janus penta-PdSeTe monolayer via biaxial strain
abstract: Structure and compressive strain enhanced thermoelectric performance and reversed anisotropy of the Janus penta-PdSeTe monolayer.

title: Regulating the solvation structure and adsorption behavior in zinc anodes using polar organic molecules to achieve durable dendrite-free zinc metal anodes for aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Cy molecules can form coordination complexes with Zn 2+ , thereby entering the solvated sheath of Zn 2+ . This minimizes the contact between the active molecules of H 2 O and the zinc metal anode, reducing hydrogen evolution potential.

title: Sr 4 GaNbO 8 :Mn 4+ : a novel perovskite-structured red-emitting phosphor for a luminescence lifetime thermometer with good relative sensitivity and repeatability
abstract: A new type of niobate perovskite structure Sr 4 GaNbO 8 :Mn 4+ has been prepared by us, which is different from previous perovskite compounds and has excellent relative and absolute sensitivities as well as good repeatability.

title: Impact of hydrogen-controlled thermal ALD SiO 2 insulators on IGZO channel FETs to optimize the electrical performance
abstract: The impact of hydrogen controlled by thermal ALD SiO 2 deposition temperature, to demonstrate ALD IGZO thin film transistor performances.

title: Ultrasonication-assisted synthesis of morphology-controlled gallium oxide nanoparticles for high-performance photoelectronic device applications
abstract: The shape of gallium oxide nanomaterials was controlled by modulating the kinetic pathways of Ga surface reactions.

title: Highly responsive and stable self-powered solar-blind photodetectors based on Sn doped β-Ga 2 O 3 nanorod arrays
abstract: A simple and controllable hydrothermal method has been developed to realize Sn-doped β-Ga 2 O 3 , with greatly improved photoresponsivity and response speed.

title: Lanthanide coordination polymers as luminescent thermometers: integrating theoretical modeling with experimental analysis to tune the thermal response
abstract: Combining theoretical modeling and experimental methods to understand the thermal dependence of luminescence in lanthanide( iii ) complexes.

title: Thienothiophene and single-wall carbon nanotube-based hybrid materials: design, photophysical properties and the construction of high-performance supercapacitors
abstract: Carbon nanotube decorated thienothiophene based supercapacitors are highly promising materials for energy storage due to their high capacitance and power density with super-long cycling stability.

title: Two-photon polymerization-assisted 3D laser nanoprinting: from fundamentals to modern applications
abstract: Two-photon lithography (2PL) enables high-resolution 3D nanoprinting of complex, functional microdevices. This review examines 2PL fundamentals, resolution and throughput improvements, and emerging applications in various fields.

title: Crystal growth and temperature dependence of luminescence characteristics of Pr 3+ and Tb 3+ doped solid-solution sesquioxide single crystals
abstract: Pr 3+ - and Tb 3+ -doped (Lu,Y,Sc) 2 O 3 single crystals grown using the micro-pulling-down method show promising performance as optical thermometers, providing a wide operating temperature range and relatively high relative sensitivity.

title: Photoluminescence features of few-layer hexagonal α-In 2 Se 3
abstract: Temperature- and time-dependent photoluminescence studies of layered α-In 2 Se 3 allowed us to observe defect-assisted and exciton-related bands. Distinct components, attributed to direct and phonon-assisted indirect transitions were determined.

title: Correction: Optoelectronic and NLO potential of styryl-functionalized nitroisoxazoles for OLED technologies
abstract: Correction for ‘Optoelectronic and NLO potential of styryl-functionalized nitroisoxazoles for OLED technologies’ by Karen Acosta-Quiroga et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, 13 , 9083–9098, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TC00619H.

title: Correction: Carmine–PVA composites as optical limiters: effect of carmine concentration and the role of laser intensity in optical limiting
abstract: Correction for ‘Carmine–PVA composites as optical limiters: effect of carmine concentration and the role of laser intensity in optical limiting’ by Rukshaana Rafi et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, 13 , 12329–12340, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TC05336B.

title: All-solid-state Li-ion-based electrolyte-gated synaptic transistors with dual modes of excitatory and inhibitory plasticity
abstract: Inorganic all-solid-state oxide-based synaptic transistors with a LiPON electrolyte achieve dual modes of excitatory and inhibitory plasticity in a single device by modulating the magnitude of a single voltage supply.

title: Low thermal expansion and high lattice thermal conductivity in β-sodalite Co 4 B 6 O 13 : enhancing the local rigidity of the cationic tetrahedral cluster
abstract: The introduction of Co atoms tightens the connection between the core O(1) atom and the external anionic [B 6 O(2) 12 ] 6− framework, thereby lowering α and increasing the κ L .

title: Ultrahigh detectivity of near-infrared organic phototransistor assisted by additional electron trap sites in a dielectric layer
abstract: This study introduced an additional electronic traps (ZnO nanoparticles) into the dielectric layer of the organic phototransistor, obtaining a high I light / I dark and an ultra-high detectivity while keeping the response time unchanged.

title: Surface ligand engineering of pure-red perovskite nanocrystals with enhanced stability by diphenylammonium halide molecules
abstract: The DPAI and DPABr ligands compensated for surface defects of perovskite nanocrystals through released I − and Br − anions and improved charge carrier injection by π-conjugated benzene rings.

title: Ordered fiber ionic pathways for wide-frequency regulation in electrolyte-gated transistors for bio-signal perception
abstract: Electrolyte-gated transistors (EGTs) with organic polymer electrolytes are promising candidates for wearable bioelectronic devices, owing to their inherent flexibility and biocompatibility.

title: Electrochemiluminescence of quantum dots: synthesis, mechanisms and Sensor application
abstract: This work describes the typical synthetic method, detailed mechanisms, sensor applications, current challenges and prospects of QD-based electrochemiluminescence (ECL), providing beginners a foundational overview.

title: Supramolecular assembly of phosphonate-substituted porphyrins in Langmuir layers and Langmuir–Schäfer films: structural studies and selective sensing of pyridine vapors
abstract: The supramolecular assembly of octa-phosphonate-substituted porphyrins using the Langmuir–Schäfer technique allows for the selective naked-eye detection of pyridine by monolayer films.

title: Liquid crystal-refilled polymer network templates formed by photo-polymerisation in an orientationally ordered liquid crystal
abstract: Order transfer with polymer stabilised refilled liquid crystals is discussed: the pitch limit for achieving reflectivities larger than the theoretical level, possibility of network-induced TGB phases, and orienting lyotropic liquid crystals.

title: Improving the aggregation of layer-by-layer processed all-polymer solar cells with an additive during the film deposition and thermal annealing
abstract: Optimizing the vertical component distribution, aggregation and molecular stacking of the donor and acceptor is an effective strategy to boost the power conversion efficiency (PCE) of all-polymer solar cells (all-PSCs).

title: Improved performance and bias stability in TFTs with solution-processed graphene oxide-doped In 2 O 3 active layers treated under deep UV irradiation
abstract: Xiao-Lin Wang et al. , Improved performance and bias stability in TFTs with solution-processed graphene oxide-doped In 2 O 3 active layers treated under deep UV irradiation.

title: The structure transition and luminescence performance of a high moisture resistant KNaNbOF 5 :Mn 4+ red phosphor
abstract: A pair of novel KNaNbOF 5 :Mn 4+ red phosphors (α- and β-type) was successfully prepared. The structure differences led to a variation in luminous properties and excellent moisture resistance (90.6% for 24 h immersion).

title: The role of the N 2 atmosphere in the epitaxial growth of MnO via pulsed laser deposition
abstract: The controlled growth of highly oriented MnO and Mn 3 O 4 thin films was achieved via kinetics control during pulsed laser deposition. Correlating the growth environment and the properties of the films, generates a context for epitaxial growth.

title: Enhanced photodegradation performance based on the surface plasmon resonance effect of Ag/Ca 2 Al 2 SiO 7 :Pr 3+ ultraviolet long afterglow driven in a photo-Fenton system
abstract: Long afterglow photocatalysts have demonstrated the potential to support all-weather photocatalytic reactions.

title: Understanding the thermometric behaviour of LiLuF 4 :Tm 3+ ,Yb 3+
abstract: A combination of experimental studies and kinetic modelling helps elucidate clear-cut guidelines for the use of the upconversion Tm 3+ , Yb 3+ system as a luminescent thermometer.

title: Silver-incorporated NiCo metal–organic frameworks with controlled morphology for enhanced cycling in flexible supercapacitor applications
abstract: The specific capacitance of NCA 15 -MOF/NF was 1317 F g −1 , which was significantly higher compared to the NCA 0 -MOF/NF. After 15 000 charge–discharge cycles, the NCA 15 -MOF/NF retained 89% of its initial specific capacitance.

title: Dry electrodes with a printed cellulose–graphene ink for low-profile strain sensors in electromyography
abstract: Cyrene is a green, environmentally friendly solvent, presenting a renewable, non-toxic alternative to traditionally aggressive solvents for effectively exfoliating graphene from bulk graphite towards additively manufactured biosensors.

title: High piezoelectric performance in KNN-based ceramics with multiphase coexistence for plateau climate applications
abstract: High piezoelectric response is optimized in potassium sodium niobate ceramics at extreme cold temperatures by modifying the rhombohedral–orthorhombic–tetragonal multiphase coexistence.

title: Raman spectra of the graphyne-family: graphyne, graphdiyne and graphtriyne
abstract: Graphyne-family monolayers exhibit increasing complexity of anisotropic Raman properties with more acetylene units. Wavelength-dependent non-polarized Raman spectra of graphyne-family materials are correlated with their light absorbance differences.

title: High resolution X-ray imaging via near unity emission organic–inorganic manganese bromide scintillator films using a suction filtration method
abstract: In recent years, lead-free organic–inorganic hybrid scintillators have attracted much attention in the field of X-ray imaging because of progress in their solution synthesis, low toxicity, and high photoluminescence quantum yield (PLQY).

title: Ultra-thin and thin CrSi films on Si(111): I. Formation and crystal structure
abstract: In this paper we present the results of ab initio calculations on the ground state of the CrSi bulk and experimental studies on the structure and morphology of thin and ultrathin CrSi films grown on Si(111) substrates.

title: Orthogonal small-molecule zinc porphyrin derivative as efficient hole transport material for high-performance inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: D–A type small molecule with a star-shaped orthogonal structure, constructed from a zinc porphyrin core and four DPP arms, was introduced as a hole transport material in inverted perovskite solar cells.

title: Suppressing the non-radiative energy loss of organic solar cells by embedding nitroxide radical blocks in wide bandgap conjugated polymer donors
abstract: A new strategy for the development of nitroxide radical wide bandgap polymer donors and additives, which enable the lowering of the non-radiative energy loss of non-fullerene-based organic solar cells, is presented.

title: Doping modulated solution processed single molecule fluorescence–phosphorescence dual emission pure white organic light emitting diodes
abstract: A pure organic dual fluorescence–phosphorescence emitter embedded in various hosts enables tunable exciton dynamics and emission color. The optimized device delivers efficient white light, offering a cost-effective route to high-performance WOLEDs.

title: Design and wearable sensing study of a temperature-sensitive anti-freezing biomass conductive gel based on IPANH@DES
abstract: Conductive hydrogels derived from biopolymers are rapidly gaining prominence as a key research direction in the field of flexible wearable sensors.

title: Functionalized PDMS for regulating the triboelectric output of nanogenerators: a study of charge transfer mechanisms
abstract: The triboelectric performance of PDMS films can be regulated through surface functionalization of self-assembled monolayers. A combination of nano-characterization techniques reveals the underlying mechanism.

title: Janus S-XSSe: a new family of 2D polar half-metals with stacking-engineered magnetism and antiferromagnetic spin splitting
abstract: The quest for materials uniting polarity with metallicity or half-metallicity is pivotal for next-generation multifunctional devices, yet their realization, particularly with robust magnetism, remains a formidable challenge.

title: Solution-processed epitaxial growth of PIN photodiodes made of MAPbBr 3 single crystals for high energy resolution gamma-ray spectroscopy
abstract: Lead halide perovskite single crystals (LHPSCs), due to their high atomic number, excellent carrier transport properties, and tunable bandgaps, demonstrate unique potential in gamma spectroscopy detection.

title: Reconfigurable logic operations in a MoS 2 -based resistive switching device for neuromorphic computing applications
