abstract: Neuromorphic computing, inspired by the remarkable capabilities of the human brain, has emerged as a promising paradigm for achieving high-performance and energy-efficient computing systems.

title: Enhancing performances of organic photovoltaics by incorporating small molecule stereoisomers as the third component
abstract: A stereoisomeric small-molecule acceptor, cis -ID-IC-OR, was incorporated into a PM6:Y6 system, forming ternary blend organic photovoltaics with enhanced crystallinity, charge transport and power conversion efficiency of 17.1%.

title: Growth of MnWO 4 nanowires on W(110) by high-temperature oxygen-assisted molecular beam epitaxy
abstract: We describe the growth of synthetic hübnerite (MnWO 4 ) by high-temperature oxygen-assisted molecular beam epitaxy on W(110). Only manganese and oxygen needs to be supplied, with the substrate crystal providing the tungsten atoms.

title: Buried interface passivation with bis(3-aminopyrid-2-yl) sulfide for carbon-based perovskite solar cells
abstract: Electron layer defects are one of the key factors affecting the performance of perovskite solar cells.

title: Fabrication of colloidal photonic crystal supraparticles via atomization drying for efficient passive cooling
abstract: In this work, we developed a rapid and highly efficient method for constructing CPC supraparticles using microfluidic atomization drying assembly. And, we investigated their application in passive cooling.

title: Semi-metallic Bi 2 Se 3 contact-based interface engineering on monolayer MoS 2 for high-performance field-effect transistors and flexible photodetectors
abstract: Novel semi-metallic Bi 2 Se 3 contact engineering on monolayer MoS 2 enables high-performance field-effect transistors and flexible photodetectors.

title: Epitaxial NiO/Al 0.5 Ga 0.5 N heterostructures for high-performance solar-blind ultraviolet self-powered photodetectors
abstract: High-performance solar-blind ultraviolet self-powered photodetectors based on NiO/Al 0.5 Ga 0.5 N heterostructures fabricated by room-temperature epitaxy.

title: Lightweight composite foams for electromagnetic interference shielding with high absorption and excellent mechanical properties
abstract: Electromagnetic radiation and its associated secondary pollution significantly disrupt the proper functioning of electronic devices and may even pose a threat to human health.

title: Sequential solid-state multiligand exchange of FAPbI 3 quantum dots for more efficient and stable photovoltaic devices
abstract: We present a sequential solid-state multiligand exchange process for FAPbI 3 quantum dots to enhance the power conversion efficiency of the perovskite solar cells by 28%.

title: Emergence of heavy-fermion behavior and distorted square nets in partially vacancy-ordered Y 4 Fe x Ge 8 (1.0 ≤ x ≤ 1.5)
abstract: Heavy fermion characteristics and potential superconductivity are observed in the partially vacancy-ordered Y 4 Fe x Ge 8 .

title: Metallic conduction in high Curie temperature ferromagnetic heavy rare earth monoxides RE O ( RE = Tb, Dy, Er): rare-earth-dependent carrier polarity and anomalous Hall effect
abstract: Single phase rocksalt-type heavy rare earth monoxides RE O ( RE = Tb, Dy, Er) are high Curie temperature ( T C ) ferromagnetic metals, exhibiting various carrier polarities and the anomalous Hall effect.

title: A structurally elusive phase transition with significant physicochemical consequences – the case of the [C(NH 2 ) 3 ] 3 [Dy(EDTA)(CO 3 )]·H 2 O crystal
abstract: The partial rotational dynamics of the guanidinium cation significantly affect the f–f transitions of the Dy( iii ) ion.

title: Liquid crystal trimers containing tertiary benzanilide groups
abstract: The highly bent shape of tertiary amides offers a new design element for liquid crystals with unconventional shapes. Here we report an unusual family of liquid crystal trimers, with a marked contraction of the smectic layer spacing on cooling.

title: Achieving high-accuracy multi-feature temperature sensing in chromium( iii )-doped nanophosphors using machine learning
abstract: Multi-feature near-infrared photoluminescence in Cr 3+ -doped nanophosphors was used for high-accuracy temperature prediction using machine learning.

title: Asymmetric perinone-based electron-collecting monolayer materials for n–i–p perovskite solar cells
abstract: A series of asymmetric perinone derivatives (PERNI) bearing different substituents on the benzimidazole moiety were synthesized and investigated for possible application as electron-collecting monolayer materials in n–i–p perovskite solar cells.

title: Structural phase transition, mechanics, and thermodynamics of heavy fermion metal UPt 3 under pressure
abstract: An orthorhombic phase of UPt 3 was predicted under pressure using DFT and CALYPSO. Pressure-induced phase transition, P – T phase diagram, and changes of the electronic structure, elastic and thermodynamic properties were studied in DFT level.

title: Molecular layer deposition of biomimetic amino acid-based hybrid thin films for artificial nociceptors
abstract: MLD derived biomimetic amino acid-based hybrid thin films were used as functional layer for Pt/Ti-Cys/TiN memristor, which exhibits reproducible volatile switching behavior and successfully emulates bio-pain sensation and synapse functions.

title: Design, synthesis, and optoelectronic properties of benzothiadiazole-fused sulfur and nitrogen-containing polycyclic heteroaromatics
abstract: New benzothiadiazole-fused donor–acceptor structured heteroaromatics were designed and synthesized. The BzPTT-C4C8-based OFETs exhibit the highest hole mobility (up to 0.24 cm 2 V −1 s −1 ) and the best photo-detection characteristics.

title: Correction: An ion-gating synaptic memristor based on tri-layer HfO x composition regulation
abstract: Correction for ‘An ion-gating synaptic memristor based on tri-layer HfO x composition regulation’ by Lanqing Zou et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, 13 , 5326–5331, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TC04564E.

title: Localized transport channels through nanoscale phase separation for efficient inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: Localized electron transport channel is developed in inverted PSCs with nanoscale phase-separated PM6:PCBM to inhibit interfacial recombination. Resulting PSCs exhibit high efficiency of 25.60% with good stability.

title: Synergistic sensing properties of a standalone portable prototype using an integrated graphitic carbon nitride–carbon nanotube film for ultra-sensitive and selective acetone detection
abstract: Selective room-temperature detection of ultra-low concentrations of the biomarker gas acetone remains challenging due to the limited analyte interaction with the sensor substrates.

title: Theoretical study on the optoelectronic properties of fluorinated phenylpyrrole-based hole transport materials for perovskite solar cells
abstract: Hole transport materials (HTMs) in perovskite solar cells (PSCs) are crucial for hole transport and exciton dissociation; therefore, developing efficient HTMs is key to improving the stability and photoelectric conversion efficiency of PSCs.

title: Elimination of space charge effects in passivation films: the key to substantially enhancing the dielectric properties of tantalum oxide films
abstract: This study reveals for the first time the influence mechanism of the dual-layer structure in the native passivation film on the dielectric breakdown of Ta 2 O 5 , providing critical insights for enhancing the reliability of tantalum capacitors.

title: Proximity effects in the graphene–Co 3 Sn 2 S 2 interface
abstract: Electronic and magnetic properties of a graphene overlayer are defined by the surface termination of the underlying Co 3 Sn 2 S 2 .

title: Breaking down the confinement effect on perovskite growth to fabricate efficient, carbon electrode-based mesoscopic perovskite solar cells via low-temperature and all-air procedures
abstract: The confinement effect of PVSK growth is broken down by newly released micron-sized carbon-black spheres (CBSs), helping achieve a power conversion efficiency of 16.28% and T80 of ∼690 h during Q-MPPT test for mesoscopic perovskite solar cells.

title: Dual-conductive polymeric deep eutectic solvent scaffolds containing carbon nanotubes for spinal cord reconnection
abstract: Development of dual-conductive scaffolds based on polymerizable deep eutectic monomers (DEMs) and multi-walled carbon nanotubes (MWCNTs) for spinal cord repair.

title: Sustained acid release from stimuli responsive organic crystals facilitates shape modulation in metal nanoparticle synthesis
abstract: Stimuli responsive fluorescent materials play an essential role in smart material engineering. We designed molecules that exhibited stimuli responsive luminescence and gradual proton release for the synthesis and passivation of metal nanoparticles.

title: Electro-ferroelectric characterization of nicotinium tetrachloridocuprate( ii ) memristor for prospective neuromorphic applications
abstract: Integration of bio-sourced nicotine into organic–inorganic hybrid materials in memristive devices as artificial synapses, emphasizing their potential to bridge biological systems with next-generation computing technologies.

title: Synthesis and characterization of oxynitride glass films to develop a host for divalent-europium-centres
abstract: Eu-doped oxynitride glass films were prepared using a sol–gel process involving ammonolysis. Nitrogen was incorporated into the glass network, and the photoluminescence intensity increased as the ammonolysis holding time increased.

title: Strain-responsive PH-SiBiX monolayers: computational design of multifunctional 2D materials for piezotronic and optoelectronic applications
abstract: 2D materials combining electromechanical and optoelectronic functions are ideal for next-gen adaptive devices, but designing them is challenging due to trade-offs among piezoelectricity, auxeticity, and optical tunability.

title: Dual function optically coated sapphire substrate YAGG:Ce color converters for high brightness laser-driven solid-state lighting
abstract: This illustration summarizes the working principle of the sapphire anti-reflection/high-reflection coating, presenting images of transmittance, reflectance and thermal performance before and after the coating.

title: Realization of half-metallic intrinsic ferromagnetism with high Curie temperatures by the low oxidation state of chromium in Cr 2 TeX 2 (X = Br, I) monolayers
abstract: The monolayers of Cr 2 TeBr 2 and Cr 2 TeI 2 exhibit intrinsic FM half-metallicity with T C well above room temperature (904 K and 578 K). This arises from divalent Cr 2+ ions with a high-spin state and enhanced 180° d–p–d superexchange interaction.

title: Above and beyond the laser–induced anti-Stokes broadband white light emission in rare-earth-manganese perovskites
abstract: This work investigates laser-induced anti-Stokes broadband white light emission under varying pressure and excitation conditions. We analyse mechanisms of multistep ionisation, avalanche, and blackbody radiation via energy conservation principles.

title: UV to NIR photodetection in lateral homojunction PN diode of WSe 2 achieved via IGZO sputtering
abstract: A WSe 2 -based lateral homojunction PN diode with IGZO-induced polarity modulation exhibits high rectification, photoresponsivity (40.1 A W −1 ), and quantum efficiency (13634%), showcasing potential for next-generation optoelectronic devices.

title: A controllable and high-pressure gas driven microfluidic platform towards large-scale fabrication of multicolor-emissive carbon dots
abstract: A controllable and high-pressure gas driven microfluidic platform is developed for large-scale fabrication of multicolor-emissive carbon dots (CDs).

title: Shaping scintillation and UV-VIS-NIR luminescence properties through synergistic lattice disordered engineering and exciton-mediated energy transfer in Pr 3+ -doped Lu 1.5 Y 1.5 Al 5− x Sc x O 12 ( x = 0.0–2.0) garnets
abstract: Substituting Sc 3+ for Al 3+ in Pr 3+ -doped Lu 1.5 Y 1.5 Al 5− x Sc x O 12 crystals enhances the Pr 3+ emission across the UV-Vis-NIR spectral range. This enhancement is attributed to an efficient Sc 3+ → Pr 3+ energy transfer and increased structural disorder.

title: Transport in organic and hybrid semiconductors
abstract: Yuning Li, Simone Fabiano, and Oana D. Jurchescu introduce the Journal of Materials Chemistry C themed collection on Transport in Organic and Hybrid Semiconductors.

title: Recycling acidic iron wastewater for the production of an iron oxalate anode material with superior long-cycling lithium storage ability
abstract: Mechanism of ionic synergism in solution systems and physical and electrochemical characterisation of iron( ii ) oxalate.

title: Enhanced electrical properties of lead-free sodium potassium niobate piezoelectric ceramics prepared via cold sintering assisted sintering
abstract: The CSAS method can increase the density of the ceramics and reduce the volatilization of the A-site elements, thus changing the phase structure of the ceramics and making them high piezoelectric properties.

title: Highly transient and recyclable MoS 2 /PVA pressure sensor for sustainable healthcare and consumer electronic applications
abstract: A flexible and recyclable MoS 2 /PVA-based pressure sensor was developed. It detects pressure in the range of 1–3 kPa with a sensitivity of 0.87 kPa −1 and is suitable for real-time IoT applications.

title: Improved voltage and solubility in hybrid non-aqueous redox flow batteries using a molecular 3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene (EDOT) derivative with a stable radical cation state
abstract: A molecular ethylenedioxythiophene (EDOT) derivative is explored in hybrid lithium redox flow batteries. t -Butyl groups prevent polymerization & protolysis, resulting in a reversibly oxidizable molecule with excellent positive-charge-storing ability.

title: Wearable, near temperature insensitive laser-induced graphene nanocomposite strain sensors
abstract: Near temperature insensitive strain sensors based on laser-induced graphene nanocomposite with tunable electromechanical and electrothermal performance.

title: Imide-free electron-deficient electrolytes with adjustable cathode-modifying capabilities achieved by side chain engineering for efficient organic solar cells
abstract: The performance of organic solar cells can be regulated by altering the alkyl chains on quaternary ammonium groups of cathode interface materials, and a power conversion efficiency of 18.54% is realized in D18:L8-BO-based devices.

title: Tb( iii )-functionalized MOF hybridized bis-crosslinked networked hydrogel luminescent films for arginine and dopamine hydrochloride sensing and anticounterfeiting
abstract: Dopamine and arginine are both important substances in the body and are closely related to human health.

title: Accelerated design of AgNbO 3 -based ceramics with high energy storage performance via machine learning
abstract: The machine learning technique is promoted to accelerate the development of high-performance AgNbO 3 -based AFE ceramics. Guided by the predicted values, a high W rec of 7.0 J cm −3 is achieved experimentally.

title: Enhancing electrochemiluminescence through triplet state dynamics
abstract: This review highlights emerging mechanisms that efficiently harness triplet exciton states to significantly enhance electrochemiluminescence (ECL) performance.

title: Multi-functional benzothiazole-based phenothiazine derivatives: mechanochromism, acidochromism, and anticounterfeiting application
abstract: The development of stimuli responsive materials has advanced rapidly owing to their fascinating colour switching properties and wide range of applications in optoelectronics.

title: Exploring the triplet-to-singlet conversion mechanism in persistent luminescence: insights from a host–guest system
abstract: Triplet-to-singlet energy transfer from NPB to DCJTB enables fluorescence-derived luminescence on a tenths-of-a-second timescale, driven by NPB's efficient dual emission and low non-radiative triplet decay rate.

title: Harnessing substituent and aggregation-induced effects for color-tunable emission in borafluorenium ions
abstract: A series of hexaphenylcarbodiphosphorane-stabilized borafluorenium ions were synthesized with various substituents and counteranions. These compounds exhibit stimuli-responsive behavior, including aggregation-induced emission and thermochromism.

title: Characterization of organic light-emitting diodes using impedance spectroscopy and equivalent circuits
abstract: This review discusses the studies from pioneering groups that advanced the use of impedance spectroscopy (IS) and equivalent circuit techniques to investigate operational mechanisms in organic electronics.

title: An inorganic water-based paint for high-durability passive radiative cooling
abstract: A water-based, sprayable paint formulation made of inorganic alumina particles and a sodium methylsilicate binder combines strong radiative cooling properties with durable UV resistance without the need for high-temperature calcination treatments.

title: Interplay of the laser energy density and microstructure on the properties of NdFeB manufactured by laser powder bed fusion: a review
abstract: The influence of laser energy density on the microstructural evolution and mechanical and magnetic properties of NdFeB. Postprocessing and grain boundary diffusion enhance the magnetic properties and relative density.

title: Interpretable and uncertainty-informed machine learning to accelerate the design and discovery of lead-free piezoceramics with large piezoelectric constant
abstract: Interpretable and uncertainty-aware machine learning framework for the design and discovery of high-performance lead-free piezoceramics.

title: Inner structure and outer pressure synergistically trigger highly efficient luminescence in antimony-based perovskites
abstract: Antimony halide perovskites, with the orbit coupling of p–π orbits and hydrogen bond-assisted charge transport, realize an ultra-high PLQY value of 99.2% through short-term high-pressure treatment.

title: Obtaining excellent optical molecules by screening superalkali-doped cyclo[2 n ]carbons, M 3 O@C 2 n (M = Li, Na, and K, n = 5–10)
abstract: A comparative study on geometric, electronic, and optical properties of superalkali-doped cyclo[2 n ]carbon, M 3 O@C 2 n (M = Li, Na, and K, n = 5–10), were conducted using (time-dependent) density functional theory combined with wavefunction analysis.

title: Flexible patterned mirrors prepared by stacking patterned cholesteric liquid crystal polymer network films
abstract: A patterned cholesteric liquid crystal polymer network (CLCN) mirror was prepared and was suitably applied for decoration and encryption.

title: Mapping the polymorphic phase transformations of CsPbI 3 perovskite thin films
abstract: In situ isothermal XRD and microscopy are used to quantify the time- and temperature-dependent thin-film phase transitions of CsPbI 3 from its optoelectronically-desirable black phases to its yellow non-perovskite δ-CsPbI 3 phase.

title: Quasi-ordered photonic glass materials: fabrication strategies, performance enhancement and applications
abstract: This review provides a concise overview on recent advanced strategies for the creation of the diversity of photonic glass materials with remarkable non-iridescent structural colors and unique short-range ordered nanostructures.

title: Incorporation and electronic sensing device effects of aniline functionality in diketopyrrolopyrrole–thiophene semiconducting polymers
abstract: The first synthesis of amine- or aniline-substituted thiophene polymers is reported, and field-effect transistor electronic responses to acetone are shown.

title: Low-damage encapsulation of flexible organic photovoltaic modules using alternating organic and inorganic layers
abstract: Stability of flexible OPV modules with flexible encapsulation.

title: Superhydrophobic, biocompatible MXene-based multifunctional e-textiles for wireless transmission, electromagnetic interference shielding and healthcare monitoring
abstract: The sensor were fabricated using ultrasonic welding and dip-coating on airlaid paper, resulting in superhydrophobic, biocompatible MXene-based e-textiles for wireless transmission, electromagnetic shielding, and healthcare monitoring.

title: A carbon dot anchored bacterial cellulose hybrid platform as a fluorescent sensor and photocatalytic remover of pharmaceuticals
abstract: In the present work, one arrow two hawk approach was implemented to enable fluorometric trace level detection as well as photocatalytic remediation of antibiotic drugs tetracycline and doxycycline using M-CDs@BC a luminescent hydrogel composite.

title: Self-assembled monolayer engineering improves the sensitivity and response speed of high-performance perovskite photodetectors
abstract: A hole-transporting self-assembled monolayer enables efficient energy level alignment and an interface dipole to significantly improve the sensitivity and response speed of perovskite photodetectors.

title: Improvement of the chemical and physical gas adsorption ability of poly(3-hexylthiophene) thin films by ultraviolet-ozone exposure
abstract: This study shows that UV-ozone treatment improves the gas-sensing performance of P3HT films. Treated sensors display high NO 2 sensitivity and selectivity with a 21.5 ppb detection limit. The treatment causes oxidation and chain scission in P3HT.

title: Advances and challenges in chemical mechanical polishing of silicon carbide: materials, mechanisms, and future directions
abstract: Silicon carbide (SiC), a third-generation wide bandgap semiconductor material, demonstrates immense potential in high-temperature, high-frequency, and high-power electronic devices.

title: Interfacial spreading for rapid formation of uniform gate dielectric layers on flat and curved substrates for organic devices
abstract: The interfacial-spreading method represents a reliable technique for fabricating polymer gate dielectrics, demonstrating excellent compatibility with both flat and curved substrates, as well as a wide range of polymer materials.

title: MOF-derived TiO 2 /C/g-C 3 N 4 composites with dual heterojunction surfaces for efficient electromagnetic absorption
abstract: The development of efficient electromagnetic wave-absorbing materials is essential for various technological applications, including telecommunications, radar systems, and electronic devices.

title: Rapid efficiency loss of FAPbI 3 perovskite solar cells in 2-methoxyethanol-based precursor solutions
abstract: 2-ME-based perovskite precursor solutions exhibit rapid degradation, significantly impacting device efficiency. A sequential incorporation strategy avoids the adverse effects of solution aging and enhances device efficiency.

title: Solution-processed CuBO 2 hole transport layers for stable p–i–n perovskite solar cells
abstract: Inverted p–i–n PSCs based on highly continuous and compact CuBO 2 HTLs exhibit a remarkable PCE of 18.26% and demonstrate superior open-circuit voltage and environmental stability compared with PEDOT:PSS-based devices.

title: Correction: Enhanced piezoelectric output performance in flexible polyvinylidene fluoride foam with a hierarchical dual-pore structure
abstract: Correction for ‘Enhanced piezoelectric output performance in flexible polyvinylidene fluoride foam with a hierarchical dual-pore structure’ by Xufeng Chen et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/d5tc00629e.

title: Crystal structure, magnetic and magnetocaloric properties of the new orthorhombic Y 3 Co 2 -type Gd 3 Co 1+ x Ni 1− x solid solution
abstract: The discovery of the novel Gd 3 Co 1+ x Ni 1− x compound is reported. It is shown that the magnetocaloric properties are among the best in its working temperature range and Co/Ni substitution tunes the working temperature without compromising performance.

title: Chalcogenide phase-change materials: unveiling new horizons with big data and machine learning
abstract: This review highlights how machine learning accelerates chalcogenide phase-change material research via high-throughput screening, large-scale simulations with machine learning potentials, and memory/neuromorphic device optimization.

title: Exploration of nonlinear optical materials by introducing information science
abstract: A material search scheme is proposed herein to discover novel deep ultraviolet nonlinear optical (NLO) crystals.

title: Flexible PVDF–NCMF nanocomposites: a synergistic approach to enhanced magneto-dielectric properties and sensing performance
abstract: Magnetic domain interactions at the nanoscale in flexible, free-standing PVDF–NCMF nanocomposite films demonstrated good sensing performance at a magnetic field of 50 Oe indicating their potential for utilization in various multifunctional devices.

title: Enhanced output performance in paper-based piezoelectric nanogenerators via polarization-engineered Schottky barrier contacts
abstract: Superior performance primarily results from piezoelectric polarization coupled with Schottky contact, with triboelectric contributions from the interaction between the film and PDMS.

title: Tunable Schottky barrier of GaSe/graphene heterostructure via asymmetric O doping
abstract: By asymmetric O doping modulation, the Schottky barrier of a GaSe/graphene heterostructure can be effectively lowered.

title: Solution-based exfoliation of non-layered, non-van der Waals materials and applications: current status and future directions
abstract: Recent advancements in solution-based exfoliation of non-van der Waals 3D materials are discussed, focusing on bonding anisotropy, cleavage planes, solvent selection, dispersion and chemical stability, and their applications in various fields.

title: Luminescent lanthanide complexes supported by ditopic Schiff-base/calix[4]arene macrocycles: synthesis, structure, and luminescence properties of [Ln 2 (H 2 L2)(H 2 O) 2 ] (Ln = La, Eu, Tb, Yb)
abstract: Schiff-base units in homo- and heterodinuclear lanthanide complexes can act as antennas for sensitization of Eu and Tb luminescence.

title: Crystal structure engineering of Prussian blue through a double-acting chelating agent for energy storage devices
abstract: This work utilizes aspartic acid as both an acidulant and a chelant, effectively controlling the crystallization rate of Prussian blue, reducing its usage, and enhancing its electrochemical performance.

title: Extending the light response range of organic photoelectric synaptic transistors by p-doping
abstract: A field-effect transistor-based synaptic device has been fabricated with a broadened spectral response by utilizing a charge transfer complex composed of 2,3,5,6-tetrafluoro-7,7,8,8-tetracyanoquinodimethane and poly(3-hexylthiophene-2,5-diyl).

title: The role of ring-type structures in nonconventional luminescent polyurethane derivatives
abstract: Experimental and computational studies of polyurethane derivatives provide new insights into the mechanism of luminescence in non-conjugated molecules. Applications as biological probes and information transmission materials are demonstrated.

title: Electronic structures and magnetic properties of the rare-earth-free permanent magnet α′′-Fe 16 N 2 : first-principles calculations
abstract: α′′-Fe 16 N 2 exhibits perpendicular magnetic anisotropy with a magnetocrystalline anisotropy energy of −0.66 J cm −3 and a high Curie temperature of 1369 K, making it a potential candidate material as a rare-earth-free permanent magnet.

title: Enhancing the efficiency of CZTSSe solar cells via binary solvent induced microstructure regulation
abstract: Our research focuses on a novel DMSO-IPA binary solvent system for CZTSSe thin film solar cells. Precise ratio adjustment improves film and device qualities. Adding 20% IPA boosts key parameters, increasing PCE from 3.33% to 7.18%.

title: Wrinkle-engineered multi-walled carbon nanotubes/laser-induced graphene heterostructured films for ultra-sensitive strain sensing
abstract: Flexible wrinkled MWCNTs/LIG strain sensor.

title: Interlaced NiCoO 2 nanoparticle/nanosheet films for electrochromic energy storage devices with wide-band optical modulation and robust stability
abstract: A uniform NiCoO 2 film with an interlaced nanoparticle/nanosheet structure was successfully grown on transparent conductive substrates for transparent-to-brownish grey electrochromic smart windows with improved cyclic stability.

title: Structural frustration effects by mixed alkali ions in ferroelectric Dion–Jacobson layered perovskites (Cs,Rb)NdNb 2 O 7
abstract: Dion–Jacobson phases CsNdNb 2 O 7 and RbNdNb 2 O 7 adopt two types of polar structures with different octahedral tilts. In their solid solutions, the competition between the two phases causes structural frustration effects on ferroelectric transitions.

title: The effect of bipolar charge transport of derivatives of 1-phenyl-1 H -benzo[ d ]imidazole with horizontal molecular orientation on the performance of OLEDs based on thermally activated delayed fluorescence or phosphorescence
abstract: The synthesis and properties of two derivatives of 1-phenyl-1 H -benzo[ d ]imidazole with differing numbers of tert -butylcarbazole electron-donating moieties are reported.

title: Influence of pore-confined water on the thermal expansion of a zinc-based metal–organic framework
abstract: In situ temperature studies on a zinc-based metal–organic framework reveal a reversible phase transition between a hydrated and a non-hydrated form with different thermal expansions along covalently-, hydrogen- and van der Waals-bonded directions.

title: Ti 0.87 O 2 nanosheets on Ni integrated n-Si: planar architecture with enhanced photoresponse for self-driven photodetectors
abstract: Broadband, low-cost photodetectors are essential for low-power optoelectronic applications in communication, surveillance, robotics, and scientific research.

title: Broadband microwave absorption of sandwich-like RGA/CNP/RGA composites under strong polarization relaxation at multiscale interfaces
abstract: The sandwich-like RGA/CNP/RGA microwave absorption materials can reach a reflection loss of −48.03 dB and an effective absorption bandwidth of 7.14 GHz.

title: Multiscale simulation study on the mechanical, electrical, and thermal properties of ZnSb semiconductor
abstract: This study presents a comprehensive investigation of the multifunctional properties of ZnSb semiconductors through integrated multiscale simulations.

title: Spray-coated CsPbBr 3 /CsPb 2 Br 5 thin film photodetectors from nanocrystalline inks
abstract: Herein, we developed a simple approach for the fabrication of thin film inorganic perovskite and perovskite-related photoconductors.

title: A robust, efficient ion transport polytetrafluoroethylene fibrous membrane-based separator with superior stability for ultralong-life zinc ion hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: A robust and efficient ion transport PTFE-based separator with superior stability was fabricated for zinc ion hybrid supercapacitors, demonstrating excellent electrochemical performance.

title: Efficient photocatalytic overall water-splitting characteristics driven by strain engineering in two-dimensional β-AuTe
abstract: Uniaxial strain (6–12%) makes 2D β-AuTe a stable photocatalyst with high carrier mobility and UV-VIS absorption for acidic H 2 O splitting with 20.07% STH efficiency. Our study also proposes strain as a simpler way of designing new photocatalysts.

title: Liquid-crystalline dual fluorescent dyes for white light emission
abstract: Isomeric liquid-crystalline phenanthrolines show dual emission in polystyrenes films and emit white light in OLED devices.

title: Strategic integration of MXene into FeMnO 3 matrix for superior energy density in hybrid supercapacitors elucidated via Dunn's model
abstract: FeMnO 3 integrated with MXene yielded nanoporous architectures. Electrochemical studies showed the composite to be an efficient supercapacitor electrode, offering enhanced energy density with stability.

title: High-sensitivity optical thermometer based on the luminescence of divalent and trivalent thulium ions in CaAl 4 O 7 :Tm 2+/3+ operating in cryogenic and high temperature ranges
abstract: The material CaAl 4 O 7 :Tm 2+/3+ showed an optimal relative sensitivity from cryogenic to high temperatures. A gradual change in emission color was observed in CIE diagram with varying temperatures.

title: Half-lantern Pt( ii ) complexes in deep-red hybrid light-emitting diodes
abstract: A series of “halt-lantern” [Pt(piq)(μ-N^S)] 2 complexes (piq = 1-phenylisoquinolinate) with different bridging thiolates (N^S) show bright deep-red emissions and are implemented as phosphors in hybrid light-emitting diodes (HLEDs).

title: The elegance of simplicity: a cost-effective Janus membrane for all-day radiative thermal management inspired by complementary photothermal design
abstract: A MXene/Nylon66 Janus membrane enables bidirectional thermal regulation via flipping: subambient cooling (−14.8 °C) and solar heating (+36.4 °C). Its simple, low-cost fabrication offers strong potential for scalable use in space and buildings.

title: On the impact of selective donor:acceptor structural ordering in PBDB-T:ITIC organic solar cells
abstract: Processing control allows delineating the individual effect of the donor and acceptor ordering in the performance of organic solar cells.

title: Machine learning-based prediction of fluorescence lifetimes of zero-dimensional antimony halides
abstract: This paper proposes an innovative machine learning-based approach for predicting fluorescence lifetimes of zero-dimensional antimony halides, which is for the purpose of establishing relationships between material structures and emission properties.

title: A graphene-enhanced PEDOT:PSS/β-Ga 2 O 3 microwire organic–inorganic hybrid heterojunction self-driven photodetector with high light responsivity
abstract: The introduction of graphene improves the electrical properties of the PEDOT:PSS film, and when combined with β-Ga 2 O 3 MW, it can effectively increase the strength of the built-in electric field.

title: Magnetically induced stress birefringence in stimuli-responsive hydrogels
abstract: We report the appearance of stress birefringence induced by an external magnetic field in stimuli-responsive hydrogels containing magnetic nanoparticles (Fe 3 O 4 ).

title: Constructing a smooth dense SiO 2 layer by adjusting the hydrolysis rate to promote the magnetic properties of soft magnetic composites
abstract: Dense smooth SiO 2 layers were constructed by decreasing TEOS hydrolysis rate, upgrading the overall performance of FeNi soft magnetic composites.

title: Na-doped Ga 2 O 3 electrolyte-gated synaptic transistors for neuromorphic computing
abstract: This work presents Na-doped Ga 2 O 3 three-terminal electrolyte-gated synaptic transistors that successfully emulate a range of synaptic behaviors for neuromorphic computing.

title: Ordered Co/Ni oxide nanostructures from MOFs: enhancing efficiency in hybrid asymmetric energy devices
abstract: DOCK5 acts at focal adhesions in breast cells to establish positive feedback loops via CDC42 and RAC. The stabilisation of polarity during migration promotes persistent YAP/TAZ activation which we propose drives cancer phenotypes.

title: A multifunctional, double network cellulose-acrylamide based hydrogel sensor reinforced using a liquid metal for human motion detection and dual-mode handwriting recognition via a transfer learning algorithm
abstract: A double network cellulose-acrylamide based hydrogel sensor reinforced by LM for high accuracy dual-mode handwriting recognition.

title: Abnormal electronic transport properties with Hall effect, magneto-resistivity, and phase diagram in CaFeAsF single crystals
abstract: An iron-based superconductor 1111-system exhibits rich physical properties, which can provide a very good material platform for the study of superconducting properties.

title: Thermoelectric properties of topological Weyl semimetal family RAlX (R = La, Ce, Nd; X = Si, Ge): a first-principles investigation
abstract: Weyl semimetals have emerged as an exciting class of materials with a myriad of non-trivial transport properties due to their topological nature.

title: A comprehensive study on A 2 PdH 2 : from ambient to high pressure
abstract: We present a comprehensive first-principles study of the structural stability and superconducting behavior of Li 2 PdH 2 under high pressure.

title: Promising guanidinium layered perovskite photoelectric synaptic transistors for neuromorphic computing and image recognition
abstract: A perovskite optoelectronic synaptic transistor with efficient synaptic functions was fabricated using (GA)(MA) 5 Pb 5 I 16 /Spiro-OMeTAD.

title: Glassy ultralow thermal conductivity in Cs 2 TiX 6 (X = Cl, Br, I) arises from bond weakening
abstract: Manipulating phonon transport in vacancy-ordered halide perovskites Cs 2 TiX 6 (X = Cl, Br, I) via the interplay of chemical bonding and lattice dynamics.

title: Negative differential transconductance effects on anomalous n-type characteristics in p-type WSe 2 semiconductor field effect transistors
abstract: The NDT effect with a high peak current in WSe 2 FET is demonstrated during the sweep from positive to negative voltage.

title: Solvent engineering with ACN for the growth of high-quality CsPbBr 3 single crystals
abstract: CsPbBr 3 single crystals grown via DMSO/DMF/ACN solvent system have an FWHM of 0.035° and a μτ value of 2.57 × 10 −4 cm 2 V −1 , comparable to those grown by the melt method.

title: Interlayer coupling in tri-layered van der Waals heterostructures of MX 2 (M = Mo, W; X = S, Se, Te) monolayers: optical and photocatalytic response
abstract: Electronic structure, optical and photocatalytic properties of tri-layered van der Waals heterostructures (vdWHs) composed of MX 2 (M = Mo, W; X = S, Se, Te) monolayers are investigated via first principles (hybrid) calculations.

title: Defect-induced new persistent cyan-emitting rare-earth-free phosphors for dynamic anti-counterfeiting and plant-growth LED applications
abstract: The mixture of persistent blue and deep red-emitting systems is suitable for currency and QR code anti-counterfeiting due to the persistent blue emission and as plant growth lighting due to their emission overlapping with plant pigments’ absorption.

title: Perfluorooctanoic acid-mediated self-assembly behaviour of linear and star block copolymers: the impact of intermolecular interactions on polymer micelles
abstract: Perfluorooctanoic acid-mediated self-assembly behaviour of linear and star block copolymers.

title: Metal–organic frameworks at the tip of the e-tongue: machine learning-driven disposable electrochemical sensors
abstract: Disposable e-tongues from metal–organic frameworks demonstrated a classification accuracy comparable to reusable e-tongues, while offering advantages in use and fabrication.

title: Unlocking Mo, P co-doping to boost proton intercalation in MnO 2 as a high-performance cathode material for aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Mo, P co-doping can reduce the insertion energy of H + , which is beneficial to the intercalation of H + , and explains the increase in the contribution of H + intercalation. The capacity of Mo, P–MnO 2 is 216 mA h g −1 after 1100 cycles.

title: Expanding the horizons of phase transition-based luminescence thermometry
abstract: The changes in the luminescence properties of the Eu 3+ ions associated with the thermally induced phase transition in LaGaO 3 :Eu 3+ enables the development of the highly sensitive ratiometric luminescence thermometer.

title: A damping hydrogel with high water retention and strong adhesion for precise bioelectric signal detection
abstract: A chitosan/acrylic acid/acetamide-based damping hydrogel with high water retention and strong adhesion properties.

title: Cr 3+ -doped CaMgGe 2 O 6 phosphors: crystal field effects and the synergistic role of FIR and fluorescence lifetime in multi-mode optical thermometry
abstract: Luminescence thermometry has attracted growing interest for its potential in remote and non-contact temperature sensing.

title: Cu-doped CoWO 4 /WO 3 heterojunctions as peroxymonosulfate activators for rapid degradation of organic pollutants
abstract: A Cu-doped CoWO 4 /WO 3 catalyst was synthesized using an aqueous bath and a calcination method. Rapid Co–Cu redox cycling and type-II heterojunction synergy enable efficient PMS activation and organic pollutant degradation.

title: Development of organometallic single-crystal scintillators with CuI clusters and 18-crown-6 ether–alkali metal complexes
abstract: Organometallic single-crystal scintillators with copper-iodide clusters and 18-crown-6 ether–alkali metal complexes were synthesized. (18-crown-6) 2 Na 2 (H 2 O) 3 Cu 4 I 6 showed the highest light yield of 59 000 photons per MeV upon 137 Cs γ-ray irradiation.

title: CaLuGaO 4 :Bi 3+ ,Al 3+ blue phosphor with excellent thermal stability for multiple LED applications
abstract: CaLuGaO 4 :Bi 3+ ,Al 3+ is a blue phosphor activated by near-ultraviolet light, with improved luminescence through Al 3+ incorporation. It holds promise for applications in plant lighting and near-ultraviolet white LEDs.

title: Tuning valley-Ohmic contacts and interfacial electronic properties in valley-polarized semiconductor/Dirac semimetal heterostructures
abstract: The proposed X/GdI₂ heterostructures, combining valley-polarized semiconductors and 2D Dirac semimetals, enable intrinsic Ohmic contacts and tunable valley-selective transport for reconfigurable valleytronic and spintronic devices.

title: Photo-responsive near-infrared circularly polarized luminescent liquid crystal copolymers
abstract: The first example of photo-responsive near-infrared circularly polarized luminescent liquid crystal polymer materials was reported.

title: A metal-free molecular pyroelectric material with strong pyro-photovoltaic coupling for enhancing self-powered X-ray response
abstract: We report metal-free pyroelectric materials with tunable pyroelectric via molecular design. The top candidate enables self-powered X-ray detection through photo-pyroelectric effects. This work advances the development of optoelectronic materials.

title: Multifunctional PANI–TiO 2 –Fe 3 O 4 @attapulgite 3D composites for synergistic microwave absorption and corrosion protection
abstract: Electromagnetic (EM) pollution has emerged as a pressing concern, driving the demand for high-performance microwave absorbing materials.

title: Easy-peeling growth of triangular hBN flakes and their demonstration in vacuum ultraviolet photodetectors
abstract: An easy-peeling growth method for hBN flakes with high crystalline quality was developed using a controlled reprecipitated BN powder layer. A high-performance vacuum ultraviolet photodetector was fabricated using the peeled and transferred hBN film.

title: A micro-laser grown from non-photoluminescent Cs 4 PbBr 6 nanocrystals
abstract: The no-PL emissive Cs 4 PbBr 6 nanocrystals transform into highly PL emissive CsPbBr 3 nanocrystals upon the addition of water, and over time, they grow into microrods with lasing-emitting properties.

title: Theoretical and experimental revelation of the structural, dielectric, optical and magnetic properties of Sr 1− x Ni x Fe 8 SnO 15 ( x = 0, 0.2, 0.4, and 0.6) V-type hexagonal ferrites
abstract: A novel V-type hexaferrite with the substitution of nickel, having the chemical formula Sr 1− x Ni x Fe 8 SnO 15 ( x = 0.0, 0.2, 0.4, and 0.6), is synthesized by adopting the sol–gel auto combustion method.

title: Tailoring energy bands and internal polarization in ZnIn 2 S 4 for synergistic enhancement of carrier separation and high-performance Piezo-PEC water splitting
abstract: This study proposes a Cu–Fe co-doping strategy for ZnIn 2 S 4 :Cu at Zn sites forms band gap intermediate energy levels for electron transitions; Fe at In sites affects internal polar structures, synergistically optimizing catalytic activity.

title: The thermal transport, optical, and mechanical properties of 1T-Au 6 SSe: a first-principles study
abstract: Cluster substitution and constructing Janus materials can independently regulate the physicochemical properties of materials and have been applied in multiple fields.

title: Big pyridyl Schiff base π-conjugated skeleton based cobalt/iron metal complexes: bimetallic electrocatalysts for the oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Cobalt/iron-based redox-active pyridyl Schiff base metal complexes were synthesized simply. The optimized Co 3 FePM-TPDA showed good OER performance and stability, even in simulated seawater.

title: Chiral dicarbazole-ditriarylamine hole transport materials for circularly polarized electroluminescence
abstract: This work provides an effective strategy for developing high-performance chiral HTMs, exhibiting both efficient hole mobilities and CP-EL signals.

title: Tunable magnetism in two-dimensional ferroelectric Janus NbOXY (X, Y = Cl, Br, I; X ≠ Y) by hole doping
abstract: Janus NbOXY monolayers exhibit robust room-temperature ferroelectricity with FE–AFE transitions and hole-doping-induced tunable magnetism, advancing 2D ferroelectric materials for multifunctional device applications.

title: Correction: Impact of oxygen vacancy reduction on the dielectric, energy storage, and electrocaloric properties of annealed BCZT ceramic
abstract: Correction for ‘Impact of oxygen vacancy reduction on the dielectric, energy storage, and electrocaloric properties of annealed BCZT ceramic’ by Vartika Khandelwal et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, 13 , 10178–10193, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TC00115C.

title: Correction: New frontiers in ceramic composites: tunable electromagnetic interference shielding by realizing negative permittivity in SnO 2 /LaNiO 3 nanocomposites
abstract: Correction for ‘New frontiers in ceramic composites: tunable electromagnetic interference shielding by realizing negative permittivity in SnO 2 /LaNiO 3 nanocomposites’ by Tarun Katheriya et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/d5tc01396h.

title: Near-white-light emissive upconversion NaYF 4 :50%Yb,2%Er@NaYbF 4 :1%Tm@NaYF 4 nanoparticles for the ratiometric fluorescence detection of sulfite
abstract: Near-white light emissive UCNPs and their ratiometric sensing of sulfites.

title: Laser-pumped high-power compact near-infrared light sources based on phosphor-in-glass films
abstract: A novel CZTGGZO:Cr 3+ near infrared PiG film has a high IQE (90.20%) and good thermal stability (92.32%@423 K), enabling it to be applied to near end caries detection and long distance near infrared illumination.

title: Molecular engineering of polarity control in two-dimensional materials for optoelectronic applications
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive overview of the molecular engineering techniques used to control polarity in two-dimensional materials and their applications in optoelectronic devices.

title: Lead-free lanthanide-based Cs 3 LnCl 6 metal halides
abstract: This review systematically summarized the synthesis approaches towards both polycrystals and nanocrystals, and discussed the crystal/electronic structure, luminescence principle, and optimized strategies of lanthanide-based Cs 3 LnCl 6 metal halides.

title: Recent advances in carbon assisted pyroelectric composites for energy conversion
abstract: Pyroelectric materials have enormous potential in energy conversion. This article reviews the latest theoretical and experimental advances in carbon assisted pyroelectric composites for sensing, energy harvesting, and pyroelectric catalysis.

title: Harnessing machine learning to probe dielectrics in next generation telecommunication and automotive radar applications
abstract: Generative Reinforcement Learning (GRL) Model combined with Genetic Algorithm Neural Network (GANN) is used to determine the appropriate Dielectric Material for its application in antennas for 5G and 6G transmission.

title: Flexible and conductive MXene/silver nanowire films for enhanced detection and elimination of bacteria
abstract: A schematic illustration of the MXene/AgNWs film-based bio-platform for the detection and elimination of bacteria.

title: Giant thermally induced band-gap renormalization in anharmonic silver chalcohalide antiperovskites
abstract: Silver chalcohalide antiperovskites (CAP), Ag 3 XY (X = S, Se; Y = Br, I), are a family of highly anharmonic inorganic compounds with great potential for energy applications.

title: Cr 3+ -induced broadband near-infrared I combined with near-infrared II emission via rare earth co-doping in Cs 2 NaInCl 6 for multifunctional detection
abstract: The Cr 3+ -induced Cs 2 NaInCl 6 and energy-transfer engineered Ln 3+ co-doping DPs have been developed, and demonstrate superior broad near-infrared emission for multifunctional detection.

title: Designing a machine learning-optimized nitrogen and sulfur co-doped CoNi@SiO 2 electrocatalyst for high performance oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Although various heteroatom-doped bimetallic composites have been explored for the oxygen evolution reaction (OER), they often suffer from aggregation and low electrical conductivity, which hinder their electrocatalytic efficacy.

title: The self-activated LiGa 5 O 8 storage phosphor: insights into its photo/thermo/mechano-stimulated NIR luminescence
abstract: Based on first principles calculations and experiments, a mechanism was proposed to enhance the anisotropy of physical properties, reduce the bandgap, and make the traps caused by stress/defects shallower on the basis of theoretical calculations.

title: Non-halogenated solvent-processed TADF OLEDs with polymer-based exciplex hosts
abstract: As the alkylene spacer length increases, solubility improves, allowing the use of non-halogenated solvents as EML solvents. Nevertheless, the performance remains unchanged regardless of the solvent used.

title: High-aspect-ratio photoresist nanopillar arrays with broadband near-perfect optical absorption performance using PDMS-assisted colloidal lithography
abstract: By PDMS-assisted colloidal lithography, high-aspect-ratio microstructures were employed as the structure for the successful fabrication of broadband near-perfect optical absorbers.

title: Lanthanum substitution enhanced M-type strontium ferrite absorbing performance based on the substitution amount and iron strontium molar ratio
abstract: La 3+ doping in SrFe 12 O 19 creates defect polarization centers. Coupled with Sr/Fe stoichiometry, it optimizes a lamellar microstructure, achieving excellent microwave absorption, highlighting microstructural engineering's key role.

title: Construction of a hybrid SERS chip composed of a microstructured Si metasurface and MoS x O y for sensitive molecular detection
abstract: A hybrid SERS substrate, which integrates a periodic Si metasurface with MoS x O y nanostructures, makes the detection of R6G at LOD of 10 −9 M and EF of 10 7 , due to the combined effects of localized near-field amplification and charge transfer.

title: The intersection of field-limited density of states and matter in radioluminescence: nanophotonic control of energy transfer
abstract: Nanophotonic control over radioluminescence and Förster resonance energy transfer between three copolymerized emitters in a crystalline colloidal array was demonstrated through strategic placement of the rejection wavelength ( λ rw ).

title: Modular construction of medium-to-long wavelength multi-resonant fluorescent emitters
abstract: Four multi-resonant fluorescent materials based on a N/B–C framework show narrowband emission from green to red with high photoluminescence efficiencies. While their organic light-emitting diodes exhibit external quantum efficiencies of up to 23%.

title: Effect of charge distribution at nitrogen atoms of polyaniline copolymers on spin-dependent transport: experimental analysis and density functional theory calculations
abstract: The charge distribution at nitrogen atoms of polyaniline significantly affects the spin-dependent transport properties.

title: White organic light-emitting diodes with extremely low turn-on voltage at 1.5 V
abstract: We report a white organic light-emitting diode with a low turn-on voltage of less than 1.5 V. We utilized upconversion that can produce high-energy blue emission at a small voltage, and incorporated sky-blue and yellow dopants to realize white color.

title: Simultaneous adjustment of afterglow wavelength and intensity in indium-substituted Ga 1.99− x In x O 3 :0.01Cr 3+
abstract: Replacing gallium with indium in the crystal structure of Ga 2 O 3 :Cr 3+ redshifts the emission wavelength to 830 nm and induces persistent luminescence.

title: Micromagnetic dynamics in field-free magnetization switching assisted by interlayer Dzyaloshinskii–Moriya interaction
abstract: In depth exploration of iDMI effect on SOT induced field-free switching. Digital encoding applications.

title: The leakage current suppression mechanism in a RuO 2 /SrTiO 3 /Ru capacitor induced by introduction of an ultra-thin GeO 2 interfacial layer at the bottom interface
abstract: An ultrathin GeO 2 layer effectively passivates the STO/Ru BE interface and compensates oxygen vacancies within the STO layer, thereby significantly suppressing leakage current in an MIM capacitor.

title: Inkjet-printed tunable 2D metal thiophosphate saturable absorbers for pulsed solid-state laser applications
abstract: For the first time, NiPSe 3 , CoPS 3 , and CrPSe 4 nanosheets were employed as tunable SAs in 1 μm bulk pulsed lasers, enabling a CW-pumped PQS laser, a pulsed-pumped PQS laser, and a QML laser, respectively.

title: Enhanced chromaticity and dual emission in double-site Mn 4+ -activated KLiTiF 6 red phosphors for wide color gamut displays
abstract: Dual-site Mn 4+ emission in KLiTiF 6 phosphors: enhanced chromaticity and thermal stability for advanced wide color gamut displays.

title: A high-performance self-powered CsPbBr 3 perovskite photodetector enabled by self-assembled monolayer interface engineering
abstract: We developed a self-powered photodetector using a SAM interlayer for defect passivation and electron blocking. The device shows low dark current, an on/off ratio of 7.6 × 10⁶, detectivity of 2.74 × 10¹² Jones under 365 nm light.

title: Origins of chalcogenide perovskite instability
abstract: This work assesses the thermodynamic stability of chalcogenide perovskites, evaluating tolerance factor predictions against first-principles calculations and proposing thermodynamic and chemical origins for the sparsity of these materials.

title: A self-powered and stretchable magnetic film for human–machine interface applications
abstract: We designed an efficient stretchable magnetoelectric film for self-powered energy harvesting devices under low-intensity magnetic fields. This soft wearable platform enables real-time gesture recognition.

title: Enhanced thermoelectric and mechanical properties of Cu 1.8 S 1− x P x bulks mediated by mixed phase engineering
abstract: An innovative chemical formula of Cu 1.8 S 1− x P x was proposed to tune the Cu–to–S ratio while enhancing both thermoelectric and mechanical properties by mixed phase engineering.

title: Plenty of room at the top: exploiting nanowire – polymer synergies in transparent electrodes for infrared imagers
abstract: To overcome the challenge of scalable IR-transparent top contacts, we introduce a Ag NW–PVA film with >70% IR transmittance and ∼11 Ω sq −1 resistance. Integrated with quantum dots, it enables high-detectivity (∼2.9 × 10 11 Jones) IR photodiodes.

title: Achieving over 30% photon-to-photon efficiency with tandem OLED structures in organic upconversion devices
abstract: The tandem OLED structure significantly enhances the emission efficiency of the upconversion device, thereby improving the photon conversion efficiency and enabling high-efficiency upconversion imaging.

title: Enhancing the efficiency of blue organic light-emitting diodes by using a dual emitting layer structure
abstract: Two different methods were employed to fabricate OLEDs: the first method used two different host materials, while the second method introduced TPBi as a hole blocking layer between the emitting layers.

title: Deciphering carbon–sulfur rotational distribution in a crystalline host for enhanced red persistent organic phosphorescence
abstract: A collaborative synthesis and computational approach is used to decipher confined carbon–sulfur rotational distribution in a crystalline host for enhanced red persistent phosphorescence.

title: Magnetic-field alignment of micellar lyotropic nematic gels and their memory-effect
abstract: The gelation of micellar nematic lyotropic liquid crystals, which are aligned by the action of a magnetic field, provides an easy and versatile access to macroscopically anisotropic hydrogels.

title: Work function modulated water-soluble anode interlayer with copper-ion doping for precise signal detection in organic photodiodes
abstract: Organic photovoltaics (OPVs) and photodetectors (OPDs) based on water-soluble copper-ion doped polyelectrolyte achieves improved light energy harvesting and signal detection because of the improved hole transport and internal device resistance.

title: High-performance visible-to-near infrared phototransistor based on SnSe/SnS 2 van der Waals heterostructure
abstract: As a member of two-dimensional materials, tin disulfide (SnS 2 ) holds great potential for highly sensitive detectors used in high-resolution, low-power imaging.

title: Subtle conformational variations induce distinct optical and electron transport properties in naphthalenediimide-based solids
abstract: Distinct opitcal features and computed charge transport characteristics of polymorphic forms obtained from electron deficient, aromatic end group substituted napthalenediimde derived n-type molecule is described.

title: Shortwave infrared organic phototransistors with improved performance via conjugated polymer blends and a metal reflector gate architecture
abstract: Enhanced SWIR phototransistor performance is achieved through a dual strategy combining polymer blends of two D–A type low-bandgap conjugated polymers with type I band alignment and a metal reflector design.

title: A trifluoromethyl group-incorporated spacer in 2D perovskite-based photodetectors with good responsivity and a big Pb–I–Pb bond angle: the first example of an aliphatic linearly-chained spacer forming non-classical hydrophobic hydrogen bonding
abstract: For the first time, we report a trifluoromethyl group-incorporated aliphatic linearly-chained 2D (3F-PAH) 2 PbI 4 halide perovskite (HP) with a low bandgap of 2.28 eV, minimal Pb–I–Pb bond angle distortion (∼153.5°) and a unique fluorous phase.

title: Magnetic field enhanced charge conduction in paramagnetic nickel( ii )–cysteine heterostructures
abstract: l -Cys-Ni( ii ) and d -Cys Ni( ii ) heterostructures showed a significant response to an external magnetic field, resulting in an enhanced charge conduction in solid-state electronic devices and electrochemical charge transfer processes.

title: Enhanced thermoelectric performance of Mg 3 Sb 2 -based materials by codoping with B and Te
abstract: B and Te co-doped samples of Mg 3.2 B y SbBi 1− x Te x achieved the lowest κ − κ E of ∼0.49 W m −1 K −1 and a maximum zT of ∼1.54.

title: Recent advances in the O-doped polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons
abstract: The strategic incorporation of oxygen atoms into polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) has emerged as an effective strategy for developing advanced organic materials with precisely tailored optoelectronic functionalities.

title: Dimethylamine metal halides for high-sensitivity fluorescence lifetime thermometry
abstract: Through Sb 3+ doping, DMA 4 InBr 7 : 1%Sb exhibited enhanced absorption, high quantum efficiency, and an excellent temperature-sensitive fluorescence lifetime with a sensitivity of 7.0% K −1 at 340 K, showing potential for fluorescence thermometry.

title: Imidazo[1,5- a ]pyridine–benzilimidazole conjugated (donor–π–acceptor) greenish-yellow fluorophores and their applications in white light-emitting diodes, acidochromism and anticounterfeiting
abstract: This work reports the design, synthesis and versatile applications of three D–π–A push–pull fluorophores (BPy-1, BPy-2, and BPy-FL) featuring flexible phenyl or rigid and sterically hindered diethylfluorene moieties as spacer.

title: Computational screening of MOX 2 transition metal oxydihalides with M = V, Nb, Ta, Mo, Ru, or Os, and X = Cl, Br, or I
abstract: Computational screening of the MOX 2 -phases predict several new compositions. All exhibit binding energies that indicate possibility for delamination into 2D. Some phases undergo structural distortion which drastically affect the electronic structure.

title: Stabilizing perovskite quantum dot oxygen sensors through ultra-long 2 mm horizontally aligned nanopores in anodic alumina oxide templates
abstract: Perovskite quantum dots (PQDs) offer potential for gas sensing, though stability limits use. Johan et al . enhanced PQD stability with a horizontally aligned anodic alumina oxide template, maintaining fluorescence for 3 weeks without change.

title: Performance enhancement of air-stable thieno[2,3- b ]thiophene organic field-effect transistors via alkyl chain engineering
abstract: Organic field-effect transistors were fabricated using thieno[2,3- b ]thiophene derivatives with alkyl side chains. The resulting devices exhibited well-defined surface morphology, superior air stability, and a notable hole mobility of 0.42 cm 2 /V s.

title: Dynamically reconfigurable artificial synapse transistors with organic heterojunctions for multifunctional neuromorphic applications
abstract: Artificial synapses capable of neuromorphic computation are crucial for improving the processing efficiency of existing information technologies.

title: The ESIPT-suppressed 2-(2 ′- hydroxyphenyl)benzoxazole derivative as a new photoinitiator for multiphoton polymerization
abstract: Next-generation organic devices fabrication via multiphoton polymerization demands fine-tuned photoinitiators. HBO-NBu 2 combines remarkable photophysical properties with versatility, proving to be an efficient higher-order multiphoton photoinitiator.

title: Ultralow-energy consumption SnSe/ZnO heterojunction artificial optoelectronic synaptic devices towards ultraviolet optical communication applications
abstract: Oxide-based optoelectronic synapse (OES) devices have attracted much attention for their significant potential applications in developing next-generation artificial intelligence bionic vision systems.

title: Pr-induced dual-site oxide pathway in BiFeO 3 for enhanced alkaline water oxidation
abstract: Pr doping in BiFeO 3 induces lattice distortion, tunes electronic structure and the d-band center, boosting intermediate adsorption and OPM-driven *O–O* coupling. Bi 0.6 Pr 0.4 FeO 3 @NF shows η 10 = 228 mV and stable 100 mA cm −2 for 100 h in 1 M KOH.

title: Fabrication of tetrabenzoporphyrin thin-film transistors with hydrogen-bonding networks via a thermal precursor approach and effect of the amide-group position
abstract: New hydrogen-bonded tetrabenzoporphyrin thin films were fabricated by a thermal precursor approach and exhibited p-type organic field-effect transistor properties.

title: High efficiency, low roll-off and superior color stability fluorescent white organic light-emitting diodes through triplet exciton management with phosphor and TADF sensitization
abstract: The diagram of exciton management and energy transfer processes in the TADF- and phosphor-sensitized EMLs and the EL spectra from 1000 to 10 000 cd m −2 luminance.

title: Molten salt-synthesized Er 3+ /Yb 3+ /Sr 2+ -doped L-Ta 2 O 5 with an intercalated intergrowth structure to boost the pure green upconversion luminescence
abstract: Er 3+ /Yb 3+ /Sr 2+ intercalates in L-Ta 2 O 5 , realizing a superlattice structure. The green UCL integrated intensity of L-Ta 2 O 5 :Er 3+ /Yb 3+ /Sr 2+ was 1.53 and 3.14 times greater than that of β-NaYF 4 :Er 3+ /Yb 3+ and NaY(WO 4 ) 2 :Er 3+ /Yb 3+ , respectively.

title: Simulating thermally activated delayed fluorescence exciton dynamics from first principles
abstract: Delayed fluorescence pathways modelled from first principles reveal intimate knowledge of the kinetics. With dimer effects highlighting new pathways of interest, this toolkit could be used to overcome constraints on TADF materials screening.

title: Laser-induced graphene based on the controllable angle between two irradiation steps for the fabrication of flexible sensor
abstract: Graphene, a carbon material with high carrier mobility and low current density, is a promising candidate for the fabrication of flexible sensors.

title: Stimulus-responsive multifunctions in a zinc( ii ) sulfate complex: photochromism, photoswitching nonlinear optical properties, amine detection and visual film application
abstract: A novel complex combining photo- and amine-induced chromic, switchable photoluminescence, and photomodulated nonlinear optical properties has been prepared using electron-rich sulfate and electron-deficient 2,4,6-tri(4-pyridyl)-1,3,5-triazine.

title: Developing an effective strategy to suppress dark current for high-performance planar photodetectors
abstract: The sequential deposition method can optimize the vertical phase separation of the active layer with reduced the dark current of OPDs, and introducing ultra-thin ferroelectric layer can further improve their performance.

title: A cost-effective and high-sensitivity pressure sensor for wearable electronics using an MXene/Ag NFs-coated cotton fabric
abstract: A pressure sensor was prepared using an MXene/Ag nanoflowers/cotton fabric with superhydrophobicity and it exhibited high sensitivity over a wide pressure range. It has promising potential for applications in flexible wearable devices.

title: Suppressed P2-Z phase transition and Fe migration in the Na layer of an Fe/Mn-based layered oxide cathode for advanced sodium-ion batteries
abstract: The harmful P2-Z phase transition in the Fe/Mn-based layered oxide has been completely eliminated through Ti doping, which reduces the amount of Fe 4+ O 6 and suppresses the migration of Fe ions.

title: Photoelectric memristor based on a PZT/NSTO heterojunction for neuromorphic computing applications
abstract: Inspired by the human brain and visual system, neuromorphic computing based on a photoelectric memristor overcomes the limitations of the traditional von Neumann architecture and has attracted the interest of researchers.

title: Greatly improved efficiency and stability of planar perovskite solar cells via BDADI interfacial modification
abstract: Perovskite solar cells (PSCs) based on the SnO 2 electron transport layer have been widely developed due to their exceptional power conversion efficiency (PCE).

title: Unveiling the sensing ability of new MoS 2 nanoparticles: from fundamental insights into practical applications for nitrites
abstract: The graphitic MoS 2 /C composite material, having nanoribbon morphology, proved to be well-tailored for nitrite sensing applications, with a limit of detection of 0.085 μM.

title: Nickel-regulated hierarchical bimetallic phosphides on an iron foam to promote ampere-level current density in the oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Strategically designing advanced electrocatalysts to accelerate the inherently slow oxygen evolution reaction (OER) remains a crucial yet demanding endeavor in electrocatalysis research.

title: Aerogel for random lasing and sensors with thermal insulation
abstract: This work produced a polymer aerogel with excellent thermal insulation properties that can be applied to random lasing and fiber optic sensing.

title: The balance between paraelectricity and ferroelectricity in non-chiral smectic homologs
abstract: Minimal structural modifications in non-chiral liquid crystals impact ferroelectricity, revealing key structure–property relationships. The findings highlight the potential for developing ferroelectric mixtures with broad application relevance.

title: The Au nanoparticle-induced localized surface plasmon resonance effect and synergistic catalytic sites in Au/Co 3 O 4 /Si pyramid arrays for photoelectrochemical hydrogen evolution
abstract: A Si/Co 3 O 4 /Au photocathode was obtained on pyramidal Si, which exhibited high and stable photoelectrocatalytic performance for hydrogen production. This work presents a promising approach for the rational design of effective Si-based photocathodes.

title: Two-dimensional nanomaterials as lubricant additives: the state-of-the-art and future prospects
abstract: This review explores 2D nanomaterials as lubricant additives, focusing on surface modification, composite methods, and lubrication mechanisms. Key challenges and future research directions are discussed.

title: Ultra-low loadings of gold nanoparticles significantly boost capacitive energy storage of multilayer polymer composites
abstract: The multilayer film with merely 0.0156 vol% gold nanoparticles exhibits a significantly enhanced energy density of 13.816 J cm −3 and a high efficiency of 64.7%.

title: Dual-mode colorimetric/fluorimetric pH-sensing and in situ visualization of latent fingerprints by halofluorochromic nanoprobes prepared from self-assembled multi-responsive star-shaped block-copolymers with anisotropic Janus morphology
abstract: Halofluorochromic nanoparticles with Janus morphology were prepared by self-assembly of star-shaped block copolymers in the presence of oxazolidine derivatives to develop nanoprobes for pH-sensing and in situ visualization of latent fingerprints.

title: Asymmetric contact enabled self-powered flexible photodetector utilizing formamidinium-based perovskite with a 2D MXene electrode
abstract: A highly sensitive flexible photodetector utilizing a formamidinium-cesium (FAC) perovskite thin film, integrated with an asymmetric MXene – silver electrodes featuring different work functions to enhance the charge carrier separation.

title: Electronic, phononic, and superconducting properties of FeH x ( x = 1–6) at 150 GPa
abstract: We report the effects of the hydrogen content on the electronic, mechanical, and superconducting properties of FeH x ( x = 1–6).

title: Spirofluorene adamantane-modified anthracene-based emitters enable efficient deep-blue non-doped OLEDs
abstract: The development of highly efficient and stable deep-blue light-emitting materials remains a significant challenge in the field of organic optoelectronics.

title: Ternary afterglow and dynamic anti-counterfeiting applications of self-activated zinc germanate
abstract: Self-activated zinc germanates show ternary persistent luminescence of blue, green, and near-infrared light, which are linked with native defects. Aliovalent Al 3+ -doped zinc germanates achieve new yellow emission and enhanced afterglow.

title: A twist in the molecular memory function: chemical compositions of different redox couples control the resistive switching bias polarity
abstract: The Ru–polypyridyl-based resistive switching device shows non-volatile, bipolar memory and adaptive learning behavior. Ferrocenium and cobaltocene reverse the switching bias polarity in the redox-active matrices.

title: Enhanced optical anisotropy of six-coordinated silica polymorphs via high-pressure hydrothermal treatment
abstract: Pressure-induced structural phase transition offers a promising alternative to boost the optical anisotropy of birefringent materials.

title: Conjugated polymer dotsstabilized blue phase liquid crystal for improved electro-optic and photoluminescence properties
abstract: The incorporation of polymer dots (2–7 nm) into the blue phase liquid crystal (BPLC) matrix results in a significant extension of the BP stability range, attributed to defect core replacement and an adaptive defect core targeting mechanism.

title: Assessing the influence of nanoscale morphology on the mechanical properties of semiconducting polymers
abstract: This study examines, through the combined application of atomic force microscopy (AFM) and molecular dynamics (MD) simulations, the influence of microstructural organization on the mechanical properties of IDTBT and PBTTT semiconducting polymers.

title: Reconfigurable artificial synapses with an organic antiambipolar transistor for brain-inspired computing
abstract: We demonstrated reconfigurable artificial synapses using a floating-gate-type organic antiambipolar transistor. This feature highlights the potential for a brain-like computing architecture that surpasses current von Neumann systems.

title: Temperature and locomotion dual self-sensing soft robots based on liquid crystal polymer foams
abstract: A liquid crystal polymer foam (LCPF) featuring open cell channels is prepared. Soft robots based on the crosslinked and aligned LCPF loaded with ionic liquids can perform reversible actuation and self-sensing concurrently.

title: Unraveling the influence of substrate surface and temperature on microstructural evolution of crystalline MoS 2 in atomic layer deposition
abstract: In this study, we examine the atomic layer deposition (ALD) growth behavior of crystalline MoS 2 films on various substrates, including SiO 2 , mica, and Al 2 O 3 , at deposition temperatures of ≥650 °C.

title: Metal organic frameworks as molecular encapsulants to prevent efficiency losses in perovskite solar cells
abstract: Polymer–MOF encapsulant enhances the lifespan of perovskite solar cells by preventing moisture permeation through tortuous pathways.

title: Research on nonlinear optical limiting of two-dimensional materials suspensions
abstract: The selection of appropriate solutes and solvents for optimizing the nonlinear optical limiting (NOL) performance of suspensions poses significant challenges in nonlinear optics research.

title: In situ growth of Cu-doped MoS 2 thin films via a laser-induced technique: efficient P-type doping and effective enhancement of the FET device performance
abstract: A novel in situ doping technique was proposed, wherein copper (Cu) was successfully doped into MoS 2 thin films as a p-type doping acceptor using pulsed laser-induced technology. The growth and doping processes were simultaneously completed.

title: Solid-state emissions of 4 H -chromenones with simple structures: mechanochromism and polymer-based doped ultralong room-temperature phosphorescence
abstract: A series of 4 H -chromenones using triphenylamine and 9-phenyl-9 H -carbazole as electron-donating units exhibited mechanochromic properties based on different crystal stacking modes and ultralong room-temperature phosphorescence.

title: Oxygen vacancies and CDs synergistically mediated CD/TiO 2 composite materials: investigation of electronic energy band structure modulation, photocatalytic performances, and mechanisms
abstract: The photocatalytic degradation mechanism of visible light-driven carbon dot/defect titanium dioxide (CDT) nanocomposites.

title: 1,2,4-Tris(5-aryl-1,3,4-oxadiazolyl)-benzenes: novel λ-shaped fluorescent liquid crystals
abstract: Novel electron deficient and fluorescent mesogens with intermediate shape between calamitic and discotic liquid crystals are reported.

title: Thermo-mechanical control of second harmonic generation in a ferroelectric plastic crystal
abstract: A ferroelectric plastic crystal shows four SHG states via thermo-mechanical control. Hot-pressing yields dense tablets with 3.4-fold SHG enhancement, enabling programmable optical switching.

title: Congested position isomerism enhanced mechanoluminescence of triarylboranes
abstract: Molecules undergo stress changes to alter their molecular conformation under mechanical force, effectively achieving reversible MLE luminescence.

title: Investigating perovskite nanocrystal stability though polymer encapsulation: a nano-array method
abstract: In this study, the effects of polymer coatings on the photostability of CsPbBr 3 nanocrystals were systematically investigated using a high-throughput nanoarray platform, with PS-coated samples demonstrating optimal photostability under illumination.

title: Revealing the surface oxidation mechanism and performance evolution of Nd–Fe–B sintered magnets
abstract: Via wide-range oxidation experiments, a new strategy of constructing tunable surface oxidation layer is proposed, generating an excellent synergy of improved corrosion resistance and mechanical performance without compromising magnetic properties.

title: Two-dimensional multiferroicity and half-metallicity of the carrier-doped 2H-VSiN 3 monolayer
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) multiferroicity is realized by carrier doping in 2H-VSiN 3 monolayer.

title: Facile hydrothermal synthesis of α-MnO 2 nanorods for low-cost, scalable and stable photoresponsive devices
abstract: Hydrothermally synthesized α-MnO 2 nanorods for low cost, scalable photoresponsive device.

title: Optimizing the short-wavelength infrared photoluminescence quantum yield and brightness of Er 3+ , Yb 3+ co-doped yttrium orthophosphate phosphors for tracer-based sorting
abstract: Development of efficient inorganic luminescent tracers is essential for various applications, including plastic sorting.

title: High-brightness electroluminescent white light-emitting diodes based on solution-processable, yellow-emissive carbon quantum dots
abstract: Yellow-emissive CQDs were synthesized through a solvothermal method using cyanuric chloride and 1-pyrenamine as precursors. The CQDs exhibited color-stable white electroluminescence with a maximum luminance of up to 24 092 cd m −2 .

title: Light- and radical-induced modification of magnetic and magnetocaloric effects in viologen-based lanthanide materials
abstract: Lanthanide coordination polymers (1-Dy, 1-Gd) with 4,4′-bipyridine ligands show tunable magnetic relaxation and magnetocaloric effects driven by light and photogenerated radicals, enhancing SMM in 1-Dy and reducing MCE in 1-Gd.

title: A graphene-sandwiched Al/Si/InAs nonreciprocal emitter under transverse electric polarization adjusted by azimuth angle
abstract: The graphene-sandwiched Al/Si/InAs smart structure is studied as a nonreciprocal thermal radiation emitter, which can be adjusted by changing the azimuth angle.

title: A novel high-efficiency near-infrared phosphor Mg 0.9 Ta 2 Zn 2.1 O 8 :Cr 3+ ,Yb 3+ for spectroscopy applications
abstract: Introducing Yb 3+ into Mg 0.9 Ta 2 Zn 2.1 O 8 :Cr 3+ adjusts the FWHM, increases EQE, and enhances luminous intensity and thermal stability. A NIR pc-LED using a 465 nm chip and MTZO:Cr 3+ ,Yb 3+ phosphor is applied in multifunctional optical applications.

title: Bimetal-decorated resistive gas sensors: a review
abstract: Various noble metals such as Au, Ag, Pd, Pt, Ru, and Rh are used for decorating resistive gas sensors because of their high catalytic activity and electronic effects, which boost the overall sensing characteristics of resistive sensors.

title: Molecular magnetism and solid-phase transformations of Dy, Er, and Yb tropolonate complexes
abstract: New tropolonate Ln( iii ) complexes were synthesized and shown to exhibit SMM properties, including zero-field slow magnetic relaxation in Dy( iii ) complexes and Orbach-type relaxation in Er( iii ) complexes.
