title: Highly efficient solar-thermal thermoresponsive hydrogel based on a fullerene derivative for water purification and energy harvesting
abstract: A strategy is designed to prepare the solar-thermal thermoresponsive evaporator based on solution-processable fullerene as a solar-thermal material and PNIPAm, polyvinyl alcohol double-network hydrogel.

title: Enhancing the stability of Cs 3 Bi 2 Br 9 quantum dots for anti-counterfeiting applications in leather through polyacrylate coating
abstract: Utilizing polyacrylate to improve the stability of perovskite quantum dots for application in leather anti-counterfeiting.

title: Enhanced ultra-broadband electromagnetic wave absorption using liquid metal-coated carbonyl Fe/Ni microspheres with dual dielectric polarization
abstract: The dual heterogeneous interfaces between Fe/Ni particles and the LM coating effectively control polarization losses and balance impedance matching, leading to the development of ultra-broadband, high-performance Fe/Ni@LM composites.

title: Flexible piezoelectric pressure sensors utilizing a low-dimensional perovskite–PVDF composite
abstract: A flexible quasi-2D perovskite–PVDF pressure sensor with a tunable PDMS layer shows high piezoelectric response, sensitivity, and stability. Integrated into soft robotic grippers, it enables real-time force sensing across diverse loads and shapes.

title: Magnetostructural coupling, Kondo-like behavior, and magnetocaloric performance in Fe-doped Nd 0.5 (Sr 0.4 Ba 0.1 )CoO 3 perovskites
abstract: Fe-doped Nd(Sr 0.4 Ba 0.1 )CoO 3 perovskites exhibit magnetostructural coupling, with Fe content modulating thermal expansion, magnetic transitions, and electronic transport. These findings enable a comprehensive magnetic phase diagram for this system.

title: Top-down micro and nano structuring of wide bandgap semiconductors for ultraviolet photodetection
abstract: This review focuses on top-down dry and wet etching of III-nitrides, SiC, Ga 2 O 3 , and diamond to create micro/nanostructured UV photodetectors, enabling enhanced light trapping and improved device performance.

title: Reversible photomechanical actuators with liquid crystal polymer graphene oxide nanocomposites
abstract: This study quantifies the photomechanical actuation in LC242 nanocomposites doped with azo dye and graphene oxide. Optimized doping and UV curing achieve a 35° photo-induced bend with rapid response, advancing applications in smart materials.

title: Achieving high-stability aqueous room-temperature phosphorescent materials via in situ host–guest strategy
abstract: We adopted an in situ host–guest strategy to prepare nitrogen–sulfur co-doped carbon dots (CDs) and CA host–guest composite material NS-CDs 20 @CA via a one-step hydrothermal method, and applied it in multi-information encryption.

title: Advances in three-dimensional porous carbon-based wave-absorbing materials: from preparation strategies, structural design, and component regulation to multifunctionality
abstract: The review describes the wave-absorption principles, preparation strategies, structural design, component modulation and multifunctionality of 3D porous carbon-based materials as well as discusses their challenges and future research directions.

title: SERS tags based on polymer microspheres decorated by gold nanoparticles: layer-by-layer deposition vs. aggregation from quasi-stable colloidal solution
abstract: Surface-enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) tags based on polymer microspheres (PMS) and assembled meta-stable gold nanoparticles show significant promise for applications in liquid environments, particularly in the study of biological systems.

title: Reactive and decomposable dispersant for maximizing the properties of graphene composites
abstract: A dual-functional dispersant is developed to make the most of graphene in practical applications. A graphene composite with enhanced conductivity, mechanical strength, and solvent resistance is achieved relying on the dispersant.

title: Resonator arrays with non-uniform unit cell design for surface-enhanced infrared absorption spectroscopy
abstract: Higher SEIRA detection sensitivity with non-uniform cross nanorods compared with uniform ones.

title: Mesoporous silica-coated CsPbBr 3 :ZnBr 2 :NaBr nanocrystals with high photoluminescence and tunable wavelengths for micro light-emitting diodes
abstract: Integrating micro-LEDs (μ-LEDs) with color-conversion phosphors is pivotal for advanced displays/lighting, demanding green emitters with 525–535 nm wavelength, narrow FWHM (<25 nm), nanoscale size, high stability, and superior PLQY.

title: An advanced terahertz gallium nitride metasensor for enhanced molecular absorption spectrum analysis of analytes
abstract: This paper presents a patterned-GaN metasensor, which is used for the molecular absorption spectrum analysis of trace analytes.

title: Controlled self-assembled FeNi alloy/graphene foam composite for lightweight and broadband microwave absorption
abstract: FeNi alloy/graphene foam (FNGF) is synthesized via one-step hydrothermal method. At 5 wt% loading, sample 1# covers Ku-band (11.76–18 GHz, 2.09 mm); sample 3# achieves −63.76 dB at 15.68 GHz.

title: An ultrathin, transparent, flexible, and self-powered photodetector based on two-dimensional materials and a self-assembled polar-monolayer
abstract: An ultrathin, flexible, self-powered, and transparent photodetector made from all two-dimensional (2D) materials with a polar monolayer is proposed and demonstrated.

title: Maximizing sunlight absorption in narrow bandgap semiconducting copper( i ) iodides for enhanced photocatalytic dye degradation
abstract: Utilizing their photocatalytic activity and strong sunlight absorption capability, narrow band gap CuI-based hybrid semiconductors are highly efficient in breaking down dyes and pigments into safer, simpler molecules.

title: A novel broadband near-infrared phosphor Na 3 Mg 4 LiSi 12 O 30 :Cr 3+ : moderate synthesis and application
abstract: A novel broadband NIR phosphor Na 3 Mg 4 LiSi 12 O 30 :Cr 3+ is used to fabricate a NIR pc-LED, which shows application potential in tissue penetration, night vision, and alcohol detection.

title: A computational and Raman spectroscopic study of successive phase transitions in Co 3 TeO 6 under high pressure and high temperature
abstract: A R 3 to P 2 1 / n transition is predicted for Co 3 TeO 6 under 16 GPa (FM) or 19 GPa (AFM), while ex situ XRD data show phase coexistence of R 3 (72%) and P 2 1 / n (28%), which indicates a high energy barrier in the kinetic process of the phase transition.

title: A bifunctional tunable terahertz absorber based on a Fabry–Perot cavity: enabling broadband perfect absorption and refractive index sensing
abstract: A THz metamaterial absorber can realize broadband perfect absorption ( A > 99%, B = 3.14 THz) and RI sensing.

title: A benzothiadiazole-decorated UiO-68 metal–organic framework for diclofenac and ibuprofen luminescence sensing and adsorption in wastewater
abstract: The Zr IV metal–organic framework Zr_BTDZ is exploited as a luminescent sensor and adsorbent of the pharmaceuticals diclofenac sodium (DCF) and ibuprofen sodium (IBR) in aqueous solutions, showing a preferential interaction with the latter.

title: Voltage-control of the in-plane magnetic anisotropy in hybrid magnetoelectric Ni 90 Fe 10 /BaTiO 3 (011) heterostructures
abstract: This study focuses on hybrid magnetoelectric Ni 90 Fe 10 /BaTiO 3 (011) heterostructures, which enable the control of the in-plane magnetization of the magnetostrictive layer through electric voltage.

title: Low-temperature solution-processed growth of ternary (Bi 1− x Sb x ) 2 S 3 films
abstract: A low-temperature solvothermal method enables phase-pure (Bi 1− x Sb x ) 2 S 3 films with tunable composition and bandgap, offering a scalable route to engineered ternary chalcogenides for electronic and optoelectronic applications.

title: Enhancing near-infrared photothermal activity through precise in-plane interface engineering in CuS–Au and CuS@Au ultrathin nanoplates for combating multidrug-resistant bacteria
abstract: Ultrathin two-dimensional hybrid nanostructures are crafted with well-designed copper sulfide–gold interfaces, demonstrating exceptional near-infrared photothermal properties to kill multidrug-resistant bacteria.

title: Exploring p-type transparent conductive materials in conventional binary compounds beyond the equilibrium doping limit
abstract: A flowchart for searching optimal p-type transparent conductive materials among conventional binary compounds.

title: Ultra-high brightness near-infrared perovskite light-emitting diodes enabled by aluminum-doped zinc oxide electron transport layers
abstract: The integration of AZO as an electron transport layer enhances NIR PeLED brightness to 3313 W sr −1 m −2 , a 146% improvement over ZnO-based devices, due to improved charge injection, reduced resistance, and better carrier transport dynamics.

title: Photo-thermally controlled Cu nanoparticles density in SWCNT/Cu nanocomposites-based flexible EMI shielding electrodes
abstract: In this study, single-walled carbon nanotube (SWCNT)/Cu nanocomposites were systematically synthesized from oxidized SWCNTs and Cu formate via photothermal heating.

title: Phase transition-driven modulation of ferroelectricity and the photovoltaic effect in sol–gel-derived BiFeO 3 -based films
abstract: Ferroelectric polarization and photovoltaic effect: test model and experimental results. ( P max = 137.1 μC cm −2 , P r = 135.8 μC cm −2 , J SC = −0.12 mA cm −2 , and V OC = 63.9 mV).

title: Lowering the bandgap of poly(diketopyrrolopyrrole- alt -terchalcogenophene)s via chalcogen effects for shortwave infrared phototransistors
abstract: Modulated by chalcogen effect, polymer SeDPP-Te exhibited high hole mobility and shortwave infrared phototransistor performance, with photoresponsivity, photosensitivity and specific detectivity up to 34.0 A W −1 , 27.6 and 2.76 × 10 11 Jones.

title: Simultaneous electrodeposition of manganese oxide/poly( o -aminophenol) composites as electrode materials for aqueous electrochemical energy storage
abstract: In this study, MnO x /PoAP composite films were prepared by simultaneous electrodeposition from an aqueous sulfuric acid electrolyte containing MnSO 4 and o -aminophenol for aqueous energy storage devices.

title: Very high frequency (∼100 MHz) plasma enhanced atomic layer deposition high- κ hafnium zirconium oxide capacitors near morphotropic phase boundary with low current density & high- κ for DRAM technology
abstract: This study uses VHF (100 MHz) PE-ALD to make 4.5 nm HZO films with a record dielectric constant of 64.47 (up to 69.9 at 389 K), showing great potential for high-performance DRAM.

title: 3D sea urchin like NiCo 2 O 4 and NiCo 2 O 4 /MWCNT composites as multifunctional microwave absorbers enabled by interfacial engineering
abstract: The increasing demand for lightweight and high-efficiency microwave absorbing materials is driven by advancements in wireless communication and radar technologies.

title: An adaptable porphyrin-based pH-responsive ratiometric fluorescent nanoprobe for rapid and visual acidity detection
abstract: Water-soluble porphyrin derivative nanoparticles (WSPD NPs) were successfully utilized as an adaptable ratiometric fluorescent nanoprobe for pH detection in beverage samples, pH sensing test strips, living cells and battery electrolytes.

title: A hybrid immunosensor based on gold nanodendrimer-decorated MoS 2 nanoflowers for label-free detection of alpha-fetoprotein
abstract: A hybrid electrochemical immunosensor based on gold nanodendrimer-decorated MoS 2 nanoflowers was developed for capturing and quantifying α-fetoprotein (AFP) in the human serum matrix.

title: Asymmetric light transmission via Sb 2 S 3 -enhanced nonreciprocal gratings on a glass substrate
abstract: A schematic of the proposed photonic structure and the transmittance spectra of the grating in amorphous and crystalline phases of Sb 2 S 3 , which reveals strong nonreciprocal transmission for both TE and TM polarizations.

title: Odd–even effect on room-temperature phosphorescence for benzene-carboxylic derivatives
abstract: A series of benzene-carboxylic derivative (BCD)-acrylamide copolymers exhibited blue afterglow. Besides, an interesting odd–even effect was observed. Applications in information encryption and anti-counterfeiting ink have been realized.

title: Effect of Fe 2 O 3 additive on structural and piezoelectric properties of (Na,K)(Nb,Sb)O 3 –CaZrO 3 –(Bi,Ag)ZrO 3 lead-free piezoceramics
abstract: Fe 2 O 3 additive changed the crystal structure of NKNS–CZ–BAZ ceramic from the O–T to the R–O–T structure and thus this piezoceramic has excellent piezoelectric properties ( d 33 = 680 pC N −1 and k p = 0.47).

title: Magnet-free nonreciprocal thermal emitters via VO 2 –Weyl semimetal double-sided grating metasurfaces
abstract: Magnet-free VO 2 –Weyl-semimetal heterostructure with double-sided Ge gratings enables programmable nonreciprocal thermal emission, with angle-resolved spectra showing co-located high- Q peaks and strong directional contrast.

title: Strain and U driven phase transitions in monolayer intrinsic ferrovalley NbIn 2 As 2 Se 2
abstract: Strain and U value driven topological phase transitions in monolayer NbIn 2 As 2 Se 2 .

title: Novel layered As 2 Ge with a pentagonal structure for potential thermoelectrics
abstract: We predicted the high thermoelectric properties of As 2 Ge monolayers, and the maximum ZT of n-type doping is 4.36.

title: A stimulus-triggered luminescent hydrogel with Al 3+ for visible color tunability in advanced information encryption applications
abstract: A high-strength hydrogel with metal ion-triggered responsiveness, multi-color tunability, and self-healing properties is designed. The unique optical function could promote the development of low-cost and easy-to-process intelligent materials.

title: AgGaS 2 /Ga 2 S 3 quantum dots exhibiting efficient narrow blue emission
abstract: A synthesis method for preparing Ga 2 S 3 capped AgGaS 2 quantum dots exhibiting narrow blue emission is provided along with the in-depth study of their optical properties including time-resolved and temperature-dependent photoluminescence spectroscopy.

title: Hydrogen-bonded MXene ohmic contacts: overcoming Schottky and tunneling barriers for quantum-limit 2D MoS 2 electronics
abstract: Hydrogen-bonded MXene/MoS 2 heterojunctions enable ohmic contacts by reducing Schottky barriers and tunneling impedance through dipole effects.

title: Changes in near-bandgap photoluminescence in lead halide CsPbBr 3 perovskite subjected to extreme conditions
abstract: The effect of cyclic high-pressure treatment on the near-bandgap photoluminescence in a CsPbBr 3 single crystal is experimentally demonstrated.

title: Tunable optical and scintillation properties of two-dimensional tin mixed-halide perovskites
abstract: The controllable doping of hybrid organic–inorganic perovskites fueled strong interest as scintillating materials.

title: Reducing MoS 2 FET contact resistance by stepped annealing to optimize device performance
abstract: A well-established stepped annealing strategy was applied to MoS 2 FET optimization to improve interfacial contact and electrical performance.

title: Effect of Al 2 O 3 passivation layer and optimization of atomic-layer-deposited Zn–Sn–O and Al–Zn–Sn–O thin-film transistors
abstract: This study investigated the effects of aluminum oxide (Al 2 O 3 ) passivation layers on amorphous zinc tin oxide (a-ZTO) and amorphous aluminum zinc tin oxide (a-AZTO) thin films, along with their corresponding thin-film transistor (TFT) performances.

title: Designing efficient ligand exchange strategies for the synthesis of stable perovskite nanocrystals
abstract: Stable PeNCs are synthesized by promoting ligand exchange. High-performance devices are then fabricated based on these PeNCs.

title: Improved electrocaloric effect and working temperature span in BaTiO 3 -based ferroelectric ceramics via texture engineering
abstract: A high Δ T of 1.48 K with a wide T span of 65 °C under a low electric field of 50 kV cm −1 in BaTiO 3 -based textured ceramics was achieved, owing to the polarization rotation and increasing relaxation behavior.

title: Aspect ratio design and heterostructure construction of MoO 2 /NC nanorods for broadband microwave absorption
abstract: The 1D MoO 2 /N-doped carbon heterogeneous nanorods with different aspect ratios achieve tunable and broadband microwave attenuation capacity within low filler loadings.

title: Highly sensitive and selective triethylamine sensor enabled by Ag/ZnO heterojunctions
abstract: We have prepared an Ag nanoparticle-loaded 3D ZnO nanoflower composite and demonstrated the high triethylamine gas sensing performance of the synthesised Ag/ZnO composite.

title: A multifunctional manganese coordination polymer with polar solvent-responsive tunable dielectric properties and selective catalysis
abstract: A manganese coordination polymer offers solvent-controlled dielectric switching and excellent catalytic activity for selective oxidation of methylarenes, highlighting its potential in electronics and sustainable chemical transformations.

title: Hydrazine acetate promoted the formation of equiaxed perovskite grains for efficient solar cells
abstract: Hydrazine acetate is employed as an additive to prepare perovskite films via a simple one-step spin-coating method, which induces a transformation of the film microstructure from a rough dendritic morphology to smooth equiaxed crystals.

title: The critical role of phase transition and composition regulation in inorganic perovskite electrocaloric materials
abstract: This article reviews the critical role of phase transition types in the electrocaloric effect and how compositional regulation can achieve an electrocaloric effect with a high peak value over a wide temperature span.

title: Circularly polarized luminescence signals of photoresponsive liquid crystals and their applications
abstract: This work summarizes light-controlled CPL induction, enabling precise regulation via light wavelength and dopants. It highlights light-driven modulation of liquid crystal helices, mechanisms, and applications.

title: A thiophene end-capped centrally extended acceptor enables organic solar cells with efficiency over 19.3%
abstract: A new non-fullerene acceptor, CH-TCl, introduced as the third component, optimizes the active layer morphology, enhances charge transfer, and increases the open-circuit voltage of organic solar cells.

title: Harnessing the reversible isomerization of spiropyran to merocyanine in conjugated polymers for broadband ultra-violet to near-infrared electrochromic switching
abstract: The reversible isomerization of spiropyran to merocyanine is employed as a new strategy for achieving broadband electrochromic switching.

title: Effects of pyrolysis temperature on plant-seed-derived carbon dots
abstract: Carbon dots (CDs) derived from natural precursors were synthesized via pyrolysis, a facile and scalable method. The influence of pyrolysis temperature on their structural and optical characteristics was systematically investigated.

title: Scrutinizing the sharp magnetoelastic transition and kinetic arrest in Fe 49 Rh 51 alloy using neutron thermo-diffraction
abstract: The Fe 49 Rh 51 bulk alloy exhibits a sharp FOPT from the AFM to FM state around 332 K, with a volume change of 0.8%. A kinetic arrest of the AFM phase is evidenced up to 70 K above the FOPT temperature by neutron diffraction experiments.

title: A multifunctional additive for enhancing the performance of single-junction perovskite and perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells
abstract: J – V curves of the single-junction perovskite solar cells. J – V curve of the perovskite/crystalline silicon tandem solar cells with champion PCE 28.47% (embedded graph: chemical structure of perovskite after adding BD-Boc additives).

title: Unveiling a pyridine-based exciplex host for efficient stable blue phosphorescent organic light-emitting diodes
abstract: Using QC/MD simulation, a stable blue host with a high T1 level was developed. These hosts enhance the efficiency and stability of phosphorescent organic light-emitting diodes (PhOLEDs) due to improved triplet energy and charge balance.

title: Comprehensive survey of plasmonic nano-dendrites: from fabrication to surface-enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) applications
abstract: Fabrication methods and SERS applications of plasmonic dendrites.

title: Impact of donor modification on azaisoindigo-based polymers for n- and p-type organic electrochemical transistors
abstract: The electron-poor azaisoindigo motif, with amphiphilic side chains to facilitate ion interactions, is copolymerised with bithiophenes of varying electron-donating ability. The comonomer choice determines the dominant charge carrier species in OECTs.

title: Ex situ synergistic reinforcement of a MOF-based supramolecular polymer enables tough, highly flexible, and responsive artificial epidermis-inspired hydrogels
abstract: A flexible and tough, poly(lauryl methacrylate-acrylamide)@cobalt-manganese metal organic framework supramolecular composite hydrogel was developed for the detection of various subtle and large human motions successfully.

title: Unlocking the bifunctional potential of the NPB:OXD-7 exciplex in organic light-emitting diodes and UV-photodetectors
abstract: Demonstrating the potential of exciplex-based devices for both OLED and UV photodetector applications by leveraging the concept of spontaneous orientational polarization.

title: Blue emission in a fluorinated 2D (C 8 H 11 FN) 2 PbBr 4 perovskite with 11.17% photoluminescence quantum yield
abstract: This study reports a new 2D lead bromide perovskite (C 8 H 11 FN) 2 PbBr 4 featuring halogenated organic cations. It exhibits deep blue narrow emission with a photoluminescence quantum yield of 11.17%, highlighting its potential in light-emitting devices.

title: Redox-switchable gelation of unmodified cellulose nanocrystals
abstract: A redox-switchable cellulose nanocrystal (CNC) gel is achieved via ferrocene/ferrocenium redox cycling. Ferrocenium cations screen electrostatic repulsion between CNCs, enabling reversible modulation of gel strength for responsive soft materials.

title: Bismuth-doped soda alumino germanate glass part I: examination of the role of Na/Al atomic ratio on the glass matrix and bismuth luminescence properties in the 300–900 nm spectral range
abstract: Structural variation imparted by changes in NBO levels significantly impacted emission features of both undoped and Bi doped matrices where emission features could be directly ascribed to defects within glasses and the changing Bi valence states.

title: Plasma-enhanced ALD growth of ε-(Al x Ga 1− x ) 2 O 3 alloy films with high Al content and tunable band offsets
abstract: The low-temperature PEALD approach results in high-Al-content ε-(Al x Ga 1− x ) 2 O 3 films with high crystallinity, a tunable bandgap, and large conduction band offsets, suitable for polarization-engineered electronics.

title: Design of moisturizing and frost-resistant ionic hydrogels for multimodal sensing through water-stabilizing effects
abstract: Design of a versatile moisturizing and frost-resistant ionic (MFI) hydrogel with environmentally adaptable sensory responses on strain, temperature, and humidity by leveraging the water-stabilizing effects of hydrated ions.

title: Lead-free perovskite Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 epitaxial thin films for high-performance and air-stable photodetectors
abstract: High-efficiency and air-stable photodetectors based on lead-free perovskite Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 epitaxial thin films were prepared by pulsed laser deposition.

title: Side-chain engineering of bifunctional linkage unit-linked giant molecular acceptors for organic solar cells
abstract: The photovoltaic performance of bifunctional linkage GMA systems is significantly boosted. Systematic investigation of side-chain engineering effects on morphology, charge carrier dynamics in bifunctional linkage GMAs is performed.

title: Combining machine-learning models with first-principles high-throughput calculations to accelerate the search for promising thermoelectric materials
abstract: From high-throughput screening of high-power-factor materials, through first-principles calculation of transport properties, to training machine-learning models for identifying good thermoelectric materials.

title: Multi-mode tunable luminescence in Bi 3+ -activated oxyfluoride phosphors for multi-level anti-counterfeiting applications
abstract: A series of color-tunable Sr 3 Ga 1− x Ge x O 4+ x F 1− x :1%Bi 3+ oxyfluoride phosphors have been designed by chemical unit co-substitution for applying in the multi-level anti-counterfeiting field.

title: Intricate carrier dynamics of bismuth halide perovskites: localized excitons and polarons
abstract: The interaction between carriers and photons in halide perovskites gives rise to intriguing phenomena in their excited states.

title: Unique BiCuOSe/Bi 2 O 2 Se van der Waals monolayer with ultra-high electron mobility
abstract: The electron mobility of the BiCuOSe/Bi 2 O 2 Se monolayer is remarkably higher than that of bulk Bi 2 O 2 Se and the Bi 2 O 2 Se monolayer at 300 K.

title: Moderate-temperature solution-processed synthesis of incommensurate Sr 8/7 TiS 3 thin films and rod-shaped nanocrystals
abstract: This work reports the synthesis and characterization of solution-processed thin films and nanorods of the emerging semiconductor hexagonal quasi-1D Sr 8/7 TiS 3 at temperatures below 600 °C and 400 °C, respectively.

title: Organic piezosensitive charge-transfer complexes with crystal-to-crystal phase transition for flexible energy harvesting
abstract: The organic charge-transfer complex DBCz/TCAF is a good organic piezosensitive material. A complex-doped energy harvester can enhance electron energy and generate efficient electrical signals under phase transition-induced triboelectric effect.

title: Reversible luminescence of Bi 3+ ions by X-ray induced crystal field regulation
abstract: In this study, X-ray irradiation was used to repeatedly regulate the crystal field of Cs 2 KGdF 6 , and the regulation of Bi 3+ luminescence was successfully realized.

title: Through-space charge transfer in a stable 1,2,4-triazolyl radical enabled by spiro D–σ–A architecture for prolonged excited-state lifetimes
abstract: This work reported a novel 1,2,4-triazolyl radical exhibiting through-space charge-transfer (TSCT) characteristics. It offers enhanced control of exciton dynamics and as a novel design strategy for stable organic radical optoelectronic materials.

title: Hole transportation and defect passivation properties at the perovskite/SAHTM interface: the effect of heteroatom groups and alkyl chain lengths in self-assembled phosphonic acid carbazole derivatives
abstract: Modifications of various heteroatom groups were introduced theoretically in self-assembled hole transporting materials (SAHTMs). Enhanced hole transportation and reduced defects at the perovskite/SAHTM interface were anticipated.

title: Dual passivation effect of a C 60 -capped and Zn 2+ -doped CsPbCl 3 perovskite photodetector
abstract: A dual-passivation effect from both the ZnCl 2 dopant and C 60 capping layer and strong dependence of exciton binding energy ( E B ) on the thickness of C 60 and concentration of Zn 2+ dopant were demonstrated in CsPbCl 3 films.

title: Thickness-dependent hydrogen evolution reaction activity on Pd films: an insightful view from magnetism
abstract: The ferromagnetism of specific Pd films leads to enhanced magnetic HER activity.

title: Cephalopod chromatophores contain photosensitizing nanostructures that may facilitate light sensing and signaling in the skin
abstract: The photoexcited charge transfer properties of cephalopod chromatophore granules are examined within a photovoltaic cell. Photoconversion efficiency up to 0.81 ± 0.14% is recorded, highlighting a new function for these unique biomaterials.

title: Investigation of thermodynamic stability and catalytic activity on Pt single atoms and small Pt clusters anchoring on MXene supports for the hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Catalysts with excellent hydrogen evolution reaction performance were designed by anchoring Pt clusters of different sizes on MXene supports.

title: Iron tetraphenylporphyrin chloride–metal substrate interaction mediated by a graphene buffer layer
abstract: We investigate the interfacial electronic structure of monolayer iron tetraphenylporphyrin chloride (FeTPP–Cl) adsorbed on graphene (Gr) buffer layers supported by Ni(111) and Pt(111).

title: Linear and non-linear optical properties of FeSnO(OH) 5 oxyhydroxide perovskite
abstract: This work investigates the optical parameters of jeanbandyite to assess its potential applications for optoelectronic devices.

title: Machine learning and first-principles calculations for the prediction and analysis of superconductivity in Mg–B–N systems
abstract: This article explored potential superconducting materials in the Mg–B–N system using machine learning and first-principles calculations. Four stable layered superconductors were discovered at atmospheric pressure.

title: Unravelling the UV luminescence of Bi-doped LiYGeO 4 : a journey from first principles to temperature-dependent photoluminescence
abstract: The study on Bi-doped LiYGeO 4 PersL reveals a ∼6.05 eV direct bandgap, supported by hybrid DFT calculations. Li loss during synthesis was confirmed by elemental analysis. Excitation to the MMCT enables UV PersL (peaked @ 358 nm) for up to 7 hours.

title: Vertical Nb 2 O 5 micro-petal array photoelectrochemical deep-UV photodetectors for underwater weak-light photodetection
abstract: Nb 2 O 5 MPA-based PEC PDs exhibit an R of 146.44 mA W −1 under weak 254 nm light, an ultrahigh rejection ratio of 22 306, and good UV photoresponse in acidic and alkaline electrolytes and simulated seawater at 5, 25 and 45 °C.

title: Fabrication of CuInS 2 /ZnS quantum dot nanocomposite films and investigation of their influence on performance of soda glass-based luminescent solar concentrators
abstract: The concentration and thickness of the nanocomposite film with CuInS 2 /ZnS quantum dots were optimized. Furthermore, the influence of the optical absorption of the light guide plate on the luminescent solar concentrator was also investigated.

title: Selective heteroepitaxial synthesis of 2D bismuth-based mixed-anion compounds with interfacial spin–orbit interaction via atmospheric solution routes
abstract: Bi 2 OS 2 and Bi 3 O 2 S 2 Cl epitaxial thin films with strong spin–orbit interaction were selectively synthesized by mist chemical vapor deposition.

title: Theoretical design of dielectric enhancement in Tm-doped LaCOB crystals
abstract: We demonstrate that Tm doping in LaCOB enhances dielectric response through static dipole amplification and dynamic phonon symmetry breaking, transforming symmetric stretching into low-frequency asymmetric rotational modes.

title: A comprehensive study on low-temperature sintering and microwave/terahertz dielectric properties of BaO–P 2 O 5 binary ceramics
abstract: In this study, the sintering characteristics, microstructure, phase composition, crystal structure, dielectric properties, and thermal properties of ceramic samples in the BaO–P 2 O 5 binary system were comprehensively investigated.

title: Mimicking excitatory and inhibitory synaptic behaviors with optical-absorption and electrical-switch heterostructures
abstract: Optoelectronic neuromorphic devices based on SnO 2 /CsPbBr 3 QD heterostructures achieved optical potentiation with the bias-free mode and electrical inhibition synaptic behaviors.

title: Ubiquitous shallow trap states and lattice hydrogenation of ZnO particles
abstract: H uptake by ZnO nanocrystals is often overlooked despite its impact on materials electronic properties. For ZnO particles from gas phase synthesis we found that oxygen site hydrogenation (OH0O) explains the paramagnetic resonance at g = 1.96.

title: Colossal dielectric permittivity and superparaelectricity in phenyl pyrimidine based liquid crystals
abstract: Synthesized organic fluids with large longitudinal dipole moments are shown to demonstrate superparaelectricity.

title: Hybrid nano-microstructured and bioinspired conductive hydrogels with tunable multifunctionality
abstract: A conductive hydrogel with enhanced mechanical properties and excellent multifunctionality is fabricated by facile direct in situ polymerization. This hydrogel can serve as a wearable strain sensor precisely detecting relative resistance changes.

title: A study on device physics of deep ultraviolet light emitting diodes leveraging machine learning
abstract: An effective approach to optimizing the luminous performance of AlGaN-based DUV LEDs is demonstrated using machine learning. A CNN model predicts LOPD with high accuracy, revealing key features that influence LED performance.

title: Scalable and low-cost fabrication of highly oriented ultra-thin GA pressed films for 100% EMI shielding protection
abstract: Ultrathin, flexible graphene aggregate-pressed films with high orientation are fabricated, which exhibit an outstanding EMI shielding performance of up to 102 dB.

title: Achieving high-efficiency blue organic light-emitting diodes via pyridyl-unit incorporated thermally activated delayed fluorescence materials
abstract: A blue TADF emitter was designed by introducing a pyridyl group into a donor unit. This modification decreases the electron-donating ability and forms intramolecular C–H⋯N hydrogen bonds, leading to a hypsochromic shift.

title: Wide-bandgap quantum dots with large-span fluorescence switching and two-photon emission via protonation/deprotonation
abstract: Wide band-gap quantum dots exhibit long-span one-photon emission switching and the first reported two-photon emission via protonation and deprotonation.

title: Improved electrochemical performance of graphene oxide via copper ion cross-linking and plasma functionalization
abstract: Functionalizing graphene oxide via plasma and developing a nearly three-dimensional structure by Cu 2+ cross-linking synergistically enhance its electrochemical performance.

title: Enhancing ion doping and charge transport in polymer electrochemical transistors through poly(3-hexylthiophene) nanowire integration
abstract: Highly ordered nanowires enhance the ion doping efficiency in P3HT PECT devices, improving performance. Ions preferentially dope the J-aggregation region, namely the P3HT nanowires, in the resulting films.

title: High conductivity poly(ionic liquid)-based crosslinked dielectrics
abstract: A wide range of emerging applications, from organic electronics to batteries, require dielectrics that offer high ionic conductivity while preventing hazards associated with dielectric leakage.

title: High-efficiency self-powered perovskite photodetector with an electron-enhancing SnO 2 /WS 2 double electron transport layer
abstract: In this study, we report the enhancement of device performance based on perovskite photoactivity using a SnO 2 /WS 2 double electron transport layer (ETL).

title: Design of chiral AIEgens for NIR-II luminescence imaging through structural modulation with multiple AIE units
abstract: BTX, a chiral NIR-II fluorescent dye that integrates multiple AIE units with aggregation-induced emission characteristics, shows photophysical properties, deep penetration, higher resolution, and better SBR effect.

title: Post-hydration treatment and nano-SiO 2 blocking defects in anodized aluminum foils to improve the breakdown voltage of a PEDOT:PSS solid electrolytic capacitor
abstract: The post-hydration treatment of anodized aluminum foil was followed by electrochemical adsorption of a nano-SiO 2 layer covering the surface of the alumina dielectric, which resulted in a 120 V increase in the solid capacitor's breakdown voltage.

title: Disentangling the structure, optical properties, and photoluminescence emissions of NiW 1− x Mo x O 4 ( x = 25, 50, and 75%) solid solutions: experimental and DFT studies
abstract: Fine-tuning the photoluminescence emissions of NiW 1− x Mo x O 4 ( x = 25, 50, and 75%) solid solutions.

title: A monoclinic crystal structure and a reusable stress-controlled optoelectronic switch in a lead halide perovskite, CsPbBr 3
abstract: A reusable well-operating stress-controlled optoelectronic switch, based on a phase transition, has been experimentally revealed in CsPbBr 3 single crystals.

title: Unlocking high capacitive energy-density in Sm-doped Pb(Mg 1/3 Nb 2/3 )O 3 –PbTiO 3 thin films via strain and domain engineering
abstract: Sm-doped Pb(Mg 1/3 Nb 2/3 )O 3 –30PbTiO 3 relaxor ferroelectric thin films with slush-like structure demonstrate ultra-high energy density, excellent thermal stability and fatigue-free energy storage properties.

title: Ultra broadband yellow emitting lead-free metal halide perovskite like compounds with near-unity emission quantum yields
abstract: Metal halide perovskites (MHPs) are interesting semiconductor materials with potential for use in optoelectronic and photonic devices.

title: Anti-swelling and adhesive hydrogels enable amphibious sensors with long-lasting conductivity, adhesion and robustness
abstract: An amphibious sensor was designed. The mechanical properties (tensile stress = 215 kPa, adhesive strength = 15 kPa) and sensing performances (electric conductivity = 17.9 mS m −1 , gauge factor = 1.2) were stable after immersing in water for a month.

title: High sensitivity terahertz sensor based on semiconductor material for biosensing detection
abstract: A terahertz metamaterial sensor with a semiconductor–dielectric–semiconductor configuration is proposed, where it can be observed that different cell types (healthy cells and cancer cells) exhibit different resonant frequencies in the spectrum.

title: Recent advancements in chiral spintronics: from molecular-level insights to device applications. A prospect based on the interplay between physical and chemical properties of chiral systems
abstract: This review discusses recent advancements in chiral-based spin-electronic devices achieved using suitable combinations of organic, inorganic and hybrid materials.

title: Enhancing efficiency in organic electronics via J-aggregation modulation with non-halogenated solvents
abstract: The co-solvent system comprising O-XY and DIO has regulated the J-aggregation of donors and acceptors, thereby optimizing their phase separation, leading to efficient organic optoelectronic devices.

title: Sintering and enhanced ferroelectric properties of textured Bi 5 Ti 3 FeO 15 ceramics with high Curie temperature
abstract: Textured BTFO ceramics exhibit enhanced ferroelectric and piezoelectric properties, as well as high Curie temperature.

title: Dual-response photoluminescence of rare earth-based halide double perovskites
abstract: Cs 2 NaScCl 6 :Sb integrates two emission adjustment modes, including excitation wavelength and temperature, showing a variety of optical application potential.

title: Machine-learning guided discovery of ultralow-threshold organic gain materials towards electrically pumped lasing
abstract: Machine learning is demonstrated for discovering ultralow-threshold organic gain molecules with an ASE threshold of approximately 0.011 μJ cm −2 , satisfying the requirement (0.1 kA cm −2 ) for electrically pumped lasers.

title: The intersection of field-limited density of states and matter: nanophotonic control of fluorescence energy transfer
abstract: Nanophotonic control over Förster resonance energy transfer pathways and efficiency in a crystalline colloidal array with three copolymerized emitters was demonstrated through strategic placement of the rejection wavelength ( λ rw ).

title: Tailored molecular engineering of mesoporous silica for high concentration Er doping and unconventional 1640 nm luminescence
abstract: This study presents a novel method for achieving as high as 6.7 wt% Er-doping in mesoporous silica via molecular engineering, yielding a unique 1640 nm photoluminescence, thus suitable for advanced photonic applications.

title: Effects of BiAlO 3 substitution on the structures and properties of antiferroelectric PbZrO 3
abstract: The stoichiometric solid solution (1 − x )PZ– x BA undergoes a composition-driven FE-to-AFE phase transition. An optimal level of BiAlO 3 substitution significantly improves the DBS of the material.

title: Enhanced water splitting for hydrogen production via Z-scheme heterostructures of Mo@CTF-0, HfS 2 , and HfSSe monolayers
abstract: Molybdenum atom-doped covalent organic framework synergistically constructs heterostructures to drive photocatalytic overall water splitting.

title: Machine learning-guided substrate temperature optimization for anisotropic Fe(Se, Te) superconducting thin films
abstract: This paper presents the fabrication of Fe(Se, Te) superconducting films via machine-learning-assisted processes. These findings provide novel insights into the optimization of Fe(Se, Te) superconducting films.

title: Flexible cold cathodes based on graphite nanoplatelet coatings on silicone rubber
abstract: Graphite nanoplatelet coatings on flexible silicone rubber enable cold cathodes with shape-adaptive geometry and tunable electron emission, offering a scalable platform for next-generation vacuum electronic devices.

title: Origin of strong ferromagnetic couplings in ordered double perovskite semiconductors
abstract: The 180° super-exchange interactions in LaCrO 3 lead to antiferromagnetic coupling, while those in La 2 NiMnO 6 lead to FM coupling. This dramatic contrast in magnetic coupling is chiefly rooted in their differing spin-occupation configurations.

title: Quantification of interfacial trap states via bias-applied HAXPES: a chemical-state perspective
abstract: Bias-applied HAXPES enables chemical-state-resolved quantification of interfacial trap states, highlighting their formation, localization, and dynamic voltage-dependent behavior.

title: Selective reaction at grain boundaries addressing organic field effect transistor trap states
abstract: Pentacene thin-films OFETs show increased conductance and mobility after exposure to maleic anhydride which shifts the mean energy in the grain boundary via an applied dipole.

title: A surface reconstruction approach using nonprecious zirconium-enriched nanosheets on NiCo nanowire catalysts with an iron foam substrate for water splitting
abstract: Zr-enriched NiCo nanowire catalysts on iron foam achieve long-term water splitting at 500 mA cm −2 for 866 h. Surface reconstruction enhances durability and activity, offering a scalable, non-precious catalytic strategy.

title: Solution-processed antimony-doped IGZTO thin-film transistors exhibiting superior operational stability under extreme environmental conditions
abstract: By introducing Sb into IGZTO TFTs, it exhibits improved electrical performance and various bias stability under extreme conditions.

title: Structure–property relationships for exciton polarons in organic–inorganic hybrid materials
abstract: By combining materials chemistry to fine-tune crystallographic features with coherent nonlinear spectroscopy, we systematically explore how organic–inorganic interactions affect polaronic coupling in two-dimensional (2D) hybrid semiconductors.

title: An amine-mediated synthesis strategy for shape-controlled cesium lead halide perovskite nanoplatelets
abstract: Two-dimensional CsPbBr 3 NPLs synthesized by varying the amine degree resulted in different shapes of NPLs. The NPLs show an increased PLQY of 59% upon increasing the amine degree, indicating effective passivation by the higher degrees of amine.

title: One-dimensional Cu–Ni core–shell composites as liquid epoxy molding compound fillers for enhanced heat dissipation and electromagnetic interference shielding in high-bandwidth memory
abstract: One-dimensional core–shell Cu–Ni composites (CS-CN) improve EMI shielding and heat dissipation of liquid epoxy molding compounds through high thermal conductivity, all-frequency EMI shielding, and uniform filler distribution at low mass loadings.

title: Co-doped aluminum metaphosphate-based phosphor for thermally robust and efficient broadband near-infrared-II light-emitting diodes
abstract: Co-doped aluminum metaphosphate-based phosphors exhibit thermally stable broadband NIR-II emission, offering great potential for high-performance pc-LEDs and sensing applications.

title: Self-power and high gain photodetection of a type-II MAPbBr 3 single-crystal film/CdSeS quantum dot heterojunction photodetector
abstract: Ultrathin MAPbBr 3 single-crystal film and alloyed CdSeS QDs form a type-II heterojunction that establishes a built-in electric field, enabling stable high-gain photodetection with an ultralow dark current of 10 −11 A, an on/off ratio of 184 313.

title: Understanding ionic screening effect in hybrid perovskite transistors based on capacitance and polarizability of the dielectric
abstract: Ionic screening effect in hybrid perovskite transistors is investigated based on the capacitance and polarizability of the gate dielectric. Suppressed ionic motion and screening effect is achieved in hybrid perovskite-polymer semiconducting channel.

title: A chemical vapor-assisted highly crystalized SbSI solar cell and a photodetector with bifacial passivation to mitigate the charge transport loss
abstract: Controlled vapor-assisted crystallization and the double side passivation successfully improved the performance of metal chalcohalide photovoltaic devices.

title: Management of interfacial energy band alignment in wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells for performance improvement
abstract: We controlled the energy level alignment at the cathode side for improving the performance of wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells.

title: Tri- tert -butyl arylamine zinc phthalocyanine derivatives as p-type self-assembled molecules for efficient perovskite solar cells
abstract: We demonstrated that the ZnPc-1 and ZnPc-2 SAMs are effective HTMs in inverted perovskite solar cells, achieving power conversion efficiencies of up to 17.25% for the monocarboxylic ZnPc-1 and 15.70% for the dicarboxylic ZnPc-2.

title: Two-dimensional Si 2 Te 2 monolayers and Si 2 Te 2 /Sb 2 Te 3 van der Waals heterostructures: promising infrared photodetector materials
abstract: The photoelectric properties of Si 2 Te 2 monolayers and Si 2 Te 2 /Sb 2 Te 3 van der Waals heterostructures in the IR region suggest their potential to facilitate the development of two-dimensional IR photoelectric p–i–n junction devices.

title: Wide bandgap organic solar cells with improved photovoltaic performance via solid additive integration
abstract: F8T2 solid additive improves the performance of PM6:GS-ISO wide band-gap solar cells thanks to the enhancement of GS-ISO crystallinity. Efficiency up to 10% PCE was achieved under 1 sun, showing potentiality for lateral multijunction architectures.

title: Surface cooling for optimized elemental distribution and improved kesterite solar cells
abstract: A surface cooling strategy was adopted, which suppresses Sn loss and facilitates Se diffusion during the selenization process, thereby optimizing the metal element distribution and photovoltaic performance of the CZTSSe absorbers.

title: Effects of Bi and Sb ion incorporation on the optoelectronic properties of mixed lead–tin perovskites
abstract: Doping lead–tin perovskites with Bi 3+ or Sb 3+ greatly increases non-radiative recombination rates by creating deep traps, even at very low densities. However, Bi 3+ impurities can be easily removed from the perovskite precursor.

title: Photo-switching in multi-stimuli-responsive low Z ′-high Z ′ co-crystal polymorphs
abstract: Unique photo-switching in unusual low Z ′-high Z ′ co-crystal polymorphs is reported and studied to understand structure–property correlations through multi-stimuli-responsive and diffraction studies.

title: Sodium ion-induced synthesis of porous carbon for efficient electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: During the carbonization of sodium alginate powder, the generated sodium carbonate serves as both a templating agent and an etching agent, resulting in the formation of porous carbon with excellent electromagnetic wave absorption performance.

title: Novel cross-linked deep-blue fluorene-based emitters for full-solution-processed organic light-emitting diodes
abstract: Three thermally cross-linkable deep-blue emitters were synthesized and full-solution-processed OLEDs all exhibited deep-blue emissions with peaks at 440, 443, and 444 nm, wherein the device based on V-SAFCz achieved an EQE max of 1.91%.

title: Exploring CoFe 2 O 4 /TiO 2 NCs for high-efficiency UV-driven organic dye degradation
abstract: CoFe 2 O 4 NPs, TiO 2 NPs, and CoFe 2 O 4 /TiO 2 NCs were synthesized and evaluated for their photocatalytic efficiency in degrading MB dye under UV light.

title: Synergistic dynamic bonds in self-repairing elastomers boost efficiency and bendability of flexible perovskite photovoltaics
abstract: Multifunctional polyurethane with dual defect passivation and self-healing capability enables high-efficiency, stable perovskite solar cells fabricated under ambient conditions, with performance recovery after mechanical damage.

title: Effects of the thermal rectification phenomenon induced by structural regulation on the thermoelectric performance of two-dimensional Bi 2 Se 3 films
abstract: An in situ integrated method is used to measure the thermoelectric performance of Bi 2 Se 3 thin films. The coupling mechanism between thermal rectification effect caused by structural regulation and thermoelectric parameter is tentatively explored.

title: Theoretical studies on the strong phonon coherence in the type-5 penta-PbN 8 sheet
abstract: This study reveals low lattice thermal conductivity of penta-PbN 8 along x ( y )-direction at room temperature, driven by phonon coherence and scattering, using first-principles and unified transport theory, beyond reported penta-sheet types.

title: Tunable NO gas sensing behavior of α-Fe 2 O 3 nanoparticles: p–n transition mechanism based on the Langmuir model
abstract: The sensor exhibits a distinctive characteristic with a reversible transition from p- to n-type sensing behavior. The transition diagrams can be well described using a two-isotherm model based on Langmuir adsorption theory.

title: A robust UV-curable ion-liquid gel electrolyte for high-performance polymer-based electrochromic devices
abstract: Gel electrolytes with high strength and excellent mechanical and electrochemical properties were prepared using EA and ionic liquid (VEIMPF 6 ) via UV curing, and the assembled electrochromic devices exhibited superior performance.

title: Thermoelectric properties and figure of merit of Cu 2 ZnSnS 4
abstract: A comprehensive modelling study of Cu 2 ZnSnS 4 provides reference predictions for the thermoelectric properties and identifies an optimal crystallite size of 5–10 nm to yield a high-temperature figure of merit ZT > 1.

title: Odd–even effect controls twist-elasticity of an organic fluorophore in cocrystals prepared using mechanochemistry
abstract: Cocrystallization of odd–even aliphatic dicarboxylic acids with an organic fluorophore induces twist-elasticity, thus resulting odd–even effect in solid-state fluorescence as well as nanomechanical properties.

title: Magnetocaloric effects in the quasi-1D chain carbonate NaDy(CO 3 )F 2 through crystallographic directional rotation
abstract: Using a supercritical hydrothermal reaction, we synthesized millimeter-scale single crystals of NaDy(CO 3 )F 2 , featuring a unique one-dimensional Dy-based zigzag chain structure and a pronounced rotational magnetocaloric effect.

title: Tunable color-stable hybrid white OLEDs by combining fluorescent and TADF emitters in a single emissive layer
abstract: Tuning of hybrid white organic light-emitting diodes from cool and pure to warm white emission by varying the dopant concentration from 0.5 wt% to 1.0 wt%.

title: Achieving excellent corrosion resistance in Nd–Ce–Fe–B magnets via oxygen partial pressure regulation
abstract: Rapid thermal oxidation at 350 °C for 0.5 h under a controlled oxygen partial pressure synergistically enhances the anti-corrosion and mechanical performance of Ce50 permanent magnets, accompanied with a high coercivity above 12 kOe.

title: Ba 2 SiSe 4 : a promising candidate with visual light transparency and p-type electrical conductivity
abstract: Commercially available transparent conducting materials (TCMs) are typically n-type, and high-performance p-type TCMs are rare, which would impede the development of optoelectronics.

title: A low-cost strategy for photocatalytic hydrogen production by using non-noble metal catalysts and biomass as a sacrificial agent
abstract: This study develops a low-cost H 2 production system: a non-noble metal catalyst paired with urea-treated corn straw, which achieves an H 2 production of 108.37 μmol in 4 h. Provides a green approach for waste utilization and H 2 production.

title: Multifunctional azo-BODIPY-functionalised upconversion nanoparticles as sensors of hypoxia in biological environments
abstract: A near-infrared excitable hypoxia-sensitive sensor was designed with upconverting nanoparticles and an azo-dye, absorbing their visible emissions. In hypoxic environments, the azo bond is reduced, recovering the nanoparticles’ upconversion emissions.

title: Improving the current stability of perovskite quantum dot phototransistors utilizing the ferrocene–cyclodextrin host–guest supramolecules as a floating gate dielectric
abstract: A novel electroactive supramolecular floating-gate dielectric is developed by utilizing host–guest interactions between β-cyclodextrin and ferrocene to improve phototransistors’ current stability and performance with perovskite quantum dots.

title: Modulation of the microstructure and electrical properties of LaAlO 3 ceramics induced by doping with rare earth elements (Eu, Gd, Ho, and Tm)
abstract: This study synthesized La 0.9 X 0.1 AlO 3 (X = Eu, Gd, Ho, Tm) and LaAlO 3 ceramics by a solid-state method.

title: Post-transition metal Sn-based chalcogenide perovskites: a promising lead-free and transition metal alternative for stable, high-performance photovoltaics
abstract: Chalcogenide perovskites are emerging as game-changers in optoelectronics, thanks to their exceptional stability, eco-friendly nature, optimal bandgaps, strong light absorption, and remarkable defect tolerance.

title: Polypyrrole (PPy) coating concentration mediates electromagnetic coupling and oxidative protection for iron oxide core particles in biomedical applications
abstract: This work highlights the dual-functionality of PPy coatings, which simultaneously enhances electromagnetic properties and provides oxidative degradation protection in physiologically relevant environments.

title: Recent progress of optoelectronic applications based on 2D WSe 2 nanomaterials and heterostructures: a review
abstract: Tungsten diselenide (WSe 2 ) is extensively investigated as a channel optoelectrode material with remarkable optical, electrical, and magnetic properties and mechanical flexibility.

title: Design principles for ultra-low humidity sensors: a mechanism-oriented review
abstract: The precise detection of ultra-low relative humidity (<10% RH) is essential in semiconductor manufacturing, lithium-ion battery production, and aerospace systems, where even trace amounts of moisture can lead to irreversible performance degradation.

title: Analysis of solar harvesting and refractive index sensing of multifunctional metamaterial perfect absorbers based on Ag discs and B 4 C–VC cube films
abstract: This paper proposes a multifunctional metamaterial perfect absorber. The device can achieve perfect absorption of the ultra wideband and perfect absorption of three ultra-narrowbands.

title: Molecular weight optimization for intrinsically stretchable conjugated polymers: from film microstructure to strain-insensitive performance
abstract: This review provides an overview of molecular weight optimization for intrinsically stretchable conjugated polymer films, focusing on solution-assemble behavior and the relationship between film microstructure and electrical/mechanical properties.

title: Preparation of two-dimensional superconductors: a comprehensive review
abstract: This review highlights recent advances in the preparation of 2D superconductors, emphasizing fabrication methods like mechanical exfoliation, MBE, PLD, and in particular the CVD method, and discusses the future research directions.

title: Magnesium vapor-assisted deoxidation of La–Ce alloys through micro-alloying
abstract: Magnesium vapor-assisted deoxidation through micro-alloying is the key to maintaining the low oxygen concentration of La–Ce alloys.

title: Tuning of oxygen vacancies to control color and electron dynamics in alkali metal substituted bismuth layered oxides for energy-efficient coatings
authors: Vibin Lal Nayakom Mini Ancy Mini
abstract: Alkali metal variations in bismuth layered oxides modify oxygen vacancies, tuning their color and electronic properties for energy-efficient coatings.

title: Theoretical design of homojunction solar cells based on chalcopyrite AgInSe 2 : a combined study of first-principles calculations and device simulations
abstract: Lattice mismatch at the interface of homojunction solar cell is negligible. First-principles studies show AgInSe 2 can be p/n-type under different growth conditions. SCAPS-1D simulations indicate AgInSe 2 homojunction solar cells can achieve high PCE.

title: Correlative spectroscopy mapping of the prospective photovoltaic material bournonite using Raman and cathodoluminescence
abstract: A CuPbSbS 3 thin film is mapped using several techniques, with variations in Raman and cathodoluminescence further explained through DFT and STEM-EDX.

title: Near room-temperature chemical vapor deposition of 2D SbI 3 on van der Waals substrates for photodetector applications
abstract: 2D SbI 3 crystals are grown by CVD at near-room temperature on van der Waals substrates. The SbI 3 -modified monolayer WS 2 photodetector exhibits a notable enhancement in photocurrent by two orders of magnitude, as compared to pristine WS 2 devices.

title: Mechanical stimulus-responsive luminescence: dynamic and durable mechanoluminescence based on PTZ derivatives
abstract: PTZ-2BP exhibits dynamic and durable mechanoluminescence, mainly derived from conformational changes, energy transfer from the crystalline to the amorphous state, and stable molecular packing in crystals.

title: Correction: Highly stretchable and elastic PEDOT:PSS helix fibers enabled wearable sensors
abstract: Correction for ‘Highly stretchable and elastic PEDOT:PSS helix fibers enabled wearable sensors’ by Jing Chen et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2023, 11 , 13358–13369, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TC02381H.

title: A porous and flame-retardant rGO/PPTA modified PP composite separator suitable for high electrochemical performance and excellent security of lithium metal battery
abstract: A kind of separator with excellent pore structure and flame retardancy was prepared by a single-side coating method. A synchronous improvement in safety performance and high electrochemical performance was realized.

title: A mechanically robust, high electrically and low thermally conducting silicon oxycarbide ceramic composite by spark plasma sintering
abstract: A silicon oxycarbide (SiOC) ceramic composite produced via a high-temperature spark plasma sintering process exhibits excellent mechanical robustness, high electrical conductivity, and low thermal conductivity, useful for various applications.

title: Enhancing selectivity in area-selective atomic layer deposition of Al 2 O 3 on Cu using N 2 co-flow
abstract: The selectivity of Al 2 O 3 during area-selective atomic layer deposition on a self-assembled monolayer was significantly enhanced by the simple introduction of N 2 co-flow during the trimethylaluminum injection step.

title: 9,9-Dialkylfluorene- alt -benzothiadiazole-based bolaamphiphiles with the formation of complex self-assembled liquid-crystalline phases of a cubic A15 network and Col squ / p 4 gm at the triangle–square transition
abstract: Dialkylfluorene BTD based bolaamphiphiles can self-assemble into complex A15 type cubic network and Col squ / p 4 gm LC phases, as well as be successfully applied in manufacture of LE-LCDs, WLEDs and PEDOT:PSS/n-Si HSCs with 13.97% PCE.

title: Lanthanide-based metallogels with tunable luminescence: nanomolar detection of a nerve agent simulant and anticounterfeiting applications
abstract: Two lanthanide-based luminescent metallogels, fabricated by coordination-driven self-assembly, illustrate nanomolar detection of a nerve agent simulant and anticounterfeiting applications.

title: Fabrication of Cu-MOFs derived nanofiber membranes for efficient removal of environmental pollutants
abstract: A series of homogeneous nanofiber membrane materials were prepared by sol–gel and electrospinning techniques by using Cu-based derived materials, which possessed high-efficiency photocatalytic degradation activity in the water purification process.

title: Reduced lattice thermal conductivity and strong four-phonon scattering in h -B 12 assembled from boron clusters on a honeycomb lattice
abstract: Icosahedral B 12 clusters are embedded in a two-dimensional honeycomb lattice through cluster assembly, effectively suppressing phonon transport.

title: Enhanced dielectric properties of CaLnNbWO 8 (Ln = La, Nd, Sm, Gd, Ho) through an entropy engineering strategy
abstract: The advancement of dielectric ceramics with a low dielectric constant ( ε r ), high quality factor ( Q × f ), and near-zero temperature coefficient of resonant frequency ( τ f ) is crucial for optimizing the performance of microwave devices.

title: Efficient optical photoswitching of benzyloxy-substituted TCF-based D–π–A molecules
abstract: A series of five donor–π–acceptor stilbene dyes show efficient trans – cis photoisomerization and photoinduced birefringence. Their high photosensitivity and optical memory highlight potential for advanced photonic and optoelectronic applications.

title: Highly sensitive detection of n -butanol at room temperature using a SnO 2 /Nb 4 C 3 T x -based sensing platform
abstract: Few-layer Nb 4 C 3 T x MXene nanosheets were prepared and tested for n -butanol sensing. The SnO 2 /Nb 4 C 3 T x nanocomposite was also synthesized, showing excellent n -butanol sensing performance at room temperature.

title: A spherical layered heterostructure design using the upgraded beluga whale optimization algorithm for tunable polarization conversion
abstract: The details of each component of a SLH.

title: Effects of the Fe/Ni ratio in double half-Heusler composition HfFe 1− x Ni x Sb
abstract: The adjustment of the Fe/Ni ratio in the double half-Heusler composition HfFe x Ni 1− x Sb leads to a p-type to n-type transition. The thermoelectric figures of merit zT = 0.36 and 0.22 at 950 K for n- and p-type, respectively, were demonstrated.

title: Piezoelectric composites with BaTiO 3 NPs as guiding membranes: reconstructing the bioelectric microenvironment for enhanced bone regeneration
abstract: A schematic diagram illustrates the fabrication and bone repair mechanism of CSSA-BTO piezoelectric membranes. Their biomimetic application reconstructs the electrical microenvironment under biophysical drive, promoting bone regeneration.

title: KCl induces a “slow-release effect” on PbI 2 to strengthen the crystallization of perovskite and the reverse-bias stability of carbon-electrode-based solar cells
abstract: KCl induces a “slow-release effect” on PbI 2 , which improves PVSK crystallization and strengthens the reverse-bias stability of the hole-conductor-free CPSCs. Moreover, reverse-bias luminescence (RBL) behavior is newly released, with EQE of ∼5%.

title: Conductive behavior of cross-linked electropolymeric films formed by ‘star-shaped’ multifunctional precursors
abstract: The electro-crosslinked polymer formed from the “star-shaped” precursor TCTA exhibits inherent twisted conformations. While electropolymerization results in highly planar, doping-controlled films, it also limits their electrical performance.

title: Tuning a consistent near-infrared photo-initiated multimodal tumor strategy based on the core–shell structure of gold star@PB@CuS nanoparticles
abstract: Gold star@PB@CuS (GPC) nanoparticles with a core–shell structure were designed and fabricated to enable a near-infrared photo-initiated antitumor strategy.

title: Enhancement in the nonlinear optical properties of silver nanoprisms through graphene oxide anchoring
abstract: Nanocomposites consisting of citrate-passivated silver nanoprisms anchored electrostatically and covalently on graphene oxide sheets were produced. The formed hybrid nanocomposites exhibit enhanced nonlinear optical properties.

title: Optimization of the hygrosensitive response characteristics of a polyacrylate humidity sensor using halloysite nanotubes
abstract: In this study, fast-response, low-power, high-precision composite moisture-sensitive membrane materials, namely, PAC/HNTs were prepared using halloysite nanotubes (HNTs) and acrylic resins (PAC).

title: Tunable flower-like NiFe 2 O 4 –FeNi/C/SnO 2 composites with radar-infrared compatible stealth performance
abstract: Flower-like NiFe 2 O 4 –FeNi/C/SnO 2 composite achieves excellent radar stealth with a 25.24 dB m 2 RCS reduction. The infrared reflectivity of the composite can reach 0.82. The composite exhibits excellent radar-infrared compatible stealth properties.

title: Tartaric acid-based chiral polyamides: unraveling intrinsic multicolor clusteroluminescence and the solvent-modulated emission mechanism
abstract: Tartaric acid-based chiral polyamides with intrinsic multicolor clusteroluminescence and solvent-modulated emission.

title: Highly solar modulated and robust PNIPAM/HEMC smart windows
abstract: The PNIPAM/HEMC hydrogels exhibited enhanced optical characteristics, including elevated luminous transmittance ( T lum = 84.98%) and augmented solar modulation (Δ T sol = 74.97%).

title: A strongly birefringent sulfate crystal with a wide second-harmonic generation phase-matching range
abstract: A strongly birefringent ultraviolet nonlinear optical sulfate crystal was developed through π-conjugation incorporation, exhibiting high birefringence, a wide second-harmonic generation phase-matching range, and a broad UV transmission window.

title: Synergistic coupling of heterointerface Ni 2 P/Co 2 P nanocrystals anchored on MXene nanosheets for high-performance oxygen and hydrogen evolution reactions
abstract: The kinetic energy barrier ( E a ), which significantly decreases in the intermediate transition state of adsorption, can be modulated by designed heterointerface catalysts.

title: Smart-responsive luminescence and VOC sensing application of a copper( i ) cluster with a novel P 2 CuI 2 CuN 2 core
abstract: A fluorescent sensor for detection of VOCs was successfully fabricated using a new binuclear CuI-cluster, (Xantphos)Cu 2 I 2 (4-PBO) 2 (1), as a probe. The cluster can be conveniently prepared using a ball-milling method.

title: Enhanced performance of a Mg 2 Si/Si heterojunction photodetector grown with the assistance of nanostructures
abstract: This graph shows a Mg 2 Si/Si heterojunction photodetector fabricated with the assistance of nanostructures, enabling enhanced optoelectronic performance with a 173–402% improvement in responsivity and a 111–281% improvement in specific detectivity.

title: The switchable quantum anomalous Hall effect and altermagnetism in Janus monolayer and bilayer V 2 WS 2 Se 2
abstract: First-principles calculations reveal the dual functionality of bilayer V 2 WS 2 Se 2 . Interlayer sliding switches it between an altermagnetic semiconductor and a quantum anomalous Hall insulator, offering a promising platform for spintronic devices.

title: Highly sensitive flexible humidity sensors with fast response and recovery times based on the composite of graphene oxide and WS 2 for detection of human breath and fingertip proximity
abstract: Flexible humidity sensors based on a GO/WS 2 composite were realized with a high sensitivity of 1.68%/%RH and fast response/recovery times (11.3 s/12.4 s), which were successfully applied for human breath detection and fingertip proximity detection.

title: Switching of light responsive metal–organic gels from insulator to semiconductor: flexible smart semiconducting membranes for optoelectronic device fabrication
abstract: This study reports the first MOG material transitioning from insulator to semiconductor via a [2+2] reaction and Li + doping, achieving record-high Li-ion conductivity and enabling power-up LED lights.

title: Synergistically engineered PVDF/CNT/GnP hierarchical nanocomposites via scalable solution spinning for ultradurable, superhydrophobic EMI shielding wearables
abstract: Schematic diagram of EMWs absorption mechanism of PVDF-based composite fabrics.

title: Graphene with multiscale synergistic optimization: achieving superior cross-band electromagnetic wave absorption performance
abstract: Multiscale synergy strategies offer promising solutions for future multi-frequency electromagnetic pollution.

title: Research on high thermal conductivity PPENK/PVP modified BN electrospinning hot-pressed multifunctional nanocomposite films
abstract: Design and construction of PPENK/MBN composite films with ‘wafer biscuits’ structure.

title: Modulating crystallinity and mixed ionic–electronic conduction properties via terminal side chain engineering of n-type small molecules
abstract: Via versatile terminal side chain modification in n-type small molecular mixed conductors, a superior figure of merit μC * of 14.1 F V −1 cm −1 s −1 in OECTs is achieved, along with robust synaptic tunability and excellent non-volatility in OENSs.

title: Experimental evidence of the excited-state mixing in the blue emitter for organic light-emitting diodes
abstract: Superior triplet harvesting properties of TMCz-BO emitter are analyzed in liquid and solid media. The notions of dynamic and static excited-state mixing are introduced to explain dual and triple nature of S 1 and T 1 states as well as TADF mechanism.

title: Advances in integrated quantum photonics for quantum sensing and communication
abstract: Integrated photonics in quantum technology.

title: Promoting the grain growth of CZTSSe solar cells by incorporating Sb 2 Se 3 and annealing in an atmosphere devoid of toxic selenium
abstract: Sb 2 Se 3 was incorporated into precursor films. During annealing, Sb 2 Se 3 decomposes, releasing Sb and Se, which provides the necessary energy for grain growth through mass transport at boundaries, thereby promoting the performance of solar cells.

title: Highly efficient luminescence and color-tunable white emission from 0D Cs 2 InCl 5 ·H 2 O perovskites via Al 3+ and Sb 3+ codoping
abstract: The Al 3+ –Sb 3+ co-doped Cs 2 InCl 5 ·H 2 O exhibits bright broadband emission (PLQY~80%) with luminescence color closes to standard white light. Further, by changing the concentration of Al 3+ , precise control of the luminescence color is achieved.

title: Carbazole derivatives as electron transport and n-type acceptor materials for efficient organic light-emitting devices
abstract: Carbazole-containing A–D–A′ type electron transport materials show dramatically enhanced electron injection/transport properties compared to the commercial A 3 -π structured TPBI.

title: Supramolecular and electrical properties of ruthenium-based organometallic gels
abstract: A novel organometallic gel whose molecular building block contains either one (Ru 1 ) or two Ru (Ru 2 ) atoms is presented, and its supramolecular properties in solution have been investigated.

title: Nanoparticle self-assembly and domain growth kinetics in BPI: a synchrotron ultra-small angle X-ray scattering study
abstract: QDs at ≤1 wt% are confined within defect core, mimic BP lattice, and exhibit strong diffraction peaks in Synchrotron USAXS profiles. At ≥3 wt%, QDs aggregate and do not confine within defect core, with X-ray profile indicating colloidal formation.

title: Doped interlayers enabling high-mobility p-type organic transistors with copper contact electrodes
abstract: Doped interlayers were employed to improve charge injection from copper electrodes into high-mobility organic single-crystal semiconductors, addressing both economic and material sustainability challenges in organic electronics.

title: Highly thermally stable binary cross-linkable organic nonlinear optical materials based on different Diels–Alder or Huisgen cycloaddition reactions
abstract: Electro-optic coefficients of up to 234–312 pm V −1 and glass transition temperatures as high as 118–160 °C were achieved in cross-linked films QLD1 and QLD3–6.

title: Intramolecular hydrogen bonds assist in controlling the quantum well distribution in quasi-2D perovskites for efficient pure-blue perovskite light-emitting diodes
abstract: Use of an o -F-PEABr ligand containing an intramolecular hydrogen bond can effectively suppress low- n phases in quasi-2D perovskites. The resultant light-emitting diodes achieve a maximum efficiency of 7.55% and a pure blue emission at 475 nm.

title: Correction: Influence of shape on crystal structure and optical properties of heterocyclic conjugated molecules
abstract: Correction for ‘Influence of shape on crystal structure and optical properties of heterocyclic conjugated molecules’ by Elisa Guzmán et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, 13 , 954–962, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TC03509G.

title: MOF-derived, carbon-mediated construction of a hierarchical rod-shaped MoO 3 /Co 3 O 4 heterojunction towards efficient triethylamine detection
abstract: The in situ MOF-derived, carbon-mediated creation of a MoO 3 /Co 3 O 4 heterojunction has been demonstrated to achieve a high response towards triethylamine.

title: Ultra-high activity methanol oxidation electrocatalyzed by a flexible integrated Pt–Zn array electrode
abstract: This work reported a series of novel flexible integrated electrodes with Pt–Zn nanoparticles uniformly grown in situ on carbon cloth via a one-step electro-deposition method, it possess ultra-high methanol oxidation activity.

title: Channelling Er 3+ -activated vanadate garnet's multimode luminescence for high-sensitivity optical thermometers and NIR-II pc-LEDs for bioimaging
abstract: The multifunctional Er 3+ activated Sr 2 NaMg 2 V 3 O 12 garnet phosphor finds its potentiality in wide range thermometry (80–700 K) and designing NIR-II LEDs with good thermal stability for imaging biological systems.

title: Enhanced charge trapping effect in PVA/PbI 2 synaptic transistors achieved through integrated UV irradiation and thermal annealing treatments
abstract: This study investigates the effects of UV irradiation intensity and thermal annealing on PbI 2 films. By increasing the number of iodine vacancies in PbI 2 , the charge trapping capability of PVA/PbI 2 synaptic transistors is enhanced.

title: Enhancement of built-in electric field strength of BiOCl/NMT Z-scheme heterojunctions through photoinitiated defects for optimized photocatalytic performance
abstract: Under photoinitiation, the generation of additional defect structures in BiOCl induces the downward migration of E f , which enhances the built-in electric field strength between it and NMT.

title: Wearable and implantable transient bioelectronics
abstract: This review discusses the material selection, device design approaches, and state-of-the-art applications of wearable and implantable transient bioelectronics.

title: High efficiency sulfamethoxazole degradation enabled by chlorinated D–A scheme organic photocatalysts
abstract: In order to introduce chlorinated non-fullerene small molecules into the photocatalytic field, efficient, and reusable D–A organic photocatalysts have been developed in this study by using coconut charcoal shells as the substrate material.

title: Intrinsic valley polarization and manipulation via interfacial coupling in a two-dimensional GdIBr/In 2 Se 3 van der Waals multiferroic heterojunction
abstract: The valley polarization and bandgap opening behavior of the GdIBr/In 2 Se 3 heterojunction can be modulated via biaxial strain.

title: Structure and luminescence properties of multicolor phosphors based on a new phosphate Ba 2 In 4 P 6 O 23
abstract: Tunable luminescence and satisfactory luminous-thermal stability make Tb 3+ /Eu 3+ co-doped phosphor, based on a new phosphate Ba 2 In 4 P 6 O 23 , a promising material for solid-state illumination.

title: Understanding the trends of phase transition in d n ( n = 0 to 6) transition metal disulfides during carrier injection: a perspective from d-orbital filling
abstract: The trends of phase transition in transition metal sulfides can be predicted with their band edge positions.

title: Efficient narrow-band green phosphors for mini-LED displays using dual strategies of high concentration quenching and energy transfer
abstract: Narrow-band green phosphor have been realized by high doping concentration and energy transfer in GMAO. The luminous color has achieved a wide range of adjustability. GMAO:Eu 2+ ,Mn 2+ is valuable for applications in Mini-LED displays.

title: CC bond enables difluorideboron β-diketonate derivatives with high contrast mechanoresponsive luminescence for reversible writing and information encryption
abstract: CC bond cis – trans isomerization and “head-to-head” arrangement induced in high-contrast mechanoresponsive luminescence.

title: Multi-level Δ-learning for predicting the radiative decay rate constant of phosphorescent platinum( ii ) complexes
abstract: The multi-level Δ-learning method significantly advances k r prediction by bridging TDDFT results with experimental values. This approach improves prediction reliability, making it essential for accurate material evaluation.

title: Ferroelectric polarization-modulated two-dimensional homojunctions for enhanced nonvolatile multistate memory with self-powered optical readout capacity
abstract: A ferroelectric polarization-modulated two-dimensional homojunction, which has great potential for high-density data storage and offers an efficient means for self-powered optical readout, signifies a remarkable advance in storage technology.

title: Improving electromagnetic engineering of thermal conductive composites by establishing continuous thermal conductive networks with gradient impedance
abstract: Mechanism schematic of the EM wave absorption and thermal conduction of composites.

title: Preparation of lightweight layered porous SiC nanowires/RGO composites with excellent electromagnetic wave absorption performance
abstract: SiC nanowires/reduced graphene oxide (SiC NWs /RGO) composites with layered porous structures exhibit good electromagnetic wave absorption (EWA) performance.

title: A machine learning approach to identifying perovskite materials in A 2 BB′X 6 compounds
abstract: This study proposes an efficient machine learning model based on atomic statistical characteristics that can accurately predict the formation of perovskite structures in A 2 BB'X 6 -type double perovskite materials.
