abstract: Rb 3 Sb 2 X 9 (X = Br, I) halides show a temperature-driven phase transition from monoclinic ( P 2 1 / n ) to trigonal ( P 3̄ m 1). Distortion-mode analysis reveals octahedral rotations as primary drivers, with cation shifts and bond modulations stabilizing the low-symmetry phase.

title: 3D flower-like architectures assembled by ZnO porous nanosheets with higher surface area for enhanced lower temperature NO 2 gas sensors
abstract: This study presents porous ZnO nanosheets for low-concentration NO 2 detection and a facile 3D ZnO fabrication method with high surface area, revealing enhanced sensing via oxygen vacancy defects and surface adsorption mechanisms.

title: Unraveling the optical signatures of polymeric carbon nitrides: insights into stacking-induced excitonic transitions
abstract: Polymeric carbon nitrides (PCNs) are intriguing and versatile semiconductors with 2D stacked architecture and hold promise for applications in photocatalysis and optoelectronics.

title: A zero-dimensional hybrid halide with superior water resistance for high-efficiency X-ray scintillation and solid-state lighting
abstract: In recent years, hybrid metal halides have gained considerable attention for optoelectronic applications due to their outstanding photophysical properties, despite challenges with stability.

title: Deciphering the circularly polarized luminescence of covalent organic frameworks (COFs) through ab initio modelling
abstract: Chirality is a radical property largely observed in the universe, arising in objects lacking inversion symmetry, thereby making the objects exist as entities with distinct handedness.

title: Enhanced breakdown strength and polarization behavior in relaxor ferroelectric films via bidirectional design of defect engineering and heterogeneous interface construction
abstract: Through bidirectional design of heterogeneous interface construction and defect engineering, a high W rec of 83.6 J cm −3 and efficiency of 74.1% are achieved for the 2 mol% Fe-doped 0.8NBT–0.2BLT film.

title: Designing symmetrically folded scaffolds of pyridazinone and triazinone derivatives linked via N , N -diethyl-4-nitro-benzenesulfonamide to explore luminescent materials
abstract: The current study reports the synthesis and photophysical characteristics of novel folded organic molecules connected by a four-atom spacer.

title: Prediction of pressure-induced superconductivity in the ternary systems CaYH 2 n ( n = 3–6) at moderate pressures
abstract: The high-pressure phase structures of CaYH 2 n ( n = 3–6) ternary hydrides were systematically explored employing a combination of particle swarm optimization and first principles calculations.

title: Insights into light transmission through 2,5-bis(arylethynyl)pyrazine based crystals: a combined experimental and theoretical study
abstract: In the field of optical nano/micro waveguides, the ability to modulate the crystal structure and understand the relationship between structure and waveguide properties is a crucial aspect of significant interest.

title: Quantum transport simulation of α-GeTe ferroelectric semiconductor transistors
abstract: The ferroelectric semiconductor transistor is a newly proposed device that uses ferroelectric semiconductors as channel materials for integrated memory and computation.

title: Carbon-modulated reduction synthesis of dual-valent Eu-doped La 3 Si 2 O 8 Cl phosphors: single-matrix white LEDs and high-resolution solar-blind UV detection
abstract: La 3 Si 2 O 8 Cl:7%Eu phosphors for solar-blind ultraviolet detection and white LEDs have been synthesized by a carbon-modulated reduction synthesis method.

title: Green-synthesized single-benzene fluorophores exhibiting room-temperature phosphorescence and solid-state fluorescence for biological and optical applications
abstract: Single-benzene fluorophores (SBFs) offer minimalistic access to very small, bright, and electronically tunable emitters via push–pull effects.

title: Stability and electro-optical properties of the hydrogen-functionalized monolayer BC 3 H 3 : a theoretical study
abstract: Utilizing the PBE + G 0 W 0 + BSE calculations, we systematically explore the stability and electro-optical properties of the hydrogen-functionalized monolayer BC 3 H 3 .

title: Molecular interaction regulation by adding a third component with high miscibility suppresses the energetic disorder and reduces energy loss for efficient ternary solar cells
abstract: In the advancement of organic solar cells (OSCs), the ternary strategy has emerged as an effective approach for fabricating devices with high photovoltaic performance.

title: An electron-dominated lateral photovoltaic effect in ZnO-based perovskite heterojunctions and its performance tunability by pyroelectric effect
abstract: An electron-dominated ZnO-based perovskite heterojunction position sensitive detector with a MAPbI 3 /ZnO structure is successfully fabricated, and the pyroelectric effect is incorporated to modulate the lateral photovoltaic response.

title: Optimized encapsulation of CsPbBr 3 nanocrystals in metal–organic frameworks by improved synthesis
abstract: Both stabilities and luminescent performance of all-inorganic perovskite nanocrystals@MOF could be improved via two-step synthesis approach.

title: Fabry–Pérot interference-enhanced GaSe visible-blind UV photodetectors
abstract: An asymmetric Fabry–Pérot (F–P) structure consisting of a GaSe nanosheet, Al 2 O 3 spacer and Al back reflector was fabricated to realize the wavelength-selective absorption enhancement of the GaSe nanosheet in the UV region.

title: Development of high-birefringence thieno[3,2- b ]thiophene-based liquid crystalline molecules with an alkyl and alkylsulfanyl group: structural design and optical performance
abstract: Investigation of the relationship between the optical and dielectric properties and the molecular structure of novel liquid-crystalline compounds based on a thieno[3,2- b ]thiophene core.

title: Enhanced room-temperature electrocaloric performance by both multiphase coexistence and diffused phase transition in (Ba 0.65 Sr 0.3 Ca 0.05 )(Sn x Ti 1− x )O 3 ferroelectric ceramics
abstract: Multiphase coexistence and DPT are designed to enhance ECE. A very high Δ T of 1.79 K (indirect) and 2.19 K (direct) and large T span of 26 K (90% Δ T max ) and 45 K (80% Δ T max ) are obtained in the ceramics (Ba 0.65 Sr 0.3 Ca 0.05 )(Sn x Ti 1− x )O 3 for x = 0.02.

title: Enhanced energy storage performance with excellent thermal stability of BNT-based ceramics via the multiphase engineering strategy for pulsed power capacitor
abstract: High-temperature resistance and ultra-fast discharging of materials are among the hot topics in the development of pulsed power systems.

title: Ultrastable fluoropolymer-based porous conductive elastomer composites (PVDF–HFP/CB) for high-sensitivity pressure sensing applications
abstract: Flexible pressure sensors necessitate a broad detection range and high sensitivity to ensure adaptability across diverse application scenarios and reliable signal acquisition.

title: Halogen-substituted quasi-spherical tetraethylammonium cations induce reversible molecular ferroelectricity in indium-based hybrid materials
abstract: Four high-temperature ferroelectric materials: [(CH 3 CH 2 ) 3 CH 2 XN][InBr 4 ](X = C( 1 ), Cl( 2 ), Br( 3 ), I( 4 )) showing switchable phase transition, dielectric anomaly, SHG behavior, were successfully designed and synthesized.

title: Transparent photochromic Fe-doped W 18 O 49 films with ultrahigh solar energy modulation for smart windows
abstract: Fe 3+ doping, as an electron transformed mediate, significantly increases the bleaching speed of photochromic W 18 O 49 nanocomposite film and simultaneously improves its optical performance, with T lum of 82.9% and Δ T sol of 60.2% for smart windows.

title: A superhard and superdense carbon allotrope with all-sp 3 bonded helical chains of five-membered rings
abstract: Using a first-principles approach, we present a novel superhard and superdense carbon, T-C 10 , assembled from twisted five-membered rings. This structure exhibits excellent incompressibility and ultraviolet absorption.

title: Thermochromism versus piezochromism in (PMA) 2 CuX 4 (X = Br, Cl) halide perovskites
abstract: Hydrophobic 2D copper perovskites (PMA) 2 CuCl 4 and (PMA) 2 CuBr 4 show reversible thermochromism and piezochromism, with band gap shifts up to ∼90 nm. Enhanced stability enables tunable optical properties for sensing and photonic devices.

title: Deep trap state emission in vacancy-ordered double perovskite Cs 2 SnCl 6 microcrystals
abstract: The weak warm white light emission was discovered in Cs 2 SnCl 6 microcrystals, and was assigned to the deep trap state. The relaxation mechanism involving two deep trap states were illustrated by spectroscopic characters under variable P/T conditions.

title: Charge transport of silicon( iv ) and zinc( ii ) phthalocyanines by molecular junction models
abstract: The band gap energy of metal phthalocyanine-TiO 2 decreased compared to TiO 2 . Current values for silicon phthalocyanines reached up to 0.23 μA.

title: Nb 2 CT x MXene integrated DyMn 2 O 5 composites: tailored particle size and enhanced capacitance for high performance pseudocapacitors
abstract: This work presents the working principle of an asymmetric supercapacitor using DyMn 2 O 5 /Dy 2 O 3 /MXene//AC.

title: Multifunctional Eu( iii ) and Sm( iii ) coordination polymers built with silane-bridged dicarboxylate ligand: structure, luminescence and magnetism
abstract: Multifunctional Eu- and Sm-based CPs built from silane-bridged dicarboxylate ligand: 2D materials exhibiting exceptional luminescence and SMM behaviour.

title: Lead-free halide double-perovskite nanocrystals: structure, synthesis, optoelectronic properties, and applications
abstract: Lead-free halide double perovskite nanocrystals (NCs) are promising alternatives to lead-based perovskites, offering eco-friendly composition, robust intrinsic thermodynamic stability, and tunable optoelectronic properties.

title: Bulk nano-heterojunction strategy enables record charge collection in solution-processed metal oxide UV photodetectors
abstract: A novel bulk nano-heterojunction (BNH) architecture comprised of ZnO and NiO x nanocrystals is introduced for all-solution-processed, high performance ultraviolet photodetectors, enabling record external quantum efficiency and responsivity.

title: Valley topological phase transitions under the combined effects of electronic correlation and strain in the H-TiSeBr monolayer
abstract: By reducing the electronic correlation, the H-TiSeBr monolayer can sustain the valley-polarized quantum anomalous Hall state within a broader strain range.

title: Enhancing performance in blue quantum-dot light-emitting diodes by using a deep HOMO carbazol-phenyl-triazine small molecule blended hole transporting layer
abstract: By blending the HOMO darker phosphorescent small molecule TCPZ (6.13 eV) with PVK (5.70 eV) as the HTL, a low turn-on voltage of 2.90 eV and a high external quantum efficiency of 16.33% in blue CdSe/CdS/ZnS QLEDs can be achieved.

title: Effects of Li + , Na + , and K + doping on the microstructure, fluorescence thermometry, and thermochromism of Ho 3+ ,Yb 3+ :Bi 2 WO 6 materials
abstract: Effects of 1% K + K + doping on the microstructure, fluorescence thermometry, and thermochromism of 3% Ho 3+ , 10% Yb 3+ :Bi 2 WO 6 materials.

title: Unravelling the structure–luminescence relationship in two dimensional antimony( iii )-doped cadmium( ii ) halide hybrids
abstract: Luminescent zero dimensional (0D) antimony halide (Sb–X) hybrids showcase emissive properties (emission peak position; photoluminescence quantum yield – PLQY) that are strongly dependent on the local metal halide geometry/site asymmetry.

title: Emerging perovskite photochromic materials: recent advances, mechanisms, and applications
abstract: Ionic-doped inorganic photochromic (PC) materials have garnered increasing research interest due to their diverse PC properties.

title: Thermal stability of hyper-doped n-type Ge and Si 0.15 Ge 0.85 epilayers obtained by in situ doping and pulsed laser melting
abstract: Pulsed laser melting enables high electron activation in Ge and SiGe epilayers with the latter exhibiting enhanced thermal stability. Our findings highlight the trade-off between the activation level and dopant resilience.

title: A highly sensitive, biodegradable capacitive humidity sensor with aerosol jet printed electrodes on a self-standing CMC film
abstract: This study employs a facile approach for creating a single-layer flexible humidity sensor using a free-standing CMC bioplastic film, which offers both biodegradability and a rapid, sensitive response.

title: Enhanced photo-assisted lithium-ion batteries using natural dye-impregnated LiFePO 4 cathodes
abstract: We introduce a novel photocathode for photo-assisted LIBs by incorporating natural turmeric dye into LiFePO 4 via an impregnation method. The photocathode enhances capacity by 9.9% (charge) and 7.7% (discharge) at 0.5C under light.

title: Temperature-driven molecular dynamics initiate thermochromism and luminescence modulation in an anthracene-based organic semiconductor crystal
abstract: Crystals of 10-bis(( E )-2-(pyridin-4-yl)vinyl)anthracene (BP4VA) show heat-induced chromism and luminescence. Structural changes are described using variable-temperature synchrotron single-crystal X-ray diffraction, microscopy, and molecular modeling.

title: Promoting polar rotation via antimony doping to enhance the pyroelectric performance of lead-free potassium sodium niobate ceramics
abstract: Recently, lead-free pyroelectric ceramics have attracted increased attention in the context of pyroelectric infrared detector (PIRD) applications.

title: High-performance strain sensors using flexible micro-porous 3D-graphene with conductive network synergy
abstract: The flexible strain sensor shows exceptional electromechanical performance and stability under various strain conditions. It accurately captures a range of human activities such as facial muscle contractions, breathing patterns and joint movements.

title: Fabrication of a high-efficiency hydrogen generation Pd/C 3 N 5 -K,I photocatalyst through synergistic effects of planar and spatial carrier separation
abstract: Non-metallic I heteroatoms and metal Pd nanoparticles construct the charge transfer pathways within and across the CN layers, enhancing electron–hole separation.

title: Highly linearly polarized CdSe/CdS/ZnS nanoplatelet-assembled microsphere laser
abstract: Edge-up configuration of nanoplatelet-assembled microsphere lasers enables highly polarized output with low threshold and a high cavity quality factor.

title: Multilevel carbon composite construction of NASICON-type NaVPO 4 F/C/CNT cathode material for enhanced-performance sodium-ion batteries
abstract: NVPF/C/CNT composites with a multilevel carbon structure were synthesized using the sol-gel method. This structure restricted particle growth, increased surface area, and reduced degradation.

title: Growing ferromagnetic Fe–Ni alloy nanoparticles on nanodiamond nanotemplates: the role of sp 2 -type carbon in their development and in the appearance of a martensitic-type phase
abstract: Ferromagnetic fcc Fe–Ni nanoparticles, enveloped by sp 2 graphitic-type nanostructures, are grown on nanodiamonds matrices, which provide the carbon atoms that further diffuse in the metallic Fe–Ni alloy to form a non-extensive martensitic-type phase.

title: Sprout-inspired polypyrrole-integrated electrodes for reduced impedance and enhanced biological compatibility
abstract: Sprout-inspired confined electropolymerization builds bioelectrodes with high conductivity and antifouling. Vertically grown PPy in SBMA hydrogel enhances interfacial integration and electrochemical performance.

title: High- T c Ag x BC and Cu x BC superconductors accessible via topochemical reactions
abstract: Ab initio modelling indicates that a metastable AgBC phase can be accessed via standard topochemical ion exchange and exhibit two-gap superconductivity above 50 K.

title: Exploring novel anti-perovskites X 4 A 2 O (X = K, Rb, Cs; A = Cl, Br, I) with excellent photoelectric performance towards photovoltaic applications
abstract: A theoretical study of the anti-perovskites X 4 A 2 O was conducted based on first-principles calculations. The results show that it possesses excellent light absorption coefficients, with a maximum power conversion efficiency of up to 31.04%.

title: A dual-inhibition aptamer gated OPECT biosensor based on a MOF-derived CAU-17/Bi 2 S 3 Z-scheme heterojunction for rapid detection of bacterial quorum sensing signal molecules
abstract: A dual-inhibition aptamer gated OPECT biosensor based on a MOF-derived CAU-17/Bi 2 S 3 Z-scheme heterojunction was constructed for the rapid and ultrasensitive detection of bacterial quorum sensing signal molecules.

title: K 2 Be 2 P 2 monolayer: a predicted strain-tunable two-dimensional topological insulator exhibiting multifunctional properties
abstract: Exploring new two-dimensional (2D) materials with unique electronic and topological properties is key to developing next-generation electronic devices.

title: MOF-derived NiO/γ-Fe 2 O 3 p–n heterojunctions for ethylene glycol sensing
abstract: A MOF-derived NiO/γ-Fe 2 O 3 heterojunction sensor delivers record EG sensitivity and stability, enabled by engineered defects, mesopores and interfacial charge control.

title: Preferential crystallographic orientation via α-CN stereo-directional effect for superior perovskite indoor photodetectors
abstract: α-CN ligand enables stereo-directional perovskite crystallization, enhancing performance for superior indoor photodetectors and energy harvesting applications.

title: Engineering the dielectric properties of t -HfO 2 thin films using first-principles calculations
abstract: First-principles calculations reveal that the dielectric properties of t -HfO 2 thin films can be modulated by thickness, strain, and interfacial effects, offering pathways toward low-leakage, high-capacitance DRAM devices.

title: Effects of varying chalcogenophene spacer units between indole and benzothiadiazole based D–A–D type semiconducting small molecules on the characteristics of organic field effect transistors (OFETs)
abstract: The introduction of selenophene analogues as alternatives to widely used thiophene derivatives has garnered significant interest in semiconductors for enhancing optoelectronic properties and performance in electronic applications.

title: Fundamentals of NaMnO 2 , the cathode material for Na-ion rechargeable batteries
abstract: Spin-up (red) and spin-down (blue) magnetization density of the two stable NaMnO 2 polymorphs reveal highly anisotropic exchange interactions and AFM-ordered MnO 2 stripes. The magnetically ordered oxygens play a key role in superexchange coupling.

title: Excitation-dependent photoluminescence, conduction mechanisms via impedance spectroscopy, and DRT investigations in Al-doped ZnO ceramics
abstract: Temperature dependent dielectric, polarization vs. electric field loop, distribution of relaxation times, excitation dependent photoluminescence, and absorbance spectroscopy of Al doped ZnO ceramics.

title: π–π-Driven aggregation-induced emissive aza-thiazole: a selective fluorescent sensor for anionic surfactants
abstract: A novel AIE aza-thiazole/TPE hybrid has been synthesized and selectively senses SDS through π-π-driven self-assembly, enabling μ M-level detection via fluorescent test strips.

title: Scalable MBE growth of MoSe 2 /GaTe van der Waals heterostructure for high-speed vis-NIR photodetection
abstract: A type-III (broken-gap) heterostructure based on MoSe 2 and GaTe for high-speed visible-NIR photodetection.

title: Solution-processed LiGdO solid electrolyte for an In 2 O 3 synaptic transistor and its application in neuromorphic computing
abstract: An In 2 O 3 synaptic transistor based on lithium doped Gd 2 O 3 (LiGdO) solid electrolyte was fabricated by a solution process, its basic synaptic functions were realized and its application in neuromorphic computing was demonstrated.

title: Synergistic bio-inspired photocatalytic hydrogen production by chlorophyll derivative sensitized Nb 2 CT x MXene nanosheets
abstract: A novel bio-inspired composite system consists of chlorophyll ( Chl ) and Nb 2 CT x nanosheets is synthesized for synergistic photocatalytic hydeogen production.

title: Towards accelerating the discovery of efficient iridium( iii ) emitters using a novel database and machine learning based only on structural formulas
abstract: A new database of experimental optical properties of 1287 iridium( iii ) complexes was used for a simple and accurate prediction of efficient iridium emitters.

title: Benzo-extended N^N^N-chelated tetracoordinate boron hetero[8]helicene featuring an inner N–B–N helical rim for circularly polarized TADF
abstract: A π-extended tetracoordinate boron [8]helicene, Hel-BNN, was synthesized, exhibiting efficient TADF and pronounced chiroptical properties. A sensitized OLED using it exhibited an EQE max of 21.9% and displayed circularly polarized electroluminescence.

title: Inverting singlet–triplet gaps by design
abstract: Inverted singlet–triplet (INVEST) emitters offer a promising path toward efficient, metal-free organic light-emitting diodes (OLEDs) by enabling exothermic reverse intersystem crossing.

title: A thermochromic material VO 2 -based metamaterial device for efficient temperature-adaptive radiative cooling
abstract: Emissivity spectra of a temperature-adaptive radiative cooling emitter based on vanadium dioxide metamaterials in the solar irradiation and atmospheric window bands.

title: Nominal kagome antiferromagnetic Mn 3 Sn: effects of excess Mn and its novel synthesis method
abstract: The magnetic structure of Mn 3 Sn depends on the Mn content. We grew more stoichiometric Mn 3+ x Sn 1− x ( x = 0.01) single crystals using Bi flux and observed the sharpest magnetic phase transition along with the emergence of multi-k helical spin orderings.

title: A flexible piezoelectric sensor based on a piezoelectric composite film with high sensitivity and excellent thermal stability for multi-scenario applications
abstract: The high-temperature flexible composite film consists of BS–PT–SBT piezoelectric ceramic particles and PI matrix. It shows a high sensitivity of 6.56 V N −1 , and can further serve as a functional component in multi-scenario sensing applications.

title: Achieved excellent energy storage performance under moderate electric field in BaTiO 3 -modified Bi 0.5 Na 0.5 TiO 3 -based lead-free ceramics via multiple synergistic design
abstract: The improved ESP via synergistic optimization strategy.

title: Enhanced piezoelectric output performance in flexible polyvinylidene fluoride foam with a hierarchical dual-pore structure
abstract: A hierarchically porous polyvinylidene fluoride piezoelectric foam with two different types of pores was created by the composite gel-filled foam templating process to achieve better mechanical flexibility and piezoelectric output performance.

title: NIR-to-NIR ratiometric and lifetime based luminescence thermometer on a structural phase transition in Na 3 Sc 2 (PO 4 ) 3 :Yb 3+
abstract: Thermally induced structural phase transition in Na 3 Sc 2 (PO 4 ) 3 :Yb 3+ can be used for the development of the ratiometric luminescent thermometer.

title: Optimized dielectric performance in bismaleimide–triazine resin via a dual-modification strategy for high-frequency electronic packaging
abstract: High-speed and high-quality signal transmission in the fifth-generation mobile communication system poses significant challenges for the dielectric properties and operational reliability of existing electronic packaging materials.

title: Synergistic influence of anisotropy and dipolar interactions on the self-heating efficiency of magnetic nanocomposites
abstract: A linear correlation between anisotropy energy and heating efficiency appears under controlled dipolar interactions, while strong dipolar regimes cause non-monotonic heating, challenging standard theoretical models.

title: Development of two-dimensional MXene nanosheet-based electrodes via screen printing for low-frequency antenna applications
abstract: A scalable screen-printed MXene-based monopole patch antenna shows tunable frequency response, stable performance, and enhanced radiation efficiency in the low-frequency range, promising for next-generation RF applications.

title: Reservoir computing determined by nonlinear weight dynamics in Gd-doped CeO 2 /CeO 2 bi-layered oxide memristors
abstract: Reservoir computing (RC) using a Pt/Gd-doped CeO 2 /CeO 2 /Pt memristor is systematically investigated, where its time-dependent weight updates and nonlinear decay characteristics are critical for extracting spatiotemporal features in RC applications.

title: Synthesis and application of isoindigo quaternary ammonium salts in copper plating
abstract: Five novel isoindigo-based levelers were synthesised for the first time. The mechanism of levelers acting through physical and chemical adsorption is proposed. The effect of leveler concentration on the filling properties was investigated.

title: A high-performance fluorophore-decorated bimodal scaffold as a target-specific ratiometric sensor and extractant for aluminum and mercury ions
abstract: A blue-emissive ligand (PTA) was developed via a one-pot sonochemical method for efficient cation sensing. PTA serves as a colorimetric and ratiometric probe towards Al 3+ and Hg 2+ in both solid and liquid phase.

title: Novel 2D-ferroic nanocomposite anti-reflection screen-printed films for EMI shielding: an experimental and theoretical study
abstract: A high-performance electromagnetic interference (EMI) shielding material, based on mulberry paper and fabricated using a screen-printing technique, has been developed to achieve excellent shielding effectiveness.

title: Tuning the band gap and structure from wide gap SrBi 3 O 4 Cl 3 to narrow gap Bi 4 O 4 SeCl 2 by aliovalent anion substitution
abstract: Aliovalent substitution can generates a complex solid solution between SrBi 3 O 4 Cl 3 and Bi 4 O 4 SeCl 2 with very different band gaps, leading to a nearly isolated Se p band and two distinct band-gap like onset energies in intermediate compounds.

title: Robust phase-separated supramolecular eutectogels regulated by triple non-covalent cross-linked networks for the construction of recyclable electronics
abstract: Robust phase-separated supramolecular eutectogels regulated by electrostatic interactions, hydrogen bonds, and metal coordination triple non-covalent interactions are successfully constructed.

title: Ferroelectric heterointerface control of spin polarization in a Janus antiferromagnet and its application in multistate storage
abstract: The multiferroic tunnel junction based on the Sc 2 CO 2 /Mn 2 ClF/Sc 2 CO 2 vdW multiferroic heterostructure, which realizes the function of four-state information storage.

title: A first-principles study on antiferromagnetic VYNF 2 with flat robust bands, valley splitting, and an out-of-plane piezoelectric response
abstract: Valley splitting is observed between K and −K points in band structures (GGA+U+SOC) of VYNF 2 . The flat valence bands lead to a robust band gap against strain, where the mechanism behind the flat valence bands is explicated by tight-binding model.

title: A novel metal–semiconductor SERS self-cleaning system with synergistic effects for highly sensitive detection of pollutants
abstract: The components of the Au NU/TiO 2 @ZnO composite self-cleaning SERS substrate maintained their respective advantageous properties and were able to interact with each other, which improved overall SERS performance and photocatalytic degradation ability.

title: Surface plasmon resonance modulation toward efficient transparent perovskite light-emitting diodes
abstract: Self-assembled silver nanoparticle (AgNP)-based surface plasmon resonance (SPR) layers are developed to facilitate the light extraction of transparent PeLEDs (TPeLEDs), enabling an average transmittance exceeding 66% and a record total EQE of 18.6%.

title: The role of flexible amide spacers in the self-assembly of star-shaped triphenylbenzenes with photoactive triphenylamine units: stimuli-responsive liquid crystals and gels
abstract: Divergent self-assembly of star-shaped molecules differing only in the length of the flexible spacer: stimuli-responsive liquid crystals and gels.

title: Nd 3+ -doped 2D perovskite scintillators with ray-type-specific response via fragment-induced crystallization
abstract: This study introduces a fragment-induced crystallization method to overcome burst nucleation in neodymium-doped 2D perovskites, achieving efficient doping (0.04–3.43%). The doped single crystals exhibit an enhanced ray-type-specific response.

title: Interface-tailored ZnO/BDPQ-Oct inorganic/organic dual sensors for improved light/gas detection and artificial intelligence-enabled gas recognition
abstract: Exfoliating organic crystalline clusters from the ZnO nanorod layer forms an amorphous organic/crystalline inorganic heterojunction, which enhances light-sensing performance by improving carrier diffusion and reducing the optical screening effect.

title: Jahn–Teller distortion induced two-dimensional ferroelasticity in Mn 2 CuO 6 monolayers with antiferromagnetic ordering
abstract: The rich interplay among the electron spin, orbital ordering and lattice deformation in Mn 2 CuO 6 monolayers will lead to stable 2D ferroelasticity and magnetic orderings.

title: Adjacent compensated codoping (alloying) of semiconductor films and its application in CdTe and CdS
abstract: Adjacent compensated codoping (alloying) is presented as an alternative approach to modify the electrical and optoelectronic properties of semiconductors.

title: Design of a photocatalyst combining graphdiyne–Cu/NiCrO 3 with Cu as an interfacial charge-transfer bridge and investigation of its photocatalytic hydrogen evolution performance
abstract: The electrons move from graphdiyne to Cu due to its higher Fermi level, then react with NiCrO₃ valence band vacancies. NiCrO₃ conduction band electrons reduce H₂, while graphdiyne holes oxidize TEOA.

title: Enhanced multifunctional broadband artificial vision through integration of ReS 2 phototransistors with embedded electrodes
abstract: This paper presents a ReS 2 optoelectronic synapse device designed to mimic the human visual system and artificial neural networks.

title: Blue-emitting iridium( iii ) phosphors with functional imidazo[4,5- b ]pyridin-2-ylidene cyclometalates: designs aimed at greater steric hindrance
abstract: Ir( iii ) carbene complexes bearing bulky 2-xylenyl substituents were synthesized and utilized in OLED fabrication. The f-ct9ax-based hyper-OLED exhibited narrowband blue emission with a maximum EQE of 31.9%, and an EQE of 20.9% at 1000 cd m −2 .

title: Contorted graphene nanoribbons from vat dyes: synthesis, properties and charge carrier mobility
abstract: Vat dyes represent an interesting class of building blocks for organic electronics as they are readily available at low cost and easy to functionalize.

title: Zn 2 GeO 4 :Cr,Mn phosphor for visible to NIR luminescence-based applications – a theoretical and experimental investigation
abstract: Zn 2 GeO 4 (ZGeO) phosphor exhibits a wide bandgap energy, making it highly suitable for luminescence-based applications spanning the entire electromagnetic spectrum, from ultraviolet (UV) to near-infrared (NIR) wavelengths.

title: Effect of germanium substitution on the structure and ionic conductivity of the hexagonal perovskite derivative compound Ba 3 WVO 8.5
abstract: A series of hexagonal perovskite derivative compounds Ba 3 WV 1− x Ge x O 8.5− x /2 ( x = 0, 0.1, 0.2, and 0.3) were synthesized using a solid-state reaction route and characterized by powder XRD, SEM-EDX and dielectric spectroscopy.

title: Retraction: Using van der Waals heterostructures based on two-dimensional InSe–XS 2 (X = Mo, W) as promising photocatalysts for hydrogen production
abstract: Retraction of ‘Using van der Waals heterostructures based on two-dimensional InSe–XS 2 (X = Mo, W) as promising photocatalysts for hydrogen production’ by Jiaming Ni et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2020, 8 , 12509–12515, https://doi.org/10.1039/D0TC02874F.

title: A silicon-based InGaN/GaN microbelt blue light-emitting-diode fabricated via low-temperature direct bonding
abstract: Integrating a nitride semiconductor light source on Si is a key to building wide-spectrum photonic systems; however, there have been many challenges to growing nitride semiconductors directly on a Si substrate.

title: Correction: A low-hysteresis, self-adhesive and conductive PAA/PEDOT:PSS hydrogel enabled body-conformable electronics
abstract: Correction for ‘A low-hysteresis, self-adhesive and conductive PAA/PEDOT:PSS hydrogel enabled body-conformable electronics’ by Qiang Gao et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2023, 11 , 9355–9365, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TC00850A.

title: Breathing-induced high-temperature negative thermal quenching and room-temperature enhancement of luminescence in CuI–NC-based MOFs
abstract: A CuI nanocluster-based metal–organic framework is reported, which exhibits high-temperature negative thermal quenching and room-temperature enhancement of luminescence induced by breathing-like single-crystal to single-crystal transformation.

title: Boosting room-temperature thermoelectricity in SrTiO 3 -based superlattices
abstract: Introducing tensile strain into STO-based superlattices increases the ferroelectric transition temperature, leading to phonon softening at elevated temperatures, which in turn boosts their dimensionless figure of merit ( ZT ) to 1.2.

title: Ultrastable InP@Al 2 O 3 nanocomposites with UV curable function for application in white LEDs
abstract: This work is mainly focused on the stability issue of InP quantum dots and proposes corresponding solutions to achieve stability that far exceeds that of other related products. And UV was used to repair its fluorescence loss.

title: Material-engineered self-compliant memristor enabling multibit synaptic learning and in-memory computing
abstract: A self-compliant Pt/Al/TaO x /Al 2 O 3 /SiO 2 /AlN/Pt memristor was developed for multibit synaptic learning and in-memory computing. Engineered layers enable stable switching, enhanced retention, and suppressed variability for neuromorphic computing.

title: Kinetic swelling-driven PTG interlayers for enhanced crystallinity and charge transport in polymer OECTs
abstract: Engineering a PVA-based interlayer substantially enhances OSC crystal growth. This results in advanced synaptic functions and a 7.2-fold performance increase in organic transistors for neuromorphic systems.

title: Bulk photogalvanic current control and gap spectroscopy in 2D hexagonal materials
abstract: Monochromatic elliptical pulses generate bulk photogalvanic currents in 2D hexagonal materials. Ab initio simulations show their dependence on pulse parameters and material properties, enabling photocurrent-based spectroscopy.

title: Achieving circularly polarized room-temperature phosphorescence inversion via a supramolecular co-assembly strategy
abstract: The inversion of circularly polarized room-temperature phosphorescence and a high dissymmetry factor were achieved in a ternary supramolecular co-assembly system. A temperature-controlled CP-RTP switch and logic gate have been realized.

title: Stability and performance improvement of cesium tin halide perovskites with astaxanthin treatment
abstract: Due to effective coordination, hydrogen and electron donation activities, a high PCE of 10.5% and excellent stability after 1000 hours of storage were achieved in an astaxanthin treated PSC.

title: Non-volatile capacitive memory based on spiropyran-derived copolymers for multi-level and ultralow-power data storage and protection
abstract: A capacitive memory with a pPFPA x - co -SP y dielectric layer achieves an eight-level storage capability, ultra-low power consumption of 1.91 × 10 −10 W, and multilevel data encryption, showcasing vast potentials in smart electronics.

title: Self-healing of defects in uniaxially aligned semiconducting polymer crystals via molecular doping: insights into crystallization from transient vs. settled amorphous phases
abstract: The self-healing of defects and enhanced crystallinity are observed in uniaxially aligned poly(3-hexylthiophene) (P3HT) crystal via molecular doping with F4TCNQ.

title: Theoretical advances and future perspectives of all-inorganic germanium-based perovskites
abstract: Several characteristics of all-inorganic germanium-based perovskites that exhibit potential in theory.

title: Tunable spin transport and spin-dependent Seebeck effect in boron-based two-dimensional MBene transition metal compounds
abstract: The high Curie temperatures, magnetoresistance, dual spin-filtering effect, and perfect spin-dependent Seebeck effect demonstrate the potential of M 2 B (M = Sc, Ti, and V) as a promising material for spin caloritronics.

title: Lightweight flexible self-powered photo-supercapacitors with good stability through photoelectrochemical deposition of tellurium on PPy–V 2 O 5 films as a new visible light active dual photoelectrode
abstract: Lightweight flexible solid-state photosupercapacitors (FSSPC) with two identical Te@PPy–V 2 O 5 photoelectrodes showed good performance and maintained functionality under different bending angles. They also demonstrated stability from −10 °C to 50 °C.

title: Stabilizing a cis viologen via co-crystal engineering: electric and magnetic fields are in action to confirm no π-mer formation
abstract: This article investigates stabilization of a non-planar cis -viologen analogue via a designed co-crystallization method and comparison of electrical conductivity with the trans analogue.

title: Eu 2 Pt 7 MP 4− x (M = Cr, Fe) phosphide platinides, intrinsic heterostructures with a high degree of tunable magnetic anisotropy for spintronics applications
abstract: Eu 2 Pt 7 MP 4− x -type structures as a unique platform for the study of complex magnetic interactions and the design of magnetic materials with tunable properties.

title: Vanillin-grafted organosilicon backbone polyimide resins with low dielectric, reprocessing and monomer recovery properties
abstract: In this work, a series of V-Si@PI- x was prepared with low dielectric properties and recyclable characteristics that can satisfy the microelectronics industry and sustainable development strategies.

title: Super-wide-range tunable emission across NIR-II and NIR-III achieved by B-site cation co-substitution in Ni 2+ -doped double perovskites for NIR light sources
abstract: Super-wide-range emission spectrum tuning was achieved in the Ni 2+ doped double perovskite structure by crystal field engineering of cationic substitution.

title: External field-engineered tunable chern number and valley-polarized quantum anomalous hall effect in Ti 3 S 3 Te 2 monolayer
abstract: Quantum anomalous Hall (QAH) insulators with tunable Chern numbers have excellent application prospects in spintronics.

title: Engineering charge transfer doping of MoSe 2 monolayers via epitaxial orientation-dependent surface electron accumulation in high- κ dielectric Gd 2 O 3 thin films
abstract: An effective strategy for engineering doping concentration in TMDCs by utilizing epitaxial surface orientation of underlying dielectric layers promoted by oxygen vacancies on high- κ dielectric substrate surfaces.

title: Cl-doped FAPbBr 3 single crystals with stabilized lattice and suppressed surface vacancies for environmentally stable photodetectors
abstract: The doping strategy enhances lattice bond energy, improves the environmental stability of crystals, and prevents the formation of surface vacancies.

title: Precision design for high-performance TADF emitters with novel interlock D–A frameworks
abstract: Achieving high-performance organic light-emitting diodes (OLEDs) remains a significant challenge, driven by the complex interdependence of charge transport, exciton dynamics, and light-emission processes.

title: Heat-dissipation regulation for improving the thermal stability and efficiency of planar perovskite solar cells
abstract: We incorporated 2D g-C 3 N 4 nanosheets into the perovskite layers to enhance heat-dissipation effect by providing thermal-transfer pathways. The target device achieved an increased power conversion efficiency of 24.19% and pronounced thermal stability.

title: Zincophilic MOF with N-functional groups for interfacial modification of stable aqueous zinc metal anodes
abstract: A two-dimensional zinc-based MOF (ZHPCA) was synthesized and employed as the interface modification layer of Zn anode, and the N-functional groups in ZHPCA can be used as binding sites for zinc ion deposition.

title: A new helical InSeI polymorph: crystal structure and polarized Raman spectroscopy study
abstract: One-dimensional InSeI helical polymorph, with a complex unit cell and directional optoelectronic properties, whose response to linearly polarized light allows identification of the orientation of the van der Waals helical chains from bulk to flake.

title: Microfluidic synthesis of monodispersed sharp emitting perovskite CsPbBr 3 quantum dots via multidimensional parameterization
abstract: A microfluidic chip is employed for continuous synthesis of CsPbBr 3 perovskite quantum dots, which feature tunable emission across various dimensions and wavelengths, and effects of multidimensional parameters on their properties are investigated.

title: In situ growth of Te-doped Cs 2 SnCl 6 scintillator composite films for X-ray imaging
abstract: Cs 2 SnCl 6 :Te/PMMA CFs were obtained by in situ self-assembly with high dispersion and crystallinity. It exhibited high PLQY and were able to easily capture high-resolution images of an internal structure in X-ray imaging.

title: Low-temperature liquid-crystalline nitroxide radical
abstract: A design strategy based on the conformational distribution gave a low-temperature liquid-crystalline nitroxide radical. Its tractability and chemical durability will make it a standard paramagnetic liquid-crystalline material.

title: Luminescence and upconversion properties of a novel ultraviolet-C emitting phosphor: Rb 2 NaYF 6 :Pr 3+
abstract: Rb 2 NaYF 6 :Pr 3+ emits ultraviolet-C under X-ray, UV, or visible light, offering strong potential for disinfection, sterilization, and DNA-deactivation.

title: Rigid short-chain cross-linking networks for high energy density and improved charge–discharge efficiency in fluoropolymer ferroelectrics
abstract: Our work demonstrates a rigid short-chain cross-linked network in fluoropolymer ferroelectrics that significantly enhances breakdown strength, charge–discharge efficiency, and discharge energy density.

title: Dual-Schottky heteroepitaxial Nb:SrTiO 3 /TiN/Si for high-performance broadband photodetection across the UV–IR spectrum
abstract: This work achieves balanced UV–IR photodetection via heteroepitaxial dual-Schottky Nb:SrTiO 3 /TiN/Si. The TiN interlayer enables high-quality Nb:SrTiO 3 growth for UV absorption and induces LSPR to boost plasmonic IR absorption.

title: Rationally designed imidazo[1,2- a ]pyridine based AIEgens for non-doped OLEDs with high efficiency and low-efficiency roll-offs
abstract: Intricate interplay between electronic conjugation and reinforced distortion within the imidazo[1,2- a ]pyridine scaffold represents a unique approach to develop newer classes of highly efficient electroluminescent materials for OLEDs.

title: Highly stable nickel metal-modified black phosphorus-based photodetectors with enhanced magnetic field-assisted photoresponse
abstract: The unique physicochemical properties of black phosphorus endow it with great potential for application in the field of photodetection, but its stability problem has been hindering its further development.

title: Mitigating efficiency loss in thick-film organic solar cells by suppressing charge recombination and energetic disorder
abstract: By incorporating 0.3 vol% DBE into a PTzBI-dF:L8-BO to improve morphological order and charge transport properties, we achieved a PCE of approximately 16% in a 300 nm thick active layer, with a fill factor of 70.73%.

title: Multi-channel information encryption based on the structural color and photoluminescence capability of cellulose nanocrystal flexible films
abstract: In this study, a multi-channel information encryption system integrating structural color, photoluminescence and chiral optical properties has been created based on a ternary flexible composite film of cellulose nanocrystals.

title: Highly flexible honeycomb-structured flexible transparent conductive electrodes by inkjet printing for light-emitting devices
abstract: The incorporation of flexible transparent conductive electrodes as the core of flexible light-emitting devices expands the potential application scenarios of wearable devices, due to their bendable characteristics.

title: The intriguing case of cyclic triimidazole: an emerging scaffold for the preparation of multiemissive, bio-medical and hybrid inorganic–organic materials
abstract: Cyclic triimidazole, TT , is an emerging scaffold to prepare multiemissive organic and coordination systems to be exploited in various fields. TTs with dual emission, RTP from aqueous aggregates, mechanochromism and vapochromism are discussed here.

title: Stimuli responsive behavior in a D–A–D organic fluorophore: the role of cold crystallization
abstract: DTBTE is a new fluorophore that exhibits luminescence changes due to transitions between crystalline and amorphous states. The combination of DFT calculations, X-ray analysis and advanced spectroscopy provided insights into this transformation.

title: Achieving high response of perovskite-based (MAPbI 3 ) ammonia gas sensors at room temperature via light enhancement
abstract: Light illumination improves the performance of resistive NH 3 gas sensors utilizing the metal halide perovskite MAPbI 3 .

title: Enhanced device performance through optimization of acceptor layer thickness relative to exciton diffusion length and ionization energy offset in bilayer organic solar cells
abstract: Simultaneous optimization of non-fullerene acceptor layer thickness relative to exciton diffusion length and ionization energy offset is critical for enhancing the performance of bilayer organic solar cells.

title: In situ passivation of the buried interface in perovskite solar cells using a SnO 2 –PACl composite electron transport layer
abstract: The incorporation of PACl effectively passivates the defects in the ETL while simultaneously slowing the crystallization rate of the perovskite, ultimately achieving a PCE of 24.39%.

title: Benzothiazole-based arylamines as hole transporting materials for perovskite solar cells
abstract: In this study, novel A(–π–D) n type small molecule HTMs based on benzothiazole (as acceptor) and arylamine (as donor) building blocks ware synthesized for application in perovskite solar cells, achieving a power conversion efficiency of 20.74%.

title: The self-assembly of polyacrylic acid nanoparticles induced by non-covalent interactions enhances the response of molecular fluorescent probes to formaldehyde
abstract: Advanced visual colorimetric reagents and paper sensors have been developed by constructing a fluorescent supramolecular polymer architecture PAA@NBHN, to meet the requirements of environmental monitoring.

title: Correction: Electrorheological elastomer for simultaneous enhancements in durability and micro-vibration suppression
abstract: Correction for ‘Electrorheological elastomer for simultaneous enhancements in durability and micro-vibration suppression’ by Sai Chen et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, 13 , 6842–6850, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TC00023H.

title: Realizing high-performance and stable organic field-effect transistors by a cost-effective carbon dot-modified silver electrode strategy
abstract: A low-cost carbon dot-modified silver electrode strategy enables stable and integration-compatible OFETs with excellent performance and storage stability, offering a promising route for scalable flexible electronics.

title: Space charge-induced electrofluorochromic behavior for C12-BTBT-based thin-film devices
abstract: Electrofluorochromism of C12-BTBT OFETs is realized and modulated by space charge regulation in dielectrics, enabling optical-electrical dual output characteristics.

title: The role of fluorine substituents in the formation of the ferroelectric nematic phase
abstract: We report on a series of materials which exhibit a range of nematic phases including the rare example of a single compound which shows both enantiotropic N X and N F phases.

title: Progress in the fabrication of high-purity semiconducting carbon nanotube arrays
abstract: High purity and density of s-SWCNT arrays achieved through different methods enable a performance close to the expected device outcomes in FETs.

title: Bio-inspired helical EGaIn/TPU conductive fibers fabricated by a twisted coating method for flexible electronics
abstract: The prepared HETFs have excellent strain sensing performance, can be woven into smart fabrics to monitor human movement and transformed into a stretchable electrode, DHETFs can be used as a non-contact sensor to monitor a finger in proximity.

title: Alkyl-chain engineering of bifunctional amino acids in optoelectronically superior lead-free copper-based perovskite Cs 3 Cu 2 I 5
abstract: Theory-guided bifunctional amino acid alkyl-chain engineering overcomes surface defects in green copper-based lead-free perovskite Cs 3 Cu 2 I 5 , significantly boosting optoelectronic performance and ambient stability.

title: Increasing the Li-TFSI doping concentration in Spiro-OMeTAD enables efficient and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: A Li-TFSI/12-crown-4 chelate not only enhances the Li-TFSI solubility, increasing doping concentration from 50 to 80 mol%, but also inhibits Li + migration, hygroscopicity and pinholes. Ultimately, PSCs achieved a PCE of 23.99% and improved stability.

title: Dynamic colorimetric sensing with all-dielectric metasurfaces governed by bound states in the continuum
abstract: High-sensitivity refractive index (RI) colorimetric sensing is achieved by utilizing the all-dielectric bound in continuum (BIC) metasurface in this work.

title: Multifunctional benzonitrile derivatives with TADF and mechanofluorochromic properties and their application in OLEDs
abstract: Solid-state photophysical properties of D–A–D′ benzonitriles with phenoxazine/carbazole donors show mechano- and thermochromic luminescence driven by polymorph transitions.

title: Polyoxometalate-based self-adhesive hydrogels with both proton conductive and photochromic functions
abstract: Polyoxometalate-based semi-interpenetrating polymer network hydrogels are fabricated and show potential applications in the fields of information storage and motion monitoring.

title: Enhanced X-ray detection of one-dimensional DABCO–NH 4 I 3 perovskites via anisotropic carrier transmission study
abstract: One dimensional metal-free perovskite DABCO–NH 4 I 3 displays anisotropy of carrier transport in different crystal directions.

title: A comprehensive review of laser-induced-graphene for sensor applications: fabrication, properties, and performance evaluation
abstract: This paper reviews the research, application and working principle of laser-induced graphene (LIG) in four fields: energy storage devices, mechanical sensors, gas sensors and biosensors.

title: Metal chalcogenide complex ligands enhance the photoresponse in hybrid graphene/PbS quantum dot photodetectors
abstract: Ligand engineering was employed to enhance charge transfer at graphene/PbS interfaces, boosting carrier transfer efficiency and photodetector detectivity.

title: III–V semiconductor wires for optical switches in solid-state lasers
abstract: This paper reviews the recent advancements of III–V semiconductor wires as optical switches in solid-state lasers, while addressing the impact of factors such as strain and diameter on their electronic structure and thermal conductivity.

title: A low-cost, high-performance, dopant-free phenyl-based hole transport material for efficient and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: Benefiting from the expansion of the central structure, the carrier mobility of HTMs is elevated to 2.56 × 10 −4 cm 2 V −1 s −1 . Thus, the PCE of PSCs with dopant-free HTL is increased from 17.57% to 20.37%.

title: Efficient printable mesoscopic perovskite solar cells without post-thermal annealing processes by introduction of a volatile co-solvent
abstract: This work refines perovskite precursor chemical environment using THF and CHP solvent, achieving 18.29% PCE—among the highest for printable mesoscopic perovskite solar cells with annealing-free films.

title: An eco-friendly sodium-based thermoplastic starch solid electrolyte for energy-efficient and sustainable electrochromic devices
abstract: This work presents an eco-friendly ECD using a sodium-based starch electrolyte, showing 58.5% transmittance change, 6 s fast coloration switching, 11.5 h self-bleaching, and excellent stability for energy-saving applications of ECDs.

title: High thermoelectric performance of BiCuSeO by optimized carrier concentration and point defect scattering through a Cr-induced compositing effect
abstract: This work illustrates the improvement of thermoelectric performance of BiCuSeO oxyselenides through the composite effect induced by Cr addition.

title: Distinguishing photodegradation pathways of organic semiconductors on ITO and Ag electrode contacts using IR reflectance–absorbance spectroscopy with multivariate analysis
abstract: IRRAS with multivariate analysis allows identification of extended oxidative degradation with interchain coupling for fluorene-containing OSCs and distinguishing differences in chemistry at ITO and Ag contacts.

title: Low loss and excellent stability of Zn 0.7 Mg 0.3 TiO 3 ceramics with V 2 O 5 –TiO 2 addition for application in low-temperature co-fired ceramic technology
abstract: Ceramics composed of Zn 0.7 Mg 0.3 TiO 3 are employed in creating microwave devices because of their outstanding dielectric characteristics.

title: Exploring an electrodeposited cobalt-based coordination polymer for the advancement of supercapacitor performance
abstract: We investigate an electrodeposited cobalt and tartaric acid-based coordination polymer for application as an electrode in a supercapacitor device.

title: Plant-derived cellulose nanocrystals as a pre-alignment layer for ferroelectric mesogens in display applications
abstract: An environment benign pre-alignment layer based on cellulose nano crystals have been developed for ferroelectric liquid crystal displays.

title: Quinoidal propylenedioxythiophene dimers for air-stable n-type semiconductors: achieving crystallinity and solution processability
abstract: A quinoidal propylenedioxythiophene dimer exhibited good crystallinity, solution processability, and air-stable n-type organic field-effect transistor properties. The electron mobility was maintained for more than 30 days in air storage.

title: Rapid microwave-assisted synthesis of morphology-controlled luminescent lanthanide-doped Gd 2 O 2 S nanostructures
abstract: Control of the metal ion ratio in a rapid microwave-assisted approach allows for the formation of colloidal lanthanide-doped Gd 2 O 2 S nanostructures of selective morphologies, which show photo- and X-ray excited luminescence.

title: Electron beam co-deposited Bi 1− x Te x
abstract: BiTe-thin films were deposited by electron beam co-evaporation. The influence of the stoichiometry on the Seebeck coefficient, electrical conductivity and the derived power factor is investigated as-deposited as well as after several annealing steps.

title: Conjugated polyelectrolyte with a backbone integrated by benzene and fluorene for mimicking neural synapses and associative learning
abstract: A novel POF-NMI+Br− is synthesized. The as-fabricated Al/POF-NMI+Br−/ITO device has been used to successfully emulate neural synapses and associative learning toward improving an individual's adaptability.

title: The role of terminal and bridge ligands in the molecular upconversion of lanthanide( iii ) 1D coordination polymers
abstract: The upconversion of trivalent lanthanide ions (Ln III ) has gained significant attention in recent decades due to its potential applications in energy conversion and biological imaging.

title: A flexible and self-healable moisture-driven energy harvester based on 2D vanadium pentoxide nanosheets
abstract: Harvesting energy from the surrounding environment holds significant potential for the self-powering of sensors and diagnostic platforms.

title: Comparative study on electroluminescence efficiencies and operational stability of blue TADF materials based on xanthone or triphenyltriazine acceptors
abstract: Using the blue TADF molecule (PytBuCz-XT) containing a xanthone acceptor as an emitter or a sensitizer in OLEDs provides longer operational lifetimes than the control molecule (PytBuCz-XT) containing a triphenyltriazine acceptor.

title: Robust dihydroxyacetone production via photoelectrochemical glycerol oxidation using an oxygen vacant BiVO 4 photoanode
abstract: Oxygen vacant BiVO 4 was proposed for robust dihydroxyacetone production via photoelectrochemical glycerol oxidation and furthermore, the fundamental origin for the advance was systematically elucidated.

title: Retraction: Integration of photovoltaic organic materials into mm-wave technologies: towards self-powered phase shifters
abstract: Retraction of ‘Integration of photovoltaic organic materials into mm-wave technologies: towards self-powered phase shifters’ by Suraj Manikandan et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2024, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TC02828G.

title: Correction: A printable liquid metal–montmorillonite ink for high-resolution stretchable bioelectronics
abstract: Correction for ‘A printable liquid metal–montmorillonite ink for high-resolution stretchable bioelectronics’ by Ziang Cui et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/d5tc02256h.

title: A water-based screen printable silver metal–organic decomposition ink for low-temperature curing of high precision conductive patterns
abstract: A water-based screen printable silver metal–organic decomposition (MOD) ink was developed, featuring a low curing temperature, high conductive accuracy and excellent conductivity, making it a potential conductive ink for flexible electronics.

title: High mobility field-effect transistors produced by direct growth of C 70 single crystals from a solution
abstract: Field-effect transistors (FETs) produced by direct growth of C 70 single crystals from a solution exhibited a high electron mobility of 1.14 cm 2 V −1 s −1 that was more than two orders of magnitude compared to the previously reported C 70 FETs.

title: PEDOT quality: another key factor determining the thermal stability of organic solar cells
abstract: PEDOT quality, a newly identified factor, is found to be pivotal in influencing both the polaronic stability of PEDOTs and the morphological evolution of BHJs during thermal aging, thereby significantly impacting the thermal stability of OSCs.

title: Interface engineering of 2D van der Waals heterostructures for high-temperature optical communication
abstract: We employed hBN encapsulated MoS 2 and WSe 2 /MoS 2 p–n junctions, resulting in enhanced operation stability at temperatures above 500 K. These findings can pave the way for the development of 2D FETs and photodiodes in extreme environments.

title: Evolution of charge pathways through amorphous aluminum–cerium electrode for stable organic photovoltaics
abstract: The amorphous metal electrode as a top contact for organic photovoltaics improves stability by preventing air and moisture permeation. Cerium’s interaction with molybdenum enhances charge conductance, boosting power conversion efficiency.

title: Ferroelectric and field-induced ferroelectric phase formation in atomic-layer-deposited ZrO 2 thin films with TiN electrodes
abstract: This study explores the origin of ferroelectric phase formation in 10-nm-thick ALD ZrO 2 films by analyzing structural and electrical properties as a function of deposition temperature (280–310 °C) and post-metallization annealing.

title: Surface modification of Bi 2 O 3 and preparation of Bi 2 O 3 @Epoxy resin composites: structure, properties and application in γ-ray shielding
abstract: Surface modification of Bi 2 O 3 and fabrication of Bi2O3@epoxy composites for γ-ray shielding.

title: Unraveling the adhesion characteristics of ruthenium as an advanced metal interconnect material using machine learning potential
abstract: This study combines machine learning potential and steered molecular dynamics to provide atomic-level understanding of the adhesion properties of Ru/SiO 2 interfaces.

title: Ultrafast ambipolar switching in electrochromic copolymer thin films of zinc( ii ) tetrakis(4-aminophenyl)porphyrin – 3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene
abstract: Electrocopolymerization of different monomers is a useful approach for developing materials with tailored electrochromic properties and expanded color palettes.

title: Nonlinear optical properties of CsPbI 3 : synthesis, characterization, and application in passively Q-switched lasers
abstract: Halide perovskites, especially CsPbI 3 , have gained for their strong absorbance across the visible to near-infrared spectrum and a unique electronic structure, enhancing their nonlinear optical and optoelectronic applications.

title: Plasma treatment of bismuth-doped MoS 2 with excellent supercapacitor performance
abstract: Bi doped MoS 2 after plasma treatment exhibits excellent electrochemical properties.

title: C1-substituted bipolar spiro host for high-performance organic light-emitting diodes
abstract: High-performance organic light-emitting diodes (OLEDs) are essential for both academic research and industrial applications.

title: Enhanced performance of a-GaO x thin-film transistor photodetectors via in situ hydrogen incorporation using water vapor
abstract: This study uses H 2 O vapor deposition to enhance a-GaO x photodetectors. In situ hydrogen incorporation and vacuum annealing boost responsivity and speed, resolving the responsivity-response time trade-off.

title: Multiferroic metallic monolayer Cu(CrSe 2 ) 2
abstract: Cu(CrSe 2 ) 2 monolayer: a new and rare 2D multiferroic metal suitable for spintronic applications.

title: Carbon nanotube-regulated growth of metal–organic framework nanosheets for enhanced electrochemical energy storage
abstract: A one-step solvothermal strategy synthesizes 2D Ni-Tdc MOF/CNTs composites, where CNTs inhibit nanosheet stacking, facilitate electron transfer, and alleviate electrode deformation.

title: Solving the cellulose I polymorphic structural riddle: disorder in hydrogen bond networks activates diagnostic terahertz dynamics
abstract: Disorder in cellulose hydrogen-bond networks activates distinct terahertz vibrations, enabling direct spectroscopic differentiation of subtle polymorphic structures.

title: Chiral hybrid manganese( ii ) chloride single crystals for achieving second harmonic generation and moderate circularly polarized luminescence
abstract: Heated ( R / S -2-mpip)MnCl 4 ·2H 2 O exhibited obvious the circular dichroism and circularly polarized luminescence.

title: Optimization of photocatalytic activity in transition metal-modified mesoporous silicas: fine-tuning properties to elucidate radical reaction pathways by EPR
abstract: ROS generation mediated by fine tuning of synthesis parameters for photocatalytically active mesoporous materials.

title: Research on the Ga 2 O 3 /ZnGa 2 O 4 mixed-phase films and solar-blind photodetectors prepared directly by annealing Zn alloying Ga 2 O 3 films
abstract: The transition from a single-phase to a mixed-phase thin film forms heterogeneous interfaces that significantly enhance solar-blind photodetector performance.

title: High-performance, narrow-band green-emitting phosphors for white LEDs: recent advances and perspectives
abstract: This review systematically summarizes the selection methods, modulation strategies, enhancement of the thermal stability and quantum efficiency, and multi-applications of green-emitting phosphors with enhanced luminescence performance.

title: Achieving strong low-frequency electromagnetic absorption in tellurium nanowires
abstract: Tellurium (Te) nanowires, synthesized via a hydrothermal reduction strategy, demonstrate exceptional electromagnetic wave attenuation capabilities, attributed to their distinctive one-dimensional morphology and defect-induced polarization.

title: Electronic coupling regulation in MOF derived Mn/Fe co-doped Ni 2 P through Mn/Fe integration for enhanced overall water splitting
abstract: Mn/Fe co-doping modulates the electronic structure of transition metal phosphides (Mn–Fe–Ni 2 P) to form electronic coupling, which enhances the performance and stability of electrocatalytic overall water splitting.

title: Rare-earth doped upconversion-photopolymerization hydrogel hybrids for in vivo wound healing
abstract: We developed a hydrogel controlled by near-infrared light. The NIR light excites nanoparticles to produce upconversion luminescence and polymerizes gelatin monomers into the long-chain compound GelMa in the presence of the photoinitiator LAP.

title: Multi-site occupancy high-efficient CaY 2 ScZrAl 3 O 12 :Cr 3+ ,Ce 3+ phosphors for application in broadband NIR pc-LEDs
abstract: The introduction of Ce 3+ in CYSZA:Cr 3+ improves the optical properties of the near-infrared phosphor.

title: Novel scintillators based on cerium-doped garnets in amorphous silica: crystal quality at the cost of glass
abstract: Low-cost scintillators consisting of single-phase microcrystalline garnets in silica were fabricated using a two-step sol–gel procedure and characterized by time-resolved photo- and cathodoluminescence spectroscopy.

title: Correction: Ordered Co/Ni oxide nanostructures from MOFs: enhancing efficiency in hybrid asymmetric energy devices
abstract: Correction for ‘Ordered Co/Ni oxide nanostructures from MOFs: enhancing efficiency in hybrid asymmetric energy devices’ by Xiaolong Leng et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, 13 , 9653–9666, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TC00922G.

title: Synergistic mixed halide and additive strategy for efficient pure red quasi-2D perovskite light-emitting diodes
abstract: Stable pure red PeLEDs were achieved via a synergistic mixed-halide and TDA strategy, where TDA concurrently passivates defects and anchors halides to suppress spectral redshift for high-performance optoelectronics.

title: Controlling ambipolar OECT threshold voltage through acceptor unit engineering of conjugated polymers
abstract: We demonstrate that the threshold voltage of single-component ambipolar organic electrochemical transistors can be effectively regulated by modifying the electron-withdrawing acceptor units in the conjugated polymer backbone.

title: Multimodal anti-counterfeiting inks: modern use of an ancient pigment in synergy with a persistent phosphor
abstract: Multi-level luminescent, transparent and non-permanent inks for anti-counterfeiting systems and security were developed.

title: High dielectric permittivity sulfonyl-modified polysiloxanes as a dielectric for soft actuators
abstract: High-permittivity elastomers were made by grafting sulfolane groups onto polysiloxane. Tuned polarity, thin films, and UV cross-linking yielded DEAs with 7.2% lateral strain at 14 V μm −1 , enabling reliable low-voltage soft stack actuators.

title: Application and challenges of organic electrochemical transistors in neuromorphic computing: bionic synapse and multi-mode integration
abstract: Application and challenges of E- and D-mode organic electrochemical transistors for neuromorphic computing, linking ion–electron coupling to synaptic plasticity and complementary circuits.

title: New frontiers in ceramic composites: tunable electromagnetic interference shielding by realizing negative permittivity in SnO 2 /LaNiO 3 nanocomposites
abstract: The shielding effectiveness and underlying mechanism are presented, demonstrating the tunable EMI response of the conducting ceramic composite (CCC) system.

title: A printable liquid metal–montmorillonite ink for high-resolution stretchable bioelectronics
abstract: A printable liquid metal–montmorillonite ink was developed to enable direct printing of high-resolution, stretchable, and highly conductive patterns for stretchable bioelectronics.

title: A NiCo alloy nanoarray foam with an oriented structure for high-performance electromagnetic interference shielding
abstract: NiCo nanoarray foam prepared via magnetic field-induced deposition: >70 dB shielding in the high-frequency band (18–26.5 GHz), with low reflection/high absorption, facilitating the design of lightweight shielding materials.

title: Low-cost synthesis of soluble bay-substituted terrylenes for p-type charge transport and efficient photodetection on field-effect transistors
abstract: A novel palladium catalyst-free synthesis of highly soluble terrylenes, employed in OFET and OPT devices for the first time achieving high mobility and high photoresponsive metrics.

title: Advanced numerical modeling of multi-absorber Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 /CsSnCl 3 solar cells: unveiling charge dynamics, trap phenomena, and noise characterization of high-efficiency photovoltaics
abstract: High-efficiency solar cells with Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 /CsSnCl 3 absorbers achieving 21.75% PCE. Impedance and noise analyses reveal enhanced charge transport.

title: Engineered semiconductor-dielectric interfaces in polymer ferroelectric transistors
abstract: A comparison of atomic layer deposited Al 2 O 3 on PVDF-based copolymers in polymer transistors shows a significant improvement in the subthreshold swing for PVDF-HFP devices compared with PVDF-TrFE. Al 2 O 3 passivates the interfacial traps.

title: Design of moderate-pressure superconductivity in a ternary hydride system
abstract: Hydrides hold great promise for realizing high-temperature superconductivity, which is a great challenge to achieve at ambient pressure.

title: Recent progress in molecularly tailored organic hole transporting materials for highly efficient perovskite solar cells
abstract: This review explores different paths to replace traditional HTMs, it discusses recent advancements in conjugated organic-HTMs, organic-SAMs and polymeric-HTMs, aiming to improve the efficiency and stability of lead and tin-based PSCs.

title: Fluorination-driven optimization of non-fused small molecular acceptors for high-efficiency organic solar cells
abstract: Organic solar cells (OSCs) represent a promising technology in the renewable energy sector, with non-fused small molecular acceptors (SMAs) emerging as efficient alternatives to traditional fused counterparts.

title: Optimizing CsPbBr 3 nanowires for high-performance optoelectronics: focusing on blue shift and superfast kinetics through amine-rich synthesis
abstract: High-purity CsPbBr 3 perovskite nanocrystals (NCs) and nanowires (NWs) had been synthesized using a hot-injection method within an amine-rich environment.

title: Elastic blue light-emitting diode based on InGaN/GaN microwires and SWCNT-on-PDMS matrix electrode
abstract: Flexible and stretchable light-emitting devices are required for novel optoelectronic applications, including deformable screens and microdisplays.

title: Vertical p–i–p perovskite photoconductors combining intrinsic and doped organic transport layers
abstract: We report fully vacuum-deposited vertical p–i–p perovskite photoconductors with a quantum efficiency >3000%. Combining intrinsic and doped transport layers yields low dark currents and efficient charge extraction for high-sensitivity detection.

title: Self-assembly of size-controlled perovskite crystallite arrays on modulated substrates for laser applications
abstract: A MAPbBr 3 crystallite array was obtained using a poly-TPD film. The perovskite crystals exhibit exceptional laser performance with low laser threshold, narrow FWHM and high Q factor. The impact of grain size on the laser threshold is investigated.

title: Polymer-assisted synthesis of mixed-halide quasi-2D perovskites for tunable blue-green lasers
abstract: A polymer-assisted synthesis method fabricated mixed-halide quasi-2D perovskites with superior lasing properties. It suppressed phase separation, enabling low-threshold blue-green micro-lasers with precise wavelength control.

title: Critical behaviour and magnetocaloric properties of Fe 88 Zr 5 Hf 2 B 4 Cu 1 metallic glass for near-room temperature magnetic refrigeration application
abstract: The escalating global demand for energy has precipitated a rapid expansion of the refrigeration industry, necessitating the development of innovative, sustainable, and economically viable methods.

title: Improving the efficiency of all-polymer solar cells through morphology control via a combination approach: polar solvent additives and external electric field
abstract: This study combines polar solvent additive-induced pre-aggregation with electric field treatment to optimize BHJ film morphology in all-polymer solar cells, improving crystallinity and fibrillar structure to achieve a 35.7% PCE increase.

title: Multifunctional organic ammonium dopants for electron transport layers in efficient inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: Three nonvolatile ammonium salts are doped into PCBM to improve its electrical properties and defect passivation ability. The doping effect varies with the aromatic ring, and the TEAI-doped PCBM devices exhibit the best performance.

title: Flexible OLEDs with graphene electrodes on renewable cellulose platforms
abstract: OLEDs with CVD graphene anodes on cellulose substrates demonstrate an alternative for environmentally friendly, transparent and flexible devices.

title: Colossal barocaloric effect in fatty acid methyl esters
abstract: The structures of MP and MS can be tuned by both temperature and pressure. The barocaloric effect can be obtained by structure change induced by a certain pressure near their phase transition temperatures.

title: Synthesis by size focusing of lithium tantalate nanoparticles with a tunable second harmonic optical activity
abstract: Nonlinear optics at the nanoscale has emerged as a sought-after platform for sensing and imaging applications.

title: Two-step spin-coating of vacancy-ordered double perovskites enables growth of thin films for electronic devices
abstract: A two-step process was developed to spin-coat thin films of the vacancy-ordered double perovskite, Cs 2 TeX 6 . These films enabled characterization of the electronic transport properties of Cs 2 TeBr 6 .

title: High stability modified CsPb(ClBr) 3 @glass@PS for wide color gamut mini-LED backlight displays
abstract: Perovskite quantum dots (QDs) were considered as a new generation of emitters for lighting and displays due to their high photoluminescence (PL) efficiency and pure color.

title: Enhanced hydrogen evolution activity by modifying SrBi 4 Ti 4 O 15 with Pd as a piezo-photocatalyst
abstract: In this study, palladium nanocrystals (Pd-NCs) have been deposited on the surface of SrBi 4 Ti 4 O 15 (SBTO), resulting in the successful construction of an efficient hydrogen evolution catalyst based on the synergistic effect of piezo-photocatalysis.

title: Dynamic and multimodal luminescence of Mn 2+ -doped Mg 4 Ga 8 Ge 2 O 20 persistent phosphor for anti-counterfeiting applications
abstract: Illustration of dynamic multicolor anticounterfeiting application of MGGO:Mn 2+ materials (Mn 2+ content 0.005–0.5).

title: Unveiling the microscopic origins and thermoelectric performance of full-Heusler compounds K 2 RbSb and Rb 2 KSb
abstract: The strong anharmonicity of alkali metal atoms result in extremely low lattice thermal conductivity ( κ L ) for these compounds. These findings reveal the importance of chemical bonding in achieving ultra-low κ L and excellent TE performance.

title: Efficient narrow band pure red emitting molecular europium complexes for white light-emitting diode and anticounterfeiting applications
abstract: The luminescent ternary Eu( iii ) complexes of phenanthro-imidazole derivatives and dibenzoylmethane (DBM), Eu(DBM)3Phen-pCH3-mCF3 and Eu(DBM)3Phen-pCH3-p-CH3, were synthesized and explored for versatile applications.

title: Unveiling the properties, structure, and preparation of monoelemental main group Xenes for enhanced nonlinear optics applications
abstract: This review summarizes the classification, properties, structure characteristics, and bottom-up/top-down synthesis of monoelemental main group Xenes for nonlinear optical performance and applications.

title: The magic methyl effect of thermally activated delayed fluorescent emitters on blue organic light-emitting diodes
abstract: Three new blue thermally activated delayed fluorescent emitters have been designed and synthesized to explore the magic methyl group effect. The methyl group effect on the photophysical and thermal properties and device performance has been studied.

title: Highly UV-responsive indium-free Zn–Sn–Al–O phototransistor for optoelectronic artificial synapses
abstract: Indium-free Zn–Sn–Al–O phototransistors demonstrate exceptional UV responsiveness and effectively mimic synaptic plasticity, accurately replicating experiential learning behaviors, advancing sustainable optoelectronic synaptic systems.

title: Self-induced MBE-grown InAsP nanowires on Si wafers for SWIR applications
abstract: Phosphorus-rich InAsP nanowires are grown on silicon via molecular beam epitaxy. Nanowires demonstrate room temperature photoluminescence at wavelengths as short as 1480 nm which is promising for optical communication and biomedical imaging.

title: Synergistic effects of triplet–triplet annihilation and reverse intersystem crossing in a platinum-based electrochemiluminescent metallopolymer emitter
abstract: Mechanism study of dual ECL of p-PtBTD, driven by singlet and triplet excited states through RISC and TTA pathways.

title: Electro-elastic features of Ca 3 Ta(Ga 0.7 Al 0.3 ) 3 Si 2 O 14 single crystals grown in different atmospheres
abstract: The electrical resistivity of CTGAS crystals grown in a N 2 atmosphere is higher than those of crystals grown in an O 2 -mixed atmosphere. Furthermore, both types of crystals demonstrate good thermal stability in piezoelectric coefficient d 11 .

title: Physical aging of glasses of an organic semiconductor
abstract: Organic semiconductor films and their bulk counterparts demonstrate remarkably similar recovery kinetics. Vapor-deposited films display significantly enhanced resistance to physical aging compared to liquid-cooled films.

title: Solution processed tellurium films for photodetection
abstract: Ensembles of oxide passivated tellurium nanoparticles are synthesized for photodetection in the infrared. These materials improve light absorption without compromising photoresponse.

title: Ultra-thin and thin CrSi films on Si(111): II. Transport and magnetic properties
