abstract: The magnetotransport and magnetic properties of UT and thin CrSi films are investigated. UT films exhibit quantum magnetoresistance (3–30 K) and out-of-plane ferromagnetism (FM), and thin CrSi films exhibit 3D conductivity and weak FM.

title: A high strength and dendrite free low-temperature nanocellulose composite PVA gel electrolyte for zinc ion batteries
abstract: This paper reports a gel electrolyte prepared by introducing C-CNFs into PVA based gel. ZIBs assembled with this electrolyte can stably cycle over 3000 times. At −20 °C, the capacity retention rate reaches 60% after 1200 cycles.

title: Incorporating electron-deficient Cu nanoparticles in photoactive Zr-MOFs for highly efficient amine oxidative coupling with H 2 O 2 photosynthesis
abstract: Incorporating electron-deficient Cu nanoparticles in electron-rich Zr-MOFs for efficiently improving photogenerated charge transport to promote benzylamine oxidation coupled with H 2 O 2 generation under visible-light irradiation.

title: Metal–organic frameworks built from a carborane linker isolating ideal one-dimensional large-spin chains of Co ( S = 3/2) or Ni ( S = 1)
abstract: Bulky 3D carborane-based linkers enable the isolation of 1D antiferromagnetic chains in isostructural (Co and Ni) 3D metal–organic frameworks, opening new pathways for low-dimensional magnetic systems’ design.

title: In situ construction of MnIn 2 S 4 /TpBpy Z-scheme heterojunction composites for photocatalytic reduction of U( vi )
abstract: Z-scheme heterojunction MnIn 2 S 4 /TpBpy was engineered and fabricated for U( vi ) photoreduction. U( vi ) was photo-reduced to UO 2 in the presence of ˙O 2 − and e − reactive species. The generated H 2 O 2 contributed to the deposition of U( vi ) as (UO 2 )O 2 ·2H 2 O.

title: Centimeter-sized anthracene single crystals for low-dose X-ray detection via tailored Bridgman growth
abstract: A tailored Bridgman growth strategy enables large, high-quality anthracene single crystals with ultrafast decay and ultralow detection limits, achieving high-sensitivity X-ray detection for low-dose applications.

title: Tuning luminescence thermal quenching performance of K x Mg x Sc 1.95− x Mo 3 O 12 :5%Eu 3+ phosphor via synergistic negative thermal expansion and charge compensation effects
abstract: The synergistic effect of negative thermal expansion and charge compensation mechanisms allows Eu 3+ ions to retain high luminescence intensity even at elevated temperatures, exhibiting remarkable thermal stability.

title: A novel PIGF system with high thermal conductivity and luminous efficiency based on TeO 2 glass@t-SiO 2 for laser lighting
abstract: A novel PIGF system based on TeO 2 glass@t-SiO 2 for laser lighting was successfully synthesized. The laser damage threshold of 16 W mm −2 resulted in a maximum luminous flux of 1823 lm.

title: Infrared photothermal enhancement of piezoelectricity in γ-glycine/PVA films via targeted interfacial hydrogen bond engineering
abstract: An infrared laser illumination strategy is proposed to strengthen interfacial hydrogen bonds between γ-glycine's carboxylate and PVA's hydroxyl groups via nanoscale photothermal effect, thus promoting dipole alignments and piezoelectric improvements.

title: Suppressed lattice thermal conductivity due to K + ion vibration results in high thermoelectric performance in K 4 ZnAs 2
abstract: By using density functional theory and the Boltzmann transport equation, we herein explored the electronic structure and thermoelectric transport behavior of K 4 ZnAs 2 .

title: Design and mesomorphism of a triskelion shape-persistent HBC-TP hybrid: 2,8,14-tris(triphenylen-2-yl)hexabenzocoronene
abstract: A triskelion-shaped mesogen was synthesized by connecting three radial mesomorphic triphenylenes to a central hexabenzocoronene core via sigma bonds. This compound shows a columnar rectangular phase and possesses ambipolar charge transport behaviour.

title: Molecular engineering of π-extended pyrrolo[1,2- α ]quinoxaline-based D–A materials for electrochromic devices
abstract: Pyrrolo[1,2- α ]quinoxaline, a new class of nitrogen-containing skeleton, offers a wide range of applications in the development of optoelectronic materials.

title: Application of downshifting and antireflection stacked layers synthesized using a wet chemistry method with broad UV excitation for silicon heterojunction solar cells
abstract: Downshifting and antireflection materials enhance the short-circuit current density of the solar cell module while reducing ultraviolet degradation in SHJs.

title: A one-dimensional organic–inorganic hybrid ferroelectric exhibiting a dielectric-optical duple switch
abstract: A one-dimensional organic – inorganic hybrid ferroelectric, (C 4 H 8 NH 2 ) 2 [BiCl 5 ], exhibiting a dielectric-optical duple switch is reported. Theoretical study discloses that the ferroelectricity originates from both organic cations and inorganic anions.

title: Recent advances in multi-field manipulations of the metal–insulator transition in correlated vanadium oxides enabling interdisciplinary applications
abstract: This review systematically summarizes the multi-field manipulations of the metal–insulator transition in correlated vanadium oxides, focusing on their phase transition mechanisms, advantages, and interdisciplinary applications.

title: Luminescent lanthanide coordination polymers for the detection of nitro explosives and efficient identification of latent fingerprints
abstract: An emissive Eu( iii ) coordination polymer (Eu-CP) was designed and assessed for ultra-sensitive and selective rapid detection of nitro explosives and for luminescence-based detection of latent fingerprints (LFPs).

title: Linking the pyramid distortion to luminescence in Sb-based metal halide by best fitted ideal polyhedron characterization
abstract: Pyramid-like structures of [SbCl 5 ] are analyzed by the best fitted ideal polyhedron method to show correlation between distortion and photoluminescent yield.

title: Mie scattering induced a dominant electromagnetic enhancement on the Bi 2 WO 6 @Bi 2 O 3 SERS substrate via submicron-morphology design
abstract: Semiconductors are making significant strides in surface enhanced Raman scattering field, but they face challenges of being hard to match noble metal substrates, as their limited chemical enhancement stems from a low charge transfer contribution.

title: Fiber electronics for wearable interactive systems
abstract: Fiber electronics possess flexibility, light weight, and seamless fabric integration, making them well suited for wearable interactive systems.

title: High-performance photomultiplication-type quantum dot IR photodetectors at low applied voltages
abstract: Narrow bandgap colloidal quantum dots (CQDs) are ideal materials for realizing high-sensitivity infrared photodetectors.

title: All-inorganic lead-free double perovskite Cs 2 NalnCl 6 for fiber-based optical temperature sensing for temperature and safety monitoring
abstract: Double halide perovskites are widely utilized in photocatalysis, photovoltaics, and light emission owing to their nontoxicity and chemical stability.

title: VS 4 –NiAl LDH composite electrodes for next-generation high-performance supercapacitors
abstract: This manuscript reports a symmetric supercapacitor using VS 4 + LDH(5%) with a hierarchical flower flake structure, achieving 6.65 W h kg −1 energy and 598.5 W kg −1 power density, offering a sustainable, non-toxic solution for next-gen energy storage.

title: Tunable dielectric properties of a parylene dielectric layer through surface-modulation by click chemistry
abstract: Ethynyl parylene, fabricated via CVD, modulates dielectric properties through azide/alkyne click reactions. Surface modification increases the dielectric constant, leading to drain current variation in TFTs, enabling its use as a sensor device.

title: Advances in metal oxide semiconductor gas sensor arrays based on machine learning algorithms
abstract: In this article, we summarize the progress of materials, mechanisms and ML-assisted gas sensing data processing for MOS gas sensor arrays, with a view to providing a breakthrough direction for future research.

title: A flexible and ion-conductive hydrogel as an artificial nose for odorous H 2 S detection
abstract: By learning the mechanism of human olfactory epithelium, a flexible and ion-conductive hydrogel is designed as an artificial nose to detect malodorous H 2 S gas at concentrations below human olfactory thresholds.

title: High-throughput growth of organic scintillating single crystal fibers for neutron/gamma pulse shape discrimination
abstract: A mold-embedded growth method is presented for the batch production of organic scintillating single crystal fibers (SCFs). These SCFs can be integrated into a pixelated scintillator array, showing high performance in neutron detection.

title: Unveiling excitation-independent visible photoluminescence of SnO 2 nanodots for precise latent fingerprint identification powered by artificial intelligence
abstract: Various defect states contribute to the visible photoluminescence of SnO 2 nanodots. Their broad photoluminescence has enabled successful identification of latent fingerprints on different substrates, thereby improving forensic analysis.

title: Synthesis of luminescent dinaphthopentacene isomers and their application in OLEDs
abstract: We report on the synthesis of two novel nanographenes, syn - and anti -dinaphthopentacene. This small variation in structure leads to stark differences in structural and photophysical properties as well as OLED device efficiency.

title: Strain-modulated ferroelectricity and ferromagnetism in two-dimensional multiferroics of CuCrP 2 X 6 (X = S, Se)
abstract: Tensile strain enhances the ferroelectric phase stability and ferromagnetic transition temperature of 2D multiferroic CuCrP 2 X 6 (X = S, Se) monolayers, providing ideal candidates for non-volatile memory devices.

title: Enhancing the stability of perovskite solar cells and modules by two-dimensional (PM) 2 PbI 2 Cl 2
abstract: We added (PM) 2 PbI 2 Cl 2 to the precursor solution, inhibiting the formation of the δ-phase at high temperatures and greatly enhancing the stability of perovskite solar modules.

title: Electroactive naphthalimide and naphthalenediimide interlayers for inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: The capacity to access low-dimensional perovskites incorporating electroactive naphthalimide- and naphthalenediimide-based moieties was demonstrated to modify or replace fullerene electron-transport layers in inverted perovskite solar cells.

title: A high-performance solution-processed near-infrared phototransistor with a non-toxic Ag 2 Te colloidal quantum dots/ZnO heterostructure channel
abstract: A high-performance NIR phototransistor with a non-toxic Ag 2 Te colloidal quantum dots/ZnO heterostructure.

title: Insights into quantum cutting and downshifting contributions to near-infrared Yb III luminescence in 1D coordination polymers
abstract: Yb III -doped phosphors are promising near-infrared (NIR) emitters for telecommunications and energy conversion.

title: The isovalent alloying assisted anomalous valley Hall effect in a hexagonal antiferromagnetic monolayer
abstract: Exploring the combination of antiferromagnetic (AFM) spintronics and the anomalous valley Hall effect (AVHE) is one of the most important aspects for developingvalleytronic applications.

title: Synthesis of multifunctional PEDOT-block copolymers by combining controlled and chemical oxidative polymerization for bioelectronics
abstract: This article presents the synthesis of the first EDOT-based RAFT chain transfer agent and a new family of multifunctional PEDOT-block copolymers (PEDOT- b -PMMA, PEDOT- b -PSS and PEDOT- b -PNIPAM) for different bioelectronic applications e.g. OECTs.

title: Optical and electrical characteristics of lead-free Cu-based halide perovskites in electrospun polystyrene nanofibers
abstract: CHPs–PS nanofibers exhibit enhanced optical emission and tunnelling-driven electrical transport, highlighting their potential for next-generation optoelectronic devices.

title: Flexible piezoelectric nanogenerator based on Ti 3 C 2 T x -coated electrospun PVDF–TrFE nanofibers
abstract: Electrospun PVDF–TrFE nanofibers coated with Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene form a flexible nanogenerator with enhanced piezoelectric output driven by interfacial polarization and surface charge transport.

title: A high-efficiency blue-LED-excitable broadband Cr 3+ /Ni 2+ co-doped garnet phosphor toward next-generation spectroscopy applications
abstract: Cr 3+ /Ni 2+ introduces a strong crystal field Ca 3 Al 2 Ge 3 O 12 host to obtain a coordinated energy level position. Heavily doped Cr 3+ selectively transfers energy to Ni 2+ , significantly enhancing the absorption efficiency of blue light.

title: Tuning the liquid crystal behavior of subphthalocyanines: effects of substitution, chirality, and hydrogen bonding
abstract: We demonstrate that the liquid crystal behavior of subphthalocyanine bowl-shaped aromatics is governed by their chirality and the number and nature of peripheral substituents.

title: Experimental and DFT study of boron nitride films grown on SiO 2 /Si substrates via chemical vapor deposition
abstract: The hysteresis phenomena of I – V curves are reduced for graphene devices based on BN direct growth on SiO 2 /Si substrate.

title: 2D hexagonal CuBi x Ga 1− x Se 2 nanosheets for a visible light photodetector
abstract: 2D-hexagonal CuBi x Ga 1− x Se 2 nanosheets synthesized using microwave-assisted synthesis at varying Bi/Ga ratios showed clear hexagonal structures with higher bismuth content and improved reflectance that led to improved photocurrent response.

title: Boosting the long-term stability of all-polymer solar cells by using natural cellulose as an interlayer
abstract: Natural cellulose exhibits exceptional water resistance and interfacial compatibility, optimizing organic/inorganic interfaces to improve the stability of all-polymer solar cells.

title: Nonlinear transport behaviors in anti-aromatic cyclo[ n ]carbon-based ( n = 4 k ) molecular devices
abstract: Anti-aromatic cyclo[ n ]carbon-based ( n = 4 k ) molecular devices all exhibit nonlinear transport behavior and show obvious pattern in their transport properties.

title: Tailoring Ga 2 O 3 epitaxial films on sapphire: impact of gallium ligand precursors and growth temperature using mist-CVD
abstract: The selection of gallium precursors and growth temperatures affects the crystallinity and phase composition of Ga 2 O 3 epitaxial films using the mist-CVD. The gallium halide precursor shows lower carbon impurities and forms high-quality Ga 2 O 3 films.

title: Fabrication of rod-like porous Co@graphene/CNT composites for superior wideband electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: Rod-like porous Co@G/CNT composites achieve ∼10 GHz ultrabroad electromagnetic wave absorption, surpassing most reported counterparts.

title: High-performance n-type stretchable OFETs enabled by molecular engineering of flexible polymers
abstract: Three n-type stretchable polymers (P1–P3) based on azo-BDOPV balanced flexibility and charge transport. Stretchable OFETs retained high performance under 15–50% strain. P3 showed the highest initial mobility (0.52 cm 2 V −1 s −1 ) and strain retention.

title: First-principles understanding of hole mobility and intrinsic transport mechanisms in Sn( ii ) oxides
abstract: Transparent conducting oxides play a key role in optoelectronics, but high-mobility p-type materials remain rare. This work highlights Sn( ii ) oxides as promising candidates based on first-principles calculations.

title: Programmable chiroptical multilayer films assembled from self-healing stretchable elastomers for information encryption
abstract: Programmable chiroptical films with ultrahigh ellipticity and dissymmetry factors were achieved via stretchable elastomer–plasmonic composites for optical encryption.

title: Reversible stress-induced doping and charge trap generation in IDT-BT EGOFETs
abstract: We show that IDT-BT EGOFETs are susceptible to bias induced doping and charge trap generation, but that they nonetheless can achieve a high degree of stability and lifetimes in excess of 100 hours under bias.

title: Photon-avalanche for developing a high-sensitivity 3D-printed optical temperature sensor
abstract: First photon-avalanche-based 3D-printed temperature sensor with high sensitivity (4.9% K −1 ) and low uncertainty (0.32 K).

title: Unveiling the magnetic, magnetocaloric and critical behaviour of melt spun lanthanum iron silicon alloys: a comprehensive study
abstract: This article provides a thorough examination of the magnetocaloric effect (MCE) and critical behaviour of lanthanum iron silicon (La–Fe–Si) alloys, which were produced using arc melting and melt spinning techniques.

title: Enhanced dielectric properties of alternative NO-gas-based SiO 2 films via plasma-enhanced chemical vapor deposition for high-performance indium–gallium–zinc oxide thin-film transistors
abstract: The effects of N 2 annealing on dielectric properties of NO-based SiO 2 films fabricated through plasma-enhanced chemical vapor deposition and device performance of IGZO TFTs with them were investigated underlying the charge-transport mechanism.

title: Retraction: Low energy loss (0.42 eV) and efficiency over 15% enabled by non-fullerene acceptors containing N -bis(trifluoromethyl)phenylbenzotriazole as the core in binary solar cells
abstract: Retraction of ‘Low energy loss (0.42 eV) and efficiency over 15% enabled by non-fullerene acceptors containing N -bis(trifluoromethyl)phenylbenzotriazole as the core in binary solar cells’ by María Privado et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2022, 10 , 13174–13182, https://doi.org/10.1039/D2TC02289C.

title: Ultralow lattice thermal conductivity in double perovskite Cs 2 AgRhCl 6 : the effect of anharmonic phonon renormalization and wave-like phonon tunneling
abstract: The lattice thermal conductivity in a prototypical lead-free halide double perovskite Cs 2 AgRhCl 6 is predicted by incorporating three-/four-phonon scattering processes, phonon renormalization, and dual transport channels.

title: Artificial vision system design and implementation based on BaSrTiO 3 & Nd 2 O 3 composite memristors for efficient pattern recognition
abstract: The artificial vision system based on the principle of a biomimetic vision system is used for digital recognition of optical signals.

title: High-performance meso–macroporous SiO 2 antireflective coatings with enhanced optical and mechanical stability for solar energy applications
abstract: A high-performance meso–macroporous coating for antireflection and antifogging is prepared via a low-cost and scalable sol–gel phase separation method.

title: Citric acid and plasma treated MoS 2 for high-performance supercapacitors
abstract: Plasma treatment of molybdenum disulfide (MoS 2 ) presents several advantages that enhance its performance and broaden its applications.

title: Theoretical insights into PtSSe–SnSSe heterostructures for renewable energy applications
abstract: We present a comprehensive study on the structural, dynamical and optoelectronic properties of PtSSe–SnSSe van der Waals heterostructures in four distinct configurations based on the interaction between chalcogen atoms at the interface.

title: Structural, magnetic, and anisotropic magnetocaloric properties in antiferromagnetic compounds MBaYb(BO 3 ) 2 (M = K, Na)
abstract: Anisotropic magnetocaloric properties of MBaYb(BO 3 ) 2 (M = K, Na) single crystals were studied, showing field-direction-dependent adiabatic temperature change (Δ T ad ) under varying magnetic fields at low temperatures.

title: Effects of the number of methoxy groups in the thiophene π-bridges on the photovoltaic performance of the A–π–A type quasi-macromolecular acceptors
abstract: Modulating the number of methoxy groups in the thiophene π-bridges modifies the A –π– A type quasi-macromolecular acceptors. Employing 3-methoxythiophene as the π-bridge enhances molecular planarity and V OC , achieving a high PCE.

title: A 2D Te/Mxene Schottky junction for a self-powered broadband photodetector with high polarization-sensitive imaging
abstract: The 2D Te/Mxene Schottky junction for self-powered broadband photodetector with high polarization-sensitive imaging, showing high responsivity and specific detectivity, switching ratio, anisotropy ratio of 4.

title: Accelerating the discovery of high-performance nonlinear optical materials using active learning and high-throughput screening
abstract: An open first-principles dataset of second-harmonic generation tensors for known and hypothetical inorganic semiconductors, constructed using active learning, propels the data-driven discovery of nonlinear optical materials.

title: Enhancing energy storage density in lead-free BiFeO 3 -based relaxor ferroelectric ceramics through multiple optimization designs
abstract: This study implements a multiple optimization strategy, enhancing the breakdown strength of BF-BT-based ceramics through improving the material's microstructure and the adjustment of its electronic structure, achieving an ultrahigh energy density.

title: Designing spiro-fused chiral frameworks toward high-efficiency deep-blue circularly polarized thermally activated delayed fluorescence emission
abstract: A spiro-functionalized indolocarbazole donor enables a deep-blue thermally activated delayed fluorescence emitter with high efficiency and strong circularly polarized luminescence.

title: Polymerization interface design in wrinkled ferrite@PPy core–shell composites to boost electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: Wrinkled ZnNiFe 2 O 4 @PPy core–shell composites are designed via interface engineering, achieving exceptional electromagnetic wave absorption (−56.8 dB at 1.5 mm) through optimized impedance matching and synergistic loss mechanisms.

title: Mn-doping method boosts Se doping concentration in Cu 2 S towards high thermoelectric performance
abstract: The Mn, Se co-doping method increases the doping limit of Se. High-concentration Se doping not only optimises the electrical transport but also decreases the lattice's thermal conductivity, resulting in the zT value of 0.97 at 723 K.

title: Defect engineering and carrier dynamics in gallium-doped zinc oxide nanowires for light-emitting applications
abstract: The high doping levels of Ga in ZnO nanowires grown in an oxygen-rich environment quench the V Zn -related green luminescence and lead to the formation of Ga Zn V Zn intraband states responsible for intense orange luminescence.

title: Machine learning-assisted screening of intrinsic rattling compounds with large atomic displacement
abstract: Materials with extreme high/low mean square displacement are discovered using machine learning models.

title: Possible gapless spin-liquid state in the Fe-based halide perovskite Cs 4 BiFeBr 10 derived from the antiferromagnet CsFeBr 4
abstract: CsFeBr 4 possesses antiferromagnetic Fe–Fe zigzag chains with a T N of 12 K. Insertion of Bi atoms into the lattice induced long-range magnetic frustration appeared in Cs 4 BiFeBr 10 with the possible gapless spin-liquid ground state at low temperature.

title: Preparation, color formation mechanisms and hydrophobic properties of anodic zirconia films
abstract: Tunable structural-colored hydrophobic ZrO 2 films were fabricated by AC/DC anodization. Their voltage-dependent microstructure, optical properties, and hydrophobic behavior are explained through thickness, roughness, and microstructure control.

title: Accelerating the prediction of remanent polarization in multicomponent ferroelectrics by using variational autoencoder-based data augmentation
abstract: Using a variational autoencoder to expand the dataset to 20 000, six key descriptors for the remanent polarization of the (K 1− x − y − z Na x Ba y Ca z )(Nb 1− u − v − w Zr u Ti v )O 3 system were identified, enabling accurate predictions for new experimental systems.

title: Conjugated polymers from acenaphthene imide and acenaphthylene imide: significant modulation of optoelectronic properties by different fused-pentagon rings
abstract: Dehydrogenation-oxidation of the pentagonal rings fused in π-systems can create π-extended strong acceptors and their structurally derived conjugated polymers with tunable optoelectronic properties.

title: Amphoteric doping and thermoelectric transport in the CuInTe 2 –ZnTe solid solution
abstract: The solid solution behavior of ZnTe in CuInTe 2 complicates defect analysis.

title: Multifunctional piezoelectric PVDF–Ba 0.97 Sr 0.03 TiO 3 composite films for electrostatic energy storage, bio/force sensing, and optical applications
abstract: A PVDF–BST3 polymer–ceramic composite film with recoverable energy storage density, energy harvesting with varying mechanical loads, stability performance over 50 N loading and unloading cycles, and bandgap.

title: Revealing the one-dimensional (1-D) Heisenberg antiferromagnetic state in pyrochlore α-Cu 2 V 2 O 7 from critical exponent analysis
abstract: The critical exponents of α-Cu 2 V 2 O 7 obtained from the MAP closely matches the theoretical values of ( d : n ) = (1 : 3) estimated using renormalization group theory, revealing that α-Cu 2 V 2 O 7 can be considered a 1-D Heisenberg antiferromagnetic system.

title: Sensors and actuators based on magnetic materials for haptic interfaces
abstract: The increasing advances in magnetic materials have opened new possibilities for developing high-performance haptic sensors and actuators. We provide an up-to-date overview of haptic interfaces utilizing magnetic materials.

title: Effect of heat treatment on the martensite phase and magnetic properties of chemically synthesized Co 2 NiGa nanoparticles
abstract: The reversible martensite ↔ austenite phase transition in Co 2 NiGa nanoparticles is depicted schematically, demonstrating the Curie temperatures of both phases and structural evolution with heating and cooling.

title: Neuromorphic computational artificial synapses based on a BTO/STO memristor under Au ion implantation
abstract: The successful fabrication of artificial synapses is essential for developing highly integrated neuromorphic devices.

title: Natural binders for synchronous management of iodine species adsorption and redox toward sustainable carbon-based perovskite solar cells
abstract: This work proposed a strategy for the simultaneous management of iodine species through adsorption and redox for sustainable HTL-free C-PSCs. As a result, the champion PCE of 19.53% is achieved for the HTL-free C-PSCs.

title: Electrorheological elastomer for simultaneous enhancements in durability and micro-vibration suppression
abstract: Electrorheological elastomers (EREs) with tunable stiffness and damping properties have emerged as advanced smart soft materials.

title: High-performance triethylamine gas sensors based on SnO 2 –Au nanotubes fabricated via an electrospinning method
abstract: Au-doped SnO 2 nanotubes via one-step electrospinning and subsequent calcination as gas sensors show excellent response to 50 ppm triethylamine (TEA) at a relatively low operating temperature (144 °C), good reproducibility and long-term stability.

title: Unraveling low-temperature structural and dielectric characteristics in lead-free bismuth halide perovskites
abstract: Bismuth halide perovskite crystals have garnered a lot of interest lately because of their superior optoelectronic qualities, affordability, and ease of processing.

title: Organic field-effect transistor-based sensors: recent progress, challenges and future outlook
abstract: OFET-based sensors consisting of small molecules or polymers as an active layer have garnered significant attention in recent years owing to their high flexibility and sensitivity, low fabrication cost and excellent substrate conformity.

title: Tailoring chain-packing structure in co-blended aramid composites for high mechanical and insulating performance
abstract: A meta -aramid is blended with a heterocyclic aramid to prepare co-blending paper. Both mechanical and insulating properties are highly enhanced by a strengthened hydrogen bonding network.

title: Inverted resistive switching mechanism in polycrystalline PBTTT-C14 polymer devices based on contact geometry and molecular packing for neuromorphic memory
abstract: Poly[2,5-bis(3-tetradecylthiophen-2-yl)thieno[3,2- b ]thiophene] (PBTTT-C14) based two-terminal devices with hexagonal bottom contact geometry show resistive switching memory from low resistive state (LRS) to high resistive state (HRS).

title: Perovskite materials empower sensors and batteries: environmental health monitoring and flexible wearable innovation
abstract: With the development of the Internet of Things and flexible electronic technology, perovskite materials have become the core driving force for the innovation of self-powered sensors due to their excellent optoelectronic properties.

title: Highly efficient Zn:CuInSe 2 /ZnS quantum dots for near-infrared optical wireless communications
abstract: By adjusting the stoichiometric ratio of the CISe core and doping with Zn 2+ , highly efficient PLQY, near-infrared and environmentally friendly CISe QDs have been successfully synthesized.

title: Deoxyribonucleic acid brick crystals-based memristor as an artificial synapse for neuromorphic computing
abstract: This work presents a high-performance DNA brick crystals-based memristor to improve the resistive switching characteristics of double stranded DNA-based memristors.

title: In situ synthesis of multicolor phosphorescent films of polyacrylamide by regulating the conjugation of guest molecules
abstract: Schematic of the synthesis of 4-Ep@PAM and 9-Ph@PAM, and the actual images of flexible phosphorescent films.

title: Passivation of defects by tetrafluoroterephthalonitrile introduced into MAPbI 3 for high-performance perovskite photodetectors
abstract: Methylammonium lead iodide is widely used in the preparation of photodetectors because of its excellent photovoltaic properties.

title: ZIF-8-assisted aqueous synthesis for highly stable green and blue perovskite phosphors
abstract: ZIF-8-assisted aqueous synthesis for green and blue perovskite phosphors with high stability.

title: Promoting the photocatalytic conversion of carbon dioxide to methanol on molybdenum dioxide using sodium species
abstract: Sodium species on MoO 2 (Na/MoO 2 ) can act as an effective photocatalyst for selectively converting CO 2 into CH 3 OH under visible light irradiation.

title: A metal–organic framework with dual fluorescent emission based on 3-aminoisonicotinate: luminescence thermometry and Fe 3+ sensing in hybrid membranes
abstract: A metal–organic framework with dual fluorescent emission for applications in luminescence thermometry and Fe 3+ detection in hybrid membranes.

title: Bis(phenylethynyl)benzenes enable stable visible-to-ultraviolet sensitized triplet–triplet annihilation upconversion
abstract: Ultraviolet (UV) light is relevant for applications ranging from photochemistry to sterilization and water treatment. Triplet–triplet annihilation upconversion is an attractive process to convert low-power visible into UV light.

title: Superior piezoelectric performance in high- T C sodium–bismuth titanate ferroelectric ceramics through spark plasma sintering
abstract: Textured sodium bismuth titanate (Na 0.5 Bi 4.5 Ti 4 O 15 , NBT) ceramics with a strong (00 l ) orientation (Lotgering factor F of 82%) were fabricated via spark plasma sintering (SPS), which exhibited excellent piezoelectric performance ( d 33 = 35.8 pC N −1 ).

title: A terahertz metamaterial absorber with independently tunable absorbance and bandwidth based on BP neural network optimization
abstract: A metamaterial absorber with independently adjustable absorbance and bandwidth, which is optimized using a BP neural network.

title: Enhanced performance of perovskite solar cells via construction of benzothiadiazole-based hole transport materials utilizing an asymmetric strategy
abstract: Benefiting from optimized molecular co-facial conformation and π–π stacking by using an asymmetric strategy, the B-TPA based PSCs yielded a PCE of 23.2%.

title: The influence of stabiliser concentration on the formation of In 2 O 3 thin films
abstract: This work connects the chemistry of sol–gel solutions with varying stabiliser concentrations to the quality of indium oxide thin films.

title: Heterostructured binary/ternary MoO 3 /Bi 2 MoO 6 metal oxide-based acetone sensing devices relevant to non-invasive disease monitoring
abstract: This study focuses on the fabrication and optimization of chemiresistive sensing devices relevant to non-invasive disease monitoring, specifically diabetes.

title: Emergence of circularly polarized luminescence from achiral o -carborane-based molecules through molecular vibrations coupled with intramolecular charge transfer in solid states
abstract: Circularly polarized luminescence (CPL) were obtained from achiral molecules through stretching vibration of C 1 –C 2 bond coupled with molecular orientations. It caused molecular symmetry breaking, resulting in intense CPL with a larger g lum value.

title: Multi-color emission and temperature promoted luminescence efficiency of stimuli-responsive stoichiomorphic ionic co-crystals
abstract: Stoichiomorphic ionic co-crystals from a single reaction exhibit hetero-aggregation-induced tunable emission (HAITE), rare temperature promoted crystallinity, enhanced emission, and exceptional thermal stability, enabling advanced stimuli-responsive luminescent materials.

title: Silver-adsorbed Ti 0.87 O 2 nanosheets and UV irradiation synergistically improve the energy storage performance of polyvinylidene fluoride-based nanocomposites
abstract: The energy storage performance of PVDF-based nanocomposites was enhanced by the synergistic effect of TiNSs@Ag nanosheets and UV irradiation, effectively suppressing conduction losses under high electric fields.

title: UV-C emitting phosphors of Pr 3+ -doped fluoroborate nano-glass composites for disinfection and as fast scintillators
abstract: Pr 3+ -doped nano-glass composites: efficient UV-C phosphors (PLQY of UV-C band: 25.93%) for disinfection and as fast scintillators (48.18 ns).

title: Structure–property relationship of p -alkoxyazobenzenes as molecular solar thermal phase change material energy storage systems (MOST-PCM)
abstract: We systematically investigated p -alkoxy-azobenzenes with different chain lengths to study their effects on MOST-PCM properties.

title: Perylene bisimide J-aggregates in a polymer matrix: controlling self-assembly and fluorescence properties
abstract: The self-assembly process of tetraphenoxy-substituted perylene bisimide dyes into J-aggregates via hydrogen bonds is investigated in three different polymer matrices resulting in solid-state materials with fluorescence quantum yields up to 28%.

title: Developing bimetallic FeM–organic frameworks based on ferroalloy trinuclear clusters for high-performance supercapacitors
abstract: This study explores a novel strategy for designing multi-metallic metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) with alloy-like properties, significantly enhancing their electrochemical performance and stability.

title: A MOF-embedded multifunctional dielectric layer for an energy-efficient organic memtransistor and brain-inspired computing
abstract: The development of energy-efficient neuromorphic devices has driven interest in alternative computing beyond traditional von Neumann architectures, as they emulate brain-like functionalities by integrating memory and synaptic operations.

title: Memcapacitors in neuromorphic architectures: the mechanisms, challenges, and applications
abstract: This work examines the four fundamental physical mechanisms of memcapacitive devices for synaptic emulation and computational applications, highlighting key challenges and future prospects in neuromorphic hardware.

title: An ultra-stable and high-performing flexible transparent heater using tungsten oxide-covered silver nanowire films embedded in polymer
abstract: AgNW films-covered with WO x embedded in polyimide demonstrate high quality transparent heater performance achieving 197 °C surface temperature within 10 s at an applied voltage of only 5 V.

title: Advances in hyperbolic metamaterial sensors: a comprehensive review
abstract: Harnessing hyperbolic anisotropy: state-of-the-art hyperbolic metamaterial sensors across diverse applications.

title: Enhancing small-area and module device performance in organic photovoltaics through insulating polymer-induced manipulation of active layer morphology
abstract: This study reveals a synergistic effect of incorporating PS, an insulating polymer. It regulates molecular stacking and cuts carrier recombination, boosting device efficiency. Also, its efficacy in module devices shows good scalability.

title: 1D Cs 2 AgBr 3 :Mn 2+ crystals to realize high-resolution X-ray scintillation imaging
abstract: Herein, Mn 2+ doping as an effective method is employed to transform nearly non-luminescent Cs 2 AgBr 3 crystals into strong green luminescent material for high-resolution X-ray scintillation imaging.

title: The fluorescence distinction of chiral enantiomers: a Zn coordination polymer sensor for the detection of cinchonine and cinchonidine
abstract: A Zn coordination polymer was constructed to identify chiral cinchonine and cinchonidine with high sensitivity, selectivity, and reproducibility.

title: High-temperature energy storage capability of flexible polyimide with a fluorinated pendant group
abstract: Lattice distortion caused by the large pendant group contributes to the free volume inside bulk PI. The presence of –CF 3 effectively hinders the diffusion of charge carriers, which results in the enhanced dielectric reliability.

title: Recent advances in conjugated polymer lanthanide-doped upconversion nanoparticles and their biological applications
abstract: This review shows polymer-modified lanthanide-doped UCNPs enhance stability, biocompatibility and multifunctionality, enabling advances in bioimaging, phototherapy, drug delivery and biosensing, and outlines routes toward clinical translation.

title: Fabrication of loss-less La 3 Si 6 N 11 :Ce 3+ phosphor-in-glass color converters using oxygen-free ZIF-62 glass
abstract: An oxygen-free sintering environment afforded by the organic–inorganic hybrid glass ZIF-62 is utilized for the design of a loss-less LSN-in-ZIF-62 glass converter.

title: A fast-response ionogel with switchable conductivity triggered by the photothermal effect
abstract: Smart ionogels that can respond rapidly to light irradiation with switchable conductivity have been designed by introducing graphene nanosheets into a physically-co-chemically cross-linked double-network elastomer matrix.

title: Molecular engineering, synthesis, and atomistic structure–property relationship of indoloquinoxaline-capped small donors for efficient organic solar cells
abstract: Three indoloquinoxaline-capped small-molecule donors are designed and synthesized. Integrated experiment-DFT analysis shows efficient electronic, optical, and charge-transfer properties, highlighting their promise for viable organic solar cells.

title: Self-trapped exciton luminescence of tellurium doped zero-dimensional tetraethyl tin chloride for optical thermometry
abstract: Te 4+ doped (TEA) 2 SnCl 6 MCs were synthisized by solvothernal method for the first time, of which structure was obtained by crystal/DFT. Maximium absolute/relative temperature sensitivities reach 0.57% K −1 and 1 × 10 −2 K −1 for optical thermometry.

title: Engineering the electronic structures and ferromagnetism of Fe 2 C monolayers via surface functionalization
abstract: By surface functionalization of the 2D TMC Fe 2 C monolayer (Fe 2 CTT′, where T, T′ = H, F, Cl, Br), the performance of the Fe 2 C monolayer is significantly improved, and its bandgap is opened, transforming it into a bipolar magnetic semiconductor.

title: Experimental design of (Cs,Rb) 2 Sn(Bi)Cl 6 blue phosphors by cation-substitution-induced lattice strain
abstract: Blue self-trapped-exciton photoluminescence of (Cs x Rb 1− x ) 2 Sn y Bi 1− y Cl 6 perovskites is affected by lattice strain from Cs/Rb and Sn/Bi substitutions, the emission intensity and dynamics correlating with the strain magnitude.

title: Alkylammonium passivation for 2D tin halide perovskite field-effect transistors
abstract: Optimal-sized alkyl ammonium cations passivate A-site defects in 2D Sn halide perovskites, promoting ideal octahedral formation. FETs with these cation additives, having optimal length and multiple functional groups, show enhanced performance.

title: The impact of anionic group arrangement on the optical properties of the arsenate series
abstract: This study shows arsenates' optical anisotropy and nonlinear optical properties depend mainly on [AsO 4 ] group density and arrangement. High-density “Neat Arrangement” maximizes birefringence; symmetric alignments enhance second-harmonic generation.

title: GaAsPN core–shell nanowire-based red microLEDs overcoming the efficiency cliff problem
abstract: We introduce a bottom-up microLED structure based on epitaxial GaP/GaAsPN/GaP NWs arrays grown on low-cost Si wafers. The opto-electrical characteristics of the suggested microLEDs remain with the miniaturization to microscale lateral size.

title: Room temperature synthesized green perovskite nanocrystals with enhanced thermal stability enabled by fluorinated ligand engineering for light-emitting diodes
abstract: FAPbBr 3 PeNCs synthesized via LARP showed superior thermal stability. CF 3 -PEA passivation reduced thermal quenching, retaining 75% PL at 380 K. PMMA encapsulation further improved stability. The champion EL device achieved a maximum EQE of 20.4%.

title: An ion-gating synaptic memristor based on tri-layer HfO x composition regulation
abstract: This study developed tri-layer HfO x /HfO 2 /HfO x memristors with an ion-gating HfO 2 interlayer, enabling precise control over conductive filaments and achieving high consistency and linearity.

title: Multifunctional interface modification to enhance the performance of perovskite solar cells
abstract: PEAI:HEC modification enhanced the hydrophilicity of the poly-TPD surface, promoted HOMO level alignment between poly-TPD and perovskite, yielding a PCE increasement from 17.75% to 20.92%.

title: Silicon phthalocyanine-based n-type organic mixed ionic-electronic conductor in organic electrochemical transistors
abstract: An easily synthesized silicon phthalocyanine with hydrophilic axial substitutions is used to fabricate the first phthalocyanine-based n-type organic electrochemical transistor.

title: Si nanohole array (Si NHA)-based type-I heterojunction for filterless self-powered ultraviolet photodetection
abstract: An Si nanoholes array (NHA, with a period of 300 nm)/SnO type-I heterostructure functions well as a self-powered and filterless UV photodetector and exhibits promising potential for anti-interference UV communication.

title: Modulating excitation light to alter temperature-dependence of two-photon upconversion luminescence in Yb 3+ /Tm 3+ co-doped LuAG transparent ceramics
abstract: A schematic illustration of two opposite luminescence thermal behaviors under the excitations of both λ ex = 974 nm and λ ex = 932 nm, with their photophysical processes.

title: Machine learning-driven soft plasma etching for precision structuring of biofunctional organic semiconductor films
abstract: This study pioneers a novel in situ strategy for depth-resolved analysis of organic thin films by integrating soft plasma etching with real-time spectroscopy.

title: Non-diffusive behavior of aluminum and yttrium dopants in ZrO 2 /Al 2 O 3 and ZrO 2 /Y 2 O 3 bilayer thin films
abstract: The primary driving force for Al and Y dopant diffusion in nano-scale ZrO 2 thin films is the reduction of grain boundary energy through the formation of through-grains, rather than the concentration gradient.

title: Rapid and efficient microwave-assisted synthesis of Mn-doped cesium bromide to phase engineered cesium manganese bromide nanocrystals with color-tunable RGB emission
abstract: Mn-doped CsBr (Mn 2+ :CsBr), along with phase-engineered Cs 3 MnBr 5 and CsMnBr 3 perovskite nanocrystals, were synthesized using a simple and rapid microwave-assisted method that allows for tunable emission colors and high color purity.

title: Mechanically robust, thermally stable, bio-sourced and flexible eutectogel electrolytes for MXene-based symmetric supercapacitors
abstract: Environmentally benign and novel quasi-solid-state gel electrolytes comprising gelatin and an eco-friendly deep eutectic solvent (DES) or DES–water mixtures (water content ∼20 wt% and ∼50 wt%) have been prepared and characterized.

title: Emerging chiral molecular carbon materials for chiroptoelectronic applications
abstract: This review highlights emerging chiral molecular carbon materials for circularly polarized luminescence, emphasizing chirality control, property tuning, integration challenges, and future opportunities in chiroptoelectronic technologies.

title: Enhancing optoelectronic performance of organic phototransistors through surface doping of tetra-bromo perylene diimide single crystals
abstract: A novel chiral organic semiconductor ( S )-4Br-PDI-Ph was synthesized and self-assembled into single-crystalline nanowires. Aniline surface vapor doping markedly improved their optoelectronic performance in phototransistors.

title: Donor-functionalized aza-benzannulated perylenediimide-based ternary blend efficient inverted organic solar cells
abstract: Donor-substituted azabenzannulated PDIs were synthesized via Pictet–Spengler reaction, and used as secondary acceptors in D18:Y6 ternary OSCs, which enhanced V OC , J SC , and FF.

title: Advancing trace liquid detection: colloidal gold-based quasi-BIC metamaterials in terahertz biosensing
abstract: The colloidal gold-based metamaterials offer the possibility to reduce the interference of water on THz signals and achieve highly sensitive detection of trace liquids, and this pioneering approach opens up a new avenue for liquid biosensing.

title: A graphene-based tunable polarization insensitive terahertz metasurface absorber for multi-band high-efficiency applications
abstract: A graphene-based polarization insensitive tunable terahertz absorber achieving eight distinct absorption peaks across 3.9–9.73 THz with 99.3% average absorption for advanced THz applications.

title: Engineering Ag 2 Se thermoelectrics via amorphous nano-Si 3 N 4 : a dual-functional strategy for enhanced zT and mechanical strength
abstract: The incorporation of amorphous nano-Si 3 N 4 enhances the zT value of Ag 2 Se from 0.72 to 0.95 by simultaneously reducing both the electronic and lattice thermal conductivity. Mechanical properties increased by ∼15.4% owing to the pinning effect.

title: Polymorphism in mechanochromic luminogens: recent advances and perspectives
abstract: Polymorphism in mechanochromic materials is a subject of growing research interest due to its implication in various optoelectronic devices.

title: Recent advances in semi-transparent perovskite solar cells: fabrication, structural engineering, applications, and future challenges
abstract: Bifacial–tandem synergy leveraging albedo harvesting and eco-encapsulation breaks 28%-efficiency ceiling (stabilized at >1200 h) for facile BIPV/wearable commercialization.

title: Advancements in microwave dielectric ceramics with K20 for 5G/6G communication systems: a review
abstract: Microwave dielectric ceramics with dielectric constant ( ε r ≈ 20) for 5G/6G communications, focusing on four material systems—MgTiO 3 –CaTiO 3 , (Ca,La)(Al,Ti)O 4 , LnNbO 4 , and Li 2 TiO 3 .

title: MXene nanosheets modified with different intercalators as the separator for lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: MXene nanosheets were prepared using an HF-HCl mixed acid etching method and applied as separators in lithium–sulfur batteries. The effect of different intercalants on the performance of lithium–sulfur batteries was investigated.

title: Halide double perovskite Rb 2 AgBiX 6 (X = Br, Cl) exhibits excellent stability and optical properties under high pressure
abstract: Hydrostatic pressure enhances the stability and optoelectronic properties of Rb 2 AgBiX 6 (X = Cl, Br) perovskites. By reducing the bandgap, it improves optical response, offering key theoretical insights for optoelectronic devices.

title: Giant magnetocaloric effect of the K 3 Gd 5 (PO 4 ) 6 compound at ultra-low temperature
abstract: Magnetic refrigerants possessing large low-field magnetic entropy changes (−Δ S M ) in the sub-Kelvin temperature region are urgently desired for adiabatic demagnetization refrigeration (ADR).

title: The effect of critical micelle concentration on the amphiphilic species response using P3HT-based organic electrochemical transistors
abstract: P3HT-based OECT devices represent a promising platform to estimate the critical micelle concentration but also to sense the presence of certain amphiphilic species based on the monolayer formation in the OFF mode and reversion in the ON mode.

title: Fast, slow and reverse polymorph transformations in thin films of a 5,10-dihydroindolo[3,2- b ]indole derivative
abstract: The polymorph transformation dynamics of a new, strongly fluorescent, organic p-type semiconductor (C 8 -C-inin) are explored. Depending on deposition method, film morphology and annealing, metastable polymorphs are stabilized or induced.

title: Cr 3 X 2 Y 2 (X, Y = S, Se, Te) monolayers: valley-polarized quantum anomalous Hall insulators driven by electric field
abstract: The valley-polarized quantum anomalous Hall (VP-QAH) insulators, combining valleytronics and topology in materials, have promising applications in low-power-consumption spintronic devices.

title: In situ growth and ionic switching behavior of single-crystalline silver iodide nanoflakes
abstract: Single-crystalline β-AgI nanoflakes are synthesized and characterized, and two-terminal devices are fabricated to evaluate the ionic switching characteristics. Samples exhibit a large electrical resistance change and memristive switching.

title: Correction: A highly adhesive, self-healing and perdurable PEDOT:PSS/PAA–Fe 3+ gel enabled by multiple non-covalent interactions for multi-functional wearable electronics
abstract: Correction for ‘A highly adhesive, self-healing and perdurable PEDOT:PSS/PAA–Fe 3+ gel enabled by multiple non-covalent interactions for multi-functional wearable electronics’ by Qiang Gao et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2022, 10 , 6271–6280, https://doi.org/10.1039/D2TC00613H.

title: Structural, optical and improved magnetic properties of nanostructured Co doped BiFeO 3 powders prepared by sol–gel route
abstract: Successful substitution of Co for Fe in the R 3 c structure of the BiFe 0.9 Co 0.1 O 3 . The improved magnetic response and intriguing optical behavior suggest applications as voltage-controlled magnetic devices for spintronics or in optoelectronic devices.

title: Dual functionality of phosphorescence and photothermal conversion through light-activated open-shell singlet diradicals in silver metal–organic frameworks
abstract: Photochromic silver MOFs with thermally excited triplet radicals show room-temperature phosphorescence emission, and the outstanding photothermal conversion promotes efficient solar-driven water evaporation.

title: Achieving black-to-transmissive conjugated copolymers enabling ultrabroad spectrum modulation based on benzobisthiadiazole derivatives
abstract: Black-to-transmissive electrochromic (EC) materials, possessing superior electrochromic properties, still face limitations in smart windows, military camouflage, etc. , due to their narrow spectrum modulation (below 800 nm).

title: Unveiling the mechanism behind shell thickness-dependent X-ray excited optical and persistent luminescence in lanthanide-doped core/shell nanoparticles
abstract: We reveal an optimal shell thickness of approximately 3 nm for both XEOL and XEPL of homogeneous NaYF 4 :Tb@NaYF 4 and heterogeneous NaYF 4 :Tb@NaLuF 4 core/shell NPs.

title: Femtosecond laser writing of silicon vacancy color centers with specific defect orientations in silicon carbide
abstract: High yield and precise spatial controllability of silicon vacancies can be achieved using femtosecond laser processing. Selective fabrication of V2 color centers is attainable under specific parameter conditions.

title: Integrating machine learning and generative models for the intelligent design of TADF materials with circularly polarized luminescence
abstract: We propose a machine learning and generative approach for circularly polarized TADF. A robust model finds key g lum features, and a VAE generates candidates with high g lum and good synthetic accessibility for chiral optoelectronics.

title: Molecular aggregation and crystallinity control enables improved performance of all-polymer solar cells
abstract: New n-type diimide-based random copolymers with controllable aggregation were developed for all-polymer solar cells that can be processed by high-boiling-point solvents.

title: Efficient host-free delayed fluorescence organic light-emitting diodes
abstract: Efficient non-doped bluish-green to green thermally activated delayed fluorescence (TADF) OLEDs are fabricated with two TADF emitters, namely HDT1 and 36Me-HDT1, achieving maximum external quantum efficiencies of 16.5% and 21.0%, respectively.

title: On the ferroelectricity in wurtzite materials
abstract: The wurtzite structure is five-coordinated rather than four-coordinated, and the system's ionic character determines its ferroelectricity.

title: Aggregation-induced new functions: structure–property relationships in molecular crystals
abstract: This review shows how molecular aggregation enables emergent photophysics (RTP, radical emission, NIR luminescence, photothermal, X-ray scintillation) unseen in single molecules.

title: Dibenzothiophene sulfone-based n-type emissive organic semiconductor
abstract: We designed and synthesized an n-type luminescent organic semiconductor, DPIDBSO. The symmetric silver electrode-based DPIDBSO OLET devices showed obvious n-type electroluminescence with a stationary emission zone.

title: A graphene oxide enhanced terahertz metamaterial for ultra-sensitive detection of d -aspartic acid solution
abstract: Graphene oxide enhances the THz electromagnetic response and promotes THz transmission, while immobilizing target biomolecules via chemical bonding. Polyimide capillaries enable solution concentration detection in the THz wave range.

title: Angular dependence and proximity effect of dense arrays of cobalt nanowires as rare-earth-free permanent magnets
abstract: Co nanowires with a mean diameter of 13 nm and a mean length of 185 nm were synthesized using the polyol process, and their angular dependence and proximity effects on magnetic properties were studied using FORC analysis.

title: Synergistic film optimization and interfacial modification to boost the performance of wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells
abstract: A synergetic strategy combining Pb(SCN) 2 additive engineering and quasi-2D interlayer improves the performance of wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells by improving crystallization, passivating traps, and aligning energy levels.

title: Graphene oxide-enhanced mixed-structure quasi-2D perovskites for stable low-threshold amplified spontaneous emission
abstract: Two-dimensional Ruddlesden–Popper (RP) phase perovskites show promise for facilitating stimulated emission due to their ambient stability and carrier confinement.

title: Coplanar angle change inspired liquid polarity sensing based on pyrene bonded graphene nanoribbons
abstract: Liquid polarity plays an important role in healthcare, cell biology, molecular biology, drug delivery, and cell culture applications, and therefore the development of polarity-sensing sensors is of great importance.

title: Low oxygen content β-SiAlON ceramics derived from polysilazane as host materials for blue-emitting Ce 3+ -based phosphors
abstract: We exploit the polymer-derived ceramic (PDC) route to design β-SiAlON ceramics and dope them with a high Ce 3+ content for blue-emitting Ce 3+ -based phosphors through the modification of polysilazanes with aluminum and cerium.

title: In situ -engineered interfaces in copper oxynitride (Cu x O y N z ) systems with synergistic properties for photocatalytic H 2 production and N 2 fixation applications
abstract: Compositional engineering of Cu/oxide/nitride-rich systems, exhibiting synergistic plasmonic properties and band alignments for multiplex photocatalysis, is described.

title: Conductive hydrogel-based epidermal electrodes for electrophysiological monitoring
abstract: This review provides an in-depth overview of recent developments in the design and application of conductive hydrogels for epidermal electrophysiological electrodes.

title: Effects of halogen termination (F, Cl, and Br) and surface symmetry on the adsorption performance of Ti 3 C 2 MXenes for H 2 S, SO 2 , and NO 2 gases
abstract: Asymmetric halogen-terminated Ti 3 C 2 MXenes are found to be potential candidates for both chemiresistor-type NO 2 sensors and spintronic/magnetic-type NO 2 sensors, revealing their versatility as frontier functional materials.

title: Bipolar fully spin-polarized photocurrents in zigzag graphene nanoribbons
abstract: The bipolar fully spin-polarized photocurrent induced via the linear photogalvanic effects (LPGEs) is demonstrated through the application of a lateral voltage to a ZGNR device.

title: Liquid-based cationic ligand engineering in one-dimensional bismuth bromide perovskites: A-site influence on scintillation properties
abstract: Low-dimensional bismuth-based hybrid organic–inorganic halide perovskites (Bi-HOIPs) are intriguing scintillating materials due to their anisotropic nature.

title: Ureidopyrimidinone based poly(ionic liquids) as all solid-state electrolytes for electrochromic devices
abstract: The growing demand for flexible electrochemical devices has prompted the development of safer and more mechanically compatible electrolytes.

title: Hierarchical Co–C@PDA@BN/PAM composites: synergistic enhancement of microwave absorption and thermal conductivity through heterostructure design
abstract: The Co–C@PDA@BN/PAM gels show −55.3 dB minimum reflection loss at 9.4 GHz and 0.464 W m −1 K −1 thermal conductivity with a Co–C to BN ratio of 1 : 20. This work presents a novel composite for tailored thermal and electromagnetic applications.

title: A Bi( iii )-based halide with near-unity photoluminescence quantum yield as a blue-light-excited red phosphor for WLEDs
abstract: By introducing a benzyltriphenylphosphine cation, a red-emitting Bi( iii )-based halide with near-unity photoluminescence quantum yield was obtained and applied to WLEDs as a blue-excited red phosphor.

title: Water-soluble CaO sacrificial layer heteroepitaxially grown on yttria-stabilized zirconia substrate for large ferroelectric BaTiO 3 sheets
abstract: The freestanding BTO films obtained using a water-soluble CaO sacrificial layer and YSZ (110)-oriented substrate exhibit excellent ferroelectric and piezoelectric properties.

title: Efficient short-wave NIR emission induced by energy transfer from Cr 3+ clusters to Ni 2+ in LiAl 5 O 8 :Cr 3+ ,Ni 2+ phosphor-in-glass for spectroscopy applications
abstract: This work presents a novel energy transfer pathway that enhances SWIR emission, with high IQE and thermal stability.

title: Selenium-doped hematite (α-Fe 2 O 3 ) hollow nanorods for highly sensitive and selective detection of trace NO 2
abstract: The Se-doping strategy optimizes the electronic structure of α-Fe 2 O 3 , thus enhancing the surface Lewis basicity. The Se–Fe 2 O 3 based sensor demonstrates highly sensitive and selective NO 2 sensing performance.

title: Construction of S-scheme CoMn 2 O 4 /ZnCdS p–n heterojunction for enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: The S-scheme CoMn 2 O 4 /ZnCdS p–n heterojunction was constructed by physical mixing, which effectively promotes the separation of photogenerated electron–hole pairs and improves the photocatalytic hydrogen production performance.

title: Self-sustained soft robots based on liquid crystal elastomers
abstract: This paper summarizes the mechanism of self-sustained soft robots based on LCEs, which rely on negative feedback loops.

title: Dual-channel regulation of a single white-light-emitting compound and its application in time-dependent information encryption
abstract: Dual-channel regulation of single-molecular white-light fluorescence is proposed.

title: Push–pull carbazole twin dyads as efficient sensitizers/co-sensitizers for DSSC application: effect of various anchoring groups on photovoltaic performance
abstract: DSSCs were fabricated using nine novel twin carbazole-based donor–acceptor type organic dyes linked by non-conjugated alkyl chains to multiple acceptors. These dyes served as sensitizers and co-sensitizers with the Ru-based dye HD-2.

title: Backbone modulation of thermally activated delayed fluorescence polymers for efficient orange-red emission in solution-processed OLEDs
abstract: Backbone engineering of TADF polymers by incorporating carbazole and dibenzofuran units enables tuning of excited-state dynamics. Optimized polymer pNAI-DBF3705 achieves a PLQY of 78% and an EQE of 10% in solution-processed orange-red OLEDs.

title: Flexible capacitive pressure sensor with chemically reduced graphene oxide on a pyramid-structured melamine sponge array by laser engraving
abstract: Herein, we proposed a novel dielectric layer of pyramid-based melamine sponge (MSponge) obtained through simple laser engraving as a flexible capacitive pressure sensor.

title: Stabilization of top-gate p-SnO transistors via ultrathin Al 2 O 3 interlayers for hysteresis-free operation
abstract: Hysteresis-free and stable operation achieved in top-gate SnO TFTs by inserting an Al 2 O 3 interlayer that protects the surface of the metastable SnO channel.

title: The role and evolutionary pathway of spin states in CoFe Prussian blue analogues for photo-assisted water oxidation electrocatalysis
abstract: In this work, the ratio of the low-spin state in CoFe Prussian blue analogues (PBAs) has been found to play an important role in the enhancement of the current density in the photo-assisted water oxidation reaction process.

title: Type-II CsPbBr 3 /SnS 2 heterostructure nanocrystals: toward a high-performance self-powered UV photodetector
abstract: We fabricated a self-powered photodetector based on the type-II CsPbBr 3 /SnS 2 heterostructure nanocrystals. It exhibits a detectivity of 1.13 × 10 13 Jones under 365 nm. This work offers a strategy for the development of high-performance photodetector.

title: Design integration of hollow structures in graphene aerogel for multifunctional electromagnetic wave absorption applications
abstract: A lightweight hollow glass microspheres/graphene aerogel enables high-performance electromagnetic absorption with thermal insulation and hydrophobicity for aerospace and telecommunication applications.

title: Switchable circularly polarized luminescent Mn-based hybrid metal halides
abstract: This approach offers a straightforward strategy to create high-quality, stimulus-responsive chiral metal halide single crystals with large and switchable circularly polarized luminescence for use in anti-counterfeiting and luminescent devices.

title: Fullerene-based single molecule diodes with huge rectification ratios: a DFT-NEGF study
abstract: A molecular device composed of a fullerene and phenylacetylenes sandwiched by gold electrodes has a huge rectification ratio of 22 thousand. The coupling between the fullerene, gold tip electrode and coplanar phenylacetylenes plays a dominant role.

title: Thiophene-based conjugated networks tailored with phenylacetylene motifs for advanced lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Conjugated microporous polymers (CMPs) have attracted increasing interest for their use as electrode materials for lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) due to their microporous structures and robust conjugated networks.

title: Ferroelasticity versus superelasticity in molecular crystals: the role of weak switchable interaction motifs and low shear moduli
abstract: Replacing strong nitrile⋯nitrile interactions with weak C–H⋯F interactions leads to changes in the mechanical properties of molecular crystals. Superelastic crystals show higher stiffness and shear moduli than their ferroelastic analogues.

title: Molecular perspective: study on the adsorption behavior of Ni 2+ in solution on [001]-oriented and amorphous SiO 2 surfaces
abstract: The primary finding of this study is that the adsorption of Ni 2+ on the SiO 2 surface is facilitated by surface hydroxyl modification.

title: Composition-controllable growth of GeTe x thin films via combined atomic layer deposition and chemical vapor deposition
abstract: This study advances selector device development by enabling low-temperature, composition-tunable GeTe x film deposition for 3D integration, suppressing substrate-dependent growth via passivation layers, and demonstrating robust OTS performance with >10 5 cycles and detailed material/device analysis.

title: Thermally driven resonance tuning in nanobipyramid plasmonic substrates
abstract: Using in situ correlative micro-spectroscopy techniques, we study the temperature-directed shape change of anisotropic gold bipyramids (AuNBP), leading to localized plasmon resonance tunability of more than 250 nm.

title: Lignin carbon dot/nanocellulose films for enhanced UV stability and efficiency in perovskite solar cells through selective light transmission, blocking, and conversion
abstract: Lignin carbon dots/nanocellulose films were developed to boost perovskite solar cells (PSCs) by blocking UV and some IR while converting them to visible light, enhancing both stability and efficiency through selective light transmission.

title: 1,2,5,6-Tetrathiocin as a scaffold for improved charge transport in double-cable oligothiophene systems
abstract: Four small molecules featuring central 1,2,5,6-tetrathiocin (TTC) units fusing two oligothiophene chains have been prepared, their structures elucidated through X-ray crystallography and their charge transport properties studied in OFETs.

title: Coral-like porous tubular Ni doped g-C 3 N 4 nanocomposites as bifunctional templates for photocatalytic degradation and fluorescence detection of sunset yellow in beverages
abstract: A one-step calcination method was used to prepare coral-like porous tubular g-C 3 N 4 (Ni–CN) bifunctional composites with Ni doped N vacancies, which can be used for photocatalytic degradation and fluorescence detection of sunset yellow in beverages.

title: Advanced PbS quantum dot superlattices for high-performance electrical transport and infrared photodetection integrated with two-dimensional MoS 2
abstract: PbS QD SLs were prepared via a liquid/solid-phase method and transferred onto MoS 2 , exhibiting great photogating performance, achieving an R of 738 A W −1 , a gain of 59 201%, and a D * of 5.08 × 10 10 Jones at 1550 nm.

title: Integrated experimental and simulation studies on chiral–MgO nanoparticle synergistic tuning of PDCLC devices
abstract: MgO NP-doped PDCLC devices achieve tunable light modulation with a low driving voltage. Experiments and FDTD simulations reveal great potential for smart windows and energy-efficient display technologies.

title: Enhancement of ambipolar charge transport for quinoidal bithiophene-diazaisoindigo-based donor–acceptor copolymers via fluorine substitution strategies
abstract: This research article describes the reduction of energy levels of quinoidal D–A IID-based copolymers, the enhancement of film crystallinity and ambipolar transport characteristics in OFETs by introducing fluorine atoms into the QBTAIID unit.

title: Prediction of high-temperature superconductivity in LaH 4 at low pressures
abstract: A new rhombohedral LaH 4 phase is predicted to be a high- T c superconductor with a critical temperature above 100 K at 25 GPa.

title: Metal oxide-based resistive switching memristors for neuromorphic computing
abstract: Metal oxide-based resistive switching memristors are emerging as promising nanodevices for the hardware implementation of neuromorphic computing. We focus on the development of a neuromorphic computing system based on metal oxide memristors.

title: High-mobility and nonhalogenated-solvent-processable n-type organic semiconductors enabled by alkyl-side-chain engineering
abstract: TDPPQ-B exhibits an exciting electron mobility of up to 2.17 cm 2 V −1 s −1 in OFETs processed from 2-MTHF solution by edge-casting, which is the first report on nonhalogenated-solvent-processed OFETs with electron mobility above 1 cm 2 V −1 s −1 .

title: Hydrogel-based soft bioelectronic interfaces and their applications
abstract: The properties, classification, design criteria, and applications of hydrogel-based soft bioelectronic interfaces are reviewed along with summarizing their limitations, challenges and development prospects.

title: X-Ray imaging and enantioselective photopolymerization enabled by chiral Eu( iii ) complex nanoscintillators
abstract: This study develops chiral Eu(NTA) 3 (BINAPO) complexes as X-ray scintillators with unique circularly polarized luminescence and imaging potential, advancing X-ray-activated enantioselective photocatalysis for diverse environmental applications.

title: Trends in hydrogen-, lithium- and oxygen-based electrochemical RAM: materials, mechanisms, and applications
abstract: ECRAM devices, including Li-, H-, and O-based types, enable low-power, analog memory for neuromorphic computing. This work reviews materials, architectures, performance, and challenges, guiding future development for scalable, reliable AI hardware.

title: Semi-insulating 4H-SiC based PIN photodiodes for X-ray detection
abstract: A semi-insulating 4H-SiC PIN photodetector was developed by employing organic semiconductors to replace conventional doped layers. The device achieves remarkable X-ray and strong UV sensitivity, and showed excellent long-term stability.

title: Recent progress and challenges of flexible supercapacitor-sensor integrated systems based on conductive polymers
abstract: Breakthroughs in flexible electronic devices achieved through the development of high-conductive polymers, fabrication of flexible electrode materials, micro-nano-fabrication and integration of energy harvesting technology are discussed.

title: Pressure controlled anti-solvent spreading for improved current signal with effective grain growth in perovskite optoelectronic devices
abstract: Low-pressure solvent injection yields large-grained, highly crystalline perovskite films, boosting solar cell PCE from 19.43% to 21.07% and reducing dark current by 9.37 times. This enables dual-mode operation for solar cells and photodetectors.

title: Water-triggered optical response in Tb 3+ -doped CaSnF 6 crystals for high-security anti-counterfeiting application
abstract: An irreversible phase transformation of CaSnF 6 :Tb 3+ crystals upon exposure to water was reported for the first time, accompanied by the formation of CaF 2 :Tb 3+ nanocrystals and a notable increase in green emission intensity.

title: Enhanced ammonia sensing with the MAPbBr 3 perovskite and ( R / S )-OBN-tCz: a study on sensitivity and stability improvements
abstract: The performance of an NH 3 sensor featuring MAPbBr 3 as the luminescent core is enhanced with the introduction of an AIE material through FRET and defect passivation, enabling this nanofiber-based NH 3 sensor to achieve high sensitivity and stability.

title: Improved performance of all-inorganic quantum-dot light-emitting diodes using an all-solution process at low temperatures
abstract: A simple UV-ozone treatment strategy is proposed to modify the Mg–NiO hole transport layer in all-solution-processed, all-inorganic QLEDs.

title: 808 nm laser excited upconverting hydrophilized fluid velocimetric probe with a record sensitivity
abstract: A hydrophilic upconversion velocimetric nanoprobe excited at 808 nm was designed and fabricated. By introducing an energy migration process, the velocity sensitivity was promoted to a record value.

title: High-strength, self-healing conductive polyurethane with covalent crosslinking and reversible dynamic bonds for multifunctional strain sensors
abstract: Flexible conductive elastomer materials are promising for applications in wearable, flexible sensor devices, human motion monitoring, and other fields.

title: Welding complex-shaped actuators from dynamic liquid crystal elastomers
abstract: A weldable liquid crystal elastomer (LCE) with dynamic disulfide bonds enables complex shape transformations via cut-and-paste assembly of surface-aligned LCE segments while preserving mesogen alignment.

title: Optoelectronic and NLO potential of styryl-functionalized nitroisoxazoles for OLED technologies
abstract: Styryl-functionalized nitroisoxazoles exhibit enhanced nonlinear optical properties and high thermal stability. Compound B7 shows promising red electroluminescence performance for potential use in OLED devices.

title: Enhancing AC stress stability in amorphous indium gallium zinc oxide thin-film transistors via controlled hydrogen diffusion
abstract: To optimize the hydrogen content in a -IGZO TFTs, hafnium oxide (HfO 2 ) passivation via atomic layer deposition (ALD) and subsequent annealing were used. As a result, AC stress stability was improved, reducing the I on degradation rate from 69% to 9%.

title: Dual switching of dielectric and SHG triggered by thermal driven helical axis transformation with fluorescence characteristics
abstract: Two novel one-dimensional Pb-based hybrid perovskites with dual switching of dielectric and SHG generated by helix axes transformation.

title: Preparation of PDMS-modified epoxy composite coatings by Mn/Cl co-doping of g-C 3 N 4 and their enhanced photocatalytic activity and aging resistance
abstract: Recently, photocatalytic coatings have been widely used as an effective solution for removing pollutants from building facades, owing to their simplistic architecture, broad applicability, and cost-effectiveness.

title: Nickel-deposited hexagonal boron nitride composites synthesized via chemical vapor deposition: unlocking enhanced magnetic properties for advanced technologies
abstract: Nickel nanoparticles deposited on hBN via CVD at 190 °C achieve 1.85 emu g −1 magnetization, calculated by VSM.

title: Correction: Novel 2D-ferroic nanocomposite anti-reflection screen-printed films for EMI shielding: an experimental and theoretical study
abstract: Correction for ‘Novel 2D-ferroic nanocomposite anti-reflection screen-printed films for EMI shielding: an experimental and theoretical study’ by Vaishnavi Khade et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, 13 , 18991–19004, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TC00775E.

title: Nanoconfinement-induced orientation changes in liquid crystalline block co-oligomers
abstract: AAO nanoconfinement precisely controls the polymorphism and orientation of azobenzene-based liquid crystalline block co-oligomers (Azo-LCBCOs), inducing diverse smectic phases and enhancing parallel smectic layer alignment in nanostructures.

title: Critical importance of the hole-transporter and emission layer interface for prolonging lifetime in a phosphor-sensitized hyper-OLED based on an MR-TADF emitter
abstract: A green hyper OLED with CIE (0.31, 0.66), 159.9 lm W −1 , and LT 95/90 lifetimes of 600/2400 hours at 1000 cd m −2 was developed by preventing carrier trapping with MR-TADF emitter at hole-transporter/emission layer interface.

title: A frequency-reconfigurable conformal metastructure absorber with liquid crystal temperature control for RCS reduction
abstract: The RCS reduction spectra of PA and PEC at 40 °C and 120 °C.

title: Room-temperature ferroelectric nematic liquid crystals: the influence of substituents on properties
abstract: The influence of substituents on the properties of ferroelectric nematic liquid crystal molecules.

title: Photoluminescence enhancement of quasi-2D perovskite films by plasmonic silver nanoparticles
abstract: 7.4-Fold plasmonic enhancement of PL emission from quasi-2D (PEA) 2 (MA) n −1 Pb n I 3 n +1 perovskite films with n = 10 phase by Ag NPs sputtering coating.

title: Study on the corrosion resistance and antibiofouling performance of mortars with slippery liquid-infused porous surfaces based on fine coral powder
abstract: The anti-corrosion and antibiofouling properties of superhydrophobic surfaces deteriorate with prolonged water immersion.

title: A mussel-inspired AgNWs/PDA-fabric multifunctional wearable flexible strain/pressure sensor for human motion detection
abstract: A mussel-inspired multifunctional wearable flexible sensor was fabricated using the AgNWs/PDA-fabric. It possessed high sensitivity, outstanding flexibility, stability, rapid response/recovery time and reliable strain/pressure sensing capabilities.

title: NiFe 2 O 4 nanoflowers with Mo doping for self-powered hydrogen production at large current density
abstract: The in situ -formed Mo–NiFe 2 O 4 /IF displays enhanced electrochemical properties compared to NiFe 2 O 4 /IF, and can operate stably and efficiently during a self-powered H 2 production reaction.

title: Low-bandgap oligothiophene-naphthalimide oligomeric semiconductors for thermoelectric applications
abstract: Oligothiophene-naphthalimide oligomeric semicoductors for organic thermoelectrics.

title: Magnetic and luminescent lanthanoid (Ln = Tb, Dy) 2D MOFs on functionalized silicon
abstract: Lanthanoid 2D MOFs [Ln(MeCOO)(PhCOO)₂] (Ln = Dy, Tb) were grown on silanized Si(100), retaining significant magnetic moments and green Tb luminescence, paving the way for magneto-optical and quantum information devices.

title: FeSe 2 /MoSe 2 heterostructures: interface engineering for enhanced high-rate sodium-ion storage
abstract: FeSe 2 /MoSe 2 heterointerface engineering tailors built-in field and interfacial coupling, enabling high-rate Na + storage (391.6 mAh g −1 ) and cycling stability.

title: Trace of altermagnetism in GdAlSi films: towards the 2D limit
abstract: GdAlSi ultrathin films are shown to preserve bulk-inherited altermagnetic behavior down to the 2D limit.

title: Enhancing the reversibility of thermochromism of polydiacetylene-based nanostructures embedded in a natural polymer matrix
abstract: Reversible thermochromism in polydiacetylene nanosheets is enhanced via amide-functionalization and embedding in alginate, yielding stable, responsive nanocomposites with up to 70% color recovery for sensing and smart material applications.

title: Chemical structure and processing solvent of cathode interlayer materials affect organic solar cells performance
abstract: Interlayer toxic solvent processing and instability remain a challenge in organic solar cells. We test F-PDIN-EH in three solvents against the well-established PDINO to understand the structure–property–performance relationships in device stability.

title: Deformation-tolerant linkage of silicone rubbers and carbon-based elastomers via chemical and topological adhesion
