abstract: A chemical and topological adhesive (CTA) is proposed to bridge silicone rubbers and carbon-based elastomers, offering a general solution for deformation-tolerant integration of stretchable electronics.

title: Origins of electrical conductivity in 3D iron-tetrazole-based metal–organic frameworks
abstract: First-principles calculations reveal protonic ordering and mixed valence enhance charge transport in Fe 2 (BDT) 3 MOF polymorphs via distinct conduction pathways.

title: Cold atmospheric plasma-assisted deposition of ammonium functionalized glass coatings for enhanced antibacterial properties
abstract: This study demonstrates plasma-based deposition of ammonium-functionalized SiO x coatings onto various metallic and polymeric surfaces, offering broad-spectrum efficacy against implant-associated bacterial pathogens while preserving biocompatibility.

title: Achieving clear ferroelectric polarization reversal in room-temperature multiferroic ε-Fe 2 O 3 systems through grain boundary engineering
abstract: Ferroelectric switching in ε-Fe 2 O 3 -related oxides is improved by reducing grain boundary density via codoping, highlighting their potential as room-temperature multiferroics.

title: Layered Cs 3 Bi 2 I 9 perovskite nanosheets on TiO 2 nanorods for high-performance heterostructure photodetectors
abstract: An inorganic Cs 3 Bi 2 I 9 perovskite heterostructure layer deposited over TiO 2 nanorod arrays via halide chemical vapor deposition for photodetector applications.

title: Manipulation of intramolecular hydrogen bonds in conjugated pseudoladder polymer for semiconductivity and solution-processability
abstract: The conformational coplanarity and local rigidity of π-conjugated backbones are critical for the semiconducting performance of organic electronic materials.

title: A halide perovskite/Nb:SrTiO 3 heterojunction-based interfacial-type memristor
abstract: An interfacial-type Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 /Nb:SrTiO 3 heterojunction memristor via ionic migration-induced barrier modulation for artificial vision and neuromorphic computing.

title: Carrier relaxation and exciton dynamics in chemical-vapor-deposited two-dimensional hybrid halide perovskites
abstract: Transient absorption spectroscopy from chemical vapor deposited 2D perovskites shows differences in carrier dynamics depending on the organic cations. The decay reveals a strong contribution from the Auger recombination process at early times.

title: Recent progress of two-dimensional photothermal nanomaterials in personal thermal management
abstract: 2D photothermal nanomaterials, including transition metal hydrides (TMHs), transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs), MXene, graphene, and their derivatives, are being utilized in current photothermal applications.

title: Recyclable chloride trapping and sustained nicotinate release monitored by photoluminescence of Eu 3+ -doped layered yttrium hydroxide
abstract: The photoluminescence ‘on/off’ by the NA inclusion and chloride trapping demonstrates potential applications of NA-LYH:Eu as a corrosion inhibitor and a luminescent carrier for sustained NA release.

title: Boosting low-field energy storage performance in a weakly coupled lead-free ergodic relaxor of BaTiO 3 –Bi(Zn 2/3 Ta 1/3 )O 3
abstract: A novel weakly coupled lead-free ergodic relaxor BaTiO 3 –Bi(Zn 2/3 Ta 1/3 )O 3 is designed to achieve good energy storage properties under low electric fields. The underlying mechanism of the enhanced properties is elucidated.

title: Superconductivity above 30 K due to the introduction of oxygen in CaFeAsF
abstract: Superconductivity with an onset T c up to 30.7 K is uncovered in air-annealed CaFeAsF. The occurrence of superconductivity is most likely caused by the doping effect of the O element at the F positions during the annealing process.

title: Enhancing triboelectric performance in PVDF-based folded spring-type triboelectric nanogenerators via heat-induced phase transition and dielectric optimization for energy harvesting and self-powered sensing
abstract: As a promising and sustainable power source for smart electronic devices and self-powered devices, triboelectric nanogenerators (TENGs) have gained significant attention owing to their high energy conversion efficiency and versatile applications.

title: Conductive polymer doped carbon electrode for high-performance hole transport layer free perovskite photovoltaics and self-powered photodetectors
abstract: We fabricate HTL-free carbon electrode perovskite solar cells with polypyrrole in carbon paste, achieving a PCE of 18.48% under AM 1.5G and 26.74% at 2000 lux, along with a peak detectivity of 1.96 × 10 13 Jones in photodetector mode.

title: Electrochemical epitaxial lift-off and transfer of InGaN/GaN heterostructure films for vertical micro-LED arrays
abstract: A vertical Micro-LED array was fabricated via electrochemical epitaxial lift-off (EELO). Tafel analysis shows high EELO efficiency in HNO 3 , resulting in reduced n-GaN roughness. This stems from NO 2 dissolution kinetically accelerating GaN corrosion.

title: Boosting photoelectricity based on self-supported flexible metal–organic framework arrays for photo-Fenton-like degradation of organic pollutants
abstract: Boosted adsorption and degradation of organic pollutants based on self-supported flexible metal–organic framework arrays.

title: Polariton emission properties of an organic dye-doped polymer microcavity
abstract: The N , N ′-bis(2-phenylethyl)-3,4,9,10-perylene dicarboximide (EP-PDI) dye-doped polymer, with stable and strong excimer emission at a specific concentration, high PLQYs, can achieve strong exciton–photon coupling.

title: Enhancing C 2 production in CO 2 electroreduction using hierarchical copper–organic links: a molecular engineering approach for advanced metal–organic framework catalysts
abstract: A molecularly engineered MOF system based on hierarchical copper–organic links is designed. The calcined CuBTC exhibits a stronger adsorption of reaction intermediates, successfully converting CO 2 into ethylene.

title: Bandgap engineering in quasi-1D Zr 1− x Ti x S 3 (0 ≤ x ≤ 1) solid solutions
abstract: We synthesized a series of Zr 1− x Ti x S 3 solid solutions (0 ≤ x ≤ 1) via a direct reaction between Zr–Ti alloys and sulfur vapor at 600 °C. These solid solutions have a tunable bandgap in the 1–2 eV range that linearly increases with the Zr content.

title: Asymmetric acceptors with fluorinated and chlorinated end-groups enable organic solar cells with almost 19% efficiency and high thickness tolerance
abstract: A novel core-asymmetric non-fullerene acceptor L36 was synthesized through a pyridine-regulated method, which displayed a superior PCE of 18.94% with excellent thickness-tolerance in the L36-processed device.

title: Low clearing asymmetric BTBT mesogens with perfect ambipolar charge transport
abstract: A perfectly ambipolar balanced charge carrier mobility has been found for a cyano-substituted BTBT mesogen.

title: Deviate from the norm: ambipolar-acceptor frameworks as a new design paradigm for low bandgap conjugated polymers
abstract: A series of low bandgap polymers demonstrate a new design model that pairs the ambipolar unit thieno[3,4- b ]pyrazine with traditional acceptors to give bandgaps of ca. 1 eV, with initial non-optimized devices showing promise as NIR photodetectors.

title: Electrically tunable coloration in polymer dispersed liquid crystal films for adaptive camouflage
abstract: A novel design of a colorful PDLC film incorporating an IL and Cr 2 O 3 was presented for adaptive camouflage applications. The obtained PDLC film exhibited reversible color changes between yellow and green.

title: Large magnetic anisotropy and a rotating cryomagnetocaloric effect in single-crystalline paramagnetic lanthanide calcium oxyborates LnCa 4 O(BO 3 ) 3 with Ln = Pr, Nd, Gd, Er, and Tm
abstract: Lanthanide calcium oxyborate LnCa 4 O(BO 3 ) 3 compounds exhibit strong magnetic anisotropy and large rotating magnetocaloric effects, highlighting their potential for low-temperature magnetic cooling applications.

title: Tunable synaptic plasticity in MoS 2 neuromorphic transistors using Li + incorporated chitosan electrolytes
abstract: MoS 2 transistors with chitosan electrolytes mimic biological synapses for neuromorphic computing. Li + -incorporation effectively enhances synaptic plasticity by boosting ionic conductance in chitosan.

title: Structure–property correlations of tetraphenylbenzidine-based self-assembled monolayers for perovskite and organic solar cells
abstract: Structure–property correlations of tetraphenylbenzidine-based self-assembled monolayers are established, guiding the tailored design of efficient p–i–n perovskite and organic solar cells.

title: High throughput molecular design of electron donors and non-fullerene acceptors using machine learning combined with substructure importance
abstract: In this study, we constructed a database to train a random forest model, identified key substructures through feature importance analysis, and designed and performed virtual screening for high-performance novel donor–acceptor molecules.

title: Deep learning accelerated high-throughput screening of organic solar cells
abstract: Deep learning models were built to predict the PCE of OSCs based on a newly established experimental dataset. High-throughput screening of 45 430 virtual D/A pairs was conducted to identify candidates with high PCEs.

title: Extremely durable supercapacitor enabled by disordered porous carbon with a capacity retention up to 60 000 cycles
abstract: Porous carbon materials have been prepared through a salt template method. The as-fabricated supercapacitors show an excellent cycling stability. These supercapacitors hold great potential for application in the field of energy storage.

title: Self-healing scintillator films consisting of hybrid manganese bromides for non-planar X-ray imaging
abstract: A self-healing and ultra-stretchable (1870%) scintillator film based on hybrid manganese bromides enables high-resolution X-ray imaging of curved 3D objects with a low detection limit of 15.37 nGy air s −1 .

title: Ultralow lattice thermal conductivity and superior thermoelectric performance in AgAlS 2 and AgAlSe 2
abstract: Low-cost and earth-abundant AgAlX 2 (X = S, Se) chalcopyrites with ultralow lattice thermal conductivity and high figure of merit are promising anisotropic materials for advanced thermoelectric applications.

title: Broadband nonlinear optical response and ultrafast carrier dynamics in defect-engineered Fe–Co 3 O 4 for photonics
abstract: Fe-doped Co 3 O 4 was successfully synthesized, and its ultrafast carrier dynamics and broadband nonlinear optical properties were thoroughly investigated. As a saturable absorber, it enabled the generation of 1–2 μm ultrafast pulsed lasers.

title: Sustainable reservoir computing with liquid egg albumen
abstract: Novel bio-inspired physical reservoir computing system using colloidal albumen proteins.

title: Comprehensive theoretical analysis of XPtSi (X = Ti, Zr, Hf) half-Heusler alloys: bridging the Slack equation and BTE for accurate thermoelectric predictions
abstract: This study highlights the role of carrier relaxation time in PF estimation & electronic thermal conductivity. Lattice thermal conductivity is assessed via Slack model & BTE, showing BTE is more accurate, crucial for reliable ZT calculation.

title: Constrained photoinduced electron transfer (PET) luminescence enabling dual modal microenvironment probing and information encryption
abstract: By controlling electron donor/acceptor capacity and microenvironmental stiffness in TPEDMI&polymer blends, this work develops a dual-mode platform for rapid solid-state polymer detection and information encryption.

title: Strain-engineering quantized spin Hall conductivity in sliding multiferroic RuCl 2 bilayers
abstract: Strain-engineering can induce band inversions and quantized spin Hall conductivity in sliding multiferroic RuCl 2 bilayers, leading to the quantum layer spin Hall effect.

title: Heliconical smectic phases with transversal polar order
abstract: Helicity and polar order in bent-core liquid crystals. Fundamental insights into the synergistic roles of electrostatic energy, transient molecular helicity, and phase structure in controlling heliconical twist of achiral bent-core molecules.

title: Role of p–p and d–p orbital interactions in the electronic structure and phonon localization driving high thermoelectric performance
abstract: Orbital interactions in HfSe 2 -based superlattices modulate conduction band features—p–d coupling in HfSe 2 –ZrSe 2 induces resonant bands boosting the Seebeck coefficient, while HfSe 2 –SnSe 2 introduces linear bands enabling enhanced carrier mobility.

title: Non-hydrostatic pressure induced α to β phase transition in group IV–VI monochalcogenide GeSe
abstract: The α-to-β phase transition in 2D vdW GeSe, induced by non-hydrostatic pressure and mediated by an intermediate Cmcm phase, is directly observed and validated through experimental characterization and first-principles calculations.

title: Top gate overlaid carbon nanotube transistor electronic synapse arrays for high-performance image recognition
abstract: We develop an optimized top-gate overlaid carbon nanotube field effect transistor (TGO-CNTFET) with a large on/off ratio and the annealed TGO-CNTFET exhibits energy consumption as low as 1.27 pJ per spike and the recognition accuracy is 93.2%.

title: Highly efficient organic solar cells based on a simple polymer donor derived from a difluorinated benzene-quarterthiophene skeleton
abstract: Simple polymer Po2F composed of non-fused rings of o -difluorobenzene-quarterthiophene possesses low synthetic complexity and affords OSC devices with a notable efficiency of 15.45%.

title: Tuning the magnetic state and topological transition of monolayer Kagome Co 3 Pb 3 SSe with large magnetic anisotropy
abstract: Strain-induced magnetic and topological phase transitions in the Co 3 Pb 3 SSe monolayer reveal strong coupling between magnetism and topology.

title: Thickness-dependent quasi-two-dimensional β-Ga 2 O 3 solar-blind photodetectors prepared via GaSe oxidation
abstract: β-Ga 2 O 3 nanosheets are prepared by GaSe oxidation, and the thickness dependence of their photodetectors are investigated. The operational stability of the devices improved by decreasing thickness.

title: A novel co-substitution strategy for the design, growth, and optoelectronic performance optimization of Cs 2 ZrCl 6 :Ce,Li single crystals
abstract: The authors developed a novel unit co-substitution strategy to improve the optoelectronic properties of perovskite crystals and achieved excellent X-ray detection performance.

title: 2D Bi 2 Se 3 –ZnO nanoparticle heterojunction based ultrafast photodetectors for enhanced detection in the UV to telecommunication wavelength
abstract: 2D Bi 2 Se 3 –ZnO nanoparticle heterojunction-based ultrafast photodetector for enhanced UV to telecommunication wavelength detection.

title: Local structure regulation of multiple upconversion emission selectivity in highly doped lanthanide-based core–shell nanocrystals
abstract: A local structure regulation strategy of multiple upconversion emission selectivity by using stoichiometric ratio deviations was proposed in highly doped lanthanide-based core–shell nanocrystals.

title: Optical and excitonic properties of calcite CaCO 3
abstract: Calcite has been successfully used as a substrate or coating material in optical devices, but very little is known about its own optical properties.

title: The impact of axisymmetric and centrosymmetric molecular architectures in non-fused ring electron acceptors on photovoltaic performance
abstract: Two non-fused ring electron acceptors with axisymmetric and centrosymmetric molecular architectures were synthesized to study their photovoltaic performances.

title: Benzil-imidazole blue fluorophores and their applications in blue/white light-emitting diodes, sensing and anticounterfeiting
abstract: A series of benzilimidazole-based D–π–A blue fluorophores are designed, synthesized and applied for generation of blue/white light-emitting diodes, sensing of picric acid and acid–base vapor as well as anticounterfeiting.

title: A deep learning-driven forward and inverse cooperative network for circular dichroism in chiral metasurfaces
abstract: A fully connected forward and inverse collaborative network was utilized for implementing the prediction of structural parameters and circular dichroism of the chiral metasurface.

title: ZnF 2 modulation of a CsPbBr 3 @glass composite: a heat treatment-free approach to reach stable and bright green emission for backlight display devices
abstract: ZnF 2 facilitates the heat treatment-free precipitation of CsPbBr 3 PeNCs within borosilicate glasses with a bright and pure emission. The fabricated composite was then double-encapsulated with a PDMS polymer and used for potential light-panel pixels.

title: Sustainable and degradable electrochemical sensor from a bamboo substrate with in situ polymerized PPy and electrochemically deposited dendric nano-Ag
abstract: The structural design of incorporating PPy and dendric nano-Ag on bamboo resulted in a sustainable and degradable electrochemical sensor with high electrochemical performance.

title: Recent progress in carbazole-based small-molecule single-component organic room-temperature phosphorescence
abstract: Carbazole-based single-component RTP emitters have emerged as promising candidates for applications in theranostics, encryption and anti-counterfeiting, OLEDs, and smart materials due to their particular optoelectronic properties.

title: Nanoporous anodic alumina photonic crystals for solid-state lasing systems: state-of-the-art and perspectives
abstract: In this review we provide an up-to-date overview of recent advances in the field of NAA-PC technology and its application in lasing systems. We also provide a list of challenges and opportunities, and the future prospects of this exciting field.

title: Upconversion phosphors of CaLaAl 3 O 7 :RE 3+ /Yb 3+ (RE = Tm, Ho) and their multifunctional applications for multi-color anti-counterfeiting and laser displays
abstract: Novel upconversion phosphors of CaLaAl 3 O 7 :RE 3+ /Yb 3+ (RE = Tm, Ho) exhibit exceptional multi-color luminescence under 980 nm laser excitation, making them highly promising for advanced applications such as anti-counterfeiting and laser displays.

title: Flexible Cu(In,Ga)Se 2 photovoltaics for bending applications: advances from materials to panels
abstract: This review covers the progress and applications of flexible CIGS solar cells from lab research to commercialization, emphasizing methods for evaluating mechanical flexibility and bending-induced phenomena.

title: Acidified naphthalene diimide covalent organic frameworks with superior proton conduction for solid-state proton batteries
abstract: Naphthalene diimide covalent-organic framework are introduced as a novel solid-state superprotonic conduction electrolyte, demonstrating an expanded electrochemical stability window and stable cycling performance in proton batteries.

title: Nonvolatile electrical control of the electronic and valleytronic properties by ferroelectricity in the VSi 2 P 4 /Al 2 S 3 van der Waals heterostructure
abstract: An effective way for nonvolatile electrical control of the electronic and valleytronic properties by ferroelectricity in the VSi 2 P 4 /Al 2 S 3 multiferroic van der Waals heterostructure.

title: Interface energies of Ga 2 O 3 phases with the sapphire substrate and the phase-locked epitaxy of metastable structures explained
abstract: Ga 2 O 3 /Al 2 O 3 interface energy calculations reveal that the α phase is preferred, explaining the phase-locking epitaxy.

title: Carrier transport mechanisms in polycrystalline semiconductors: from grain boundary physics to device performance
abstract: A unified view of grain-boundary physics, transport mechanisms, and engineering strategies that dictate carrier transport and performance in polycrystalline semiconductors.

title: Recent advances in organic stimuli-responsive tunable circularly polarized luminescence materials
abstract: This review summarizes the organic stimuli-responsive tunable CPL materials based on various types of stimuli in recent years, focusing on the design principles, performance modulation mechanisms and potential applications.

title: Investigating the effect of hydrothermal carbonisation reaction times on the photoluminescence of bio-oil-derived carbon polymer dots
abstract: Carbon polymer dots (CPDs) have favourable properties such as broad spectral absorption, strong photoluminescence, low toxicity, and high specific surface area.

title: Donor–acceptor π-stacking interactions boosting capacitive energy storage of g-C 3 N 4 /PEI composite films at elevated temperatures
abstract: Polymer-compatible and self-stable g-C 3 N 4 fillers are incorporated to optimize the PEI chain conformation, suppressing high-temperature leakage current and enhancing polarization.

title: Conductive network enhanced self-assembled diphasic Prussian blue analogs for aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: The mechanism of structural evolution and ion insertion/extraction for diphasic Prussian blue analogs via the synergistic effect of multiple strategies.

title: A high-sensitivity epitaxial Ge/PbSe/CdSe/Bi 2 Se 3 p + pBn + barrier heterojunction for uncooled middle infrared detection
abstract: A new p + pBn + barrier infrared detector was designed via MBE technology, significantly reducing the room temperature dark current and enhancing the detectivity, which provides a promising solution for next-generation uncooled MWIR detectors.

title: Tunable multi-color room-temperature phosphorescence via melamine-based supramolecular self-assembly for anti-counterfeiting and humidity sensing
abstract: Herein, we report a novel multi-component supramolecular self-assembly strategy for the development of tunable multi-color room-temperature phosphorescent (RTP) materials with dual functionalities.

title: Photoresponsive circularly polarized long-lived fluorescence in chiral liquid crystals via tunable stepwise energy transfer
abstract: This research work provides a feasible strategy for preparing long-lived circularly polarized luminescent materials with excellent photocontrolled CPL properties.

title: Correction: A breathable and reliable thermoplastic polyurethane/Ag@K 2 Ti 4 O 9 composite film with an asymmetrical porous structure for wearable piezoresistive sensors
abstract: Correction for ‘A breathable and reliable thermoplastic polyurethane/Ag@K 2 Ti 4 O 9 composite film with an asymmetrical porous structure for wearable piezoresistive sensors’ by Ziwei Chen et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2022, 10 , 12986–12997, https://doi.org/10.1039/D2TC02611B.

title: Correction: Bimetal-decorated resistive gas sensors: a review
abstract: Correction for ‘Bimetal-decorated resistive gas sensors: a review’ by Ka Yoon Shin et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TC00145E.

title: Investigation of 3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene and 3,4-dimethoxythiophene as linkage units for multi-dimensional dimeric acceptors
abstract: Organic solar cell utilizing the 3D dimeric acceptor CH8-9, which features a 3,4-dimethoxythiophene linkage unit, achieved a higher power conversion efficiency of 16.3% compared to CH8-8, which employs a 3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene linkage unit.

title: A single-walled carbon nanotube-based phototransistor for neuromorphic vision applications
abstract: This schematic shows the key parts of our neuromorphic device and the interaction of pre- and post-synaptic neurons. Input and output pulses emulate biological synapses, enabling energy-efficient neural networks and adaptive learning applications.

title: Fluoride-target-assisted growth of Pb 2 OF 2 oxyfluoride films with anion ordering and high dielectric constant
abstract: Fabrication methods for mixed anion compounds are presented through the synthesis of lead oxyfluoride films. The work shows that controlling substrate temperature enables oriented growth, which enhances the material's dielectric constant.

title: A new luminescent material LaTiSbO 6 :Mn 4+ /Er 3+ with high optical temperature sensing sensitivity based on fluorescence intensity ratio
abstract: In this work, a Mn 4+ /Er 3+ co-doped high-sensitivity phosphor was designed and synthesized, with sensitivity greatly improved compared to that of single-doped Er 3+ . Additionally, a method to further improve the sensitivity was proposed.

title: Efficient piezoelectric catalytic degradation of organic pollutants based on defect engineering of heterovalent Al 3+ doped (Ba 0.85 Ca 0.15 )(Zr 0.1 Ti 0.9 )O 3 solid solutions
abstract: A low concentration of oxygen vacancy defects reflects excellent piezoelectric catalytic performance. Piezocatalysis of a 0.25 mol%Al 2 O 3 doped BCZT material is optimal. The strong piezoelectric effect enhances the efficiency of carrier separation.

title: Prediction of room-temperature antiferromagnetism in V 2 CT 2 (T = Cl, Br, I) MXenes
abstract: Systematic DFT calculations reveals V 2 CT 2 (T = Cl, Br, I) MXenes as high- T c 2D AFM materials with room-temperature magnetism via superexchange interactions. Monte Carlo simulations using spins2 code elucidate the strain-dependent variations in T c .

title: Fabrication of large-area nanostructures of pine needles with a dewdrop array for surface-enhanced Raman spectroscopy
abstract: A sensitive SERS substrate is fabricated by two-step deposition using an self-assembled polystyrene microsphere tempalate and a ultra-thin alumina membrane mask, and its good performance is characterized.

title: n-Type polymer semiconductors based on conformation-locked π-extended bithieno[3,4- c ]pyrrole-4,6-dione (BTPD) acceptor units for organic thermoelectrics
abstract: Using a “two birds with one stone” design, we devised the electron deficient monomers BTPD BTz and BTPD TTz and polymerized them via direct C–H arylation. Upon n doping, the resulting polymers achieved an electrical conductivity of 7.91 S cm⁻ 1 .

title: Recent advances in the stability of Sn–Pb mixed perovskite solar cells
abstract: This review discusses different strategies including compositional, additive, and interfacial engineering to suppress the oxidation, reduce defects, and enhance the operational stability of Sn–Pb mixed low-bandgap perovskite solar cells.

title: Prodigious half Heusler alloys VIrX (X = As and Sb) for waste heat recovery systems and spintronics applications – unravelled by DFT
abstract: Vanadium-based stable half Heuslers with exceptional ZT values have been proposed. The non-zero magnetic moment implies possible spintronic applications. The mechanical properties ensure their manufacturability in thermoelectric device settings.

title: Design and analysis of a vertically extended gate field effect transistor (VEG-FET)-based hydrogen gas sensor: a comprehensive modeling and simulation approach
abstract: In this work, Pt–H 2 interaction and Pt work function dependency on H 2 pressure and desorption area were modeled. Based on the analysis, a novel vertically extended gate FET (VEG-FET) based amplifier was developed for varying H 2 pressures.

title: Recrystallization of single crystal CsCu 2 I 3 perovskites by I 2 treatment for enhanced UV detecting abilities
abstract: Single crystal copper halide perovskites (CsCu 2 I 3 ) have gained extensive interest due to their nontoxic composition and superior ultraviolet (UV) photodetecting abilities.

title: Multimodal temperature sensing in hybrid perovskites doped with Cr 3+ : a strategy for optimizing luminescent thermometers
abstract: Optical thermometry has gained significant attention due, in particular, to its usefulness, with high sensitivity, rapid response, and remote measurement capability.

title: Selenium-doped zeolites as sustainable NIR emitters: a comprehensive photophysical study
abstract: With increasing selenium loading, selenium-doped zeolites transition from red emission, attributed to Se 2 − species, to long-lived NIR-emitting clusters, offering stable and sustainable materials for advanced optical applications.

title: Stronger polar ferromagnetism and narrower optical band gaps in two-dimensional hybrid copper perovskites induced by bromine-substitution
abstract: A ‘dual-action’ halogen-substitution strategy, a heavier atom Br substituted in organic moieties and within inorganic octahedral moieties, results in structures (3/4-BrbaH) 2 CuBr 4 with the highest Curie temperatures and the narrowest optical band gaps.

title: Intermolecular proton-coupled electron transfer reconstructs aggregates for near-infrared-light-driven hydrogen evolution
abstract: The PCET-induced formation of reconstructed AT aggregates with excellent NIR-light capture as well as efficient charge separation, enabling highly efficient hydrogen production with NIR light.

title: Reconfiguration of band-edge states via intermolecular packing in organic semiconductor-incorporated perovskites
abstract: A new strategy for tunable interlayer interactions in organic semiconductor-incorporated perovskites (OSiPs) by introducing the proper intermolecular packing of thiophene-based isostructural polymorphs.

title: Highly stretchable, adhesive and 3D-printable eutectic ion conductor for wearable electronics and self-powered sensors
abstract: A highly stretchable, adhesive and 3D-printable eutectic ion conductor was prepared and applied for wearable electronics and self-powered sensors.

title: Neural ordinary differential equations for predicting the temporal dynamics of a ZnO solid electrolyte FET
abstract: A neural ordinary differential equation continuous time model of a ZnO/Ta 2 O 5 synaptic Solid Electrolyte FET, with a unique negative differential resistance in its gate current characteristics.

title: Pure red emitting elastic organic single crystals as flexible optical waveguides and circuits
abstract: Pure color red emitting elastically flexible organic crystals with a pyrene core and showcasing active waveguiding properties were designed, and their potential for fabricating reconfigurable photonic integrated circuits was displayed.

title: Crystallinity-driven ferroelectric and piezoelectric properties of hydrothermally synthesized zinc stannate: exploring the substantial role of mineralizer NaOH
abstract: Hydrothermally synthesized polycrystalline ZnSnO₃ at 0 M mineralizer changes to single-crystalline at 0.25 M. At 0 M, Sn⁴⁺ ions dominate, causing lossy ferroelectric behavior, while 0.25 M stabilizes Zn²⁺ ions, resulting ideal ferroelectricity.

title: Solution-processed self-assembling charge-transfer cocrystal/TIPS-pentacene heterojunctions for artificial synapses
abstract: Crystalline p–n heterojunctions with phase-separated heterostructures are fabricated using a one-step drop-casting strategy. The as-prepared artificial synaptic devices achieved a validation accuracy of over 97% on the MNIST dataset.

title: Diversifying peripheral aromatic units of pyrrolo[3,2- b ]pyrrole-containing conjugated polymers and the resulting optoelectronic properties
abstract: Pyrrolo[3,2- b ]pyrrole represents a synthetically-simple conjugated scaffold that enables accessing polymers with tailorable properties useful for organic electronics.

title: Formation of intermediate-phase free ultrasmooth quasi-two-dimensional perovskite films for high quality lasers
abstract: We demonstrate that an intermediate-phase free ultrasmooth solution processed quasi-2D perovskite can be obtained by using antisolvents with different polarities and boiling points to engineer the crystallization process.

title: Molecular trap engineering enables superior high-temperature charge–discharge efficiency in a polymer blend with densely packed molecular chains
abstract: We demonstrate a strategy to hinder charge transport in all-organic polymer composites by incorporating high electron-affinity molecular semiconductors into densely packed polymer blends.

title: Far-red emitting phosphors for plant growth applications: fitted and enhanced via cation substitution of Gd 3+
abstract: A novel phosphor was designed via cation substitution of Gd 3+ , with excellent external quantum efficiency and thermal stability, encapsulated into pc-LEDs and applied in experiment of lettuce growth, showing an enhancement in biomass by 30%.

title: A multi-scale interface-induced 3D percolation network in polymeric heterostructures for EMI shielding application
abstract: Mechanical compounding and hot pressing were used to prepare a composite material. The material retains excellent EMI shielding capability under simulated harsh marine environments and enables highly flexible mechanical interlocking strategies.

title: Enhancing the NO 2 detection ability of surface acoustic wave sensors with ZnO-decorated N-doped porous carbon nanosheets
abstract: The ZnO provides abundant adsorption sites of NO 2 to the N-PCNs, optimizing the sensing mechanism of N-PCNs for NO 2 , and significantly reinforcing the NO 2 detection ability of sensing layers for SAW sensors.

title: The structure design of poly(ester imide)s with low dielectric loss and high mechanical properties
abstract: Combining ester groups and ether bonds balanced the dielectric and mechanical properties of poly(ether-ester imide).

title: Aggregation induced emission versus aggregation caused quenching: tuning the emission behaviour of liquid crystalline materials
abstract: The unique combination of two classes of luminophores, showing either aggregation-induced or aggregation-caused quenching within a liquid crystal yielded a thermo-sensor with a temperature induced emission shift from green to red.

title: Integrating environmental assessment into early-stage wearable electronics research
abstract: This perspective explores the intersection between materials research and environmental assessment during the early-stage development of next-generation wearables, encompassing flexible, stretchable, soft, transient, and printed electronics.

title: Theoretical design of a Z-scheme photocatalyst for water splitting with excellent catalytic performance: ZnTe/ZrS 2 heterojunction
abstract: BIEF & band bending force exclusive path 2 recombination, proving Z-scheme. This boosts carrier separation while preserving strong redox power. ZnTe side is HER site, ZrS 2 OER site. ZnTe/ZrS 2 heterojunction achieves 14.296% STH efficiency.

title: Single crystal purification reduces trace impurities in halide perovskite precursors, alters perovskite thin film performance, and improves phase stability
abstract: Single crystal purification of halide perovskite precursors removes both organic and inorganic impurities. Thin films fabricated with purified precursors demonstrate improved phase purity and stability under 1-sun illumination at 85 °C.

title: Halogen-assisted colour modulation in a flexible copper coordination polymer crystal-based hybrid photonic splitter
abstract: Tuning the optical band in flexible nano- and micro-crystalline structures using halogen atoms offers a promising route for fabricating functionally complex soft optical devices.

title: Ultra-sensitive and specific detection of ascorbic acid using a laser-engraved graphene electrode modified with a molecularly imprinted polymer
abstract: A novel electrochemical microsensor combining molecularly imprinted polymer technology with a laser-engraved graphene electrode for ultra-sensitive and specific detection of ascorbic acid has been developed.

title: Viscoplastic photoalignment modeling of asymmetric surface restructuring in azopolymer films by elliptically polarized light
abstract: Accurate reproduction of azopolymer surface restructuring in 2D polarization fields is achieved through viscoplastic photoalignment modeling, provided that the self-induced rotation of elliptically polarized interference patterns is accounted for.

title: A high-performance self-powered solar-blind UV photodetector based on an annealed Sc 0.74 In 1.26 O 3 ternary alloy film
abstract: A self-powered Sc 0.74 In 1.26 O 3 solar-blind UV photodetector achieves superior spectral selectivity, fast response speed, and ultra-stable cyclic repeatability via enhanced crystallinity and reduced oxygen vacancy, enabling high-sensitivity detection.

title: Enhanced photocatalytic nitrogen fixation over MnO x -modified O-KNbO 3 : impact of oxygen vacancies and MnO x loading
abstract: Oxygen vacancy engineering and MnO x loading synergistically boost nitrogen fixation activity of KNbO 3 , achieving a 5.4-fold performance enhancement.

title: Immobilization of laccase on different functional groups and degree of conjugation modified COFs for simultaneous photochemical-enzymatic removal of 2,4-dichlorophenol and uranium (U( vi ))
abstract: Fabricating an efficient photocatalyst-enzyme hybrid system was performed to investigate the catalytic performance of simultaneous U(VI) photo-reduction and 2,4-DCP photo-oxidation.

title: Facet-dependent photocatalytic performance and electronic structure of single-crystalline anatase TiO 2 particles revealed by X-ray photoelectron spectromicroscopy
abstract: Facet-dependent electronic structures of anatase were measured, revealing band bending between (101) and (001) facets that enhances photocatalytic charge transfer.

title: Alkylthiophenyl side chain modulation for enhanced photovoltaic properties of polymer donors in organic solar cells
abstract: Modulating alkylthiophenyl side chains in polymer donors enhances photovoltaic performance. PB2T-S exhibits superior efficiency due to its lower HOMO, stronger pre-aggregation, and ordered packing, thereby expanding the design strategies for OSCs.

title: Thermal conduction-induced crystallization and achieving high efficiency in HTM-free carbon-based CsPbI 2 Br solar cells by regulating the dipole moment of aliphatic amine acetate additives
abstract: The thermal conduction-induced crystallization and the aliphatic amine acetate additive-optimized dipole moment have successfully modified the properties of the CsPbI 2 Br film, leading to high efficiency in HTM-free carbon-based CsPbI 2 Br solar cells.

title: A boronic acid-based neutral two-component ferroelectric for piezoelectric energy harvesting and charge-storage applications
abstract: A novel amine-boronic acid-based co-crystal, AP·FPBA, exhibits excellent ferroelectric and piezoelectric properties and utilized for piezoelectric energy harvesting and charge storage applications.

title: Influence of mechanical stress on flexible electrolyte-gated organic field-effect transistors
abstract: The impact of mechanical deformation on the electrical properties of flexible electrolyte-gated organic field-effect transistors is explored. Large gauge factors are observed, likely due to changes at the electrical double layers.

title: Mid-infrared bifunctional high- Q plasmonic metasurfaces with strong intrinsic chirality and imaging-based biosensing
abstract: A high Q -factor plasmonic metasurface-based platform is capable of achieving intrinsic chiral response and imaging-based molecular-fingerprint detection.

title: Portable upconversion-based hydrogel sensors for visual quantitative detection of HOCl
abstract: A hydrogel sensor for the visual detection of hypochlorous acid (HOCl) based on the sensing principle of the inner filter effect (IFE) between upconverting nanoparticles (UCNPs) and carmine dye molecules.

title: Random lasing in liquid crystals: advances, challenges, and future directions
abstract: A comprehensive review of random lasing in liquid crystals, highlighting principles, unique properties, applications, challenges, and future research directions for innovations in photonics.

title: Graphitic carbon nitride-based materials: applications in medical diagnostics, therapeutics, and combined cancer therapies
abstract: g-C 3 N 4 exhibits versatile applications from photocatalysis to biomedicine. This schematic illustrates synthesis, modification strategies, characterization, and biomedical implementations in theranostics, biosafety, and cancer therapy.

title: Poly(dibenzothiophenylene sulfide)s: sulfur-rich annulated frameworks with a wide-range ultrahigh refractive index
abstract: Poly(dibenzothiophene sulfide)-based polymers exhibit outstanding ultra-high refractive indices ( n D up to 1.85) and thermostability ( T g up to 205 °C) attributed to their annulated, sulfur-containing, and rigid dibenzothiophene backbone.

title: High-performance photoelectric and self-powered properties of a p–n GaSe/SnS 2 heterojunction by a built-in electric field
abstract: The advancement and exploration of multifunctional, self-powered devices are significantly enhanced by the van der Waals (vdW) heterojunctions formed between two-dimensional (2D) materials.

title: Fabrication of a solution-processed low voltage TFT by using colloid 2D ZnO nanosheets and its application as a UV photodetector
abstract: The fabrication of solution-processed low-voltage thin-film transistors (TFTs) using colloidal 2D ZnO nanosheets enables high-performance, low-power electronic devices, demonstrating excellent UV photodetection capabilities suitable for UV sensors.

title: Sustainable Materials in Terahertz Electromagnetic Interference Shielding Technologies
abstract: The need for efficient electromagnetic interference (EMI) shielding solutions has increased due to the quick development of terahertz (THz) technologies in wireless communications, imaging, and sensing. Traditional metal-based shielding materials...

title: Semiconductive MOFs as a promising platform for green and efficient electromagnetic shielding
abstract: Despite significant advancements in the use of composite materials derived from metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) for electromagnetic wave absorption, achieving the inherent electromagnetic shielding properties of MOFs remains challenging.

title: Density functional theory and EXAFS spectroscopy studies of formamidoxime-intercalated molybdenum disulfide for the removal of Eu( iii ) and U( vi )
abstract: Controlling radioactive pollution is crucial for the sustainable development of nuclear energy.

title: Correlation between cation distribution and catalytic production of free radicals in mixed ferrite nanoparticles
abstract: The site occupancy and valence states of 3d cations in mixed ferrite nanoparticles are correlated with the free radical generation during heterogeneous Fenton-like reactions.

title: Wearable resistive graphene-based sensor for human behavioral and mental monitoring
abstract: A noninvasive sensor is suggested for testing the dynamic changes in the human emotional state associated with stress of different natures and intensities.

title: Performance optimization of solution-processed TADF-OLEDs using core-identical small, medium, and high molecular weight hosts
abstract: The physical interactions between the host and emitter in solution-processed organic light-emitting diodes (OLEDs) significantly influence device performance.

title: Analysis of EUV induced depolymerization and side reactions of polyphthalaldehydes for dry develop EUV resist applications
abstract: With the emergence of extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography, poly(phthalaldehyde) (PPA) has regained attention as a potential photoresist four decades after its initial introduction to the field of lithography in the 1980s.

title: Enhanced electrical properties and stability of solution processed IYZO thin film transistors by controlling deep level oxygen vacancies
abstract: By introducing Yb into IZO, deep-level oxygen vacancies are effectively controlled, resulting in enhanced stability and electrical properties of the TFTs.

title: An Ag 2 S-engineered heterojunction enhances the overall water splitting efficiency of NiS x catalysts
abstract: Engineered Ag 2 S/NiS x heterostructures dramatically enhance overall water splitting efficiency and achieve OER current densities of 100 mA cm −2 at overpotentials of only 180 mV and 100 mA cm −2 at 1.81 V during electrolysis.

title: Fluorination in core-only calamitic liquid crystals: how many and where should they go?
abstract: Crystallization temperatures as low as 130 °C were obtained for strongly fluorescent conjugated calamitic liquid crystals by selective fluorination and without the aid of flexible side-chains.

title: Photothermal response and precise control of circularly polarized luminescence via double-layered films based on cholesteric liquid crystals
abstract: A double-layered film strategy is used, where the emission band of a luminescent layer overlaps with the selective absorption and reflection bands of a N*-LC layer, enabling photothermal response and precise control of CPL performance.

title: Self-organised ordering of scandium into basal monolayers of aluminium nitride and its implication for the growth of well-crystallized (Al,Sc)N materials for electronic devices
abstract: Extensive annealing of (Al,Sc)N sputter films reveals an ordering of scandium into basal monolayers. The structural characterisation, a thermodynamic formation model and its implications on (Al,Sc)N material growth and device stability is presented.

title: Internal field and interfaces in organic and perovskite optoelectronic devices investigated via electroabsorption spectroscopy
abstract: This review summarizes the progress of electroabsorption spectroscopy in characterizing built-in potentials and interface states in semiconductor devices, and outlines future research directions and technical challenges in the field.

title: 2D elastic fluorinated donor–acceptor type π-conjugated molecular crystals and their optical crystal–polymer hybrid films
abstract: We designed 2D elastic crystals based on asymmetric fluorinated donor–acceptor–donor molecular structures for hybridization with polyimide films, achieving 3D active optical waveguide properties.

title: Electrical and magnetic properties of atomic layer deposited cobalt oxide and iron oxide stacks
abstract: We observed that thin films exhibit exceptional magnetic coercivity and saturation magnetization. These results demonstrate a route to engineer ferrite-based thin films with superior magnetic performance at nanoscale thicknesses.

title: Interfacial structural analysis with X-ray reflectivity for elucidation of alkyl-chain effects on hole injection in quantum-dot light-emitting diodes
abstract: X-ray reflectivity analysis on the interface structure of hole transporting polymers and quantum dots revealed that a long alkyl chain shows a strong interaction with quantum dots, which induces interfacial mixing to enhance hole injection.

title: A full-spectrum color converter based on tricolor phosphor-in-glass films for laser-driven white lighting
abstract: A novel tricolor phosphor-in-glass film (PiGF) with high luminous efficiency (LE) and color rendering index (Ra) was designed and fabricated by optimizing the glass layer thickness, phosphor ratio, film structure, and sintering process.

title: Controlling optoelectronic properties through protonation with π-extended triphenodioxazine diimides
abstract: The dramatic effect of protonation on the optoelectronic properties of triphenodioxazine diimides is studied for the first time.

title: Tuning liquid crystal properties with 0D carbon dots: exploring the impact of functionalization of carbon dots
abstract: CD decreases conductivity whereas ODACD increases conductivity of the NLC matrix. CD increases elastic constant ratio better as compared to ODACD in NLC.

title: Magnetocaloric effect in Mn-rich Heusler-derived alloys for room temperature-based applications
abstract: The study involves obtaining a CALPHAD-assisted thermodynamic database for Mn–Ni–Ga and choosing the appropriate atomic concentrations of the elements in order to obtain highest entropy changes for magnetocaloric applications.

title: Efficient deep-blue crystalline OLEDs via hot exciton nanoaggregate sensitizing TTA emitters
abstract: Embedding hot exciton nanoaggregates with TTA dopants into an emitting layer crystalline host matrix is effective for fabricating high-efficiency crystalline OLEDs.

title: Determining interfacial energy levels between the crystalline emitting layer and the amorphous electron transport layer: UPS-assisted efficiency optimization in crystalline OLEDs
abstract: Using ultraviolet photoelectron spectroscopy to determine interfacial energy levels between the crystalline light-emitting layer and the amorphous electron transport layer the efficiency of crystalline organic light-emitting diodes can be improved.

title: Oxide Lu 2 TeO 6 single crystals for X-ray detection with an ultralow detection limit
abstract: Semiconductor-based X-ray detectors have become critical in medical diagnosis, industrial inspections, and scientific research.

title: Side-chain regulated topology of 2D covalent organic frameworks and its impact on photocatalytic synthesis of H 2 O 2
abstract: Two kinds of COFs with kgm and fes underlying nets were synthesized via tuning side-chain lengths of organic building units, where topologies of COFs exhibit significantly different activities for H 2 O 2 photosynthesis.

title: Quantum spin Hall effect and tunable topological states in M 2 O 3 (M = Nb, Ta) bilayers
abstract: The Nb 2 O 3 bilayer undergoes a transition from a topological insulator to a Dirac semimetal when compressive strain is applied.

title: Recent advances in engineering electronic and thermal properties of PEDOT:PSS for efficient thermoelectric energy conversion
abstract: Recent advances in engineering PEDOT:PSS to optimize power factor and suppress thermal conductivity for high-performance thermoelectric devices are reviewed.

title: Strongly fluorescent spiro-type tetracoordinate complexes of dibenzo[ b , e ][1,4]thiaborinine dioxide with functionalized 2-(benzo[ d ]heterazol-2-yl)phenolate ligands displaying TADF
abstract: A series of spiro tetracoordinate organoboron chelate complexes showing strong and tuneable luminescence as well as TADF character were synthesised and comprehensively characterized.

title: Contact resistance reduction in amorphous In–Ga–Zn–O thin film transistors by interposing an atomic-layer-deposited amorphous Zn–Sn–O interlayer
abstract: In this study, thin-film transistors were fabricated to examine the effects of interposing a 2-nm-thick amorphous ZnSnO interlayer with 42 and 54 Sn at% between the amorphous InGaZnO channel and the source/drain electrodes on the contact resistance.

title: Probing charge behaviour in multilayer organic light-emitting diodes via electronic sum-frequency generation spectroscopy
abstract: Understanding the charge behavior inside organic layer interfaces in multilayer organic light-emitting diodes (OLEDs) is essential for improving device efficiency and lifetime.

title: A highly efficient 3D/0D CdIn 2 S 4 /Cu 2 O photoanode with a p–n type heterojunction for boosted photoelectrochemical water splitting under visible light irradiation
abstract: We prepared a high-performance CdIn 2 S 4 /Cu 2 O photoanode material for PEC water splitting using a hydrothermal method combined with SILAR and annealing processes.

title: Exploring structure and thermoelectric properties of p-type Ge 1− x In x Sb 4 Te 7 compounds
abstract: We determined that GeSb 4 Te 7 -based compounds exhibit site-occupational disorder of Ge/Sb cations and that In-doping significantly lowers the thermal conductivity, enhances the Seebeck coefficient, and improves the power factor of pristine GeSb 4 Te 7 .

title: Modification of mixed-halide quasi-2D perovskites by aminophylline towards efficient and spectrally stable blue light-emitting diodes with low efficiency roll-off
abstract: Aminophylline modification enables mixed-halide blue quasi-2D perovskite LEDs to achieve low efficiency roll-off and excellent spectral stability.

title: Correction: Facile synthesis of silicon quantum dots with photoluminescence in the near-ultraviolet to violet region via wet oxidation
abstract: Correction for ‘Facile synthesis of silicon quantum dots with photoluminescence in the near-ultraviolet to violet region via wet oxidation’ by Yizhou He et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2024, https://doi.org/10.1039/d4tc02095b.

title: Wet etching of atomic-layer-deposited amorphous zinc–tin–oxide thin film using oxalic acid
abstract: This study investigates the wet etching process of amorphous Zn–Sn–O ( a -ZTO) thin films deposited by atomic layer deposition (ALD).

title: A facile end-capping strategy with strong electron withdrawing groups for enhancing field-effect mobility
abstract: This study presents a simple yet effective “strong electron-withdrawing group end-capping” strategy for designing high-mobility polymer semiconductors by reducing energy levels and optimizing molecular packing.

title: Magnetotransport properties of a single-atom-thick GdPb 3 kagome compound on Si(111)
abstract: Electronic transport and magnetotransport measurements on the single-atom-thick GdPb 3 kagome compound on Si(111) revealed anomalies, the occurrence of which is interpreted as a sign of a non-trivial magnetic structure, like a skyrmion lattice.

title: Anion-induced chiral assembly: construction of Ag( i ) coordination polymers for photocatalytic degradation of organic dyes
abstract: Assembly of a racemic ligand with different Ag( i ) salts leads to homo/heterochiral polymers. The photocatalytic experiments indicate CPs 4 – 8 are active for the degradation of rhodamine B and methylene blue under UV irradiation.

title: Construction of heterogeneous wave-absorbing structures inspired by the structure of cucumbers
abstract: One-dimensional carbon nanofibers (CNFs) hold great potential as lightweight microwave absorbers, mainly attributed to their remarkable plasticity and stability.

title: Polymorphism-induced multi-functional crystal photonics achieved by a highly luminescent benzofuranyl molecule having a tetrafluorophenylene core
abstract: Beyond just finding different optical “characteristics”, we found “specific functions” for each polymorph due to the rational design of a polymorphic and highly luminescent compound.

title: 19.35%-Efficiency organic solar cells and reduced non-radiative recombination energy loss by a ternary copolymerization strategy
abstract: A third unit, TQC, is introduced into D18 to regulate the energy levels and miscibility of terpolymers, thus reducing the energy loss and improving the phase separation in OSCs. Among them, DQ20:L8-BO based OSCs achieve an impressive PCE of 19.35%.

title: SrTiO 3 -based memristor with a metal/oxide bilayer electrode for high recognition accuracy neuromorphic computing
abstract: A Pt/LSMO bilayer electrode memristor achieves on/off ratio = 1.2 × 10 5 , V set = 0.58 V. It emulates biological synapses, executes decimal logic operations, and enables 95.4% traffic sign recognition accuracy with 80% robustness under 10% noise.

title: Enhancing the crystallinity and dielectric performance of ALD-grown SrTiO 3 films by introducing a sub-nm-thick Pt layer
abstract: SrTiO 3 (STO), which has an exceptionally high dielectric constant, is a promising candidate for capacitor dielectrics for dynamic random-access memory (DRAM) applications.

title: Navigating the ZnO/metal phthalocyanine interface in OLEDs: challenges, perspectives, and engineering strategies
abstract: The interface between inorganic charge injection/transport layers and organic functional layers as the most strategic area for tuning the performance and stability of organic light-emitting diodes (OLEDs).

title: Self-healing epoxidized natural rubber flexible sensors based on hydrogen bonding interactions
abstract: Flexible strain sensors find extensive applications in the domains of human health monitoring and human–computer interactions.

title: A transparent YPO 4 :Eu 2+ glass-ceramic scintillator for high-resolution X-ray imaging
abstract: A transparent YPO 4 :Eu 2+ glass-ceramic scintillator with high XEL intensity and a high spatial resolution of 18 lp mm −1 is reported.

title: High-performance organic thin-film phototransistors based on stacked p–n heterojunctions for enhanced optoelectronic response
abstract: We designed a tandem heterojunction with p- and n-type semiconductors to enhance exciton dissociation and transport, offering potential for optoelectronic advancements.

title: Plasmon-enhanced organic field effect transistors
abstract: The integration of plasmonic nanoparticles into the channel or gate dielectric of organic field effect transistors enables enhanced functionality and performance in a range of optoelectronic applications.

title: Flexible porous PDMS–CNT frameworks with flash-decorated Pd nanoparticles for high-performance hydrogen sensing
abstract: A highly sensitive hydrogen sensor that operates at room temperature was developed using a porous polydimethylsiloxane structure coated with carbon nanotubes and decorated with palladium nanoparticles through a flashlamp-assisted reduction process.

title: In situ surface modification engineering for synthesizing nano-absorbers with self-forming heterointerfaces and prominent electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: This work proposes a facile in situ surface modification strategy to fabricate heterogeneous nano-absorbers with high EMW absorption strength and thin matching thickness in one step compared with typical Fe-based EMWAs with core–shell structure.

title: A superhydrophobic SiO 2 /rGO composite coating with enhanced photothermal properties for anti-icing/deicing
abstract: A superhydrophobic SiO 2 /rGO composite coating integrated with photothermal properties for enhanced anti-icing and rapid deicing.

title: Process optimization of contact interface layer for maximizing the performance of Mg 3 (Sb,Bi) 2 based thermoelectric compounds
abstract: This study demonstrates the reduction of contact resistivity through the optimization of sintering temperatures, leading to a maximum conversion efficiency ( η max ) of 9.3% at Δ T = 380 K for the SS f /Mg 3 Sb 1.5 Bi 0.5 /SS f TE single leg with good stability.

title: Reconstructing 2D MXene into 1D length-tunable aminofunctionalized periodic mesoporous organosilica@MXene/Ni structures for enhanced microwave absorption performance
abstract: Reconstructing 2D MXene into 1D length-tunable aminofunctionalized periodic mesoporous organosilica@MXene/Ni structures for enhanced microwave absorption performance.

title: Molecular dipole interfacial engineering for high-performance quantum-dot light-emitting diodes
abstract: A Br-2PACz with large dipole moments is employed to modify the TFB/QD interface. The fabricated QLEDs present an outstanding EQE of 25.03% and a long lifetime of 14 695 h ( T 95 @1000 cd m −2 ).

title: Balancing the mechanical and optoelectronic properties of light-emitting copolymers via precise control of the soft-to-rigid segment ratio
abstract: Blue light-emitting copolymers with tunable mechanical properties were developed by adjusting rigid-to-soft segments. They showed efficient deep-blue emission and low turn-on voltage, offering insights for stretchable optoelectronic material design.

title: Deep-red TADF dendronized polymer for efficient non-doped solution-processed OLEDs
abstract: A novel deep-red TADF dendronized polymer with AIE characteristics achieved 15% PLQY at 659 nm. The resulting OLED showed a record EQE of 2.6% for solution-processed, non-doped deep-red TADF devices.

title: Broadband and high internal quantum efficiency near-infrared phosphors obtained utilizing a chemical unit co-substitution strategy for plant lighting
abstract: Near-infrared phosphor-converted light-emitting diodes (NIR pc-LEDs) have been widely used in plant cultivation.

title: 2D V 2 C and V 2 AlC layers: ascendant deep ultraviolet optical limiting materials
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) transition metal carbides (MXenes), known for their unique properties, have ignited a surge of extensive interdisciplinary research.

title: Thermally induced reversible phase transition and photoluminescence switching behavior of (NH 4 ) 2 MnBr 4 (H 2 O) 2 crystals
abstract: In this work, a novel and highly stable (NH 4 ) 2 MnBr 4 (H 2 O) 2 single crystal was synthesized, exhibiting PL switching behavior through a thermally induced reversible phase transition, and applied in anti-counterfeiting.

title: Hollowing integration engineering to construct MOF-derived carbon composites for lightweight and efficient microwave absorption materials
abstract: Hollow particle-integrated Co-ZIF-HPT-700 nanotube composites reach RL min values of −76.93 dB at 8.96 GHz (10 wt% filling). Tuned thickness enables full-band absorption in the X and Ku bands, demonstrating superior microwave absorption potential.

title: Preparation of high-performance blue-red dual-emission carbon dots and their application in light conversion films
abstract: CDs were prepared via the one-step hydrothermal method and combined with PVA to construct a dual-emission light conversion film. The film can absorb UV and green light, convert them into blue and red light, and remarkably boost vegetable growth.

title: Revisiting the SrAl 2 O 4 :Eu 2+ ,Nd 3+ persistent phosphor
abstract: The SrAl 2 O 4 :Eu 2+ ,Nd 3+ persistent phosphor emits green (Eu 2+ ) and infrared (Nd 3+ ) persistent luminescence (PersL) and the PersL excitation (Ex.) spectra of Eu 2+ and Nd 3+ are different from their respective photoluminescence (PL) excitation spectra.

title: A more random and secure image encryption method: a novel true random number generator based on a W/Ta 2 O 5 /Ag memristor
abstract: A true random number generator (TRNG) based on a W/Ta 2 O 5 /Ag memristor was developed, which achieved image encryption of MNIST handwritten digits. This method effectively disrupts the pixel correlation of the original image.

title: MAPbI 3 piezoelectric semiconductor and its multifunctional light-force detector
abstract: In this work, excellent semiconductor properties and macroscopic piezoelectricity are achieved in a MAPbI 3 semiconductor single crystal, and an associated multifunctional device was prepared to simultaneously sense pressure and detect light.

title: Highly linear wearable ionic gel based on self-assembled discoid liquid crystal towards human motion monitoring
abstract: A novel type of ionic gel, constructed from self-assembled discoid liquid crystals, is demonstrated to exhibit high linearity, outstanding stability, and holds promise as a flexible strain sensing material.

title: Molecular crystals: mechanics and photonics
abstract: Focusing on the mechanics and photonics of molecular crystals, Rajadurai Chandrasekar, Panče Naumov, Xue-Dong Wang and Kristin Hutchins introduce a themed collection showcasing a series of articles that delve into their structural properties and broad range of functional applications.

title: Design and synthesis of single-benzene-based fluorophores with red/NIR emission for dual-function optical waveguides and photodynamic therapy
abstract: wx-1 exhibits two polymorphs with distinct emissions (600 nm and 657 nm); polymorph B functions as an NIR waveguide. In solution, wx-1 generates singlet oxygen and effectively kills EMT6 tumor cells under white light.

title: Concurrent improvements in coercivity and corrosion resistance for Nd 6 Fe 13 Ga-reconstructed Nd–Fe–B sintered magnets
abstract: The generation and existence of the Nd 6 Fe 13 Ga phase provide new insights into the corrosion mechanism of Nd–Fe–B sintered magnets. Furthermore, both the coercivity and corrosion resistance have been improved concurrently.

title: Cluster expansion augmented transfer learning for property prediction of high-entropy alloys
abstract: We propose a deep learning model that combines transfer learning with a cluster expansion method for accurately predicting the physical properties of high-entropy alloys (HEAs).

title: Design of a humidity-resistant triboelectric nanogenerator based on a CAU-10-(SO 3 H) 0.08 (OH) 0.92 /PVC composite film for producing hydrogen via water electrolysis application
abstract: A triboelectric nanogenerator based on the CAU-10-(SO 3 H) 0 . 08 (OH) 0 . 92 /PVC composite film, exhibiting excellent moisture resistance and cycle stability, was designed and successfully applied to water electrolysis for hydrogen production.

title: Highly sensitive biosensors with resonant coupling of plasmon-waveguide resonance to localized surface plasmons
abstract: The Au–TiO 2 –AuNPs biosensor achieved the resonant coupling of plasmon-waveguide resonance to localized surface plasmons. The limit of detection for the specific detection of carcinoembryonic antigen was as low as 3 ng mL −1 .

title: A flexible pressure sensor array based on an ionic gel for pulse detection
abstract: A mixture of PVA, CS and ILs was prepared into an ionic gel by freeze–thaw cycles as the dielectric layer for a capacitive sensor, which had high sensitivity and rapid response time.

title: Sweet side chain: a glycopeptide-based alignment medium to measure residual dipolar couplings in neat acetonitrile
abstract: Herein, we present a polyglutamate with a per-acetylated glucose side chain being the first alignment medium forming LLC-phases in neat acetonitrile. The polymer proves to be a versatile alignment medium concerning solvent and solute compatibility.

title: Anomalous resistivity and superconductivity in the polymorphs of (TiZrHf) 95 Sc 5
abstract: Low temperature resistivity and heat capacity measurements indicate that superconducting transitions are present when the material has either a body centered cubic or hexagonal close packed crystal structure. The body centered cubic superconductivity is robust to an applied field.

title: Electrochemical properties of a ferrocene-containing poly(vinyl alcohol)-based redox hydrogel and its application as a matrix for electrochromic devices
abstract: A redox-active hydrogel with a ferrocene/cyclodextrin complex in a PVA/borax matrix enables electrochromic function while retaining viscoelasticity.

title: Modulation of carrier dynamics via dual-hole transport layers in high-efficiency antimony selenide thin-film solar cells
abstract: The dual phthalocyanine/nickel oxide hole transport layer achieved a 7.27% efficiency in Antimony Selenide solar cells by establishing a gradient band alignment that improved carrier dynamics.

title: Enhanced thermoelectric performance of Cu 2 Se through Cu-deficiency and Te-substitution
abstract: Copper selenide (Cu 2 Se), a phonon liquid electron crystal (PLEC), is a promising thermoelectric material due to its efficient electron transport, but it suffers from copper ion migration, compromising its chemical stability.

title: Mechanoluminescent-energy harvesting bimodal sensors for self-powered communication sensors
abstract: A wearable ML+PENG platform using ZnS:Cu in a PDMS polymer matrix, generating light and electrical energy from bending for self-powered underwater communications.

title: Emerging avalanche field-effect transistors based on two-dimensional semiconductor materials and their sensory applications
abstract: 2D heterostructure-based avalanche FET's enable efficient photo/gas/biomolecule sensing via enhanced carrier dynamics and tunable band alignments under forward/reverse bias.

title: Porous Al 11 Ce 3 intermetallics as effective sulphur host networks for stable lithium–sulphur batteries
abstract: Al 11 Ce 3 intermetallics with porous network structures are fabricated, which present excellent adsorption and catalytic properties for polysulfide conversion, and show stable electrochemical properties as sulfur host networks for Li–S batteries.

title: A-site cation modification of Cs-based perovskite thin film for green light-emitting diodes
abstract: Examining A-site cation modification and anti-solvent effects on Cs-based perovskites reveal that using toluene coupled with a small amount of FA + affects the optoelectronic, structural, and morphological properties of the thin films and device.

title: (NH 4 ) 2 MnF 6 : a highly soluble, K + -free Mn 4+ precursor for red fluoride phosphors
abstract: A K + -free compound, (NH 4 ) 2 MnF 6 , with approximately two times higher solubility than K 2 MnF 6 , was synthesized via a reduction-precipitation-purification process.

title: Large magnetoresistance and thermoelectric properties of a quasi-skutterudite Ca 3 Pt 4 Sn 13 single crystal
abstract: We conducted a detailed study of the quasi-skutterudite Ca 3 Pt 4 Sn 13 single crystal through measurements of electrical resistivity, Hall effect, specific heat, and thermoelectric properties.

title: Dopant-free tert -butyl Zn( ii ) phthalocyanines: the impact of substitution on their photophysical properties and their role in perovskite solar cells
abstract: We synthesized molecular hole-transporting materials featuring a zinc phthalocyanine (ZnPc) central core and modulated the non-peripheral position with different numbers of tert -butyl groups.

title: Selectivity studies and modification strategies for high-efficiency photocatalytic CO 2 reduction to methanol products
abstract: Research on photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (CO 2 ) has extensively progressed.

title: Impact of alkyl chain length on the thermal, optical and semiconductor properties of the symmetric 4-alkylphenyl derivatives of [1]benzothieno[3,2- b ]benzothiophene
abstract: The impact of alkyl chain length on the physico–chemical and semiconducting properties of novel symmetric 4-alkylphenyl BTBT derivatives (C n -PBTBT) was revealed.

title: Medium bandgap A–DA′D-A type small molecule acceptors prepared using synergetic modification strategies enable high-performance organic solar cells
abstract: Four synergistic strategies guided the design of the medium-bandgap small-molecule acceptor Cl24-H, which exhibits excellent miscibility and complementary absorption with PM6:BTA-E3 system, resulting in ternary OSCs with a remarkable PCE of 20.2%.

title: Antiferromagnetic semiconductor La 0.6 Ba 0.15 Ca 0.15 Ag 0.1 MnO 3 perovskite with strong magnetocaloric and giant magnetoresistance effects
abstract: La 0.6 Ba 0.15 Ca 0.15 Ag 0.1 MnO 3 exhibits strong structural–magnetic–transport coupling with AFM→FM→PM transitions, leading to giant magnetoresistance and significant magnetocaloric effects.

title: Trap engineering for improved thermal stability and optical properties of Ce:LuAG phosphor ceramics
abstract: Highly efficient phosphor conversion materials with superior thermal stability are indispensable for high-power white light-emitting diodes or laser diodes (WLEDs/WLDs).

title: Recent advances in stimuli-responsive covalent organic frameworks: from mechanisms to applications
abstract: This review summarizes the recent advances in stimuli-responsive covalent organic frameworks (COFs), including the mechanisms and structure–function relationship and their diverse applications.

title: Molecular design of organic photovoltaic donors and non-fullerene acceptors: a combined machine learning and genetic algorithm approach
abstract: A 480-pair donor–acceptor database with 43 descriptors predicted photovoltaic parameters via random forest. Novel pairs via genetic algorithm achieved 16.85% efficiency, offering an efficient method for organic solar cells and molecular design.

title: Paramagnetic discotic liquid crystals based on planar benzo[ e ][1,2,4]triazin-4-yls: synthesis and properties
abstract: Photochemical synthesis of paramagnetic discotics with a tight molecular packing.

title: Generating cyan emission via ammonia-thermal nitridation: Na 18− x Ca 13+ x Mg 5 (PO 4− y N y ) 18 :Eu 2+
abstract: As phosphor-converted LEDs continue to displace traditional light technologies, developing new down-conversion materials remains essential for improving color quality and efficiency.

title: Pyrite–bismuth telluride heterojunction for hybrid electromagnetic-to-thermoelectric energy harvesting
abstract: The FeS 2 /Bi 2 Te 3 heterostructure RF–thermoelectric device, where RF absorption at the Ti/FeS 2 Schottky interface induces localized heating that is converted into electrical power by Bi 2 Te 3 .

title: Oxygen-induced downshifting and lanthanide upconversion luminescence in Sr 2 YbF 7 nanoparticles for dual-mode security applications
abstract: Oxygen-induced downshifting and Ho 3+ , Tm 3+ upconversion in Sr 2 YbF 7 nanoparticle-based luminescent ink exhibits excellent characteristics for dual mode security under UV and NIR light.

title: Indium-doped α-Ga 2 O 3 nanorod arrays for ultrasensitive solar-blind UV photodetector application
abstract: We designed a solar-blind UV photodetector based on In-doped Ga 2 O 3 arrays that demonstrates competitive performances with excellent photoelectric response times of 24.8 ms (rise) and 27.6 ms (decay), and high detectivity (1.55 × 10 14 Jones).

title: Phenazine-based non-fused electron acceptors for high-performance organic solar cells
abstract: Excellent low-cost non-fused ring electron acceptors (NFREAs) are important for future large-scale application of bulk heterojunction organic solar cells (BHJ OSCs).

title: Recent progress of inorganic phosphors in artificial plant cultivation LEDs
abstract: Absorption spectra of photoreceptor pigments together with corresponding activators for plant growth LEDs.

title: Tailoring the pseudocapacitive performance of hierarchical α-MoO 3 /CoS 2 nanostructures for enhanced electrochemical properties of aqueous symmetric supercapacitors
abstract: Interfacial coupling between α-MoO 3 and CoS 2 optimizes Na + storage and redox activity, delivering high energy density and stability in symmetric supercapacitors.

title: How far can a minority charge carrier of an organic semiconductor walk? An in situ observation by scanning photocurrent microscopy
abstract: This work focus on the direct observation of intrinsic minority charge carrier diffusion length of organic semiconductors, which is experimentally on the order of micrometers and corresponds to their good performance in thick films.

title: 3D-Printed polymer microstructures doped with lanthanide ions for ratiometric and lifetime-based luminescence thermometry
abstract: High-resolution 3D printing of Eu 3+ -doped polymer resins yields functional microstructures with temperature-sensitive luminescence. The resin modification enables remote thermal sensing by adding new optical functionality to the printed material.

title: A fast, efficient and reversible approach to enhance the electrical conductivity of carbon nanotube films
abstract: The modification of carbon nanotube (CNT) films is of great significance for improving their performance and expanding their applications.

title: Emerging sulfur quantum dots: from synthesis strategies to optical property engineering towards multidisciplinary applications
abstract: Sulfur quantum dots (SQDs), a novel class of semiconductor nanomaterials, have garnered increasing attention due to their exceptional luminescent properties, robust biocompatibility, low toxicity antibacterial activity, and facile functionalization.

title: A core–shell carbon–ceramic fibrous aerogel derived from aramid-polysilsesquioxane for broadband electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: The hierarchical carbon–ceramic fibrous aerogel with interconnected networks and a core–shell skeleton demonstrate exceptional promise for broadbandmicrowave absorption in extreme environments.

title: Effect of yttrium feeding time on the electrical and structural properties of atomic layer deposited Y-doped TiO 2 films for dynamic random-access memory capacitors
abstract: Controlling the Y feeding time enabled microstructural tuning and dielectric optimization in ALD-grown rutile TiO 2 capacitors.

title: Diphenylanthracene-based trimeric systems for efficient photon upconversion through triplet–triplet annihilation
abstract: Photon upconverting materials emit radiation that is higher in energy than the absorbed one. In TTA-UC, this is achieved through intermolecular triplet collisions. However, in specific cases, intramolecular triplet fusion can also contribute.

title: Reconfigurable amplified circularly polarized photo/electro-luminescence from simple solution self-assembly of a chiral deep-blue fluorene-based conjugated polymer
abstract: Through in situ self-assembly, a deep-blue fluorene-based conjugated polymer circularly polarized photo-luminescence is enhanced to g lum −0.21. The self-assembly process PLED improves performance and resulting CP-EL with g factor of −0.028.

title: A first-principles study of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon-mediated PbS QD/2D BP 0D/2D hybrid-dimensional systems
abstract: Our first-principles calculations demonstrate that PAHs-mediated hybrid PbS QDs/2D BP establishes type-II band alignment and reinforced π–π stacking, offering theoretical foundations for 0D/2D hybrid photodetector design.

title: Assembly of van der Waals structure from CVD-grown 2-dimensional materials using plasma-treated polyvinyl chloride
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) van der Waals (vdW) stacking structures have gathered significant attention owing to their unique properties.

title: Crystalline pseudorotaxane enabling dual-wavelength photothermal mechanical actuation
abstract: We report the synthesis and photothermal-mechanical conversion behavior of a photoresponsive pseudorotaxane comprising an ammonium salt bearing ferrocenyl and anthryl groups as the axle and dibenzo[24]crown-8 as the macrocyclic ring.

title: Precursor engineering for controlled self-doping and enhanced performance in MAPbCl 3 single crystals
abstract: This study demonstrates an innovative precursor engineering approach to control perovskite self-doping via stoichiometry optimization. Precise adjustment of the PbCl 2 /MACl ratio (MA + =CH 3 NH 3 + ) significantly enhances MAPbCl 3 single-crystal performance.

title: Ultrafast carrier and coherent phonon dynamics in van der Waals ferromagnet CrI 3
abstract: We investigate the dynamics of carriers and coherent phonons in CrI 3 . The relaxation of phonon A11g undergoes a sudden change at 60 and 220 K, which is attributed to the weakening of spin–phonon coupling and increased vacancy concentrations.

title: Stable operation of two-dimensional field-effect transistors with van der Waals integrated SrTiO 3 top-gate dielectrics
abstract: We present high-performance top-gated monolayer MoS 2 FETs with STO films. STO protection provides excellent stability in air: devices retain performance after month-long air exposure and resist degradation under 100 °C thermal/electrical stress.

title: Flexible organic–inorganic hybrid crystals of tin( iv ) chloride and naphthalenediimide: exploring elasticity, mechanochromism, and photothermal conversion
abstract: OIMHs incorporating NDI cations exhibit flexible deformation under stress and function as mechanochromic and photothermal conversion materials, demonstrating their soft and multifunctional nature.

title: Low-temperature atomic layer deposition of metastable MnTe films for phase change memory devices
abstract: This work demonstrates an atomic layer deposition (ALD) process for achieving the MnTe film with metastable β phase at a growth temperature of 100 °C.

title: Pi-extended hypervalent iodine macrocycles and their supramolecular assembly with Buckminsterfullerene
abstract: Hypervalent iodine based macrocycles with enlarged aromatic cores can form extended supramolecular assemblies with Buckminsterfullerene.
