title: Electronic, optical and structural properties of MnPS 3 : advanced spectroscopy and theoretical investigation
abstract: In the field of van der Waals semiconductor research, studying antiferromagnetic order in materials like MnPS 3 has highlighted potential applications in heterostructures.

title: Crystalline organic thin films for crystalline OLEDs (III): weak epitaxy growth of phenanthroimidazole derivatives with a dual inducing layer
abstract: Using a dual inducing layer is a novel strategy for growing high-quality crystalline thin films via weak epitaxy growth. This work shows that a designed dual inducing layer can address critical hole injection challenges in crystalline OLEDs.

title: Strong near-infrared saturable absorption of nanocrystalline TiN for pulse generation
abstract: TiN nanocrystalline films fabricated by magnetron sputtering exhibit ultrafast saturable absorption in the near-infrared regime, enabling stable mode-locked pulse generation in an Er-doped fiber laser.

title: Localized redox filament formation in biopolymer-based RRAM devices for in-memory volatile molecule sensing
abstract: Memristors and resistive random-access memory (RRAM) utilize a range of active ions, material compositions, and structural designs, each influencing switching performance in different ways.

title: A wireless, battery-free temperature sensor utilizing the morphotropic phase boundary of Hf x Zr 1− x O 2 thin film
abstract: This study presents a battery-free wireless temperature sensor using HZO morphotropic phase boundary dielectric changes and passive LC resonance for signal transmission.

title: Building-block-selective deposition of self-assembled monolayers for enhancing interface polarity in solution-processed thin film transistors
abstract: A selective SAM deposition method was developed to functionalize only the glass substrate in glass/ITO templates. This strategy positively affected the interface dipole and improved charge transport in p-type, n-type, and ambipolar transistors.

title: Magnetic assembly approach to hierarchical photonic stripes
abstract: Driven by shape anisotropy, magnetic Fe 3 O 4 @SiO 2 nanorods assemble into tetragonal photonic stripes through offset packing along a size-dependent critical angle rather than the well-known end-on assembly.

title: B-site substitution-induced band edge shifts in perovskite-type Cu(Nb,Ta)O 3 solid solutions for visible-light-driven hydrogen evolution
abstract: Band engineering in Cu(Nb,Ta)O 3 via Ta substitution and symmetry transition enables efficient visible-light-driven H 2 evolution.

title: Novel molecule design for surface modification enables highly selective DMMP detection with silicon nanoribbon FET sensors
abstract: A novel HFIPPUT molecule was designed and synthesized for surface modification of a silicon-based FET, which enables highly selective and sensitive detection of DMMP.

title: Flexible single-crystal fibers of tetraphenylphosphonium iodide for stimulated Raman scattering
abstract: High-quality flexible single-crystal fibers of tetraphenylphosphonium iodide, achieved via seeded rapid growth in solution at room temperature, exhibit effective stimulated Raman scattering, a low output threshold, and high laser stability.

title: Multimode stimulated luminescence of LiNbO 3 :Pr 3+ /ZnS:Cu/PDMS flexible films
abstract: Flexible films have multi-mode luminescence and can produce a variety of colors by changing the excitation mode.

title: Correction: Recent developments in emerging two-dimensional materials and their applications
abstract: Correction for ‘Recent developments in emerging two-dimensional materials and their applications’ by Karim Khan et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2020, 8 , 387–440, https://doi.org/10.1039/C9TC04187G.

title: Correction: Local structure regulation of multiple upconversion emission selectivity in highly doped lanthanide-based core–shell nanocrystals
abstract: Correction for ‘Local structure regulation of multiple upconversion emission selectivity in highly doped lanthanide-based core–shell nanocrystals’ by Yuxuan Qiu et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, 13 , 17109–17119, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TC00657K.

title: Self-activated nonreciprocal transmission isolation via absorption-asymmetry-triggered directional phase transition in VO 2 -based terahertz metamaterials
abstract: This study establishes a novel strategy for self-activated nonreciprocal transmission isolation, exploiting the absorption asymmetry to trigger directional phase transition under high-intensity illumination.

title: Versatile BODIPY dyes for advanced optoelectronics: random lasing, harmonics generation, and OLED application
abstract: New BODIPY derivatives exhibit multifunctional photonic behavior, including nonlinear optics and stimulated emission, with compatibility in diverse matrices and OLED perspectives for integrated optoelectronics.

title: Phase stability of iron oxides epitaxial thin films under O 2 , CO 2 and H 2 O environmental conditions
abstract: Phase stability of FeO, Fe 3 O 4 , and γ-Fe 2 O 3 thin films studied under O 2 , CO 2 , and H 2 O. Stability diagrams reveal deviations from bulk predictions. SrTiO 3 substrate acts as an oxygen buffer, influencing phase transitions.

title: Ultra-thin layer of oxygen vacant amorphous titania for enhanced photocatalysis
abstract: A ∼4 nm amorphous surface layer engineered via plasma sputtering significantly enhances TiO 2 photocatalytic activity, achieving a 13-fold improvement. The amorphous layer contains oxygen vacancies and four-coordinated Ti.

title: Enhancing thermal stability of n-type conduction in carbon nanotubes via cation replacement mediated by bicyclic guanidinium salts
abstract: Stabilization of n-type carbon nanotubes is achieved by separate n-doping and cation replacement processes utilizing bicyclic guanidinium cation as stabilizer.

title: Bacteria inspired soft robots with responsive flagellar bundles
abstract: Similar to peritrichous bacteria, soft robots consisting of a magnetic head and liquid crystal gel bi-flagella can undergo a “run-to-tumble” transition when the flagella bundle and unbundle in response to a temperature change.

title: Short-range aggregation regulation of conjugated polymers: high mobility and cyclic tensile stability driven by chemical crosslinking
abstract: By introducing chemical crosslinking to regulate the aggregation of the conjugated polymer films, excellent carrier mobility and stretchability can be achieved simultaneously. It shows great potential in wearable electronics.

title: Optoelectronic stimuli-driven switchable memristors with multilevel resistance states for neuromorphic vision sensors
abstract: The developed optoelectronic synaptic devices with resistive switching enable efficient, fault-resistant image recognition. The PMMA/ZnO/2IF memristor array advances neuromorphic vision systems, offering high accuracy and low power consumption.

title: Mott–vanadium dioxide-based memristors as artificial neurons for brain-inspired computing: a view on current advances
abstract: A review of correlated electron VO 2 -based memristors in neuromorphic circuitry towards memory computing and sensing applications.

title: Mechanochemical synthesis of a carbazole isomer phosphor with mechanofluorochromic and AIE properties
abstract: The carbazole isomer derivative Br-Bd was synthesized by mechanical ball milling, followed by the synthesis of Bd-TPE and Cz-TPE, which exhibit the properties of AIE, MFC and RTP.

title: Light-induced magnetic switching in a coumarin-based Tb single molecule magnet
abstract: [Tb(coum) 3 (batho)]·[0.7EtOH] is a versatile multifunctional molecular material combining single-ion magnet behavior, high quantum yield luminescence and light-induced magnetic switching.

title: Tailoring intraband transition via composition in self-doped Ag 2 S x Se y alloy nanocrystals
abstract: This study demonstrates that intra band transitions in Ag 2 S x Se y alloy colloidal quantum dots can be modulated by altering their composition. This capability expands material options, offering new prospects for infrared optoelectronic device design.

title: In situ formation of a low-dimensional perovskite structure for efficient single-crystal MAPbI 3 solar cells with enhanced ambient stability
abstract: The in situ formation of a low dimensional PEAI structure on a perovskite crystal surface.

title: Well-designed sunlight-like Bi 3+ -activated white-light phosphor for general lighting
abstract: We propose a simple but effective strategy for customizing the spectral behavior of Bi 3+ emissions using BO 3 –PO 4 polyanionic units. The as-prepared phosphors with multiple emissions enable full-time sunlight-simulation WLEDs.

title: Scalable all-polymer dielectric films with aligned structures for high-temperature energy storage applications
abstract: An all-polymer composite was prepared by precisely aligning polyvinylidene fluoride nanofibers within a polyetherimide matrix, achieving a high discharge energy density of 9.39 J cm −3 at 150 °C.

title: A rapid, green and cost-effective synthesis of pH- and hydroxyl group sensitive carbon dots for sensing applications
abstract: A rapid, green, and cost-effective method for synthesizing carbon dots (CDs) was developed using non-toxic, low-cost precursors via a simple combustion process.

title: Enhanced synaptic characteristics of electrolyte-gated oxide transistors enabled by optimizing interface states at the oxide semiconductor/electrolyte interface
abstract: Advanced bionic neuromorphic systems hold great promise for e-skin applications due to their efficient parallel processing and sensory capabilities.

title: Entropy engineering strategy boosting high-entropy spinel oxide electrode with impressive capacitance and energy density
abstract: Spinel oxides are promising supercapacitor electrode materials due to their abundant active sites and tunable composition, yet their low conductivity limits energy density.

title: Synergistic effects of Pd single atoms and nanoclusters boosting SnO 2 gas sensing performance
abstract: SnO 2 -supported Pd single atoms coupled with small cluster structures, incorporated into sensing platforms, exhibit exceptional performance and selectivity toward CO.

title: No strain, no piezoelectric gain: pushing piezoelectric boundaries via composition-dependent strain engineering in wurtzite scandium-doped aluminum nitride
abstract: By applying appropriate strain to scandium-doped aluminum nitride (Al 1− x Sc x N), the piezoelectric performance can be boosted by over 100 times! This strain-engineering charts a practical, lead-free path to high-performance piezoelectrics.

title: Rhombohedral-to-cubic structural phase transition in (LaBi) 0.7 Ba 0.3 MnO 3 manganites: an investigation on the “Cause and Effect” and magneto caloric response
abstract: Synthesis to application: a road map of the cause and effect of structural phase transition in La 0.7− x Bi x Ba 0.3 MnO 3 manganites, narrating the physical properties and scope for application in magnetic hyperthermia therapy.

title: Stretchable organic room temperature phosphorescence systems
abstract: Recent progress in stretchable organic room temperature phosphorescence systems is reviewed.

title: High performance BaTiO 3 –SrTiO 3 ceramics with refined microstructure obtained by current-controlled reactive flash sintering of mixed powders
abstract: Current-controlled flash sintering has been demonstrated to be a powerful tool for microstructural engineering in dielectric materials, providing new pathways for developing energy-efficient storage systems through enhanced electrical homogeneity.

title: Two dimensional CuInP 2 S 6 /h-BN/MoTe 2 van der Waals heterostructure phototransistors with double gate control
abstract: Ferroelectric materials enhance optoelectronic processes in CuInP 2 S 6 /h-BN/MoTe 2 photodetectors, improving photocurrent, suppressing dark current, and achieving high responsivity, making them promising for high-performance optical sensing.

title: Dual-use synthesis of an asymmetric anthraquinone heptyl viologen (AQHV) for solution and gel-polymer electrolyte-based electrochromic devices
abstract: Viologens, renowned for their exceptional color-switching properties, are widely utilized as electrochromic materials.

title: In situ degradation and radiation-responsive behavior of metal organic frameworks under high-energy irradiation: implications for sensing and shielding
abstract: This review examines the promise and challenges of metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) in high-radiation environments, focusing on their sensing and shielding applications.

title: Poria cocos waste-derived cellulose-based biochar as an advanced porous anode material for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The biochar converted from waste materials has shown excellent performance as an anode material for lithium-ion batteries (LIBs), and is considered to be one of the effective strategies to solve environmental pollution and energy shortage.

title: Synthesis of a novel DOPO-based ionic liquid flame retardant and its application in epoxy resin
abstract: In contrast to conventional flame retardants, this study presents the development of a reactive flame retardant that significantly enhances both flame resistance and mechanical properties.

title: Length-dependent thermopower of self-assembled monolayers of alkanethiolates depends on direction of temperature gradient
abstract: Self-assembled monolayers of alkanethiols on gold are often used as a benchmark for molecular junctions, particularly as a reference for length-dependence.

title: Temperature-dependent trion-induced tunability of the terahertz signal response enabled by intraband transitions in monolayer WS 2
abstract: Exploring temperature-dependent trion effects in monolayer WS 2 , enabling tunable terahertz signal response through intraband transition mechanisms.

title: Evidence of ferrimagnetism in Fe 3 GaTe 2 via neutron diffraction studies
abstract: The van der Waals material Fe 3 GaTe 2 exhibits long-range ferrimagnetism above room temperature as evidenced by temperature dependent neutron diffraction.

title: Influence of a diketopyrrolopyrrole spacer on the ultrafast nonlinear optical properties and excited state dynamics of dimeric zinc porphyrin molecules
abstract: This work highlights the significance of adding a DPP unit to the zinc-porphyrin core with ethynylene bridges to enhance third-order NLO properties under femtosecond laser excitation.

title: Defect mechanism of the Bi x Sb 2− x Te 3 alloy catalyst for lithium–oxygen battery applications
abstract: The concentration of antisite defects and holes is modulated by adjusting the molar ratio of Bi and Sb in the 2D layered Bi x Sb 2− x Te 3 cathode material, which act as catalytic sites to enhance the electrochemical performance of LOBs.

title: Excellent electromagnetic wave absorption performances of FeCoNiAlTi x high-entropy alloys with superior oxidation resistance
abstract: In this work, FeCoNiAlTi 0.6 HEA with strong electromagnetic-wave absorption performances and excellent oxidation resistance was developed by introducing the element Ti into the FeCoNiAl-based HEA to control the phase structure transformation.

title: Recent advances in structural designs and fabrication of flexible polymer composite films with high thermal conductivity and electromagnetic interference shielding performance
abstract: Various structural designs and fabrication of flexible polymer composite films with high thermal conductivity and electromagnetic interference shielding performance.

title: Pressure-mediated structural evolution and luminescent properties of MgGeO 3 :Mn 2+ nanoparticles: a new candidate for optical high-pressure sensors
abstract: MGO:Mn nanoparticles undergo a phase transition from Pbca to C 2/ c induced by high pressure. A red shift in photoluminescence spectra reveals high sensitivity of Mn 2+ emission to compression, making the material a promising optical pressure sensor.

title: Fabrication of single-crystal porous hedgehog-sphere structured TiO 2 –rGO aerogel as a strong broadband electromagnetic wave absorber
abstract: Excessive conductivity of graphene oxide (GO) often contributes to the inferior impedance matching performance of the material, which imposes extreme restrictions on the field of electromagnetic wave (EMW) absorption.

title: Enhanced three-photon absorption excited at near-infrared laser pulses and stability of MAPbBr 3 quantum dots encapsulated in mesoporous single MOF-5 crystals
abstract: The composite material MAPbBr 3 @MOF-5 exhibits not only significantly enhanced stability but also superior nonlinear optical properties under near-infrared laser pulse excitation.

title: An ultra-thin InO interlayer as an oxygen reservoir for defect passivation and enhanced ferroelectricity in hafnia devices
abstract: An ultrathin InO interlayer supplies oxygen to HZO, curing the interface and thinning the dead layer, enabling more uniform ferroelectric switching with lower leakage and improved reliability.

title: Recent progress of flexible and wearable ultrasound electronics: fabrication techniques and applications in healthcare
abstract: This review outlines mechanisms, fabrication techniques, and healthcare applications of flexible and wearable ultrasound electronics, and discusses challenges and future directions for advancing accessible intelligent healthcare.

title: Intensity-driven resurgence of reverse saturable absorption and vibrational modes in hexagonal silver nanoplates
abstract: We report a correlation between the unique third-order optical nonlinearity and mechanical vibrational modes in hexagonal silver nanocrystals (AgNCs-550) having localized surface plasmon resonance (LSPR) at 550 nm.

title: Bend, don't break: exploring the mechanochromic, AIE and nonlinear optical properties of carbazole–thiobarbituric acid hybrids
abstract: Multifunctional organic NLOphores are being developed to expand the market potential of organic NLO materials by combining NLO properties with other desirable functionalities.

title: X-ray-irradiation-induced photoluminescence and photochromic LiNbO 3 phosphors for anti-counterfeiting and X-ray imaging
abstract: We report novel LNO:0.01Eu 3+ oxide phosphors with unique photochromic behavior under X-ray irradiation, with potential applications in multimodal display encryption, information security detection, advanced anti-counterfeiting and photoelectrics.

title: An ion-conductive honeycomb hydrogel with triple network structures for 3D tactile sensing and interaction
abstract: A honeycomb hydrogel with triple networks enhances ion conductivity for 3D tactile sensing and interaction.

title: Comment on “Photodetection and scintillation characterizations of novel lead-bismuth double perovskite halides” by F. Maddalena et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2022, 10 , 11266
abstract: Reanalysis of Maddalena et al .'s data ( J. Mater. Chem. C. , 2022, 10 , 11266) reveals that the synthesized materials deviate from the claimed Cs 4 PbBi 2 X 12 double perovskite compositions.

title: Correction: The leakage current suppression mechanism in a RuO 2 /SrTiO 3 /Ru capacitor induced by introduction of an ultra-thin GeO 2 interfacial layer at the bottom interface
abstract: Correction for ‘The leakage current suppression mechanism in a RuO 2 /SrTiO 3 /Ru capacitor induced by introduction of an ultra-thin GeO 2 interfacial layer at the bottom interface’ by Heewon Paik et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/d5tc02736e.

title: Anomalous near-infrared emission and the underlying “structure-luminescence” correlation in the multi-peak emission system of KScF 4 :Yb 3+ ,Mn 2+
abstract: Anomalous broadband NIR emission (both in Stokes and UC) is achieved in KScF 4 :Yb 3+ ,Mn 2+ phosphor, which is attributed to the super-exchange coupled Mn Sc1 –Mn Sc2 dimers. This phosphor has application prospects in night vision, bio-imaging, etc .

title: A flexible stretchable pressure sensor featuring a carbon nanotube porous sponge bionic–skin stratum spinosum electrode for health monitoring
abstract: Flexible pressure sensors play a crucial role in human health monitoring.

title: Converting aggregation-induced emission of N-BF 2 merocyanines to colour-tunable ultralong room temperature phosphorescence via Dexter triplet energy transfer: circumventing restriction of intersystem crossing
abstract: The pure π–π* transition-based molecular design along with embedded intramolecular interactions and structural diversity endowed the guest N-BF 2 merocyanines with long τ P s, good Φ P s, and phosphorescence colour tunability via a simple TTET mechanism.

title: Induced blue, green, and red-colour circularly polarized luminescence from single dye-doped homochiral poly(lactic acid) microspheres
abstract: The supramolecular assembly of poly( l -lactic acid) with the fluorescent dye forms microspheres that display various-colour fluorescence. The microspheres generate induced circularly polarized luminescence with the dissymmetry factor of 3.1 × 10 −2 .

title: Symmetry-breaking induced transition among net-zero-magnetization magnets
abstract: Net-zero-magnetization magnets mainly include PT -antiferromagnets, altermagnets and fully-compensated ferrimagnets, and studying transitions among them is essentially research on symmetry breaking.

title: A metal–organic framework enhanced single network organohydrogel with superior low-temperature adaptability and UV-blocking capability towards human-motion sensing
abstract: A UiO-66-NH 2 nanoparticle reinforced organohydrogel with anti-freezing and UV-blocking properties was synthesized for sensing complex human movements and transmitting different messages even at subzero temperature.

title: Suppressing charge carrier recombination in halide perovskite solar cells by ferroelectric polarization
abstract: Cesium-based perovskite solar cells (PSCs) are favorable alternatives to address the photovoltaic performance issues of organic–inorganic halide perovskites.

title: Interface engineering of oxidized Mo electrodes for imprint stability and enhanced endurance in hafnia-based ferroelectric devices
abstract: Short O 2 RTA converts Mo into a MoO x -rich interface ( φ ≥ 5.5 eV), suppressing trap formation and stabilizing imprint in HZO. This in-situ route avoids sputtered MoO 3 and enables robust FTJ endurance.

title: Machine learning-guided discovery of multifunctional Nb 2 CX 2 MXenes (X = S, Se, Te): insights into mechanical properties and thermal conductivity
abstract: We have investigated the elastic properties and lattice thermal conductivity of Nb 2 CX 2 MXenes using MLFF. MLFF outperforms DFT in efficiency, revealing how surface functionalization tunes thermal transport for next-gen energy applications.

title: Nanoscale scintillating materials for X-ray imaging: fundamentals and applications
abstract: This review focuses on the recent progress of nano-scintillators (NSs) from fundamental theory, material properties, structural engineering to practical application, aiming to promote the innovative research of NSs in X-ray imaging.

title: Effect of ortho -fluorine substituted hole transport materials for perovskite solar cells: influence of rigid vs. flexible linkers
abstract: Newly developed HTMs with D–π–D structures often cause film aggregation due to rigid π-units. Incorporating flexible π-group reduces film aggregation, blocks air and moisture, and enhances efficiency and stability in perovskite solar cells.

title: Structural isomerization engineering of hole transport materials for efficient perovskite solar cells
abstract: TABD shows intramolecular non-covalent interactions, which facilitate better molecular planarity and enhanced hole mobility. Perovskite solar cells based on TABD exhibited high efficiency and excellent stability.

title: Polymer donors with a new electron-deficient unit for efficient organic solar cells
abstract: A new electron-deficient building block, dithienobenzobisthiadiazole, was designed to construct wide-bandgap polymer donors for efficient organic solar cells.

title: Magnesium halides as a lead-free family with unique optoelectronic properties
abstract: The synthesis and photoluminescence properties of Mg halides have been investigated. These materials have application prospects in white LEDs and anti-counterfeiting.

title: Laser-induced kirigami structures of liquid crystal elastomers for multimodal morphing
abstract: Laser-engraved LCE kirigami actuators enable multimodal morphing for smart soft robotics.

title: In situ hydrogen doping of titanium dioxide via PAALD for enhanced MIM diode performance in high-frequency applications
abstract: Metal–insulator–metal (MIM) diodes based on black titanium dioxide are fabricated and characterized for the first time by in situ hydrogen doping via plasma assisted atomic layer deposition (PAALD).

title: Polar-end 3,5-diarylisoxazole liquid crystal s with SmA and highly ordered CrE mesophases
abstract: Polar-end 3,5-diarylisoxazol liquid crystals (ILCs) with nitro, amine and bromine polar terminal groups were thermally and structurally characterized.

title: Correlation between persistent luminescence and mechano-luminescence of Sm 3+ -doped YPO 4
abstract: Herein, it was demonstrated that the persistent luminescence and mechanoluminescence of YPO 4 :Sm 3+ , although generated under different external stimuli, are produced from the same trapped charge carriers.

title: Synthesis and characterization of NFA-based polymers for solar cells with improved thermal stability
abstract: Design of polymers via Stille polymerization enables high-performance organic solar cells with improved stability, processability, and compatibility with non-halogenated solvents.

title: Weak Fermi level pinning and low barrier interfacial contact: 2D lead-free perovskites on multilayer GaN
abstract: GaN as an electron transport layer for the CsSrI 3 perovskite achieves low loss contact. The Ba–CsSrI 3 /GaN interface effectively overcomes both FLP and high barriers, exhibiting an interface electron transfer conductivity of up to 7.79 × 10 3 S cm −1 .

title: Advances in the syntheses, mechanisms and applications of room-temperature phosphorescent carbon dots
abstract: In this review, the structure and luminescence mechanisms of RTP-CDs are clearly demonstrated. Based on the potential strategies to optimize their luminescence performance, the applications and future perspectives of RTP-CD systems are presented.

title: Molecular arrangements in the first monolayer of Cu-phthalocyanine on In 2 O 3 (111)
abstract: Well-ordered organic layers on oxide surfaces are key for organic electronics. By combining STM, AFM, and DFT calculations, the coverage-dependent ordering of CuPc until a densely packed superstructure fully covers the surface is explored.

title: A mesogenic unit based low melting point solid additive for efficient and stable organic solar cells
abstract: In this work, a novel low melting temperature solid additive CB8-Br was designed and synthesized through a simple and reliable method, and a superior PCE of 18.12% with excellent device stability was obtained in CB8-Br-processed devices.

title: Tactile/thermal dual-modal perceptual platform by integrating a thermoelectric generator and a triboelectric nanogenerator with an oxide neuromorphic transistor
abstract: A tactile and thermal dual-modal perceptual platform was proposed by integrating a thermoelectric generator and a triboelectric nanogenerator with a proton-gated oxide neuromorphic transistor with a bottom gate and a co-planar gate configuration.

title: Macrocyclic covalent encapsulation of diketopyrrolopyrroles: dual-state emission and mechanochromism
abstract: A series of macrocycle-encapsulated diketopyrrolopyrrole derivatives and their corresponding polyurethane elastomers were synthesized. The encapsulated DPPs demonstrated dual-state emission and exhibited stimuli-responsive luminescence behaviors.

title: Frequency and temperature dependent dielectric properties of CoFe 2− x Y x O 4 : polarization and conduction mechanisms related to crystallographic symmetry and electronic transitions
abstract: We highlighted the enhancement of dielectric properties in CoFe 2 O 4 with yttrium substitution and explained its polarization and conduction mechanisms with respect to temperature and frequency from a novel perspective.

title: Construction of a partially rGO multifunctional aerogel with multi-stage pores for hydrophobicity, thermal insulation and efficient microwave absorption property
abstract: The superelastic partially rGO aerogel with multi-stage pores exhibits a remarkable RL min of −67.89 dB and an expanded EAB of 7.72 GHz, combining outstanding mechanical properties, hydrophobicity, thermal insulation and piezoresistive sensing.

title: Te 4+ doped zero-dimensional perovskites for dual-mode thermometry in electronic devices
abstract: Ratiometric and lifetime-technology temperature measurement are prominent techniques in the field of optical thermometry due to their advantages of visual detection and high sensitivity in temperature precision, respectively.

title: Influence of K 4 Nb 6 O 17 secondary phase on ferroelectric behavior of K 0.5 Na 0.5 NbO 3 thin films
abstract: K-enriched KNN target effectively prevents alkali cation deficiency, ensuring typical piezoelectric and ferroelectric behavior. In particular, it avoids the secondary phase K 4 Nb 6 O 17 in KNN thin films obtained by PLD using a stoichiometric target.

title: Low-voltage perovskite light-emitting transistors: a novel approach utilizing solution-processed high- k inorganic dielectrics for full-color emission
abstract: Low-voltage-operating RGB perovskite light-emitting transistors based on bandgap-tunable quasi-2D perovskite thin films are realized by incorporating a high- k dielectric layer of HfO x .

title: Effects of non-fused and fused substituents in quinoxaline-based central units on conformation, aggregation, and photovoltaic properties of non-fused ring electron acceptors
abstract: Substituents of the quinoxaline core influence the aggregation properties of S-shaped acceptors.

title: Ultra-high stretchable hydrogels with wet adhesion properties as wearable electronic devices for underwater environments
abstract: We report a hydrophobically associating hydrogel. This conductive hydrogel boasts excellent stretchability, outstanding anti-swelling ability, and strong underwater adhesion. The hydrogel-based sensor holds great promise for underwater communication.

title: A carbon dot-based ratiometric fluorescent probe for the fluorescence “on–off–on” detection of Fe 3+ and amitrole herbicides
abstract: A dual-carbon ratiometric probe enables highly sensitive dual-mode detection of Fe 3+ and herbicide AMT via an ‘on–off–on’ mechanism, integrated with smartphone colorimetry and wearable devices for environmental and biological sample analysis.

title: Coupling between ferroelasticity and magnetization in two-dimensional organic–inorganic perovskites (C 6 H 5 C 2 H 4 NH 3 ) 2 M Cl 4 ( M = Mn, Cu, Fe)
abstract: Magnetization shift is observed in two-dimensional organic–inorganic perovskite (C 6 H 5 C 2 H 4 NH 3 ) 2 FeCl 4 with coexistence of ferroelasticity and canted antiferromagnetism.

title: Fe/Mn-doped Chlorella biochar for enhanced metronidazole degradation via PMS activation: mechanistic insights into catalytic performance
abstract: The catalysis material generated from Chlorella powder modified by metals could activate PMS to generate radicals (SO 4 ˙ − , ˙OH) and non-radical ( 1 O 2 ), resulting in 94.3% degradation of MNZ within 120 min using 0.6 g L −1 catalyst and 1 mM PMS.

title: Additive manufacturing of electrically conductive polymers: a comprehensive review
abstract: This review highlights the rapid progress in 3D and 4D printing of electrically conductive polymers and critically outlines key challenges, such as printability and scalability, that must be overcome for broad industrial and biomedical applications.

title: Ab initio quantum transport simulation of the sub-1 nm gate-length monolayer and bilayer α-MoTe 2 field-effect transistors
abstract: This work used DFT + NEGF to explore sub-1 nm Lg ML/BL α-MoTe ₂ FETs. The n-type ML α-MoTe ₂ FETs meets ITRS criteria for HP/LP, extending the application of ML α-MoTe ₂ in sub-1 nm Lg FETs. The p-type ML and BL α-MoTe ₂ FETs failed to meet ITRS criteria.

title: Adhesive, biocompatible, and conductive reduced graphene oxide hydrogel-based bioelectrodes for epidermal electrophysiological signal monitoring
abstract: Adhesive, biocompatible, and conductive epidermal bioelectrodes based on reduced graphene oxide hydrogels were designed to monitor multiple electrophysiological biosignals during human physical/chemical activities.

title: Highly sensitive OFET based room temperature operated gas sensors using a thieno[3,2- b ]thiophene extended phthalocyanine semiconductor
abstract: A novel thienothiophene (TT) and phthalocyanine (Pc) based conjugated material was designed, synthesized, fabricated for an organic field effect transistor (OFET) and utilized as an OFET based gas sensor for hazardous gases such as NO 2 and SO 2 .

title: Carbon dot engineered trimetallic hydroxyl carbonates: a strategy for enhanced redox-diffusion coupled charge storage
abstract: A 3D flower-like Ni–Co–Zn hydroxyl carbonate on nickel foam shows redox-diffusion charge storage. Trachyspermum ammi carbon dots enhance performance by boosting conductivity and ion diffusion.

title: A hybrid-ligand exchange strategy for high-performance PbSe quantum dot short-wave infrared photodetectors
abstract: A hybrid-ligand exchange strategy significantly enhances the performance of PbSe colloidal quantum dot short-wave infrared photodetectors, increasing the external quantum efficiency from 2.8% to 31.4% at zero bias.

title: Zr-doping-assisted wet etching for non-destructive transfer of β-Ga 2 O 3 films to fabricate high-performance wearable ultraviolet photodetectors
abstract: Zr doping facilitates the non-destructive transfer of flexible β-Ga 2 O 3 films, significantly boosting solar-blind detection performance and enhancing corrosion resistance.
